Cicero.line: For the Night Mother!
...sweet Night Mother.
Ooh. That had to hurt.
You... you did? He has?
You're as good as dead!
Come on, show yourself!
Must've scared 'em off.
That's the end of that.
Anything good in there?
Until next time, sister.
I'm going to find you...
Until next time, brother.
You'll... pay for this...
I knew I heard something!
Oh, yes. Goodbye, goodbye.
With pleasure, my Listener!
Ooh! Listener! That tickles!
Cicero bids you... farewell.
And another soul for Sithis.
How may I serve the Listener?
Humble Cicero lives to serve.
Stab you, stab you, stab you!
I'm bleeding! Ha ha ha ha ha!
And killed them. In his honor.
What? Who is the Night Mother?
He he he he he he he he he he.
I must've been hearing things.
That'll teach you to cross me.
And there is... no... Listener!
Nothing else? I'm always eager.
Guess it was nothing after all.
Such was my... honor. As Keeper.
You want me to do something or not?
Wait... the Listener is serious? Oh.
Is that the best the Listener can do?
Come on! Do it again! Ha ha ha ha ha!
Ow... It hurts when I laugh. He he...
The silence became almost... maddening.
Ha! Surely you're pulling Cicero's leg!
You caught me! I surrender! Ha ha ha ha.
You are an inspiration. Do you know that?
Oh, of course. Mother needs me, after all.
Ooh. What a fun trick. Teach me, teach me!
Oh, we have unfinished business you and I.
Yes, Listener? How can Cicero be of service?
Oh yes, Cicero and the Listener! On the hunt!
Oh yes, mistress. You're the "boss." For now.
Oh yes, mistress. Perfectly! You're the boss.
Oh Listener. Can't you see that's impossible?
You require the services of the Fool of Hearts?
Poor mother... Her new home seems so very far...
Oh, I like that! Very good, very good! Creative!
Oh! Oh, you jest! You jest with gullible Cicero!
Our sweet Lady is maiden, mother... and crone...
Oh, yes yes yes! Thank you, thank you, thank you!
...tra la la, tra la lee, da da dum dum, dee dee...
What have I done, Listener? Has Cicero offended you?
What? Why would the stranger attack innocent Cicero?
Now Loreius. Loreius has learned the meaning of ugly.
What do you expect to find? My juggling balls? He he.
Oh. Oh yes! Yes, the kindly stranger can certainly help!
And now we come to the end of our play. The grand finale.
Cicero is at your service, oh great and powerful Listener!
See if they have any cheese. I'm getting a bit... peckish.
It's... it's like watching an artist create a masterpiece.
Does it involve sneaking and stabbing? Ooh, please say yes!
He he he... Crazy? Cicero? He he he he... That's madness...
You will talk when you're ready, won't you? Won't you...
...Cicero is hungry... ...need a sweetroll... or a carrot...
Damn that Loreius. Oh, how he will pay for this treachery...
If I had anything worth giving, I'd have offered it already.
...need to get Mother some flowers... pretty, pretty flowers...
Hey, that's my lint! Ha, Cicero is just joking. You can have it.
...must oil Mother soon... ...get all the hard to reach places...
Are you excited? Oh, you should be. Things are finally looking up!
Another member of the Family! Hello, hello. So very good to meet you.
Home again, home again! I'll return to the Night Mother straightaway!
For someone called the "Listener," you do an awful lot of shouting...
...oh if I chance to see a cat, I'll feed its corpse to my pet rat...
...and if I spy a singing bird, I'll snap its neck before it's heard...
Oh, what a kind and wise wizard you are. Sure to earn our Lady's favor.
Why talk? Cicero has nothing to say to you. Call it a matter of... trust.
...need to sharpen my blade... make it shiny, gleamy, and oh so deadly...
...dear Cicero will keep you from harm, sweet Mother. Forever and always...
No time to talk now, my friend. No time. Out mistress needs tending. Keeping.
Traitor? Me? Silly assassin. So confused, so confused... and they say I'm mad!
I am! I am! But not just my mother. Our mother, hmm? The Night Mother! Oh yes!
I am! I am! But not just my mother. Our mother, hmm? The Night Mother! Oh yes!
I am! I am! But not just my mother. Our mother, hmm? The Night Mother! Oh yes!
You are the Listener! You are the Listener! I have served mother well, I have!
...Oooh... Cicero wants some Skyforge Steel! Sharp sharp, for easy stabbing...
Cicero can talk for a moment. Just a moment. Then Mother must be... tended to.
Hmph. "Solitude." Lonely Cicero could tell you a thing or two about solitude...
Wait, oh wait. I know you! Yes, yes. From the road! Cicero never forgets a face.
...when I next meet, that fair maid Nelly, I'll plunge my knife into her belly...
Do what you will. Cicero has no fight left. In the end, Sithis will judge us both.
Ha! Why you are, silly! You are the Listener! You are the Listener! Such a kidder...
...I saw a dwarf! I did! I did! Cicero saw a dwarf! There... Oh. No, sorry. No dwarf...
Oh thank you, kind stranger. Thank you for helping Cicero and his poor, stranded mother.
She... she said that? She said those words... to you?  "Darkness rises when silence dies"?
And you helped me! You helped poor Cicero! You talked to Loreius, got him to fix my wheel!
Riften. Cicero likes Riften. Cheats and ruffians and cutthroats abound. Finally, some fun.
Ahhh.... gotcha! Oh, Listener, you should see the look on your face! Ha ha ha ha ha ha ha!
Oh, but how can I defend you? How can I exert your will? If you will not speak? To anyone!
Ha ha! Behold the final trick of the Fool of Hearts! You think me near death? Think again!
Oh, the Listener, well... listens! The Night Mother speaks to you. Guides you on your path.
...Forsworn think they're so scary... Cicero will show you who's scary... Stupid Forsworn...
If I'm a traitor, so are you! Have you not heard the maiden's voice? Are you not the Listener?
In the meantime, I'll make myself at home in the Sanctuary. I'm sure Mother needs... tending.
Now wait a minute, Listener. Even Cicero has his limits! Not many, mind you... but I do have them...
Oh, but the Listener jests with poor Cicero. You already have company. You don't need my help at all.
...and I said to the baker, "You're not dead! You're a faker!" But if that's your wish, I'll oblige...
Ah, it doesn't matter. What's done is done, right? Cicero is here! As is the Night Mother! Joy of joys!
I will wait for Loreius! Oh yes, mother and I will wait right here, right here until he fixes our wheel.
Oh, all right then, Listener. Cicero will just carry on with what he was doing. Until you say otherwise.
...madness is merry, and merriment's might, when the jester comes calling with his knife in the night...
Agh! Bother and befuddle! Stuck here! Stuck! My mother, my poor mother. Unmoving. At rest, but too still!
Oh, you prefer to listen, eh? Of course, of course! The Listener listens! A joke! A funny joke! I get it.
Convince Loreius to fix my wheel! Do that, and poor Cicero will reward you. With coin! Gleamy, shiny coin!
Oh, I do wish we could get to know each other better. But not now! I must tend to mother. See her settled.
Hmm... That's like telling you about the cold of space, or terror of midnight. Sithis is all those things.
Oh, I do wish to get to know you better. But I must tend to mother. Maybe we can talk for just a moment...
Poor mother... she's been waiting here so long... Stupid farmer and his stupid tools! Why won't he help us?
So Cicero brought our Lady to her new home. Here! This is the only Sanctuary left in all of Skyrim, you see.
...and he says to the man, "That's not a horker! That's my wife!" Ha ha ha ha ha... Ah... I love that one...
...What? Mother? Is that your voice I hear? Hmm... No, no... Just my head playing tricks... Foolish Cicero...
It's true, it's true! The Night Mother has spoken! The silence has been broken! The Listener has been chosen!
...is there singing in the Void? Dancing...? Surely the Dread Lord will at least allow poor Cicero to caper...
Here, here. For your troubles! Shiny, clinky gold! A few coins for a kind deed! And thank you! Thank you again.
Oh stranger! You have made Cicero so happy! So jubilant and ecstatic! But more! Even more! My mother thanks you!
The Night Mother is part of you now! You get to hear voices inside your head! The rest of us should be so lucky.
Oh, you may have pleased me, but you have surely pleased the Night Mother. And our mother, she will never forget.
He said I was transporting weapons! For the war! In mother's coffin! He made me... open it! Indignity! Sacrilege!
Oh, but this isn't at all what Mother would want. You kill the Keeper or I kill the Listener? Now that's madness.
But I am not the Listener. Oh no. You are the Listener! You heard the words! And everything will soon be put right.
Oooh... So silent. So menacing. A true assassin of the old way. Cicero likes you. Oh yes, Cicero likes you, indeed.
...Cicero never understood thieves, really... take someone's things before you kill them? And they call me crazy...
The farmer is at his farm! Where else would he be? Loreius is his name. Talk to him. Convince him to help poor Cicero.
She... spoke to you? More treachery! More trickery and deceit! You lie! The Night Mother speaks only to the Listener!
I'm taking mother to a new home. A new crypt. But... aggh! Wagon wheel! Damnedest wagon wheel! It broke! Don't you see?
But you disappeared! You were there... then you weren't! Did you mean to help poor Cicero or not? I'm left to wonder...
Oh, but Cicero can't talk to you. You've been naughty, naughty. I think you need to clear this whole mess up with Nazir.
Oh! Oh! Maybe Cicero will go to Jorrvaskr and dance for the Companions! They'll... Um... On second thought... maybe not.
Oh... Well... yes. I did. I did indeed. I tried to listen. Tried so hard. But the Night Mother never spoke to poor Cicero.
Are we alone? Yes... yes... alone. Sweet solitude. No one will hear us, disturb us. Everything is going according to plan.
Poor Cicero is stuck. Can't you see? I was transporting my dear, sweet mother. Well, not her. Her corpse! She's quite dead.
He said I stole something! Me! Poor Cicero. A common thief. The very thought is insulting. One might even say... maddening.
Poor Cicero has failed you. Poor Cicero is sorry, sweet mother. I've tried, so very hard. But I just can't find the Listener.
Well of course the Night Mother will speak to you again! She might speak now, or later, or... I don't know! But speak she will!
...the High King's court needs a jester... But not me. No, not Cicero. The Fool of Hearts is busy enough, thank you very much...
What? What treachery! Defiler! Debaser and defiler! You have violated the sanctity of the Night Mother's tomb! Explain yourself!
But killing me would be a mistake! Oh yes. You would displease our mother, hmm? For she's your mother too, isn't she... Listener?
So polite! So nice! Cicero likes you. The Night Mother is sure to like you too. Oh, we're going to be fast friends. Fast friends.
And what do you do? Nothing! Not... not that I'm angry! No, never! Cicero understands. Heh. Cicero always understands! And obeys!
Then... it is true! She is back! Our Lady is back! She has chosen a Listener! She has chosen you! Ha ha ha! All hail the Listener!
What? What treachery! Defiler! Debaser and defiler! You have violated the sanctity of the Night Mother's coffin! Explain yourself!
Oh, Cicero takes care of our Lady's body. Oils it, preserves it, keeps it safe. Makes sure nobody disrespects our Matron's coffin.
He said I attacked someone! On the road! But it never happened. Never. Sweet mother and I were just waiting. My knife was sheathed!
The Night Mother's crypt in Bravil was... desecrated. The Imperial Province is ravaged by strife. Nowhere there is safe, at present.
Kill me if you must. But I can't promise I won't haunt you till the end of your days if... if the Dread Lord lets me. Ha ha ha ha ha.
Listener! Is that you? Oh, I knew you'd come. Send the best to defeat the best. Astrid knew her stupid wolf couldn't slay sly Cicero.
You of course know that the Night Mother is our Unholy Matron. The undying spirit of a great woman who birthed the children of Sithis.
What about you? Have you... have you spoken to anyone? No.... No, of course not. I do the talking, the stalking, the seeing and saying!
But the Night Mother is mother to all! It is her voice we follow! Her will! Would you dare risk disobedience? And surely... punishment?
I spoke only to the Night Mother! I spoke to the Night Mother, but she didn't speak to me. Oh no. She spoke only to her! To the Listener!
I spoke only to the Night Mother! I spoke to the Night Mother, but she didn't speak to me. Oh no. She spoke only to him! To the Listener!
Wh... What? A crime? Me? Preposterous! Poor Cicero is just standing here! Cicero loves order. Oh yes! He would never disrespect the law!
Go to the farm - the Loreius Farm. Just over there, off the road. Talk to Loreius. He has tools! He can help me! But he won't! He refuses!
Return to the pretender, tell her I'm dead! Tell her you strangled me with my own intestines! Ha ha! But lie! Yes, lie! Lie, and let me live!
Oh, well, the Listener is the only person the Night Mother speaks to. It is the highest honor attainable by a member of the Dark Brotherhood.
Someone lied. Someone told the guard poor Cicero did something he did not. We were detained, delayed! The Night Mother kept from her new home!
Oh, but you didn't help poor Cicero! You helped that stupid farmer! Stupid Loreius! Oh, the guard said cruel things about us. Untrue things...
You must realize what a great honor this is. For the Night Mother to have chosen you... I am just in awe. You have Cicero's utmost confidence!
Me? Oh, Cicero is just the Keeper! I... keep! I look after our matron, you see. The Night Mother. I keep her clean, and protected, and happy...
Me? Oh, Cicero is just the Keeper! I... keep! I look after our matron, you see. The Night Mother. I keep her clean, and protected, and happy...
Walk away! Let poor Cicero live! Tell the pretender Astrid you did the job! Stabbed, strangled, drowned poor Cicero! One little itty bitty lie!
So walk away! Let poor Cicero live! Tell Astrid you did the job! Stabbed, strangled, drowned poor Cicero! A tiny white lie for a dark assassin!
Then listen to this - don't kill me. Let poor Cicero live! I attacked the strumpet Astrid, I did! And I'd do it again! Anything for our mother!
But I am not the Listener. Oh no. There is no Listener. Not yet! But some day, some day, some day I pray, that one will come to hear her say...
Unfair! Unjust! Cicero has done nothing wrong! Loreius will pay for his treachery! Oh, how he will pay... And you! You were no help! Not at all!
...ho ho ho, and hee hee hee, break that lute across my knee... and if the bard, should choose to fight, why then I'll set his clothes alight...
Kill me or don't, Listener! The power is yours! But spare poor Cicero, and a friend you will have! Oh, such a friend, until we enter the Void...
Do you mistrust poor Cicero? I am, as you say, just an innocent jester. A fool! Oh, but my work is not foolish, no. For I am our mother's Keeper.
Ah, but there has not been a Listener in years and years. Our Lady has not chosen Cicero, and certainly not Astrid. Or... anyone. But some day...
Oh yes, it is Cicero! You were a fool to spare me. What, did you think I would be grateful? Cicero should be Listener! Not you! Now you will die!
Oohhh... Cicero's heard about the Butcher. Interesting knifework. Just... Stab, stab, stab, stab, stab! And then... Stab, stab, stab, stab, stab!
Of course our Lady is dead, so she doesn't talk with words, since her lips are... rotted. But inside the Listener's head. I hear it's... intimate.
The others... I've spoken to them. And they're coming around, I know it. The wizard, Festus Krex... perhaps even the Argonian, and the un-child...
Looks like Cicero's not the only only one to go a little crazy from time to time. Eh, Listener? You should talk to Nazir, work this whole mess out.
Ouch! Pointy pointy! My home is well defended. I always have been a stickler for details. Get it? "Stick-ler." Ha ha ha ha ha ha ha! Oh, I slay me!
If it's any comfort, I do feel slightly bad about Veezara. Stupid lizard got in my way! But please tell me that hulking sheepdog has bled to death.
You're... still alive. Cicero respects the Listener's abilities, of course, but could you at least slow down a bit? I'm not what I used to be. Heh.
Oh, but that was then! This is now! You're the Listener, and the Night Mother chose you for a reason, I'm sure! Cicero will remain the happy Keeper.
But you wouldn't know anything about that, would you? Hmmm? No. No... Of course not. Because then Cicero would get angry, and that would be... ugly.
Oh, Cicero has returned! Not to kill the kind Listener, but to serve, until one of us dies horribly, in service to our Mother! Best friends forever!
Cicero admits, he thought the Listener would be dead by now. Heh. Maybe we could just forget all this? Hmm? Let bygones be bygones? What do you say?
Brrr! Chilly! You'll enjoy this. Not an original part of the Sanctuary, per se. Let's call it a "forced addition." Forced by what? Oh, come and see!
But those are the words. The Binding Words. Written in the Keeping Tomes. The signal so I would know. Mother's only way of talking to sweet Cicero...
But when the Night Mother speaks, the Listener must obey. You must! For her word is the will of Sithis. And Sithis is the Dark Brotherhood incarnate.
Yes, Listener! I will remain in this very spot until you tell me otherwise. Spiders could eat my face off and faithful Cicero would not move an inch!
All right, so Cicero attacked that harlot, Astrid! But what's a fool to do, when his mother is slandered and mocked? Surely the Listener understands!

Brynjolf.line: I'll give no quarter!
You need to hear this!
No, it... it can't be.
I can smell your fear.
No mercy for the weak!
I hardly felt a thing.
Where'd you come from?
I... Willl... kill you!
You're as good as dead!
Is that all you've got?
You fight like a child!
Come on, show yourself!
You can't hide forever!
Must've scared 'em off.
That's the end of that.
Now, on to other things.
Was there anything else?
Until next we meet, lad.
Until next we meet, lad.
Come on, put up a fight!
Finally! Let's end this.
I'm going to find you...
Maybe you'd like a taste?
Maybe you'd like a taste?
Changed your mind I hope?
Have anything for us yet?
Was there something else?
Until next we meet, lass.
Until next we meet, lass.
How are you feeling, Vex?
Enough of this! Fight me!
You'll... pay for this...
I knew I heard something!
What wasn't I clear about?
So, it's to the pain then!
All right, all right. Here.
You can't get away from me!
Grow back that missing limb!
Live for thousands of years!
And that's why I'm the best.
I trust everything went well?
Keep following, it isn't far.
Only twenty septims a bottle!
This way everyone! Over here!
I think they got the message.
Aye, you've got a point there.
I'll be here when you're done.
Let me put it to you this way.
Vex! Put it away... right now.
Now everyone get back to work.
I must've been hearing things.
That'll teach you to cross me.
You're trying my patience, lad.
I can't think of anyone better.
I'm sorry lass, I... I can't...
Guess it was nothing after all.
You're trying my patience, lass.
I'm just as puzzled as you, lad.
Damn it! There's no way through.
My blade thirsts for your blood!
I didn't think you'd get pinched.
Quickly, we have no time to lose.
I'm just as puzzled as you, lass.
Yes, lad? Did you need something?
See into other people's thoughts!
Come out and face me, you coward!
Okay lad, ready to make some coin?
What's become of the Skeleton Key?
Now go and make us some coin, lad!
Yes, lass? Did you need something?
Everyone! Everyone! Gather 'round!
Wield powerful magic incantations!
I promise a swift but painful end!
Killing him was a bit... impulsive.
Okay lass, ready to make some coin?
Come back when you grow a backbone.
Now go and make us some coin, lass!
Aye, lass. Nothing's getting by me.
Ah good, I could use some practice!
Look, I'll make this simple for you.
Eyes open and walk with the shadows.
I guess I expected too much from you.
I suppose it's not a total loss then.
Keerava, Bersi Honey-Hand and Haelga.
Let me take a look at what you found.
Was there anything else she told you?
Good to see you, lad! How are things?
It's just this way. Keep following me.
Then I have an important task for you.
Good to see you, lass! How are things?
Damn, just as I was getting warmed up.
I can't believe this is happening, lad.
It's good to see you in one piece, lad!
Things are looking up around here, lad.
No pushing, no shoving. Plenty of room!
Shor's Bones! Look at that monstrosity.
Hmm. The gate below still looks closed.
It's all about sizing up your mark, lad.
No, no. Mercer wants him alive, for now.
I can't believe this is happening, lass.
It's good to see you in one piece, lass!
Things are looking up around here, lass.
Mercer, aren't you forgetting something?
Again? Buy some food this time will you?
Aye, lass... and we'll be ready for him.
Do you want me to hold your hand as well?
Do you want me to hold your hand as well?
It's all about sizing up your mark, lass.
Passing on a golden opportunity is worse.
Keep your eyes on him, he's quite crafty.
Until then, we have quite the task ahead.
Until then, we have quite the task ahead.
By Talos, Mercer has a lot to answer for.
I'm telling you, this one is different...
The stench... this place reeks of Falmer.
Aye, lass... and I've come to a decision.
So tell me, did Aringoth enjoy your visit?
Out with it. What happened at the meadery?
This is enough to make your head spin, eh?
Delvin, please tell me you have good news.
Come on. Show me you've got what it takes!
Running a little light in the pockets, lad?
I'm ready when you are. Just give the word.
I'm busy right now, lad. We can talk later.
Running a little light in the pockets, lass?
All right, then. Let's put that to the test.
Not now, lad. Karliah said it was important.
I'm busy right now, lass. We can talk later.
We can talk after the Guild Master ceremony.
You just made the last mistake of your life!
He's as pig-headed a man as you'll ever find.
Better question would be "what did he leave."
Not now, lass. Karliah said it was important.
Please hurry, I have only a few bottles left!
Who knows what powers the elixir will unlock!
Aye. Whatever you want to do, we're with you.
Now what's this so-called proof you speak of?
That's what happens when you cross the Guild.
All eyes are on you, lad. Don't disappoint us.
Oh come now, resorting to petty tricks are we?
All eyes are on you, lass. Don't disappoint us.
I don't have a clue. That door is impenetrable.
That'd be Vald. A real piece of work, that one.
Lads and lasses I give you, Falmerblood Elixir!
Looks like I chose the right person for the job.
All right, I'll entertain you a bit more. Go on.
Ha! Spoken like the leader of the Thieves Guild.
Learn a library's worth of knowledge in moments!
Actually, I admire how you handled the situation.
It's under your feet, lad. Just follow the smell.
I'll give you one good reason. Maven Black-Briar.
Dying breed, eh? Well what do you call that then!
Mercer was able to knock this thing down? Gods...
This can't be true. I've known Mercer too long...
And in my line of work, extra hands are well-paid.
It's under your feet, lass. Just follow the smell.
Okay, lass. We've got these getups on... now what?
What's... what's happening... I can't stop myself.
I think we should trust the lass and take the deal.
Let's get out of here before we end up like Mercer!
What sort of arrangement? I need to know the terms.
Meet me then if you've still got the stomach for it.
And here you go... your payment, just as I promised.
I think you'll do more than just fit in around here.
Here you go, I think you'll find these quite useful.
Of course you'll get a cut. We take care of our own.
So Sibbi finally got the nerve to bare his teeth, eh?
Aringoth sold Goldenglow? What's that idiot thinking?
Hey, I'm not done talking to you yet. Come back here!
So this is what we heard. The entire tower collapsed.
Sorry... I usually have a nose for this kind of thing.
Sorry... I usually have a nose for this kind of thing.
Arrogant, eh? Play it however you want, but listen up.
I want you to explain to them the error of their ways.
Without them at our backs, we'd be in serious trouble.
Blast. Well, I'll check my sources and speak to Mercer.
Mercer took everything. Even all of our plans are gone.
Aye, there will be blood today. I can promise you that.
That means your out. No jobs, no payouts... no nothing.
I can't wait to get my hands around that bastard's neck.
No tricks, Karliah or I'll cut you down where you stand.
If it means the end of Mercer Frey, you can count me in.
Your pockets... they're a little low on coin. I can tell.
Now, you tell me when you're ready and we'll get started.
Keerava's stubborn, but she's got a soft spot for family.
Of course... your pay. Haha! You're smart as a whip, lad.
I'd just assume die before I'd let Mercer get those gems.
Only twenty gold septims! Hurry before my supply is gone.
Then this is it. We do this for Gallus and for the Guild.
Of course... your pay. Haha! You're smart as a whip, lass.
Maven has been nagging us about her request. Any thoughts?
Well done. And it would seem I owe you something in return.
Word on the street is Goldenglow's been hit. Good job, lad.
No time for idle chatter. Mercer is awaiting your presence.
Never mind then, lad. If you change your mind, come find me.
I'll be at the market come morning. You better be there too.
Word on the street is Goldenglow's been hit. Good job, lass.
Mercer's holding something over his head, keeping him loyal.
Karliah and I had a long discussion before you arrived here.
Karliah and I had a long discussion before you arrived here.
I think we can trust Karliah. Let's see what she's on about.
Do the Falmer also have no sense of smell? This place reeks!
You need anything, anything at all... don't hesitate to ask.
Never mind then, lass. If you change your mind, come find me.
Dumping bodies and keeping the guards quiet can be expensive.
Don't sound so eager, it's not a social visit. It's business.
Aye, and some of what Karliah said is starting to make sense.
The way they walk, what they're wearing. It's a dead giveaway.
The way they walk, what they're wearing. It's a dead giveaway.
Oh, but that's where you're wrong, lad. Wealth is my business.
Oh, no. Nothing like that. You're calling on her for business.
That's between you and Maven and I prefer to keep it that way.
That's between you and Maven and I prefer to keep it that way.
Until you're done with Vex, we don't have anything to discuss.
Oh, but that's where you're wrong, lass. Wealth is my business.
The catch is that you can't burn the whole place to the ground.
Let me show you the operation, then you can ask your questions.
Mercer? This is the one I was talking about... our new recruit.
Just keep your ears open and your mouth shut and you'll do fine.
Aye, the last thing we want to be doing is crossing our clients.
Then this is beyond coincidence. First Aringoth and now Sabjorn.
I can take a hint, lad. You want to make some coin, come find me.
When I'm done here, I'd be more than happy to sell you an elixir.
What is it with you and all these questions? Very well, ask away.
I suggest you listen to what Mercer has to say... if you want in.
Exactly! Now you're beginning to see how our little system works.
Maven wants to see you right away. I suggest you head right over.
You better have a damn good reason to be here with that murderer.
I have something amazing to show you that demands your attention!
I can take a hint, lass. You want to make some coin, come find me.
Reliable and headstrong? You're turning out to be quite the prize!
Trying to make Mercer look bad in front of Maven, eh? Clever lass.
Just take a step back and let me figure this out. Then we'll talk.
Sorry Mercer, they no longer wish to be involved in our operation.
You have wax in your ears? I said no one dies. We're not assassins.
Now if you need any details on your marks, I'll be here. Get going.
At least you remembered one of the things I asked. Let me see that.
That important client Mercer mentioned would be furious if you did.
And if I were you I'd hurry. I've never seen him this angry before.
If I'm not being clear enough, that means we don't want him killed.
Crime is one thing, murder is another. Mercer will answer for this.
Please, please... hold all your questions until after I've finished!
Keep doing right by us and there's plenty more where that came from.
Keep doing right by us and there's plenty more where that came from.
It's as if the merchandise and coin are simply falling from the sky.
We can't afford to lose our heads... we need to calm down and focus.
That was a simple misunderstanding, but this item is the real thing.
By the Eight! It's gone, everything's gone! Get in here, all of you!
Lost it? Well, at least you showed signs of initiative by telling me.
I'm certain Maven wishes you to be in Whiterun, not wasting time here.
Good luck in Solitude. Keep Gulum-Ei alive, but remind him who we are.
Thank you, Vex. You've always been the most reliable one in the Guild.
Well, I see that my time is up. Come back tomorrow if you wish to buy.
Talk to Vex. She used to know him very well... if you catch my meaning.
I'm good at what I do, maybe even one of the best. But it's all I know.
I have some preparations of my own to make. I'll meet you at the stone.
The Eyes would go a long way in helping get the Guild back on its feet.
I'll bet my last septim that there's a mechanism around here somewhere.
Glad to see you finally came to to your senses. Ready to make some coin?
Get there in one piece and we'll see if you've really got what it takes.
Can't say I'm surprised with the way things have been going around here.
I've smoothed things over with her for now, but you can forget your cut.
I just wanted to give you a proper thank you for everything you've done.
Sorry, lad... I've got important things to do. We'll speak another time.
Get the damn Key and let's get out of here before we end up like Mercer!
Good. If you need any details on your marks, I'll be here. Now get going.
No risk, no reward. That's the way it goes, so you better get used to it.
Sorry, lass... I've got important things to do. We'll speak another time.
Well, I see your wounds have healed but your pride still needs some time.
I've got a bit of an errand to perform, but I need an extra pair of hands.
When you come to your senses, find me there and we'll discuss your future.
Aye. Aringoth sent the city guard packing and fortified the entire island.
In return, Maven allowed us to extort Aringoth and bring in a huge payout.
You watch yourself on that island. Those mercenaries don't take prisoners.
The Guild is back on its feet again and on its way to a prosperous future.
I have. I think we may have to turn this one over to the Dark Brotherhood.
Then when you have the ring, you pickpocket Brand-Shei and leave it behind.
Besides, you look like your pockets are a little light on coin, am I right?
I guess I shouldn't be surprised with the way things are going around here.
They owe our organization some serious coin and they've decided not to pay.
Do this right, and I can promise you a permanent place in our organization.
Careful at Mercer's place, I don't want to lose anyone else to that madman.
Use your key on the vault, Delvin. We'll open it up and find out the truth.
And I'm busy. You help me out, and I'll help you out. That's just how it is.
I'm going to put my neck on the line. We need people like you in our outfit.
We've run into a rough patch lately, but it's nothing to be concerned about.
You're going to have to buy him off; it's the only way to get his attention.
Stubborn or not, he's the only contact we've got at the East Empire Company.
Be careful, lad. This is the last place in Skyrim I'd ever want to send you.
Delvin, Vex... watch the Flagon. If you see Mercer, come tell me right away.
Two reasons. He's rich and because we need every sword we can get right now.
Be careful, lass. This is the last place in Skyrim I'd ever want to send you.
Time's wasting and Mercer's still out there. Let's get this show on the road.
Good. Wait until I start the distraction and then show me what you're made of.
So I've heard. Thanks to you, this should be the last time they forget to pay.
A word of warning though... I don't want any of them killed. Bad for business.
No. It's some sort of crazy contraption Mercer commissioned for quick escapes.
You've done it now, whelp. You broke the rules and now you're knee deep in it.
Well, well... color me impressed, lad. I wasn't certain I'd ever see you again!
Oh, and talk to Tonilia in the Flagon... she'll set you up with your new armor.
I'd wager a well-placed shot at the ramp's mechanism would lower it in a hurry.
Look, I've never been good at these things, so I'm just going to keep it short.
With that in mind, I propose that the position of Guild Master should be yours.
When you make up your mind, come find me there and we'll talk about your future.
Well, well... color me impressed, lass. I wasn't certain I'd ever see you again!
The Guild has a lot riding on this. Don't make me look foolish by mucking it up.
Someone's trying to take us down by driving a wedge between Maven and the Guild.
There's plenty of gold out there for the taking... if you're up to the challenge.
I guess I shouldn't be surprised. Nothing around here has been going well lately.
I guess I shouldn't be surprised. Nothing around here has been going well lately.
But for now, you're off to speak to Maven Black-Briar. She asked for you by name.
At least Sabjorn has found himself in Whiterun's prison. How unfortunate for him.
I've never been one to lead. Never desired it, never cared for it. Don't want it.
I suggest you talk to Karliah first. She has some news that'll turn your stomach.
Wealth is my business. Help me out and I can add to yours. Would you like a taste?
The walls have ears and you're being tested, lad. We'll talk in the Ragged Flagon.
That's it then. After all of those years of helplessly watching the Guild decline.
The walls have ears and you're being tested, lass. We'll talk in the Ragged Flagon.
Then it's decided. When this entire sorry affair is over, we'll handle the details.
Aye, there's always a catch. But at this point, I suppose there isn't much to lose.
You kept your cool and you didn't kill him. That's the mark of a professional thief.
So we send in Vex and find out he's hired a bunch of mercenaries to guard the place.
I couldn't be more proud to be a part of the Thieves Guild, or its new Guild Master.
I couldn't be more proud to be a part of the Thieves Guild, or its new Guild Master.
Take a good look around you! Have you ever see the Guild in such a prosperous state?
Take a good look around you! Have you ever see the Guild in such a prosperous state?
You have the ring. All you have to do is pick Brand-Shei's pocket and plant it there.
Once you have it, I want you to place it in Brand-Shei's pocket without him noticing.
That's the spirit! Larceny's in your blood... the telltale sign of a practiced thief.
Are you daft? I told you not kill any of them. How much clearer do you need me to be?
Shor's beard! He's going after the Eyes of the Falmer? That was Gallus's pet project.
Looks like we can take the low road or the high road across this chamber. Your choice.
We can take the beast on or sneak around. It's your call, lad. We're right behind you.
I'm saying you've got the coin but you didn't earn a septim of it honestly. I can tell.
Never done an honest day's work in your life for all that coin you're carrying, eh lad?
Simple. Do as you're told and keep your blade clean. We can't turn a profit by killing.
You want to get back into our graces, you need to talk to Vex. She'll set you straight.
Look at the size of this place. Have you ever seen anything like it in your life, lass?
We can take the beast on or sneak around. It's your call, lass. We're right behind you.
I'll be out in the market all day, from eight in the morning until eight in the evening.
Never done an honest day's work in your life for all that coin you're carrying, eh lass?
So, job's done and you even brought the gold. Best of all you did it clean. I like that.
We've scoured the town and I've spoken to every contact we have left. No sign of Mercer.
Just don't expect us to at your back when Maven has your head on the executioner's block.
Tell you what. You keep making us coin and I'll worry about everything else. Fair enough?
I wasn't sure that he'd see the light, but I'm sure you "explained" it to him. Well done!
She's as stubborn as she is beautiful, isn't she? Glad you found a way to get her to pay.
Just keep on his tail and he's bound to step into something he can't scrape off his boot.
Now... if you're done bellyaching like a child, how about handling a few deadbeats for me?
Now... if you're done bellyaching like a child, how about handling a few deadbeats for me?
The Guild depends on an arrangement of influential people to keep things running smoothly.
Old Delvin kept calling it a curse and we all laughed at him. Looks like the joke's on us.
Patience, Brand-Shei. This is a rare opportunity, and I wouldn't want you to get left out.
They're built like small fortresses to resist the weather, but their one weakness is flame.
Karliah and I will meet you there. Prepare yourself, lad. This will be a fight to remember!
Don't miss out... purchase a bottle of my genuine Falmerblood Elixir! Only 20 septims each!
You're going to have to sneak over to Madesi's stall and use your lockpick on the strongbox.
Bah. My organization's been having a run of bad luck, but I suppose that's just how it goes.
Bah. My organization's been having a run of bad luck, but I suppose that's just how it goes.
Don't get me wrong, he could scam a beggar out of his last septim... but he's no mastermind.
If he gets his hands on them, you can be certain he'll be gone for good and set up for life.
Karliah and I will meet you there. Prepare yourself, lass. This will be a fight to remember!
Oh, and one last thing. Here, I want you to take this. It's sort of a tradition around here.
Good question. I've only set foot inside a few times myself and that was in Mercer's company.
Well, my friend... the time's come to make it official. It's time to become our Guild Master.
In fact, Vex barely made it out of there alive. You should talk to her about it before you go.
If I have to hold your hand through everything, maybe you aren't cut out for this kind of work.
If I have to hold your hand through everything, maybe you aren't cut out for this kind of work.
Welcome to the family, lad. I'm expecting you to make us a lot of coin, so don't disappoint me.
Just don't lose yourself in the role. We have a bit of an errand to run before your coronation.
After we're done here, head over to Tonilia and she'll set you up with your Guild Master Armor.
Yes, I know. That last job was my fault. I obviously thought Niruin was ready, but I was wrong.
Woah there, lass. I appreciate the armor, but becoming a Nightingale? That was never discussed.
Oh, excuse me, milady. I'll be glad to sell you an elixir after I'm done with these fine people.
Oh, excuse me, milord. I'll be glad to sell you an elixir after I'm done with these fine people.
Besides, nothing tells the people of Riften we mean business better than a huge column of smoke.
Welcome to the family, lass. I'm expecting you to make us a lot of coin, so don't disappoint me.
That means he has the pick of the litter from some of the finest goods to grace Skyrim's shores.
Just keep on Gulum-Ei's tail and he's bound to step into something he can't scrape off his boot.
Vex and Delvin are having to turn clients away at this point, it's almost impossible to keep up.
The way things have been going around here, it's a relief that our plan went off without a hitch.
How about the gold? Did you remember to bring it or is that too difficult an instruction as well.
He isn't exactly in the Guild, but he pays us a cut of all the stuff he lifts from the warehouse.
Trouble? He's one of the most stubborn lizards I've ever met! You have your work cut out for you.
Wait a moment, you're not talking about Goldenglow, are you? Even our little Vex couldn't get in.
Now if there are no more questions, how about following me and I'll show you what we're all about.
For now, just keep on his tail and he's bound to step into something he can't scrape off his boot.
I need you to break into Mercer's home and search for anything that could tell us where he's gone.
A true professional would have kept his cool and dealt with it a different way, but I'll let it go.
There's someone that wants to see him put out of business permanently. That's all you need to know.
There's someone that wants to see him put out of business permanently. That's all you need to know.
Don't worry about it. Maven's business dealings usually involve quite a bit of gold for her people.
Well, we have a bit of an errand to run before your coronation, so don't get sentimental on me now.
Expecting free information, eh? Help me deal with business first, then we'll see how I can help you.

Ha ha. If it was like that she wouldn't be asking for you, she'd be calling on the Dark Brotherhood.
Just head right back to the Guild and get the information to Mercer. Nothing else is more important.
There were just too many obstacles blocking the way. The plans were shelved and the rest is history.
I've learned to trust her lead at this point. After all, we Nightingales need to stick together, eh?
We've been contracted to make sure Brand-Shei remembers not to meddle in affairs that aren't his own.
The group I represent has its home in the Ratway beneath Riften... a tavern called the Ragged Flagon.
The group I represent has its home in the Ratway beneath Riften... a tavern called the Ragged Flagon.
The group I represent has its home in the Ratway beneath Riften... a tavern called the Ragged Flagon.

Genuine Falmerblood Elixir for sale! Live for thousands of years... see into other people's thoughts!

You've made a mess of things and Maven's furious. I told you not to burn more than three of the hives!

As a result, we both feel that you have the potential of replacing Mercer as leader of the Thieves Guild.
My sources must remain a secret for their own protection but I can promise that the contents are genuine.

Goldenglow Estate brought in a mountain of gold for the Guild. You could almost call it our sweetest deal.
We had an arrangement with Maven. We kept an eye on Goldenglow Estate to make sure the honey kept flowing.
Mercer thinks he knows a way to identify this new thorn in our side. He wants to meet with you right away.
But never mind that, even though you fouled up the job I still think you've got that spark I'm looking for.
Burn all the hives and she'd have to import honey for Black-Briar Meadery which would cut into her profits.
Maven prefers that Aringoth remains alive, but if he tries to stop you from getting the job done, kill him.
It says Mercer's been stealing from our vault for years. Gallus was looking into it before he was murdered.
Whoa, lad. I'd do just as well drawing a blade across my own neck. Where did this foolish request come from?
Look, this sounds like a family matter and I don't want the Guild in the middle of it. You do what you want.
She was the toughest of the bunch. Honestly, I'm amazed she didn't kick you out of the Bee and Barb herself.
He has no idea the extent of Maven's fury when she's been cut out of a deal, but I'm certain he'll find out.
I warned you not to cross Maven. If I hadn't stepped in and paid amends, you'd be... well, I think you know.
What? Nightingales? But, I always assumed they were just a tale... a way to keep the young footpads in line.
Aye. A gift from the Black-Briars after they kicked the previous family out... place called Riftweald Manor.
Museums, keeps, estates... you name it. By the time Mercer took over the Guild we must have had a few dozen.
And now they know Mercer never genuinely cared about the Guild. He lacked the loyalty you obviously possess.
A few years before Mercer murdered Gallus, the Guild took in a thief who specialized in dwarven antiquities.
If you still feel like doing some jobs, I'm sure Delvin and Vex have more than their fair share to give out.
Whoa, lass. I'd do just as well drawing a blade across my own neck. Where did this foolish request come from?

As a result, we both feel that you'd make an excellent replacement for Mercer as leader of the Thieves Guild.

Aye, he does indeed... and with his fingers in the East Empire Company's pie, we'll make good use of that debt.
Look, before I have you help track Mercer down I need to know what you learned from Karliah. I mean everything.
Everyone is in agreement, so all I can do now is name you Guild Master and wish you good fortune and long life.
Tell you what. Go ahead and deal with your business for now. When you're done, find me and we'll deal with mine.
Aye, that's what the Falmer were known as long ago... before they became the blind monstrosities they are today.
So, this is Nightingale Hall. I heard about this place when I joined the Guild, but I never believed it existed.
I've spoken to Karliah, and made amends for how the Guild's treated her. Now she wishes to speak with both of us.
Just find a way in, get the information and leave. And you have permission to kill anyone that stands in your way.
Learn a library's worth of knowledge in moments or grow back that missing limb with my genuine Falmerblood Elixir!
Either way, it's been a pleasure, my friend. Here's to the future of the Guild. May it last another thousand years!
Yeah. I bet I know your guy. He's hiding out in the Ratway Warrens. Paying us good coin for nobody to know about it.
Agreed. He's taken everything the Guild has left, and to go after one of the last greatest heists is just an insult.
Thanks to your efforts, the Guild's been restored to it's full strength and you've exposed the traitor in our midst.
But enough of that... I'm confident that with you in charge, we'll soon have more gold than we could possibly spend.
Before Mercer took over, Gallus started collecting every bit of material he could on locations the Guild could heist.
Aye, that he was. And you sniffed him out because he broke one of the cardinal rules of our business... he got greedy.
Let me give you a piece of advice. If you want to make some serious coin in this business you need to listen to rules.
A mercantile group that has established ports all over Tamriel. They pretty much dominate the whole shipping industry.
Mystical beings who live in legends and were masters of great magic. Imagine the power that coursed through their veins!
Your guy's hiding out in the Ratway Warrens and paying us good coin for nobody to know about it. Well, until now that is.
Don't forget to check your tribute chest once in a while. As Guild Master you're getting a significant cut of the spoils.
So... now that I've whetted your appetite with our little scheme at the market, how about handling a few deadbeats for me?
We need you to teach him a lesson by burning down three of the estate's hives and clearing out the safe in the main house.
Mercer may have damaged our reputation and raided our coffers, but this goes well beyond even his twisted form of larceny.
Simple... I'm going to cause a distraction and you're going to steal Madesi's silver ring from a strongbox under his stand.
Well, at least you didn't do anything stupid. Next time, when you're given a job, don't wander off or take your sweet time.
Listen, lad. There's one last piece of business we need to settle before we go after Mercer... the leadership of the Guild.
I've been at this game a long time, my friend. A long time. I've stolen trinkets from nobles and framed priests for murder.
Are you daft? This isn't the Dark Brotherhood! We don't go around murdering anyone, ever. It just isn't how things are done.
Despite your mistakes, I see more potential in you than half the snot-nosed footpads that stumble their way into the Flagon.
Honestly, the debt is secondary here. What's more important is that you get the message across that we aren't to be ignored.
If the workers had a dispute, we'd rough them up. If competitors tried to buy honey from Aringoth, we'd steal the shipments.
Listen, lass. There's one last piece of business we need to settle before we go after Mercer... the leadership of the Guild.
Don't worry, I promise this will be short and sweet. If you'll just meet us in the center of the cistern room, we can begin.
Make love like a sabercat or crush your enemies to dust like a giant! Only a mere 20 gold coins and all this could be yours!
Not just ordinary gems. They're said to be flawlessly cut and as big as a man's head. Can you imagine how much they're worth?
I can't think of anyone better suited to return the Key. Just remember to keep your eyes open and walk true with the shadows.
If you can get past his trained watchdog, I think your best bet might be the ramp to the second floor balcony in his backyard.
Well, that's it. Sorry if it isn't the ceremony you were hoping for, but we're not exactly known for throwing our coin around.
Nothing happens in Riften without Maven's consent. One word from her and you could spend the rest of the fourth era in prison.
Mercer Frey tried to kill both of you, he betrayed the Guild, murdered Gallus and made us question our future. He needs to die.
If you were expecting a palace, then maybe you're not cut out for this line of work. Our methods involve secrecy and discretion.
I'll be down here, trying to coordinate everything with Delvin and Vex; to make sure the coin keeps flowing... and no one skims!
One sip of the elixir and your wishes will be granted. Great wealth, everlasting life or perhaps limitless power could be yours!
You tell this sorry bunch of thieves that they need to put down their flagons and get to work if they want to keep their status!
I've been pouring over the plans you brought us, and I'm convinced the Eyes of the Falmer are in the dwarven ruins at Irkngthand.
I've been pouring over the plans you brought us, and I'm convinced the Eyes of the Falmer are in the dwarven ruins at Irkngthand.
Look. Everyone in the Guild admires what you've done. Maybe they won't come out and simply tell you, but I promise you it's true.
Gallus and Mercer spent the better part of a month infiltrating Irkngthand, but the dwarves had protected the place far too well.
I also need to reassure Maven that it's business as usual without Mercer... and that a new Guild Master will soon be taking over.
That's not how things work around here. The Guild depends on an arrangement of influential people to keep things running smoothly.
The lass seems to think old Mercer is pulling a fast one on us... leading us here and letting the dwarven constructs wear us down.
Being Guild Master means more than just getting a cut of all the loot, it's about being a leader and keeping this rabble in order.
Besides, I'd rather die with some of Mercer's blood on my blade than spend the rest of my life regretting that I ran the other way.
One small detail though - I need all of the gold they gave you. No one skims around here. Come back when you've got every last coin.
Then it's all true... everything I heard in the stories. The Nightingales, their allegiance to Nocturnal and the Twilight Sepulcher.
With Mercer Frey gone and our influence spreading across Skyrim, the Guild's earned a new level of respect it hasn't seen in decades.
Talk to Talen-Jei at the Bee and Barb and see if you can get something out of him. They're... well acquainted if you catch my meaning.
Gulum-Ei works in the East Empire Company Warehouse. He helps maintain all of the shipments of goods that goes in and out of Solitude.
Without two keys, it's impossible to open. I have a key, Delvin has a key, and Mercer has a key. That's it. There are no other copies.
Listen, lad. I have faith in you. I know that sounds strange coming from a thief, but these recent events have changed my perspective.
The Emperor himself supposedly backs them, which means they have fairly unlimited resources... so don't get their feathers in a ruffle.
There are thieves and then there is Gulum-Ei. No honor, no code at all. He'd shake your hand and stab you in the back at the same time.
Didn't take long for Enthir to find a book in the college's library that told of Irkngthand and a great statue with gemmed eyes within.
When Gallus took one look at this statue, he knew it was something special. He took it right up to Enthir at the College of Winterhold.
Listen, lass. I have faith in you. I know that sounds strange coming from a thief, but these recent events have changed my perspective.
With Mercer Frey gone and our influence present in all of Skyrim's cities, the Guild's accumulating wealth faster than we can spend it.
Normally I'd agree, but we've already lost so many contacts across Skyrim. If we lose the ones in Riften, we may as well close up shop.
You should talk with Delvin Mallory and Vex. They know their way around this place and they'll be able to kick some extra jobs your way.
Goldenglow Estate is a bee farm; they raise the wretched little things for honey. It's owned by some smart-mouth wood elf named Aringoth.
The thief had broken into a nobleman's home somewhere in Windhelm and made off with a small figurine of a snow elf with crystalline eyes.
If that fails, follow him and see what he's up to. If I know Gulum-Ei, he's in way over his head and you'll be able to use it as leverage.
But never mind that, you did the job and you did it well. Best of all, there's more where that came from... if you think you can handle it.
The cut he's supposed to provide the Guild has dwindled as of late. He says pickings in the warehouse are slim, but I'm certain he's lying.
Now, since we're not the Dark Brotherhood, we're not going to kill him, we're just going to make sure he sits in the prisons for a few days.
Aye. I feared that was the case. From that last entry in Gallus's diary, it looks like he was getting close to exposing Mercer to the Guild.
Then it's decided. When this is all over and Delvin's contacts assure me that we've regained our footing in Skyrim, we'll handle the details.
If you discover Gulum-Ei's holding out on us and has more loot stashed away than he claims, we'd find that information quite valuable as well.
I'm glad to see you're embracing our methods. It would be a waste to lose a contact at the East Empire Company before we had the entire story.
Right now, the Guild needs someone to maintain order. Everyone is awaiting the news of Mercer's demise; it couldn't be a more precarious time.
Judging from how well you handled those shopkeepers, I'd say you've done more than simply prove yourself. We need people like you in our outfit.
Look how you sniffed out my little scheme at the North Gate. You knew it was a shakedown and you called him on it. That's what I'm talking about.
Thanks to your efforts, Mercer's treachery has been exposed. After we deal with him, all that remains is restoring the Guild to its full strength.
I've seen a puzzle like this before... there's got to be another lever to pull, but you'd better hurry. A septim says this mechanism's on a timer.
Haelga is a devout follower of Dibella and dotes over the statue to the Divine that she keeps at the Bunkhouse. Use it as leverage and she'll cave.

Delphine.line: Take that thing down!
This won't take long.
That's how it's done.
After you, Dragonborn.
We should get started.
Do you need something?
I should have guessed.
I'm not afraid of you.
This is how it's done.
Mind your own business.
Come on, show yourself!
Running won't save you.
Hold on, what was that?
Nothing there. Strange.
Sounds good. Be careful.
What are we waiting for?
We'd better keep moving.
We'd better keep moving.
Sven, we need to talk...
Come on, close the door.

For a century or more, the Thalmor had been picking away at the Empire. Valenwood was the first, then the province of Elsweyr.
You there. The Dragonborn wishes to give you the chance to join the lost guardians of Tamriel. The dragon slayers. The Blades.
The dragonstone was a map of ancient dragon burial sites. I've looked at which ones are now empty. The pattern is pretty clear.
Then by my right as acting grandmaster, I name you a Blade, with all the privileges, rights, and burdens that brings. Godspeed.
So that's your plan, huh? Even if you could survive, by the time you got inside, whatever documents they had would be long gone.
It seems to be spreading from the southeast, down in the Jeralls near Riften. The one at Kynesgrove is next if the pattern holds.
Kynesgrove. There's an ancient dragon burial near there. If we can get there before it happens, maybe we'll learn how to stop it.
The Thalmor. We're very old enemies. And if my suspicions are correct, they might have something to do with the dragons returning.
Esbern should be able to help. He's spent his whole life poring over every old book he could find about dragons and that prophecy.
You are going to brew a new batch of ale, right? Like we talked about? What we have's been sitting in that barrel for over a month.
The Nords didn't like the peace treaty much either. Thirty years later, the Empire ends up with the Stormcloak rebellion on its hands.
That's enough recruits for now. Ask Esbern about the dragon lairs he's been studying. We'll have the recruits do some hunting with you.
There's no worse enemy to humankind in Tamriel. The Empire barely survived the last war. The Thalmor don't intend to lose the next one.
It seems to be spreading from the southeast, starting in the Jeralls near Riften. The one near Kynesgrove is next if the pattern holds.
I was one of the few who escaped. For a long time, all I cared about was staying alive, and taking revenge on the Thalmor when I could.
We have as much right to be at this council as all of you. More, actually, since we were the ones that put the Dragonborn on this path.
But to answer your question, the White-Gold Concordat was the fancy name they put on the peace treaty between the Empire and the Thalmor.
Trust me, there's no need to be afraid. Think of Tiber Septim. Do you think he'd have founded the Empire if he'd listened to the Greybeards?
Are you willing to trade away all claims and titles of your former life? To live here and devote yourself to protecting Tamriel from danger?
Here's the big picture. He helped Alduin enslave our ancestors. He may have betrayed Alduin in the end, but that makes him worse, not better.
We'll look around Sky Haven Temple and see what else the old Blades might have left for us. It's a better hideout than I could have hoped for.
Let me handle that. You'll have a real invitation, don't worry. As long as you can act the part of a Thalmor toady, you'll get past the guards.

Karliah.line: Your life is forfeit!
We were... very close.
Where'd you come from?
We're right behind you.
I... Willl... kill you!
You're as good as dead!
Come on, show yourself!
You can't hide forever!
Must've scared 'em off.
That's the end of that.
Yes, fellow Nightingale?
Give me a reason to try.
I'm going to find you...
So, you reveal yourself!
A bold but foolish move.
I send you to your rest.
Nocturnal, guide my hand!
Wait, I hear something...
Your death will be swift!
You'll... pay for this...
I knew I heard something!
Will I ever see you again?
Come coward, now we fight!
You can't get away from me!
We have to get the Key back.
This is just like old times.
I'm afraid that's impossible.
Mercer's here. I can feel it.
Yes, Nightingale, what is it?
Wait a moment... what's that?
Enough games, let's end this!
You appear ready for the Oath.
What will you do now, my love?
I must've been hearing things.
That'll teach you to cross me.
Come, taste darkness's embrace!
I bring death from the shadows!
The burden of death approaches!
Guess it was nothing after all.
Are you ready to face the Guild?
It's my hope that you will, yes.
I'm afraid it's not that simple.
He didn't need to pick the lock.
It's Mercer! Look... down there!
I claim your soul for Nocturnal!
I trust the night finds you well?
I'll bury my blade in your heart!
The shadows will be your undoing!
I know you're here. Show yourself.
Glad the antidote is taking effect.
Be patient and your time will come.
May the shadows guide your journey.
Easy, easy. Don't get up so quickly.
Eyes open and walk with the shadows.
It was good speaking with you again.
Shadows preserve us. So it's true...
Hiding only prolongs the inevitable.
You were no match for a Nightingale.
Planning some exciting heists I hope?
We'll speak when the Oath is complete.
We'll have to find another way around.
The afterlife will bring you darkness.
It's just ahead. Please, keep following.
By Nocturnal's grace, I will defeat you!
Enjoying your newfound abilities I trust?
What brings you back to Nightingale Hall?
It was the first lesson Gallus taught us.
Please, make it brief. We have much to do.
Please, there isn't much time. What is it?
Things seem to be going well at the Guild.
Farewell, Enthir... words can't express...
Climb down that ledge and see if you can...
He's toying with us. He wants us to follow.
All I had hoped was to capture Mercer alive.
The time for talk has passed. We must hurry.
If you wish to talk, I'll be here for a bit.
I wouldn't take that to heart. It's her way.
I promise to join you there as soon as I can.
Mercer's been here, I hope we aren't too late.
Farewell. Eyes open and walk with the shadows.
Time is of the essence here, please hurry back.
This is Nocturnal's way of maintaining balance.
It helped avert attention from our true nature.
We have to catch up to him before it's too late.
You're closer to understanding than you realize.
I'm hearing good things about you from Brynjolf.
Fight it, Brynjolf... he's taken control of you!
This place is coming down. Let's get out of here!
Nocturnal seemed quite pleased with your efforts.
No, not gone... he's become one with the shadows.
The return certainly seems worth the risk though.
Does the journal say where this wealth came from?
Bless you, Lady of Shadows... I live another day.
Remember, speak only to Enthir. Trust no one else.
I think given time, you'll understand what I mean.
Come on, we need to show the journal to the Guild.
Choose your path and your journey will be complete.
Proceed down the hall to the gate when you're ready.
If you'll follow me, I'll try to explain on the way.
Knowing this, are you ready to undergo the ceremony?
Brynjolf and I found them like that. Mercer's doing.
I'm glad you were able to bring the Key back safely.
Remember, we have proof and all he's got is his word.
Once the Oath has been struck, the terms are binding.
With any luck, this will be Mercer's final day alive.
It's a Dwarven Centurion. Very tough and very deadly.
This isn't about religion, Brynjolf... it's business.
Farewell, Gallus. Eyes open... walk with the shadows.
She influences our luck and in return demands payment.
This is where we saw Mercer. We must be getting close.
I'll watch for Mercer, just get the information we need.
Not quick enough, otherwise Gallus would still be alive.
Then we show them Gallus's journal and hope for the best.
He's close. I'm certain of it. We must prepare ourselves.
As a Nightingale, I took an oath and was sworn to secrecy.
I'm preparing to leave for the standing stone. What is it?
We've come to seek the edge we need to defeat Mercer Frey.
Why, perhaps the greatest crime spree Skyrim's ever known.
I can't think of a better person for the job, Nightingale.
Anything else, Enthir? Anything about... the Nightingales?
We haven't much time for discussion, but what did you need?
Falmer patrol up ahead. I don't think they know we're here.
Be careful, there's no telling what lurks within these walls.
I'm afraid you'll have to face the end of your journey alone.
If this place is in danger ever again, the shadows will call.
It's through these subtle means that Nocturnal influences us.
Beyond this gate is the first step in becoming a Nightingale.
Please hurry, we need to catch Mercer before he leaves Skyrim.
No, he means to ambush us down here, I'm almost certain of it.
What's wrong, Brynjolf? I can almost hear your brow furrowing.
He's here and he hasn't seen us yet. Brynjolf, watch the door.
If Mercer leaves here with the Eyes, we'll never see him again.
Will do. Don't stray too far away or else we'll come after you.
As a consequence, I have no knowledge of what you'll be facing.
Good. After I open the gate, please stand on the western circle.
Here, take these as well, they may prove useful for your journey.
Let's listen to Enthir's translation before we decide what we do.
Are you finished your other business and ready to face the Guild?
No problem, but if you get too far away, we're going to catch up.
The only way to bring it back will be through the Pilgrim's Path.
To ensure an enemy's defeat, you must first undermine his allies.
But I can promise the next time we meet, it will be your undoing.
Shhh. Listen to Enthir... we can't afford to miss a single detail.
I felt the same way when Gallus first revealed these things to me.
If the Guild isn't willing to listen to reason, you might have to.
Well, yes. But the Key isn't only restricted to physical barriers.
What about you? Are you ready to transact the Oath with Nocturnal?
All that remains is to ensure the safe return of the Skeleton Key.
Gallus's Oath has been paid. His actions have satisfied the terms.
You've come this far, so I see no harm in concealing it any longer.
Had I intended to kill you, we wouldn't be having this conversation.
Enthir... Gallus's friend at the College of Winterhold. Of course...
Yes. Now that you're a Nightingale, you may consider this your home.
Can't say that I have. Imagine the riches hidden within these walls.
As a Nightingale, I've been sworn to secrecy regarding the Sepulcher.
I have Gallus's journal. I think you'll find its contents disturbing.
I'm relieved Brynjolf has an open mind or that could have gotten bloody.
You appear hesitant to don your Nightingale Armor. What's troubling you?
This place, it's like a maze. How did the dwarves find their way around?
I'm no fool, Mercer. Crossing blades with you would be a death sentence.
I can hear water rushing through these pipes. We must be beneath a lake.
If she's to accept you as one of her own, an arrangement must be struck.
The only reason to do that would be to block pursuit. It must be Mercer.
Until the Key is returned, I will never set foot inside that place again.
Be warned that once you've chosen, you can't reselect for at least a day.
After Brynjolf has installed you as Guild Master, we can speak at length.
You'll be provided all of the power and knowledge befitting a Nightingale.
To hold any hope of defeating Mercer, we must have Nocturnal at our backs.
We have to find another way out of here before the place fills with water!
My appetite for Mercer's demise exceeds my craving for wealth, Your Grace.
We must hasten to Riften before Mercer can do any more damage to the Guild.
Keep your eyes open. I'm not sure what to expect when we enter the cistern.
The notion that the Nightingales are a fictive group has long since passed.
And I... I can't bear to face Nocturnal after my failure to protect the Key.
I only hope that this isn't an ending to things, but actually the beginning.
By transacting the Oath with Nocturnal, you're entering into a business deal.
You merely transacted the Oath; signed the unwritten contract with Nocturnal.
Brynjolf is needed back at the Thieves Guild to keep order while you're away.
Whether you know it or not, Nocturnal dictates how well we perform as rogues.
All I can tell you for now is that he's abused his authority as a Nightingale.
I promise you, all your questions will be answered. You just have to trust me.
Keep as quiet as you can. The Falmer may be blind, but they can still hear us.
It's the Key, Brynjolf. In his hands, there's no telling what he's capable of.
Yes. That's why we need to prepare ourselves and meet Mercer on equal footing.
On moonlit nights or in darkened rooms, this agent literally becomes invisible.
I feared I would never see you again. I was afraid you'd become like the others.
Once you realize the Key can access these traits, the potential becomes limitless.
She's the mistress of night and darkness and the patron of every thief in Tamriel.
I'm afraid not. There are preparations to make and Gallus's remains to lay to rest.
I can promise you if it comes to that, and my back's to the wall, I won't hesitate.
Good, then you understand why this is about more than just Mercer's lust for power.
All right, but don't wander ahead too far or else we'll be forced to come find you.
The Agent of Subterfuge utilizes shadow to cloud the judgement of those around him.
I'm feeling a sense of inner peace... something I haven't felt in a very long time.
My original intention was to use that arrow on Mercer, but I never had a clear shot.
I can't believe it's over. Twenty-five years in exile and just like that, it's done.
Again, you have to think differently. Haven't you ever noticed how our luck behaves?
I made a split second decision to get you out of the way and it prevented your death.
It's the only outsider Gallus trusted with the knowledge of his Nightingale identity.
I'm glad you're here. I think some of these people are beginning to suspect who I am.
Before we depart, Brynjolf has some business to discuss. I suggest you listen to him.
Please, lower your weapons so we can speak. I have proof that you've all been misled!
I call upon you Lady Nocturnal, Queen of Murk and Empress of Shadow... hear my voice!
We have to be very careful, Brynjolf. Mercer is a Nightingale, an Agent of Nocturnal.
My purpose in using Snow Veil Sanctum to ambush Mercer wasn't simply for irony's sake.
I wish I knew. The journal is written in some sort of language I've never seen before.
One of their ceremonies involved the Pilgrim's Path, a so called "test of worthiness."
Gallus, Mercer Frey and I were once members of what's known as the Nightingale Trinity.
I assure you, had she been displeased with you, we wouldn't be having this conversation.
I have two others that wish to transact the Oath; to serve you both in life and in death.
The Nightingales protect the temple of Nocturnal, a place known as the Twilight Sepulcher.
Just outside of Riften, beyond the Southeast Gate is a small path cut up the mountainside.
We will honor our agreement in this life and the next until your conditions have been met.
At the same time, this tether will bolster the agent's own life force, making him stronger.
The circles at the base of the Ebonmere imbue you with powers befitting a Nightingale Agent.
The assumption that the Nightingales were just a myth was seeded within the Guild on purpose.
We should tread carefully. I wouldn't be surprised if he's left behind a few surprises for us.
There are pockets brimming with coin and coffers overflowing with riches ripe for the picking.
Does she exact payment when we die? When we suffer does she revel in our misery? No one knows.
Mercer must have triggered these pistons. We're going to have to search for a way to open them.
Now, if you'll both proceed to the armory to don your Nightingale Armor, we can begin the Oath.
This must be their hive. We'll have to keep silent if we want to avoid drawing their attention.
Everything that represents Nocturnal's influence is contained within the walls of the Sepulcher.
If Nocturnal was truly displeased with me... with any of us, she wouldn't have answered my call.
We may be Nightingales, but in our hearts we're still thieves and we're damn good at what we do.
At the end of that path is a clearing and an old standing stone. I'd ask you both meet me there.
Consider this an extremely risky job but with a massive potential for profit, and you'll do fine.
This is the headquarters of the Nightingales, cut into the mountainside by the first of our kind.
By doing this, he's compromised our ties to Nocturnal and in essence, caused our luck to run dry.
And in return, both in life and in death, you must serve as a guardian of the Twilight Sepulcher.
Did you forget the Oath we took as Nightingales? Did you expect him to simply ignore your methods?
Damn! This place is coming down! Quick, get the Skeleton Key and the Eye and lets get out of here!
This is Nightingale Hall. You're the first of the uninitiated to set foot inside in over a century.
Now it seems Mercer's broken his oath with Nocturnal and defiled the very thing he swore to protect.
Mercer lied to the Guild, branded me a murderer and slandered my name across his network of contacts.
For twenty-five years I ran, never sleeping in the same place twice and carefully covering my tracks.
I'd always intended Mercer's fate to ultimately be decided by the Guild, and it seems they've spoken.
For someone in possession of the Skeleton Key, stealing the Eyes of the Falmer would be child's play.
The Guild has welcomed me back with open arms. I feel like a void in my life has finally been filled.
Of course, they'd never come into direct contact with Nocturnal but they insisted they had her favor.
It's believed that they are literally what guides our uncanny luck... by placing their hands in ours.
Nocturnal's whim is the greatest mystery to everyone. There have been volumes written on the subject.
Should the need arise, a portal connects the Sepulcher and Nightingale Hall. Use it whenever you wish.
From the moment you were struck with my poisoned arrow at Snow Veil Sanctum, my path changed its course.
In order for us to receive our abilities... our end of the bargain, I'm afraid the Key must be returned.
I've had this bow almost my entire life, and it's never let me down. I hope it brings you the same luck.
Once the Skeleton Key has been restored to the Twilight Sepulcher, I'll make this place my home as well.
Instead of being clouded with thoughts of revenge and retribution, I feel only serenity and fulfillment.
I'm sorry Enthir, I can't say. All that matters is we deliver your translation to the Guild immediately.
Very true. In our line of work, it's quite rare we set out to return a stolen item to its rightful owner.
Mercer's been careful so far. I don't think he'd just leave those plans behind unless he had his reasons.
It's true, Brynjolf. Every word. Mercer's been stealing from the Guild for years, right under your noses.
Mercer must be brought before the Guild to answer for what he's done. He needs to pay for Gallus's murder.
In return, you'll be required to defend the Twilight Sepulcher and everything within when the need arises.
When the Skeleton Key was stolen from the Twilight Sepulcher, our access to the inner sanctum was removed.
Now his spirit becomes one with the Evergloam... the realm of perpetual twilight and the cradle of shadow.
I think by now, most within the Guild are well aware of what transpired between Mercer Frey and ourselves.
My Lady, I've come before you to throw myself upon your mercy and to accept responsibility for my failure.
All of us possess untapped abilities; the potential to wield great power, securely sealed within our minds.
Although it would benefit the Guild more than you can imagine, in the end, it would do more harm than good.
You're free to use those powers as you see fit to further your own goals or the goals of the Thieves Guild.
Even the Falmer don't deserve the pain these implements must have inflicted. The dwarves were a cruel race.
Nocturnal isn't one for worship and reverence. There are no priests and no sermons, no services and no alms.
Lady Nocturnal, we accept your terms. We dedicate ourselves to you as both your avengers and your sentinels.
I know the Guild doesn't do much to foster faith, but I'm going to have to ask that you continue to trust me.
If the Key isn't returned to its lock in the Twilight Sepulcher, things will never be the same for the Guild.
These priests weren't threat to the Skeleton Key or the conduit to Nocturnal's realm, so they were tolerated.
There were three of us. Myself, Gallus and Mercer. We were a anonymous splinter of the Thieves Guild in Riften.
More importantly, upon your death, your spirit will be bound to the Twilight Sepulcher as one of its guardians.
I have no doubt that we still hold her favor and I believe it gives us enough of an edge to defeat Mercer Frey.
He was a scholar, a master thief and a natural leader. Everyone respected him and followed him without question.
The poison on that arrow took me a year to perfect; I only had enough for a single shot and yet I used it on you.
It wasn't created for the Nightingales. It was created to test those who wished to serve Nocturnal in other ways.
Like a novice picking an impossible lock or a blind man suddenly turning to face you as you reach for his pocket?
Now that you've transacted the Oath, it's time to reveal the final piece of the puzzle to you; Mercer's true crime.
The conduit to Nocturnal's realm, the realm of Evergloam, has been in Skyrim... well, longer than recorded history.
Absolutely. When we say "walk with the shadows," we are asking those Nightingales who have passed on to protect us.
That's why the Ebonmere needed to be reopened. Without it, there's no way Nocturnal was able to allow them through.
Brynjolf, the time has come to decide Mercer's fate. Until a new Guild Master is chosen, the decision falls to you.
The Twilight Sepulcher is the temple to Nocturnal. It's what the Nightingales are sworn to protect with their lives.
In the meantime, I wanted you to have this. It belonged to Gallus, but given the circumstances I think he'd approve.
The only difference is she doesn't demand payment in the traditional sense and sometimes the cost can be quite high.
If you wish to wear your Nightingale Armor within the Guild, I'm certain that no one would give it a second thought.
If you ever feel the need to change your abilities, you can return to the Sepulcher and step onto a different circle.
I've decided to make my home at Nightingale Hall. Since it's your home as well, I hope to see you and Brynjolf there.
This isn't about religion or destiny. This is nothing more than a business transaction between yourself and Nocturnal.
As part of their "duties," the priests created all sorts of baseless rituals and ceremonies all on Nocturnal's behalf.
More than you'll ever realize. The poison on that arrow took me a year to perfect; I only had enough for a single shot.
This Agent of Strife can send forth a tendril of pure darkness into the heart of another, causing great injury to them.
It was Gallus who inducted me into the Nightingales and honed my skills to a razor sharp point. I owe everything to him.
Now, your life as a Nightingale begins. Should the need arise, you'll be summoned to the Sepulcher in order to defend it.
This is the greatest honor a Nightingale can possibly achieve. In death, he's become a part of that which we use to live.
Without the antidote I administered, you'd be as still as a statue. I treated your wounds and didn't leave you defenseless.
Perhaps I couldn't bring Mercer back alive, but together, we were able to clear my name and to put Gallus's memory to rest.
Indirectly. The Trinity is usually selected from the ranks of the Guild although its existence is a closely-guarded secret.
Of course, I may visit some of Skyrim's cities to "acquire" things from time to time. Can't afford to get rusty, now can we?
