Before both of you arrived, I recovered a journal from Gallus's remains. I suspect the information we need is written inside.
The Twilight Sepulcher was constructed around it by man and mer in order to shield it from those who would exploit its power.
By weaving the darkness to their will, this agent can manipulate others into fighting for the Nightingale for a limited time.
No, I saved your life. My arrow was tipped with a unique paralytic poison. It slowed your heart and kept you from bleeding out.
Perhaps I'll tell you more about it later. Right now, you need to head for Winterhold with the journal and get the translation.
It's through this conduit that we're given Nocturnal's greatest gift, our luck. What she gains in return is a complete mystery.
The Agent of Stealth is the master of remaining unseen. They are able to manipulate the darkness and use it to their advantage.
What are you doing talking to me? Didn't you know Brynjolf's been trying to find you? It's time to install you as Guild Master!
Think of her as a scolding mother continually pushing you harder to be successful; outwardly sounding angry but silently content.
Of course, I've been also dabbling in my old pastimes as well. Take a look around, and feel free to share the fruits of my labor.
I've been a Nightingale for a very long time. I sold my allegiance to Nocturnal in exchange for many profitable years of thieving.
Even though Nocturnal doesn't desire worship in the traditional sense, the Twilight Sepulcher propagated a small group of priests.
There's a mass of the Falmer in this chamber. We can sneak through or take them down... I don't care. As long as we get to Mercer.
I promise you, the thought crossed my mind. The poison on that arrow took me a year to perfect; I only had enough for a single shot.
Take this with you. I'm not certain if it will help within the walls of the Sepulcher, but I certainly don't need it as much as you.
The terms are quite simple, Brynjolf. Nocturnal will allow you to become a Nightingale and use your abilities for whatever you wish.
Mercer was able to unlock the Guild's vault without two keys because of what he stole from the Twilight Sepulcher... the Skeleton Key.
The crescent moon represents the Agent of Shadow, the half moon for the Agent of Subterfuge and the full moon for the Agent of Strife.
If a pilgrim was able to complete the path, it was said that they would become one with the shadows. What that means is anyone's guess.
Mercer doesn't need to die... he needs to feel the cold sting of fate as his life crumbles in front of him and he's hunted by the Guild.
You'll find that this place offers many things that will help you in your endeavors as well as a wealth of information for you to learn.
Falling in love with Gallus was wrong. It was a distraction that allowed the Sepulcher to be desecrated and it likely cost him his life.
I'll make for Riften and scout the situation; see if I can discover what Mercer's up to. When you're ready, meet me at the Ragged Flagon.
As time passed, our luck would diminish to the point of non-existence. And whether you know it or not, our uncanny luck defines our trade.
With the Skeleton Key missing from the Twilight Sepulcher, I'm afraid Mercer's seen to it that none of us can benefit from Nocturnal's gifts.
Gallus once said he felt comfortable around me; able to let his guard down. I can't help but think that I'm responsible for what happened to him.
The Trinity disbanded twenty-five years ago when Mercer Frey betrayed us by slaying Gallus and dumping his body in the ruins of Snow Veil Sanctum.

Esbern.line: I'm too old for this.
I think they're gone.
Good luck, Dragonborn.
We should get started.
We should keep moving.
Did you need something?
Your blood, Dragonborn.
Come on, show yourself!
Go away! Leave me alone!
I'm going to find you...
All right. Don't be long.
Well, good. They're gone.
You'll... pay for this...
You should have run away.
Eh? Did you say something?
Hmm. I don't see anything.
I'm afraid I've lost them.
We must succeed here today.
Give me... just a moment...
We'll cross once it's safe.
But, there's one they fear.
Good. We should keep moving.
That's the end of that, hmm?
So it wasn't my imagination.
I know, I know. I'm hurrying.
We'd best be careful in here.
This'll just take a moment...
I wasn't looking for trouble.
I'm getting too old for this.
I'm in a bit of trouble here.
That seems to be all of them.
Hmm, nothing there after all.
Oho, so that's where you went.
Let me know when you are, then.
That's better. Now we can talk.
Yes. These are Akaviri symbols.
Look, here. In the third panel.
I need some help with this one!
A shame it had to come to that.
Their defeat was merely a delay.
Oh yes! Dragonborn! Indeed, yes.
You've brought this on yourself.
I could use some help over here!
I think this might be the end...
In their tongue, he is Dovahkiin.
You're starting to make me angry.
Ah. Indeed, indeed. I do remember.
I can't wait to see what's inside!
You could trap him there, exactly.
To the time after Oblivion opened.
Alduin cannot ignore this for long.
Are you sure this is the right way?
Yes, yes. Let's see what we have...
Ah. I believe I can be of help here.
You... Dragonborn? Is it really true?
Gods above, what are you waiting for?
You can still run. It's not too late.
Must be my mind playing tricks on me.
That's where we'll find Alduin's Wall.
Ah! Here it is. Come, let me show you.
This one always sticks... there we go.
Yes, yes. We should proceed cautiously.
The Elder Scrolls told of their return.
There should be a way to turn this off.
Thalmor dog! Death is too good for you.
Seems I still remember my old training.
The dragons will surely fear your Voice.
Come in, come in! Make yourself at home!
You should run while you have the chance.
Come in, come in. Too many ears out there.
Wonderful! Remarkably well-preserved, too.
Let me see if I can find the right panel...
Please. Come in. Musn't talk in the Ratway.
You should have acted. They're already here.
A wise and foresighted policy, in the event.
Could have been my imagination, I suppose...
I don't like being out in the open like this.
There's no telling what we might find inside!
Yes. Definitely early Akaviri stonework here.
I gave up this kind of thing a long time ago.
Are we close? Thalmor spies could be anywhere.
You've done it... just as the prophecy said...
Ah. Thank you! Excellent. Now... where were we?
We must make them see their danger, Dragonborn.
Alduin's defeat is the centerpiece of the Wall.
When the Brass Tower walks and Time is reshaped
A hedge against the forgetfulness of centuries.
And no doubt you're one of them. Leave me alone!
Yes, that's it. The symbol on the middle pillar.
That's all there is to it, Dragonborn. Good luck.
Alduin has returned, just like the prophecy said!
Delphine, we're not here to rehearse old grudges.
What? You're... can it really be true? Dragonborn?
But... there's no way to know what Shout is meant.
Well, yes, but... there's no guarantee, of course.
Please... Delphine has been very worried about you.
Well, I guess that's good enough... let's be off...
And when the truth finally dawns, it dawns in fire.
Yes, yes! I think we must be close to the entrance.
Od - Ah - Viing. "Winged Snow Hunter," as I read it.
When the sons of Skyrim would spill their own blood.
Yes, that's it. The symbol on the pillar on the left.
When the Snow Tower lies sundered, kingless, bleeding
So Delphine keeps up the fight, after all these years.
So Delphine keeps up the fight, after all these years.
Yes, that's it. The symbol on the pillar on the right.
I know the prophecy by heart. Once all Blades knew it.
Patience. Give me a moment and I'll explain everything.
We must be getting close. We should proceed cautiously.
When the thrice-blessed fail and the Red Tower trembles
Or not. There's no guarantee with prophecy. Merely hope.
Or not. There's no guarantee with prophecy. Merely hope.
But no one wanted to believe. Believe they even existed.
Yes! Yes! You see, you know but you refuse to understand!
I must speak to Delphine at once. There's no time to lose.
We should be careful here. See these symbols on the floor?
You'll have to speak up. I'm a little deaf in my right ear.
Now, now my dear. I'm talking about academia, not politics.
I knew, that day, that it was truly the beginning of the end.
Yes, I'll be ready in a moment, I just need one more thing...
I told you to go away. I'm not opening this door for anybody!
If you don't find him soon, he'll return, stronger than ever!
I'm very dangerous! Don't make me come out there and hurt you!
The prophecies are clear. Only the Dragonborn can stop Alduin.
Come in, come in. Mustn't speak of these things in the Ratway.
But, give me... just a moment... I must gather a few things...
Delphine! I... it's good to see you. It's been... a long time.
When misrule takes its place at the eight corners of the world
I'm not opening this door, so you might as well leave me alone.
So this is where Delphine has been hiding all these years? Hmm.
Let's see... you have the symbol for "King"... and "Warrior"...
You'll die knowing it was the Blades that sent you to Oblivion.
No, the dragons are merely the final portent of the End of Days.
It was always just before dawn. The whole world was in darkness.
I would suggest staying on the plates with the Dragonborn symbol.
Look here! You see how the ancient Blades revered Reman Cyrodiil.
Its location has been lost for centuries, but I've found it again.
Ah! I've heard of this Shouting, but never seen it. Fascinating...
Just think. We're the first to set foot here in thousands of years.
Very good. I'll trust you to get there in one piece. Don't be long.
You'd better come in and tell me how you found me and what you want.
He must have returned to Sovngarde to feed on the souls of the dead.
The Dragon from the dawn of time, who devours the souls of the dead!
Fascinating! Original Akaviri bas-reliefs... almost entirely intact!
The World-Eater wakes, and the Wheel turns upon the Last Dragonborn.
You're right, I forget myself. I've lived without hope for so long...
When the Dragonborn Ruler loses his throne, and the White Tower falls
You mean... you don't mean to say you haven't heard of Alduin's Wall?
If my friends find out you've been bothering me, you'll be very sorry!
We must go, quickly now. Take me to Delphine. We have much to discuss.
Very true! Hmm. I wonder... do you know the Jarl's palace in Whiterun?
The Blades knew, once upon a time. Alduin's Wall is the key. Yes, yes!
He grows more powerful with every soldier slain in your pointless war!
In the manner of dreams, I cannot escape. I'm forced to wait and watch.
Oh, yes. It's all been foretold. The end has begun. Alduin has returned.
30th of Frostfall, 171. Thirty years ago. The Great War started that day.
What's that you said? Dragonborn? Then... there really is hope after all?
I've read the dragon language, but to hear it spoken is most interesting.
Thank you. Thank you, Dragonborn. You've done a great service for us all.
Here are the Akaviri - the Blades - you see their distinctive longswords.
Important? Far more than even she realizes! If you are Dragonborn, then...
I'm afraid she's right. I'm deeply sorry that this has to come between us.
Justice can be harsh. But it is still justice. Paarthurnax deserves to die.
Then... there is hope! The gods have not abandoned us! We must... we must...
By calling the dragon with the Voice, he will hear you wherever he might be.
Amazing, eh? To be standing at the very entrance to Sky Haven Temple itself!
I'll need this... No, no, useless trash... where'd I put my annotated Anuad?
I want to leave my high place, to seek shelter. From what, I don't yet know.
If we can just find out what this Dragonrend Shout is, you may have a chance.
You shouldn't be here, unless you've decided to bring Paarthurnax to justice.
Remember, this is where they recorded all they knew of Alduin and his return.
Paarthurnax? That name is familiar... the legendary leader of the Greybeards?
Ah, indeed. I'm no master of the Voice, but it is written here in this scroll.
Hasn't she told you? We were both Blades, back when that still meant something.
Only a Dragonborn can stop him. But no Dragonborn has been known for centuries.
Have you ever heard of the expression, "the scale of the Argonian that bit you?"
We heard the news at Cloud Ruler by courier, riding hard from the Imperial City.
One of the mages there may be able to put you on the trail of this Elder Scroll.
I think it very likely that he will be unable to resist investigating your call.
Shout for Odahviing from the Dragonsreach courtyard. After that, it's up to you.
Do you know the College of Winterhold? They have a deep interest in such arcana.
It was a cold day. The end of Frostfall is nearly winter in the Jerall Mountains.
Well. They've been hunting down Blades since the Great War, on general principle.
What?! No, that's not me. I'm not Esbern. I don't know what you're talking about.
If we can just find out what Shout the ancient Nords used, you may have a chance.
Are you paying attention, Delphine? You might learn something of our own history.
Ah, don't you see? The names of dragons are always three Words of Power -- Shouts.
Here, the humans rebel against their dragon overlords -- the legendary Dragon War.
Fine! I'll just... leave the rest of it. Let's go, then, if you're in such a hurry.
You'd better come inside. Quickly now. Thalmor agents have been seen in the Ratway.
I hope you will return to us soon with the news that justice has finally been done.
The Blades archives held so many secrets... I was only able to save a few scraps...
I thought she'd have realized it's hopeless by now. I tried to tell her, years ago...
I've told you where Sky Haven Temple is. I'll trust you to get us there in one piece.
Ah. Of course. Not everyone has an appreciation for the artistic wonders of the world.
Ah. Of course. Not everyone has an appreciation for the artistic wonders of the world.
Perhaps the College of Winterhold. What learning there is in Skyrim is gathered there.
Stop! Are you so blind to our danger that you can't see past your petty disagreements?
The other Blades? No. Best not to think what the Thalmor do to their victims, my dear.
Hmm? Oh, yes. Presumably something rather specific to dragons, or even Alduin himself.
I would not have expected a scholar of such an esoteric turn of mind among their ranks.
Here you sit arguing about... nothing! While the fate of the land hangs in the balance!
Hmm. Well, if that's what the Greybeards told you, I confess I don't have a better plan.
At long last. An ancient evil is avenged. The shades of many Blades salute you this day.
Can you not put aside your hatred for even one moment in the face of this mortal danger?
With both Alduin and Paarthurnax dead, a dark chapter in history will finally be closed.
And then I would wake up. And hope that it was just a dream... but know that it was not.
I still need some time to get used to the idea that there might be hope for us after all.
He's not compelled to, but dragons are prideful by nature and loath to refuse a challenge.
Interesting problem. Hmm. Yes. I've been poring over the records here in Sky Haven Temple.
I never expected... well, suddenly here you are and now I'm too muddled to explain myself.
Your Voice in particular is likely to intrigue this dragon, after your victory over Alduin.
Don't you understand the danger? Don't you understand what the return of the dragons means?
Alduin's Wall was created by the ancient Akaviri Dragonguard, the forerunners of the Blades.
Yes... yes, that's an excellent idea. Who knows what other lost treasures we might discover?
Oh, this was thousands of years ago, before the Akaviri crusaders cleansed Skyrim of dragons.
This skill should prove very useful, Dragonborn. Fighting fire with fire, as the saying goes.
Alduin has returned! The World-Eater! Even now, he devours the souls of your fallen comrades!
We should be careful. There's no telling what traps and wards the ancient Blades may have set.
Did you find the Elder Scroll? What happened when you took it back to the Throat of the World?
I've been chronicling the recent history of the Blades. What would you like your entry to say?
This is where they built Alduin's Wall, to set down in stone all their accumulated dragonlore.
It's quiet for now, Dragonborn. Perhaps later we'll find where one of those serpents is hiding.
Dragons, pah. They can be killed. The Blades killed many in their early days as dragon-slayers.
The Akaviri blood seal can only be opened with the right kind of blood. Your blood, Dragonborn.
The architecture of this temple is marvelous. Have you ever seen stonework like this, Delphine?
The Jarl's palace in Whiterun? Ah, yes... the old story about King Olaf's pet dragon. Ingenious!
It's true that his crimes are long in the past, but justice does not count the passage of years.
Yes, of course. We'll have more time to look around later, I suppose. Let's see what's up ahead.
I wish there were another way, Dragonborn, but it falls to you to serve justice upon Paarthurnax.
I used to dream of it. In the dream, I was standing... someplace high up... a tower, or mountain.
This Elder Scroll might be able to show you the past in some way, then... yes, yes, it could work.
I'm afraid there's a problem. A serious one. I've discovered who the Greybeards' leader really is.
Don't move. Don't move. I have it here, somewhere... never thought it would be any use, but now...
The prophecy which brought the Akaviri to Tamriel in the first place, in search of the Dragonborn.
It's the prophecy, don't you see? Only a Dragonborn can stop Alduin and avert the end of the world!
The entrance must be past the Forsworn camp. Look for a canyon or cave entrance into the Karthspire.
Then... there is hope after all. For so long, all I could do was watch our doom approach, helplessly.
Its location has been lost for centuries, but I've found it again. Not lost, you see, just forgotten.
Delphine? How do you ... ? This is just a trick to get me to open the door, eh? I told you to go away!
Ah... the power of the dragon is yours! There can be no doubt that you are the Dragonborn of prophecy.
I knew you could do it... I believed in you, and yet... I still didn't think I'd live to see this day.
Ah... here's the "blood seal." Another of the lost Akaviri arts. No doubt triggered by... well, blood.
Ah, you're back. I hope you've made some progress on tracking down this Shout we need to defeat Alduin?
But... I don't yet know how you can stop him. The prophecy doesn't say. But Alduin's Wall does. I hope.
Delphine really is alive, then? You'd better come in then and tell me how you found me and what you want.
Well, you see, the palace - Dragonsreach - was originally built to hold a captive dragon. Hence its name.
Haven't you figured it out yet? What more needs to happen before you all wake up and see what's going on?
Although... it might be difficult to persuade the Jarl to allow you to use his palace as a dragon trap...
Yes, yes. This here, coming from the mouths of the Nord heroes -- this is the Akaviri symbol for "Shout."
I have. One of the old Akaviri records names a dragon living nearby. Shall we send the Blades to help you?
Ah, indeed. I'm no master of the Voice like these worthy gentlemen, but it is written here in this scroll.
I'm afraid there's a further problem. A serious one. I've discovered who the Greybeards' leader really is.
But now it's too late for escape. The dragon is upon me - fire and darkness descending like a thunderbolt.
Then came the flash of light - just on the horizon, within the clouds that mark the border between worlds.
Amazing... you can see how the Akaviri craftsmen were beginning to embrace the more flowing Nordic style...
Oh, how reassuring! Most likely you're with the Thalmor and this is just a trick to get me to open the door!
Yes, exactly! Most people don't know that it was originally built to hold a captive dragon - hence its name.
Just ask whenever you're ready to take on a dragon. We will send the Blades to help you fulfill your destiny.
The Blades have been hunting him for centuries, but he was protected by the Greybeards and then the Emperors.
No, I guess not. Well, the principle applies here. It's a potion, made from those dragon parts. Give it a try.
Ah, indeed? There's a pretty puzzle. Not the kind of thing you'll find in your local bookshop. Let me think...
You see, here he is falling from the sky. The Nord Tongues -- masters of the Voice -- are arrayed against him.
May the scales of the dragonkind splinter at the touch of your arrows and crack under the weight of your sword.
Haven't you been listening to me? All the old tales agree that he has some means to travel to Sovngarde itself.
But just as Paarthurnax's later deeds do not expiate his crimes, your deeds do not allow us to ignore our duty.
I anticipated the problem. While you were arranging this meeting, I was busy in the library of Sky Haven Temple.
The matter at hand is urgent. Alduin must be stopped. You wouldn't have called this council if you didn't agree.
Now they kneel, their ancient mission fulfilled, as the Last Dragonborn contends with Alduin at the end of time.
Whether or not he has truly repented, or merely acted to save himself, justice demands that he pay with his life.
Only you can truly defeat these monsters, Dragonborn, but you do not have to do so alone. The Blades can help you.
I don't suppose they want the world to end any more than we do. Or at least, they'd prefer to end it on their terms.
Oh, yes! The prophecies make clear the signs that will precede the end times. One by one, I have seen them fulfilled.
Yes, yes, so you said. But so what? The end is upon us. I may as well die here as anywhere else. I'm tired of running.
I tried to tell them. They wouldn't listen. Fools. It's all come true... all I could do was watch our doom approach...
Not lost, you see, just forgotten. The Blades archives held so many secrets... I was only able to save a few scraps...
I am. The Blades of the 2nd Era hunted down many dragons in their day, and they have records of where the beasts lived.
It seems that the gods have grown tired of us. They've left us to our fate, as the plaything of Alduin the World-Eater.
Delphine? How do you... so you've finally found her, and she led you to me. And here I am, caught like a rat in a trap.
And not just any dragon, but the Dragon - Alduin, the World-Eater, the dragon who devours both the living and the dead.
Look, here is Alduin! This panel goes back to the beginning of time, when Alduin and the Dragon Cult ruled over Skyrim.
Good. The dragon's soul should be making you stronger. Come back another time, and we'll track down another one for you.
Not yet, not yet. When we get to Riverwood... with Delphine... I'll tell you both then, when I've collected my thoughts.
Alduin's Wall was where the ancient Blades recorded all they knew of Alduin and his return. Part history, part prophecy.
I doubt the blessing's effects last for very long, so ask whenever you're heading out, and I'll perform the proper rites.
Ah, yes. The entrance seems to be near what's now known as the Karthspire. We'll have to see what we find when we arrive.
One moment, I know, time is of the essence, but mustn't leave secrets for the Thalmor... there's one more I must bring...
Patience, my dear. The Akaviri were not a straightforward people. Everything is couched in allegory and mythic symbolism.
But if you mean me, now, in particular... maybe they've started to get an inkling of what the return of the dragons means.
Haven't you been paying attention to anything I've said? Only you can finally defeat Alduin and avert the end of the world!
Haven't you been paying attention to anything I've said? Only you can finally defeat Alduin and avert the end of the world!
This whole place appears to be a shrine to Reman. He ended the Akaviri invasion under mysterious circumstances, you recall.
Yes, yes. We must think. He must have some means of travelling to and from Sovngarde. But no tale tells where that might be.
Sky Haven Temple. An ancient Blades sanctuary, hidden deep in the crags of the Reach. That's where we'll find Alduin's Wall.
Skyrim's mountains were still infested with dragons. One of the early Nord kings caught a dragon and kept it in Dragonsreach.
Despite the far-reaching fame of Alduin's Wall at the time - one of the wonders of the ancient world - its location was lost.
I'm sure you'll manage, though. If you can defeat the World-Eater, surely sweet-talking the Jarl of Whiterun isn't beyond you.
An unguessed trove of lost lore... but the important thing is that the Blades recorded many of the names of dragons they slew.
It could have been lightning, but there was no thunder. In the dream, the sense of foreboding grew, but I could never wake up.
Now we're just fellow fugitives from the Thalmor. I didn't know she was still alive, but I'm not surprised. She was a survivor.
One of the lost secrets of the Blades. Where they recorded all they knew of Alduin and his return. Part history, part prophecy.
True, he turned traitor to Alduin and helped overthrow the Dragon Cult, but that does not excuse or expiate his previous deeds.
You're in luck. There is a blessing the Blades used on the eve of battle. It's supposed to prepare the mind for slaying dragons.
He's a dragon. Not just any dragon, but the right hand of Alduin, responsible for many atrocities during the ancient Dragon War.
All well and good. But did you know that he was Alduin's chief lieutenant in ancient times? Responsible for terrible atrocities?
Nonsense. Is a child who learns to read "not a scholar" of literature? Is a smith's apprentice "not a scholar" of blacksmithing?
I've been writing some notes about all the Akaviri designs we've discovered. If only the Imperial Library could see my research.
Given that Sky Haven Temple was sealed against the return of the Dragonborn, I would try turning all the pillars to that symbol.
Come now. You did more than just survive all those years. Your evasion of the Thalmor would be useful for future Blades to study.
After the so-called "battle" of Pale Pass, the Akaviri went into his service. This was the foundation stone of the Second Empire.
Fascinating! So the ancients were more daring... or foolhardy... similar to the Dragon Break itself, although less far-reaching...
Paarthurnax was the author of many atrocities during the Dragon War - crimes great enough to be remembered for thousands of years.
Ah, indeed. Well there's a pretty puzzle. Not the kind of thing you'll find in your local bookshop. I'm sure you'll manage, though.
No one can escape his hunger, here or in the afterlife! Alduin will devour all things and the world will end. Nothing can stop him!
This changes everything, of course. There's no time to lose. We must locate...  let me show you. I know I had it here, somewhere...
While you're out hunting, keep a sharp eye open for any dragon scales and bones you might find. I'd be interested in looking at them.
And of course the symbol for "Dragonborn." That's the one that appears to have a sort of arrow shape pointing downward at the bottom.
The Thalmor ambassador delivered his ultimatum to the Emperor Titus Mede: the heads of every Blades agent within the Aldmeri Dominion.
This is what I've been trying to explain. It's in Sky Haven Temple, an ancient Blades sanctuary, hidden deep in the crags of the Reach.
So little is known about the Elder Scrolls, almost anything is possible where they're concerned. Artifacts of truly inconceivable power.
Then, finally, realization and horror arrive together. The orange is flame, heat. The sound a roar, a challenge in their ancient tongue.
Justice demands that he die for his crimes. Until he is dead, I'm afraid my oath as a Blade prevents me from offering you aid and comfort.
We know a great deal about the situation and the threat that Alduin poses to us all. You need us here if you want this council to succeed.
And a sound, too. Distinct and indistinct. Not thunder... something else. Something I should recognize, but in the dream I cannot place it.
There he devours the souls of the heroic dead to feed his power. You must find his portal to Sovngarde before he returns, stronger than ever.
It's good to see the Blades fighting alongside the Dragonborn once again. To think we're treading in the footsteps of thousands of years past.
Cross-referencing this with Delphine's map of dragon burial sites, and I believe I've identified one of the dragons that Alduin has raised up.
You see, right here. Sky Haven Temple, constructed around one of the main Akaviri military camps in the Reach, during their conquest of Skyrim.
Yes, exactly. Well, I think so. It's certainly our best hope. The ancient Blades remembered much Akaviri dragonlore that has now been forgotten.
Alduin's Wall... one of the lost secrets of the Blades. Where they recorded all they knew of Alduin and his return. Part history, part prophecy.
Shor's bones! Here it is! Alduin's Wall... so well preserved... I've never seen a finer example of early second era Akaviri sculptural relief...
Its location was lost for centuries, but I know where it is: Sky Haven Temple, an ancient Blades sanctuary, hidden within the crags of the Reach.
Then it came again, this time more distinct. Closer. Definitely not lightning now. It was orange - brilliant orange, the color of hearth and dawn.

Dragon.line: Het siiv nunon dinok.
Alduin sizaan midroti.
Come and face me, then!
Skuldafn fen kos dinok.
Hiding will not save you!
Dovahkiin los ok dovahkriid
I do not fear you, Dovahkiin!
Thurri du hin sille ko Sovngarde!
Alduin saraan hin sil ko Sovngarde!
It's to be a real fight, then. Good!
Your Voice is strong... for a mortal.
My Voice has been silent for too long!
Dohaviin, your Voice is no match for mine!
I am Sahloknir! Hear my Voice and despair!
Dovahkiin zol mul. Zu'u gahvon miri Alduin.
Zok morokei! More souls to feed my master's hunger.
Speak, if you have aught to say worth the listening.
I see that mortals have become arrogant while I slept.
Alduin, thuri! Boaan tiid vokriiha suleyksejun kruziik?
You are brave. Balaan hokoron. Your defeat brings me honor.
My lord Alduin requires your death. I am glad to oblige him.
But I honor your courage. Krif voth ahkrin. Die now, in vain.
Brit grah. I had forgotten what fine sport you mortals can provide!
Daar sul thur se Alduin vokrii. Today Alduin's lordship will be restored.

Nazir.line: You can't be serious.
Where'd you come from?
I'm with you, Listener.
I... Willl... kill you!
You're as good as dead!
Come on, show yourself!
You can't hide forever!
Must've scared 'em off.
That's the end of that.
I've been expecting you.
Until next time, sister.
You dare invade my home?
I'm going to find you...
Ah! Well done! Well done!
Oh. Is that all? Really?
Until next time, brother.
You'll... pay for this...
I knew I heard something!
No... No, this cannot be...
What do you need, Listener?
Ha ha ha ha ha ha ha ha ha!
Ha ha ha ha ha ha ha ha ha!
You can't get away from me!
What? Well - what did she say?
Just one right now, actually.
I heard about what happened...
I must've been hearing things.
That'll teach you to cross me.
Well? What word of the Emperor?
Your payment, as per the usual.
Guess it was nothing after all.
You already know all that I know.
Things proceeding well, Listener?
Your payment, for a job well done.
I think so. Just hold on a moment.
Why you insolent little bastard...
But I digress. Here's your payment.
Your payment, my friend. Well done.
Your payment for services rendered.
By Sithis, now I am truly impressed!
So many contracts, so little time...
There are indeed. Three, to be exact.
There's nothing more I can teach you.
Is that... the best... you can do...
Tell me, sister. Are the stories true?
But to be outnumbered twenty to one...
Amaund Motierre? But that would mean...
Tell me, brother. Are the stories true?
Just come back alive, and I'll be happy.
Word certainly travels fast, doesn't it?
Here's your payment. Well earned, indeed.
Go on now. Time to get your hands bloody.
Well then, your timing couldn't be better.
You've already learned all I can teach you.
So you are alive. I was starting to wonder.
You reek of death, my friend. I salute you.
Here's your payment. Well earned, as always.
I'm sorry, I don't. Come see me again later.
You must be talking about the caravan job...
I could do this all day. Here's your payment.
In any event, nice work. Here's your payment.
But who knows? I've been proven wrong before.
Come on! We've got to find a way out of here!
What do you think? Some more Nirnroot, maybe?
Damn it, there's got to be a way out of here!
You're referring to that blacksmith contract?
And come back with a barrel full of gold, hmm?
Good luck. And try not to get yourself killed.
By Sithis, what a mess. I guess this is the end.
Babette, my girl - pack your things. We're moving.
Hmph. Yes. Yes, I believe you are. Very well, then.
Ah, you must mean my little adventure on the ship...
Oh! Well in that case, lead on. I'm right behind you.
You've got people to kill, my friend. Best get to it.
Ahh, going for the mysterious and brooding thing, hmm?
Considering most of us are now dead, I assumed as much.
He is, by all accounts, the worst bard in all of Skyrim.
She's here? By Sithis, I thought we'd lost her. Let's go!
No new boots? Does this mean the Argonian is still alive?
Your latest exploit certainly has the Sanctuary talking...
Ah, so the hunter came to know the fear of the hunted, hmm?
I see you're back from Dawnstar. Beitild lies dead, I hope?
What about you, Festus? How did that last contract turn out?
Should have figured the little lunatic would completely snap.
Ha! Clearly, we should have killed that old bastard years ago!
Astrid... By the sands, I still can't wrap my head around it...
Got to admit, I find the Night Mother's presence... comforting.
I'll follow you till I'm dust in the breeze. You do realize that?
Well then, let's get started. I've got three available right now.
I'll follow you till I'm dust in the breeze. You do realize that?
The Dark Brotherhood is on the rise again, Listener. I can feel it.
To kill a pirate captain, on her own ship no less? Simply masterful!
Come now. What everyone else is saying. It can't possibly be true...
But I'm sure you've got plenty to keep you busy right about now, hmm?
When you've completed all those, we'll see if I might have some more.
Unless, of course, that was your goal all along. Here's your payment.
Astrid did the right thing, and now it's your turn. End her suffering.
Still here? What's the matter, can't handle what I'm throwing your way?
And let's not forget her crew - loyal and bloodthirsty, the lot of them.
Ha! I knew it, you rascal! By Sithis, the old man was worth the trouble.
You need to find and kill Safia, the captain of the pirate ship Red Wave.
I'll teach you how to use it effectively, and keep you quick on your feet.
You smell like dead cat. Can I assume Ma'randru-jo has been taken care of?
It's the part with the severed head I find most impressive. My compliments.
That may work for your targets, but you'll find I'm not so easily impressed.
You're alive. Then I guess you haven't paid a visit to the vampire yet, hmm?
I need you to eliminate an old woman named Agnis, a servant in Fort Greymoor.
Well, yes. Two of them. But you're reaching the end of what I have available.
This place is already starting to feel like home. Must be all the bloodstains.
This place is already starting to feel like home. Must be all the bloodstains.
If you're still breathing in a few weeks, I'm sure we'll be the best of friends.
If I am to die today, so be it. But you'll not soon forget the Dark Brotherhood.
You've got that right. Only a matter of time before we're roasted alive. Come on!
No sarcasm this time. You faced a vampire, and lived to tell the tale. Well done.
The temple contract? Well, I certainly won't deny my involvement. He he he he he.
Look for a way out! We are not going to burn to death in our own damned Sanctuary!
How went your meeting with Ennodius Papius? Did you prove his paranoia legitimate?
Hmph. No arrows protruding from your skull... Can I assume Anoriath is dead, then?
You just worry about fulfilling the contract. I'll take care of things on this end.
I wish I had more contracts to dole out. You handled those jobs like a born killer.
Please tell me you flayed that horrible bard alive and made a drum from his skin...
So, what of the old beggar, Narfi? Is it done? Has he groveled for his last septim?
Ha! Remarkable! Well, the old bastard certainly made it worth your while, didn't he?
Ah, back from Fort Graymoor. The old servant has been put out of her misery, I hope?
It was a suicide mission! Astrid said as much. Yet here you stand, hale and hearty.
I'm going... as fast... as I can, you stupid she-devil. I don't see you... helping...
I give you three days before you screw up and someone runs a knife across your throat.
Hmph. Well, that's sure to throw a branch into Falkreath's well-oiled political machine.
Ah, yes. The pirate captain. A ruthless she-devil, by all accounts. Deadly with a blade.
Your first target is an Orc bard named Lurbuk. The other is a vampire by the name of Hern.
Your first target is an Orc bard named Lurbuk. The other is a vampire by the name of Hern.
I'm just trying to imagine you hiding inside the body of a mammoth. Bloody brilliant idea.
Well aren't we the eager one? Sorry to disappoint, but there's nothing. Try me again later.
Come now! You're joking with old Nazir! Surely the Emperor's life was worth more than that.
To kill triplets, secretly, and then have the whole thing blamed on the mother. Remarkable.
You do that, and I'll see what I can do about recruiting some new additions to our Family...
Ah, contracts like that are few and far between, it seems. Makes me long for the old days...
I haven't been touched like that since I visited a certain brothel in the city of Sentinel...
I'm afraid that concludes our business. You have exhausted all available additional contracts.
Now, of course I must ask... Killing the Emperor... How much did Motierre pay for such a thing?
Have you seen Astrid? She needs to speak with you. It seemed urgent. Come talk to me afterward.
Will there ever come a time when the people of Skyrim no longer need our services? I think not.
Your targets are the beggar Narfi, an ex-miller named Ennodius Papius, and Beitild, a mine boss.
Don't worry, my friend, I'll spare you the facetious quip about killing a defenseless old woman.
The new initiates are working out well. Eager, dedicated, and not afraid to get their hands wet.
Infiltrating an occupied fort and making it out in one piece is nothing to joke about. Well done.
Apparently so many people sought his death, Astrid had to hold a lottery to determine the client.
Kill these two, and I'll have one last contract to give you. I'm just finalizing some details now.
There's Deekus, a shipwreck scavenger. Ma'randru-jo, a traveling caravaner. And Anoriath, a hunter.
He's an accomplished wizard. And, locating him may be challenging, as he travels with the caravans.
This is my final contract, and certainly the most challenging. Be careful with this one, my friend.
Killing him secretly will certainly make things easier on you, but if there are witnesses, so be it.
It is. These aren't particularly glamorous assassinations, I'll be honest. Don't pay much, either.
Wonderful. The only good bard is a dead one, as far as I'm concerned. All that singing and... mirth.
She's a maid. Old, and feeble. But with this contract it's much more a matter of "where" than "who."
He's a hapless beggar living in some ruins just outside the village of Ivarstead. Easy even for you.
You haven't faced that pirate captain yet, have you? Safia? I only ask because you're still alive...
You mean... there's still a chance? But how? Our plan has gone to ruin, everyone is dead, the Family...
Save the niceties for now. I have no intention of getting invested in someone who may be dead tomorrow.
Of course she is. I hear the mining business is extremely cutthroat. And those hours... they're murder.
Personally, I've always found Argonians to be incredibly unpredictable opponents. Especially near water.
Pfft. You'd think an emperor would fetch at least twice that. Damn that motherless cheapskate, Motierre.
I don't know, Babette. Looks like he may have suffered a head wound. Best not let him out of our sight...
The Redoran contract? Yes, you heard correctly. In and out of Vvardenfell in less than forty eight hours.
Well, I just did what had to be done. Nothing more. Nothing less. I'm just glad I lived to tell the tale.
The contracts I dole out are fairly simple - go to a location, and kill the target. But this is different.
I don't know, Babette. Looks like she may have suffered a head wound. Best not let her out of our sight...
Good. And I hope you were careful in that lumber mill. Those splinters and rusty nails can be quite nasty.
Maybe I'll make the Potage le Magnifique, hmm? But not the way you make it! Ha ha ha. No, certainly not...
But really. A horker, some twine, three wood elves, and a hatchet? Points for creativity, if nothing else.
Ah, well, I'm Redguard, as I'm sure you've noticed. Though not like you. Hmph. No, definitely not like you.
That said... If your attack on Virane is witnessed, those Markarth guards are going to be none too happy...
I hope the elf didn't give you too much trouble. I once got set on fire by a sorcerer. Nasty business, that.
Congratulations. You slaughtered an emaciated beggar in cold blood. You are truly an opponent to be feared.
So you're the newest member of our dwindling, dysfunctional little Family. I've heard quite a bit about you.
Maybe I'll make the Potage le Magnifique, hmm? You may have killed the Gourmet, but true artistry never dies.
More work? My friend, you still haven't finished the last two contracts I gave you. Let's get those done, hmm?
Good. I'll spread the word that you've made reparations. Now let's forget the past and get back to business...
Taken care of that layabout Deekus, yet? Or does the idea of killing a fellow Argonian make your scales crawl?
You've not only earned your payment, but my respect as well. And believe me, friend - that does not come easily.
Excellent. Now we can get back to business. I think it's best we just forget this ever happened and move on, hmm?
He's the housecarl of Falkreath. You know what that means? He's the bodyguard to the Jarl himself. So be careful.
Back from your little vacation in Falkreath. So, did you enjoy the sights, sample the food... kill the housecarl?
Oh, you mean in that manor house? During the thunderstorm? Heh. What can I say? It happened, just like you heard.
Hmph. Been awhile since I've actually cleaved someone in two. Forgot how hard it was to get the scimitar back out.
Could you have brought us more wondrous news? Recent events notwithstanding, this is a happy day for us, my friend.
Whoa, whoa, whoa. Slow down. It's all right. You've been through a lot. Maybe you should just sit down for a bit...
As a matter of fact, I do. Two of them. One rather easy assignment, and another that should prove quite a challenge.
Hmph. How about, instead of giving you another contract, I simply deride you for not finishing the one you still have?
Killing Gaius Maro in a city will be challenging because of all the guards, but fights, even murder, are fairly common.
But they'll keep you busy. Just do them as you're able. There's no real time limit - the targets aren't going anywhere.
And if you contracted Sanguinare Vampiris, be sure to get that taken care of. Else you'll end up like our own Babette.
Did she, now? Well, as it turns out, there are a few lingering contracts we haven't had the chance to complete just yet.
The ship travels the northern coast, and sometimes isn't even in Skyrim at all. So you'll have to be patient. Good luck.
As it turns out, I've got two new contracts. One rather easy assignment, and another that should prove quite a challenge.
He's an Argonian layabout, likes to scavenge shipwrecks. My guess is he'll run if threatened. Or swim, if given the chance.
And more, dribbling in from time to time. I'll assign them to you as they become available. To be completed at your leisure.
How went your encounter with  Ma'randru-jo? Back in Hammerfell, I always loved watching two cats rip each other to shreds...
He's a vampire, and has blended into human society for years. He's never far from his female companion, Hert. Also a vampire.
Your targets are a dark elf wizard named Maluril, in the dwarven ruin of Mzinchaleft. And Helvard, the housecarl of Falkreath.
So, you've returned from Mzinchaleft! A Dwarven ruin... what an exotic place for a kill. Assuming Maluril is actually... dead?
Mallory is an expert "obtainer of goods." We can use the money to repair and refit this Sanctuary. Make a true home for us, eh?
In any event, here's my advice. Go to Riften's Ratway, find Delvin Mallory. I believe Astrid had you visit him once before, no?
Despite your misfortunes, you stayed true to the Dark Brotherhood. You've saved us all, and for this you have my eternal thanks.
Mallory is an expert "obtainer of goods." We can use the money to repair and refit this Sanctuary. Make a true home for us, hmm?
Now, might I offer some advice? You should go to Riften and find Delvin Mallory. I believe Astrid had you visit him once before?
Those caravaners can be an elusive bunch. Takes a fair bit of stalking to take one down. But then, that's half the fun, isn't it?
Ah, well, I'm Redguard, as I'm sure you've noticed. Though not like most of my kind you'll find in Skyrim, that's for damned sure.
Hmph. All right, then. Go. Go, my Listener. Find out what that slimy bastard Motierre has to say, then send the Emperor to Sithis.
The contract is for the male, Hern. But you'll probably have to contend with the female, too. So for Sithis' sake, watch yourself.
Yeah, well, don't expect many more stories like that in my future. I'm getting old. Being that amazing takes too much damn effort.
My past is long and storied, and not altogether proud. But the Dark Brotherhood saved me from myself. And I have never looked back.
My past is long and storied, and not altogether proud. But the Dark Brotherhood saved me from myself. And I have never looked back.
He's dangerous. Very dangerous. A rogue Dark Elf mage doing research into Dwarven artifacts should be both respected... and feared.
Then I suggest you "collect" it. Until you pay the fine, you are effectively cut off from all Dark Brotherhood duties and contracts.
Going after Cicero now would be a fool's errand. That little jester is currently being digested in Arnbjorn's stomach, mark my words.
And before you ask, no - I don't think it was you. Well, maybe I did, but you saving my sorry hide just now sort of erased any doubts.
You disrespected the Brotherhood. And it'll cost you. You want back into the Family, you pay the price. 500 gold, and all is forgiven.
You may be Listener, and head of the Sanctuary, but your recent behavior sets a bad example. I must insist you pay a fine of 300 gold.
Slaughtering the Emperor's cousin? At her wedding? Those guards will be out for blood. Wouldn't surprise me if the crowd jumped in too.
Listener, please... if Cicero is going to be a part of this Sanctuary I'll accept that. But can't you do something about the... dancing?
I learned to cook from my father. Ah, but that was a long time ago. Before, you know... I killed him. But let's not talk about that now.
She separated from her husband several months ago. Now the two run competing mining operations. Beitild's angry and desperate. A fighter.
I am a child of Hammerfell, born of the sands of the mighty Alik'r. In that great desert, blood is a shade of crimson I can scarce describe.
I am a child of Hammerfell, born of the sands of the mighty Alik'r. In that great desert, blood is a shade of crimson I can scarce describe.
So it would appear. Though I'd suspect she'd want you to complete your other contracts first. Do those, then we'll talk about something new.
I understand, Listener. And I hope you understand that until the fine is paid, I must insist you refrain from all Dark Brotherhood business.
Stop. Turn around. And go back the way you came. Astrid is waiting for you. She's down near the entrance. You must have passed right by her.
Your political affairs are your own, but any allegiance you may have in the ongoing war could very well affect your access to Fort Greymoor.
Does she? Listen, sister... Astrid is my mistress, and I worship the ground she walks on. But this Cicero business has her a bit... paranoid.
Yes. You're killing the Emperor himself. The Dark Brotherhood will get one shot at this, and you've been given the honors. So don't screw up.
Does she? Listen, brother... Astrid is my mistress, and I worship the ground she walks on. But this Cicero business has her a bit... paranoid.
Why am I not surprised to learn Cicero is alive? I still can't stomach the little clown, but if you've welcomed him here, I won't question it.
You infiltrated the position, eliminated the target, and made it look like an accident. Impressive. I'd say you earned your bonus on that one!
Hmph. I wouldn't worry too much about this Virane giving you the information you need. Give him a good scare, and he'll squawk like a Hagraven.
Ah, but when you're done, there's no use returning here, is there? I was thinking... the Dawnstar Sanctuary. We could make a proper home there.
I don't like mimes, minstrels, thespians, acrobats, jugglers, troubadours or tumblers. Flutists give me a headache. I particularly hate jesters.
The wood elf may run a stall in the city, but he retreats to the plains outside Whiterun to hunt. Kill him there, and no one will bear witness...
It's all thanks to a special technique I developed. I call it the "Razor-Winged Butterfly." I'd teach it to you... but then I'd have to kill you.
By Sithis, you slew everyone! Well, except for that one you kept alive, to serve as witness. Does your wickedness, or your skill, know no bounds?
The jobs Astrid gives you will be more important, and usually more complex. Just keep your wits about you. And for Sithis' sake, don't get killed.
More work? My friend, you still haven't finished the last set of contracts I gave you. Why don't you concentrate on eliminating that final target.
You can turn each one in as it's completed, or wait and turn in the whole group when all the targets have been eliminated. Whichever works for you.
As a rule, I'm also not crazy about the corpses of old women. For the Night Mother, I'll make an exception. But Astrid is the only mistress I serve.
You may be the Listener, or you may just be a lunatic who's started hearing voices. Either way, Astrid is still your mistress. Not the Night Mother.
He lives just outside Anga's Mill, in the woods. He was once the miller. Now Papius is just a paranoid recluse who thinks someone is out to get him.
Haven't you talked to Astrid? She's by the pool, greeting the Night Mother and her little jester. She has a contract for you. Come see me afterward.
Don't forget the ranting and raving. About the Night Mother, how she was the true leader of the Dark Brotherhood, and Astrid was just a "pretender."
Listen, when you're finished with this Emperor business, meet Babette and me there. I'll find some way to move the Night Mother. Don't worry. Now go!
Not ready? Well, my arrogant little sibling, until you pay the fine the Dark Brotherhood holds nothing for you. No contracts. No rewards. No respect.

Augur.line: Welcome to the Midden.
And so you shall be tested.
There is no help for you here.
The one who calls himself Ancano.
I am that which you have been seeking.
You are worthy. The knowledge is yours.
The trial awaits. Are you now prepared?
Step into the light, and your test begins.
Once again you have come seeking something.
Still you persist? Very well, you may enter.
There is no solace in knowing what is to come.
Your efforts are in vain. It has already begun.
Your perseverance will only lead you to disappointment.
You seek that which all who wield magic seek. Knowledge.
The circumstances differ. This time I have what you seek.
Your path now follows his, though you will arrive too late.
You seek meaning, shelter in Knowledge. You will not find it.
Indeed. And so you have come looking, though you do not know why.
You will be called upon to rely solely on your Restoration spells.
To see through Magnus' Eye without being blinded, you require his staff.
Like others before you, you blindly follow a path to your own destruction.
The Thalmor came seeking answers as well, unaware they will be his undoing.
Your path differs from most. You are being guided, pushed towards something.
But those who have sent you have not told you what they seek. What you seek.
You shall find this: Knowledge will corrupt. It will destroy. It will consume.
Survive, and you are worthy. This is the test put before you. Will you accept?
His path will cross yours in time, but first you must find that which you need.
He seeks information about the Eye, but what he will find shall be quite different.
Knowledge is power. Knowing spells can be powerful, but applying that knowledge is key.
The Thalmor sought the same thing, and it shall lead to his end as it has so many others.
Colette has already told you. You must be tested before knowledge can be imparted to you.
Arrogance will serve you poorly. Your worth must be determined before knowledge can be bestowed upon you.
You will rely on your skill as a mage. Not on your belongings, not on your scrolls and potions. Only what lies within.
Events now spiral quickly towards the inevitable center, so you must act with haste. Take this knowledge to your Arch-Mage.
No, though you may be the last. The one who calls himself Ancano has sought my knowledge as well, through very different questions.
You, and those aiding you, wish to know more about the Eye of Magnus. You wish to avoid the disaster of which you are not yet aware.
It is a good path, one untraveled by many. It is a path that can save your College. I will tell you what you need to know to follow it further.

Helgi.line: She wanted to play with me forever and ever.
I'm tired. I'm going to sleep for a while now.
Laelette was told to burn mommy and me, but she didn't want to.
She kissed me on the neck, and I got so cold that the fire didn't even hurt.
Laelette thought she could take me and keep me, but she can't. I'm all burned up.
You found me! Laelette was trying to find me too, but I'm glad you found me first.

Elisif The Fair.line: Well, what are you waiting for?!
I thought you didn't believe I was in danger!

Malacath.line: You pathetic weakling!
You dare summon me, Yamarz?
You have done admirably, little one.
Take the giant's hammer to Atub, and tell her what has happened here.
But, you took care of him and the giants. Two problems solved at once.
Yamarz was a fool. Always trying to scheme his way out of responsibility.
The tribe may yet survive, but only if you are willing to do what is right.
The tribe may yet survive, but only if you are willing to do what is right.
They will become stronger for it. And you... You have proven your strength.
Yamarz has fallen. It is now up to you to return the giant's hammer to Atub.
Yamarz was a coward and a weakling. His deceitful ways have cost you all greatly.
You have earned my favor for now, mortal, and so I give you a little gift. Use it well.
You have helped rid the Orcs of Yamarz in a way that avoids the destruction of their tribe.
You, place that hammer on the shrine. You're the only one who's proven worthy in all of this...
Well, the giants took care of him, and you took care of the giants. Two problems solved at once.
You don't deserve to call yourself an Orc! You're weak, you're small, and you're an embarrassment!
Now, take Shagrol's hammer back to Largashbur, and we'll see about whipping the rest of them into shape.
Yamarz has revealed himself as a coward and a schemer, and has earned the fate of all who follow that path.
You'll have to prove yourselves, but I'm willing to give you a chance. Gularzob's in charge now. Let's hope he's a better chief.
You let giants... Giants!... overrun my shrine. Bring me their leader's club as an offering, and I might release you from this curse!

Kodlak Whitemane.line: He might just make it.
Talos guide you, lass.
Where'd you come from?
She might just make it.
Ah yes, their trickery.
This isn't a good time.
You fight well, sister.
Who will speak for her?
Who will speak for him?
I... Willl... kill you!
You're as good as dead!
Come on, show yourself!
You can't hide forever!
Must've scared 'em off.
That's the end of that.
How's the newcomer look?
Watch it, there, sister.
I'm going to find you...
But we had been deceived.
Greetings, Shield-Sister.
Watch it, there, brother.
You fight well, brother.
You'll... pay for this...
I knew I heard something!
I've been looking for you.
Greetings, Shield-Brother.
All right, you want to go?
Aye, that sounds like him.
Down with you, damned worm!
You can't get away from me!
Yes, youngling. Have a seat.
Did he now? I can only hope.
What matters is their heart.
I don't mean to confuse you.
A stranger comes to our hall.
How goes the hunting, sister?
Yes, but it's no easy matter.
I must've been hearing things.
That'll teach you to cross me.
How goes the hunting, brother?
Turn back! Before he finds you!
You forget yourself, young one.
Guess it was nothing after all.
I hear you've been busy of late.
Some take to it more than others.
The mist is gone! A glorious day!
In any case, I have a task for you.
Lad, I know what you've been up to.
The mist is gone, my mind is clear.
Go. Grieve in whatever way you know.
This is not about your desires, lad.
Lass, I know what you've been up to.
And so slain the beast inside of me.
How are the young ones coming along?
This is not about your desires, lass.
And would you raise a mug in her name?
And would you raise a mug in his name?
There is no escape. Courage is useless.
Tradition is very important to me, son.
Of course. How are you in a battle, boy?
Do you know the way? I'm weary and lost.
Of course. How are you in a battle, girl?
I'll fight and die, if such fate is mine.
This is... he should not have gone alone.
And lead the Companions to further glory.
Come on now, boy, you know what I meant...
Would you raise your shield in her defense?
Would you raise your shield in his defense?
Vilkas will see if you're worth our trouble.
And would you raise your sword in her honor?
And would you raise your sword in his honor?
Then the judgment of this Circle is complete.
The World-Eater is dead! The way is now clear!
You saved us all. We will sing of you forever.
Beware! The World-Eater waits within the mist!
For Skyrim! For Shor! For Sovngarde's freedom!
Would you now? Here, let me have a look at you.
Hm. Yes, perhaps. A certain strength of spirit.
It's not so simple as just killing them, though.
Some nights I dream about the mists of Sovngarde.
Vilkas, here, will be the one testing your mettle.
Hold your head up. I'm sure it won't happen again.
Brave hearts beat lesser ones. That's the decider.
This isn't the military, son. There are no ranks.
My title is Harbinger, child. I am no man's master.
All hail the Dragonborn! Hail her with great praise!
All hail the Dragonborn! Hail him with great praise!
Keep up the effort, boy. Eyes on the horizon, always.
The cycle of retaliation may continue for some time.
That may be so. This is Vilkas. He will test your arm.
Your own fate will be your own choice. Just as always.
I worry that you've been spending too many nights out.
Vilkas, take him out to the yard and see what he can do.
Have you heard the story of how we came to be werewolves?
Vilkas, take her out to the yard and see what she can do.
We all do. It is our burden to bear. But we can overcome.
That's the spirit. Vilkas, here, will get started on that.
Good. Now move quickly. And don't leave any of them alive.
Keep up the effort, young one. Eyes on the horizon, always.
That's what I've spent my twilight years trying to find out.
Family and honor. That's what it means to be one of us, boy.
Greetings outsider. If you have some business here, speak it.
Family and honor. That's what it means to be one of us, girl.
Well, boy, you're one of us now. I trust you won't disappoint.
This man has endured, has challenged, and has shown his valor.
That's why it's best to not mistake foolhardiness for bravery.
What glorious prey. Come back alive, and with stories to tell!
Well, girl, you're one of us now. I trust you won't disappoint.
I'd wager old Vignar could see half a dozen of my predecessors.
This woman has endured, has challenged, and has shown her valor.
The witches didn't lie, of course. But it's more than our bodies.
Be careful around here. You don't want to anger the wrong person.
Oh you're back. Do you still think you could stand as a Companion?
Well, I grow old. My mind turns towards the horizon. To Sovngarde.
I hear you have spirit. This is good. You may yet join our numbers.
Living as beasts draws our souls closer to the Daedric Lord Hircine.
Let's not waste too much time talking when there is glory to be had.
Your hearts are full of grief, and my own weeps at the loss of Skjor.
Upon death, werewolves are claimed by Hircine for his Hunting Grounds.
No higher fate than fall in battle at Sovngarde's gates, a glorious end!
Calm down there, whelp. Don't feel like you need to show off for our sake.
And speak to Eorlund if you want a better weapon than... whatever that is.
You see only me because your heart knows only me as the Companions leader.
The disease, you see, affects not just our bodies. It seeps into the spirit.
Better to die with blade in hand than feed his hunger, hopeless in the mist.
Before I fall, you'll feel the bite of my sharp blade, as Shor's my witness.
The boy has a nugget of truth, but the reality is more complicated than that.
They won't give it willingly, but we can extract their foul powers by force.
Skjor has been a warrior for a long time. You would be wise to listen to him.
I worry that Shor won't call an animal to glory as he would a true Nord warrior.
But his death was avenged long ago. You have taken more lives than honor demanded.
Mind you, it's no business of mine what each Companion does in the name of honor.
If you want to lead this pack when I'm gone, you have to see farther. Look closer.
I thank you for this gift. The other Harbingers remain trapped by Hircine, though.
When you get to be my age, you'll come to miss the smell of blood. Strange, I know.
Brothers and sisters of the Circle, today we welcome a new soul into our mortal fold.
I remember when you were the same way. The more they train, the more they'll cool down.
And I see them all. The ones in Sovngarde. The ones trapped with me in Hircine's realm.
You're a strong warrior, Skjor. Your heart is fiery and that serves you well in battle.
Not every Companion, though. Only members of the Circle all share the blood of the beast.
Now that's just sad. Come back when you actually have something to show for your thieving.
When I woke from cold death, my doom was lifted - there was Shor's Hall, my heart's desire.
Let it beat with ours, that the mountains may echo and our enemies may tremble at the call.
Just have them focus on the calm in the battle. Control the rage, don't let it control you.
Some may prefer an eternity in his Hunting Grounds, but I crave the fellowship of Sovngarde.
Thank you for telling me. This is a day where our souls must cry, and our hearts will answer.
I hear your heart beat like the Harbingers of old. Your glories in Skyrim are seen and honored.
No one is in charge here. Each Shield-Brother is his own man, each Shield-Sister her own woman.
And that is their choice. But I am still a true Nord. And I wish for Sovngarde as my spirit home.
Of course. My fellow Harbingers and I have been warming ourselves here. Trying to evade Hircine.
But you don't need to share the worries of an old warrior. This day is to rejoice in your bravery!
They did not believe the change would be permanent. The witches offered payment, like anyone else.
If the Companions would hunt in the name of their lord, Hircine, we would be granted great power.
And they all see you. You've brought honor to the name of the Companions. We won't soon forget it.
And now I've found the answer. The witches' magic ensnared us, and only their magic can release us.
As in all matters of faith, though, the reality is more complicated than one believer would tell you.
Any warrior that gets to be old is either fearsome or a coward. I'll let you find out which Skjor is.
I see you've been allowed to know some secrets before your appointed time. No matter. Yes, it's true.
For some, this is a paradise. They want nothing more than to chase prey with their master for eternity.
But even the bravest of men consider their actions. I want you to survive long enough to take the mantle.
But this sneaking around. It doesn't befit warriors of your standing. Aela knows better, and so should you.
And bring me their heads. The seat of their abilities. From there, we may begin to undo centuries of impurity.
I guide the Companions. The Circle advises me. But we are all free warriors. Only our honor binds us together.
Sometimes the famous come to us. Sometimes men and women come to us to seek their fame. It makes no difference.
One of my predecessors was a good, but short-sighted man. He made a bargain with the witches of Glenmoril Coven.
And perhaps some day, you'll join us in that battle. But for today, return to Jorrvaskr. Triumph in your victory.
We may be rough, but we don't tolerate thieving around here. If that's your game, go join the lowlives in Riften.
Her heart beats with fury and courage that have united the Companions since the days of the distant green summers.

The approach to Riften is guarded by watchtowers. They will be full of archers. Move fast, and stay low.
But I accepted the Dragonborn's invitation to this council, and I at least will negotiate in good faith.
Legion soldiers gleam like fresh-fallen snow and clank like a kitchen. They head this way, we'll know it.
But the Moot has not yet met to name her High Queen. And they won't. Not as long as I have any say in it.
He was too privileged and too foolish, more interested in entertaining his queen than ruling his country.
Now, to prove yourself to me, I have a task for you. Take my axe, and deliver it to the Jarl of Whiterun.
Not enough to outweigh the loss of Riften. The whole south of Eastmarch would be open to Imperial attack.
Come see me in Windhelm when this is all done. There's always work for those willing to fight for Skyrim.
I expected better from you Dragonborn. I came here in good faith, despite your known Imperial sympathies.
It seems I have no choice but to let the Dragonborn decide. Although I'm starting to doubt your fairness.
He is weak. And no doubt knows it. He's no fool, he'll come, if only to give his troops a chance to refit.
It's about time they turned their gaze from the heavens, back to our bleeding homeland. What do they want?
With the dragons returning, and now a Dragonborn appearing... maybe the Greybeards know what it all means.
But I've had some disturbing reports of you... seen talking to General Tullius in Castle Dour, for example.
I suppose the Greybeards care about Skyrim's troubles, in their way, but I needed to do something about it.
The walls of Markarth may be high, but their age will have created weakness or secret entrances. Find them!
Maybe so, but bringing her here is a deliberate provocation. Tullius needs to know I won't be pushed around.
But you're right. Victory is within our grasp. We can afford to give the Empire one more chance to withdraw.
Tell me again why we're wasting time and dwindling resources chasing a legend. We don't even know it exists!
We're here to arrange a temporary truce to allow the Dragonborn here to deal with the dragons. Nothing more.
But rest assured. We've won Skyrim's right to determine her own future. And for that, every Nord praises us.
I'm glad we've finally restored the Pale. It was extremely foolish giving the Empire control of another port.
Losing the Rift gives Tullius easy access to the south of Eastmarch. Talos preserve us. We must take it back!
Well done, sister. If you can hold these Imperials to their promises, we might be able to reach an agreement.
But, I will honor your request. The gods know of your victories, and they themselves will glory in your deeds.
And that's as it should be. In the darkest days ahead, it'll be us Nords who lead the fight against the elves.
The love of the land and her people flows from your heart, even as death to her enemies flows from your hands.
Any Nord can learn the Way of the Voice by studying with the Greybeards, given enough ambition and dedication.
They always hope to teach the Dragonborn to respect the Way of the Voice as they do. They never fully succeed.
Yes. They chose me when I was just a lad. It was a great honor, of course. I was to become a Greybeard myself.
Well done, brother. If you can hold these Imperials to their promises, we might be able to reach an agreement.
I challenged him in the traditional way, and he accepted. There were many witnesses. No "murder" was committed.
Maybe so, but bringing her here is a deliberate provocation. We need to show Tullius we won't be pushed around.
Whiterun seems like a better choice to me. From there we can threaten to undermine the whole Imperial position.
Good. They've had their victories, but Tullius is no fool. The Empire can't afford to keep this up much longer.
As for you, General Tullius, I see now that Galmar was right. Talking to the Empire is just as useless as ever.
When we're done rooting out Imperial influence here at home, then we will take our war to the Aldmeri Dominion.
We do what we do out of love for our brothers and sisters. Here, take this as a token of such love reciprocated.
I'd like to offer you a place in my court, as a Thane. It's mainly an honorary title, but there are a few perks.
Well, that's good to know. I suggest you tell your people at the College to keep a tighter leash on their mages.
But no! We are farmers! We are craftsmen! We are sons and daughters of shopkeepers, maid servants and soldiers!
The blood of our fallen heroes will make Skyrim free, never doubt it. Also, never doubt me or Galmar. Now then...
By gods' jest in this grim mist together snared, Stormcloak and Imperial, we wander hopeless, waiting for succor.
I shouldn't agree to terms that so blatantly favor the Empire. I have no choice, though, under the circumstances.
Torygg was merely a message to the other Jarls. Whoever we replace them with will need the support of our armies.
I remember you... You were at Helgen with us! Come to join the war? Speak with Galmar. He handles the new recruits.
For it will be Skyrim that shall lead Tamriel in those dark days, when the fate of the world is finally determined.
But the political situation is still delicate. Not all the Jarls are fully committed to supporting me as High King.
I am indeed Ulfric Stormcloak, and at my side the man we know as Stormblade, and the world knows as the Dragonborn.
Well. Good! You favor the Empire less than I feared. Maybe we will be able to come to some agreement here after all.
I think you may have joined up with the wrong side, Dragonborn. You showed yourself a true son of Skyrim here today.
We have no interest in negotiating with the Empire. It's only a matter of time before they are driven out of Skyrim.
I'm glad we liberated the Rift. It was dangerous having Imperials skulking about on the southern border of Eastmarch.
I suspect you'll be of greater use to us with greater freedom, so you're free to engage the Imperials as you see fit.
It has its advantages. I've never liked having the Empire perched on my doorstep. And Riften would be easy to hold...
Yes. Although I rarely use my training. The Greybeards believe the Voice should be used only for worship of Kynareth.
If you think you can hold Markarth, you're as deluded as your Emperor when he signed away our freedom to the Thalmor.
I expected better from you Dragonborn. I came here in good faith, and now it seems you help the Empire at every turn.
You're different from what I expected, Dragonborn. You treat us with respect, despite your known Imperial sympathies.
It was you who fought your kin who didn't understand our cause, who weren't willing to pay the price of our freedom.
I am indeed Ulfric Stormcloak, and at my side the woman we know as Stormblade, and the world knows as the Dragonborn.
Blasted dark elves. I don't suppose you could tell them that I presently have larger concerns? Such as all of Skyrim?
We've been soldiers a long time. We know the price of freedom. The people are still weighing things in their hearts.
If you know any true sons and daughters of Skyrim, tell them to head to Windhelm. Ulfric Stormcloak wants to see them.
I wish more had the dedication and strength of character that you demonstrate. I'll be sure this gets put to good use.
You show great passion for battle, and compassion for those we fight for. You are becoming indispensable to our cause.
Blocking Pale Pass would cut one of the main Imperial supply routes... and would leave Whiterun dangerously exposed...
Hmm... the Empire is bringing in a lot of supplies through Dawnstar. Taking the Pale would stretch them pretty thin...
If you think this war is nearly won, you're as deluded as your Emperor when he signed away our freedom to the Thalmor.
I don't care if she can find a living dwarf -- I can't have her stirring up trouble. We have enough problems as it is.
The men will expect a speech. Will you stand by my side? I wish to honor you, Dragonborn and the truest of Stormcloaks.
We're fighting because our own Jarls, once strong, wise men, have become fearful and blind to their people's suffering.
Why would Tullius waste resources getting the crown only to give it to me? Poisoned perhaps? No. He's not that cunning.
I never thought I'd ever willingly stand in the same room with Tullius again. At least not without a weapon in my hand.
The Imperials have really dug into Whiterun. We'll wrest it from them soon enough. We must. Its central position is key.
When finally set free, I returned to Windhelm, and was greeted by a city in mourning, at one with my own grief and anger.
The gods have granted us peace for the moment. But that won't last long. I'm sure trouble will be stirred up soon enough.
I grant you a personal Housecarl to watch over your home and this weapon from my armory to serve as your badge of office.
Are you now? You were at Helgen, one of the prisoners. Although no one seems to know how you got mixed up in that affair.
Better than nothing, I guess. But Tullius will need to offer a lot more if he wants me to give up Riften without a fight.
I think you may have joined up with the wrong side, Dragonborn. You showed yourself a true daughter of Skyrim here today.
You surrender Hjaalmarch to us, and take Idgrod Ravencrone with you. Sorli the Builder will take over as Jarl of Morthal.
What about you, Elisif? Are these terms to your liking? Speak up. I'm sure General Tullius is waiting to do your bidding.
The Empire showed its true colors today, eh? Don't worry. I was once loyal to the Empire, too, before my eyes were opened.
And so in death, too late, I learn the truth - fed by war, so waxed the power of Alduin, World-Eater - wisdom now useless.
You did the best you could, sister. But I've learned the hard way that these Imperials are best dealt with at sword point.
If you think we'll just hand over Riften, you're as deluded as your Emperor when he signed away our freedom to the Thalmor.
You did the best you could, brother. But I've learned the hard way that these Imperials are best dealt with at sword point.
But beware - the Empire's loyalty is fickle. They will tire of this war, and then I will be the one dictating terms to you.
Whiterun's army will no doubt be bolstered with Legionnares. And those walls around Whiterun are old, but they still stand.
For too long he's been hand-picked by the Emperor, and given emphatic nods by milk-drinking Jarls addicted to Imperial coin.
So you as good as admit you're working for the Imperials. Yet you walk in here, and ask to join me. What are you playing at?
It's working, Galmar. Our patience has won us friends and allies. And our armies are systematically taking care of the rest.
The Empire may try to reclaim Skyrim, and I need everyone ready for that. Though the biggest threat, of course, is the elves.
And, as much as I'd like to kill the man myself, we can't risk an all out war with the Empire. We'll bide our time for now...
I can't agree to any peace council until I know Tullius will be there. The other Jarls could take that as a sign of weakness.
The Way of the Voice is an ancient, spiritual form of magic in which you project your vital essence into a "thu'um" or Shout.
I've never liked having the Imperials right on Windhelm's doorstep. Retaking Winterhold would help secure our supply lines...
And we have come this far because our cause is true. Because we fight as one. And because our hearts are bursting with anger!
Fear neither pain, nor darkness. For Sovngarde awaits those who die with weapons in their hands, and courage in their hearts.
Indeed, Elisif has become Jarl of Solitude, historically and conveniently home of the High King, backed by Imperial interests.
And if it isn't true... well, then the fight to free Skyrim goes on. Either way, I don't see the use in talking to the Empire.
You did the best you could, Dragonborn. But I've learned the hard way that these Imperials are best dealt with at sword point.
You're Dragonborn. The rules don't apply to you. You can Shout the way dragons do... without training, through inborn instinct.
Riften is off the table. The Empire barely has a toehold left in Skyrim. We don't need to pay such a high price for this truce.
First, let me be clear. The sons of Skyrim know from bitter experience that talking to the Empire is a waste of time, or worse.
Damn Tullius. He's pocketed men in the Rift. It's dangerous having Imperials skulking about on the southern border of Eastmarch.
But I'm disappointed to hear you ran away from the battle. The gods do not reward cowardice. Nor do I. I expect better from you.
But remember - you're one of us now, forever. Whatever Imperial loyalties you may have had... that's all in the past, forgotten.
So long as your criminal past stays in the past, and you fight for me with honor and integrity, we'll welcome you into our ranks.
Now then. The men will expect a speech. Will you stand by my side? I wish to honor you, Dragonborn and the truest of Stormcloaks.
Fair enough. I thought you might be able to see past your Imperial loyalties in the interests of peace, but I guess not. Still...
I spent almost ten years at High Hrothgar, learning the Way of the Voice. Then the Great War came... I couldn't stand missing it.
We should take Hjaalmarch. It'll be an important pressure point on the Imperials. We'd be within spitting distance of the capital.
Make your way to our hidden camp in the Rift. I can't continue to push forward when I'm worried about Eastmarch's southern border.
Now, by law and tradition, I can only grant the title to one who is known throughout Eastmarch, and who owns property in Windhelm.
