In exchange for Riften, the jewel of Skyrim? Hardly. How about the Reach? Gaining control of the silver mines would be invaluable.
I'm sure that will be a great comfort to the families of your comrades who died to free the land you just gave back to the Empire.
A man's heart is his own burden to bear, so I will honor your request, though it casts a bit of gloom on an otherwise glorious day.
For the moment we have ceased hostilities. We'll see what these Imperials have to say. I doubt we'll come to any lasting agreement.
Good. Maybe he's realized he's beaten. The way things are going, we'll be knocking at the gates of Solitude itself before too long.
We should risk letting those milkdrinkers put Thorryg's woman on the throne? She'll hand Skyrim over to the elves on a silver plate.
And in that moment, the gods will look down and see Skyrim as she was meant to be. Full of Nords who are mighty, powerful, and free!
Oh? True, I may meet my end in a dark alley with a dagger in my back. But the people have seen the truth, and they will not back down.
Now that the Empire has been driven from the Reach we can put a stop to the raping of her silver mines. That silver belongs in Skyrim.
You'll be doing whatever Galmar tells you to do, and causing as much mayhem as possible for the Empire and any Jarl who supports them.
It'll be Skyrim that leads the charge. Mark my words. And that time around, mankind will either succeed once and for all or die trying.
Multitudes of our enemies are buried in an avalanche of pain and retribution delivered by your hands. I shall call you Snow-Hammer now.
Blocking Pale Pass would cut one of the main Imperial supply routes... and Whiterun is dangerously exposed with the enemy in Falkreath.
Damn it. What were they doing there? Imperial spies are everywhere. Never forget that...  I trust you gave them a thrashing? Now then...
There is room in my court for a new Thane. It's an honorary title, mainly, but there are a few perks someone like you could make use of.
There is room in my court for a new Thane. It's an honorary title, mainly, but there are a few perks someone like you could make use of.
I, his only son, forced to deliver his eulogy via a letter I had smuggled out of prison. Such is the love of Titus Mede for his subjects.
I'm glad we've taken back Winterhold. While the Imperials were up there, important resources were tied up stiffening our northern border.
Tullius must be getting nervous. Taking Hjaalmarch we're practically in his backyard now. As soon as we're able, we'll march on Solitude.
However, I could only grant the title to someone who is known throughout my Hold, and who owns at least one piece of property in my city.
I don't like Imperial troops holed up in Winterhold... such a close march to Windhelm. So I'm sending a campaign up there to take it back.
There will be peace for a time, during which we must rebuild Skyrim into the land it once was. Strong. Self-reliant. The center of mankind.
That said, some of the Legion's staunchest men have organized themselves, and are operating out of hidden military camps in the wilderness.
I doubt he's forgiven me for leaving. And for... well, for what he'd consider blasphemy. Using Shouts for anything but worship of Kynareth.
Then it is settled. The Jarl will continue to rule Solitude, I will garrison armies here to ward off Imperial attempts to reclaim the city.
There's nothing else to talk about, unless the Empire is finally ready to renounce its unjust claim to rule over the free people of Skyrim.
As you can see, I have much that requires my attention. If you're inclined to gab, there are some nice prison guards you may like to meet...
We've driven the Imperials out of Whiterun. This is good. Very good. We now control the center. It's a powerful position. One I aim to keep.
We've driven the Imperials out of Whiterun. This is good. Very good. We now control the center. It's a powerful position. One I aim to keep.
On this day, our enemy will know the fullness of our determination, the true depth of our anger, and the exalted righteousness of our cause.
With me, you kill Skyrim's last hope. Mark my words. You'll all be serving elven masters soon. And know then, that I would have stopped them.
I killed Torygg to prove our wretched condition. How is the High King supposed to be the defender of Skyrim, if he can't even defend himself?
The Empire's ravenous hunger for Skyrim's silver makes them fierce. But rest assured, we'll win the Reach and wrest it from their greedy paws.
If royal blood was spilt, all of Cyrodiil would be up in arms. We can't afford an all out war with the Empire. So we'll bide our time for now.
The gods are watching. The spirits of our ancestors are stirring. And men under suns yet to dawn will be transformed by what we do here today.
I'm proud we liberated Falkreath from the Empire. In many ways it's the heart and soul of Skyrim. Not to mention its rather strategic position.
You'll have to make your own decision. It's a beautiful philosophy, but outside the seclusion of High Hrothgar, I was never able to hold to it.
Soon enough we will march on Solitude. And on that day the heroes of Sovngarde will march with us, and the Empire will fall to its knees in fear.
Tullius has camps in the Pale, entertaining thoughts of battle. The Empire still underestimates the force of our cause. It will be their undoing.
On behalf of the sons and daughters of Skyrim, on behalf of all that is righteous and true, take this token of our appreciation for your service.
Some brothers have taken to calling you Bone-Breaker, evidence of your ferocity and determination. A fitting name. So we shall all call you thus.
But I also want you to find our hidden camp in Falkreath. Galmar will have special tasks for you, and will need you when we liberate the capital.
I fight for my people impoverished to pay the debts of an Empire too weak to rule them, yet brands them criminals for wanting to rule themselves!
The war demands so much from us, and we give all we have to it and to the people... but I've kept a little something to offer you in appreciation.
Will she put aside her personal hatred for me, and her misplaced love for the Emperor and his coin, so that the suffering of our people will end?
True sons and daughters of Skyrim reign in every city, we've crippled the Imperial Legion, and cut off its head by killing General Tullius himself.
Are you sure? It's rightfully yours, but this money could go to getting better weapons and armor, and making repairs to the settlements we control.
I know you thought we weren't losing much when you gave it to the enemy. But I'm wasting troops guarding the border that belong on the front lines.
I have... fallen from their strict teaching, but I still don't feel it should be used lightly. Not all of Arngeir's lecturing was wasted, it seems.
But, that doesn't mean I won't start acting like it. There's much to do. The new Jarls need help building armies, and enforcing their right to rule.
I can't believe we let them take the Pale. Losing the port of Dawnstar is lessened only by the threat of Imperial forces being so close to Windhelm.
I almost can't believe it. The Empire has been defeated. The world bears witness to the power and righteousness of the sons and daughters of Skyrim.
I'm not sure why Tullius is wasting his time trying to take Winterhold from us. But if he wants to throw his men away, I'll gladly accept that gift.
The Empire thinks it holds Falkreath from us. But the souls of Skyrim's bravest are buried there and will fill our hearts and strengthen our blades.
You're alive! I'm impressed! Galmar had his doubts about you, but I knew you'd pull through. You should speak with him. He's going to need your help.
And I need capable and loyal warriors close at hand. So I'd like to offer you a home in Windhelm. Speak with my steward who'll make the arrangements.
I often think about High Hrothgar. It's very... disconnected from the troubles down here. But that's why I couldn't stay, and why I couldn't go back.
We come to this moment carried by the sacrifices and courage of our fellows. Those who have fallen. And those still bearing the shields to our right.

Hadvar.line: The guard has the key.
Wwwwwwrrrraaaaaahhhhh!
How'd you end up here?
I'll follow your lead.
Stormcloaks! Get them!
Haming, get over here!
Where'd you come from?
Send Ulfric my regards!
Take out their archers!
Damn Stormcloak rebels.
Stay close to the wall!
We have to keep moving.
I... Willl... kill you!
You're as good as dead!
Come on, show yourself!
You can't hide forever!
Must've scared 'em off.
That's the end of that.
They're up on that wall.
Up in that tower! Quick!
What next, giant snakes?
By your orders, Captain.
This looks like the way.
I'm going to find you...
We make quite a team, eh?
You better get some gear.
That the best Ulfric has?
You'll... pay for this...
I knew I heard something!
You got here just in time.
You got here just in time.
This is hopeless! Retreat!
Hhhhhuuuuuzzzzzaaaaahhhhh!
Huh. I had heard you died.
Aw, quit your bellyaching.
Gods... Everyone get back!
Let's see where this goes.
Just what I was afraid of!
Someone take out that mage!
The prisoners are escaping!
And what about Jarl Elisif?
And what about Jarl Erikur?
Go ahead, see for yourself.
Not the sneaking type, huh?
Yeah, I know what you mean.
You can't get away from me!
Can't stop to chat, citizen.
You call yourself a warrior?
Mage up there! In the tower!
We're through! Keep at them!
For the glory of the Empire!
Soon. I can almost taste it.
With me, prisoner. Let's go!
Last but not least. Let's go!
Come on. Sounds like trouble.
What are you waiting for? Go!
If you want to die, so be it.
Stormcloaks! Filthy traitors!
You won't leave Helgen alive!
We've got to get inside. Now!
Follow the Captain, prisoner.
That's how the Legion fights.
Let me know when you're ready.
Archers. Behind those windows!
Just walk towards me, come on!
Wait. You there. Step forward.
Hmm. That doesn't go anywhere.
I must've been hearing things.
That'll teach you to cross me.
Look! Up on the wall. A wizard!
Pleasant weather for a war, no?
That a boy. You're doing great.
Come on! We need to get inside!
There's a way out through here!
Guess it was nothing after all.
You're gonna get me out of here?
You leave me no choice, citizen.
Looks like we're on our own now.
Better down here than out there.
Better down here than out there.
Grab what you can, and let's go.
Damn it. No going back that way.
Hold on, now, we only want to...
Along the fence, do you see them?
By that rock over there. See 'em?
What? Now's not the time to chat.
I guess we'd better try this way.
That's what I keep telling myself.
That's what I keep telling myself.
Imperial business. Be on your way.
Not sure you belong here, citizen.
Gah! Let's get on with it already!
I think we're almost out. Come on!
Nothing for it but to keep moving.
This imperial armor is damn heavy.
Come on, you need your hands free.
They got archers up along the wall!
That's it! We're pushing them back!
Yeaaaah! Come on, we can take them!
Straight to business with you, huh?
What are your thoughts on all this?
The Stormcloaks will never stop us.
Better to stick together down here.
Better to stick together down here.
Let's teach these bastards a lesson.
Just head north - you can't miss it.
Draugr. Dammit. I hate those things.
Ulfric Stormcloak. Jarl of Windhelm.
Keep an eye out for Stormcloak spies.
We're outnumbered, but I have a plan.
We're outnumbered, but I have a plan.
They're hiding over by those stables!
So why'd you join up with the Legion?
We'd better stick together down here.
Right, the leader of the Stormcloaks.
Must be here somewhere. Keep looking.
There it is again. Did you hear that?
Good! I've got a few scores to settle.
I'm not sure if that's a good thing...
I'm not sure if that's a good thing...
This is none of your concern, citizen.
Can't wait to gut one of those rebels.
Citizens! Go home and lock your doors!
There may be a way out, down this way.
To the block, prisoner. Nice and easy.
It's you and me, prisoner. Stay close!
They're over there, next to that house!
We destroyed those Stormcloak traitors!
Damn, that dragon doesn't give up easy.
Haming, you need to get over here. Now!
Let me see if I can get that door open.
Wizard! Hiding over there by that fence!
The waiting... the insufferable waiting.
Good idea. Let me know when you're ready.
Someone put an arrow in that damn wizard!
They got archers over there by the fence!
Ralof! You damned traitor. Out of my way!
Come with us. We need to get out of here.
Look sharp. There they are. In the window!
Good job out there. Glad you were with us.
A dragon.... First in a thousand years....
Good work! Lucky we had you with us today.
There's more of them somewhere around here.
Do the men you killed haunt you? Mine do...
Let's see if we can find a way out of here.
I hope I'll see you in Solitude. Good luck.
Looks like the way out's up ahead. Come on.
Quick, I can cut you loose inside the keep.
Those binds aren't going to cut themselves.
Fall back, fall back! Regroup and fall back!
Can't wait to kill another of Ulfric's boys.
Thanks. Couldn't have done that without you.
Don't worry. We'll get out of here, somehow.
Stick close. This place gives me the creeps.
Hold up. There's a bear just ahead. See her?
Oh, fine. Keep your secrets. I see how it is.
That went pretty well, all things considered.
Look around. Grab anything that looks useful.
It's nice to be back in a friendly spot, huh?
So that's why you were in the cart. Thieving.
Keep it together! There's not many of us left!
Do the men you've killed haunt you? Mine do...
Well, well. Look who it is! How have you been?
All right, let's see if we can find a way out.
They're in! Don't let them gain further ground!
But never mind all that. We've got a job to do.
Don't look up. Just focus on me. You can do it!
Who do you think killed more rebels? You? Or me?
I hate those things. Too many eyes or something.
Find yourself a weapon. You're going to need it.
I can't believe we're the only ones who survived.
Let's keep moving. That thing is still out there.
Captain. What should we do? He's not on the list.
They've got a mage hiding in that window up there!
See if one of these chests has some armor for you.
Captain. What should we do? She's not on the list.
Where are you going? The barracks is through here!
Are your ears still ringing? I hope that goes away.
I suppose you're right... they say it gets easier...
I suppose you're right... they say it gets easier...
Fine. I hope that dragon takes you all to Sovngarde.
Didn't you hear me? I said the keep is under attack!
Go ahead. I'll follow your lead and watch your back.
All for some old crown? It's a strange war all right.
Glad you were here. Couldn't have done it without you!
Glad you were here. Couldn't have done it without you!
But never mind all that. What brings you out this way?
The Legion could sure use someone like you. Good luck.
Hear that? Stormcloaks. Maybe we can reason with them.
You picked a bad time to come home to Skyrim, kinsman.
I'm sorry. At least you'll die here, in your homeland.
But, never mind all that. What brings you out this way?
I'm pretty sure I killed more than you. I was counting.
These bastards call themselves Imperial Legionnaires...
Good luck. If you get a chance, look me up in Solitude.
I'm still sore where I took that arrow. I hate archers.
I guess we're lucky that didn't come down on top of us.
The torture room. Gods, I wish we didn't need these....
We'll get him when he comes back around. Hold your fire.
Keep an eye out for trouble. It's only a matter of time.
It's not safe out here! Return to your homes immediately!
I have to admit. This is more fun that I thought it'd be.
I still almost can't believe it... a real, live dragon...
Things look quiet enough here. Come on. There's my uncle.
Not many wood elves would choose to come alone to Skyrim.
Wait a second. Looks like there's something in this cage.
But, never mind all that. We've got a job to do. Don't we?
We sure showed them, eh? Thanks for your part in all that.
I can't decide if things are about to get worse or better.
Let's see where this goes. We can't be the only survivors.
I'm sick of waiting. When are we going to see some action?
Mage, eh? Well, to each his own. It's not for me to judge.
You made it? I was sure I'd find you face down in the dirt.
Well, it just so happens I have the perfect mission for you.
I guess that'll teach me to try to reason with a Stormcloak.
I hope that dragon isn't still flying around here somewhere.
They'll think twice before coming back here, that's for sure!
Come on! We need to get inside before that dragon comes back!
The Legion could really use someone like you, especially now.
Be careful, friend. This place doesn't seem too... forgiving.
Got to be a sword or two in one of these chests. Look around.
Dragonborn huh? Was it your ma or your pa that was the dragon?
I'm glad to be out of that place. I hope this leads somewhere.
Come on. I'll feel better once we're under cover in Riverwood.
You from Daggerfall, Breton? Fleeing from some court intrigue?
You from one of the strongholds, Orc? How did you end up here?
Stay alert. Never know when those Stormcloak dogs might charge.
From the look of it, I'd bet it leads straight into the prison.
This is it! Hold at all costs! Don't give them any more ground!
At least we don't have a dragon to worry about, for the moment.
We were stopped in Helgen when we were attacked... by a dragon.
Are you a relative of one of the Riften dock workers, Argonian?
An old storeroom. Grab anything you can, but let's keep moving.
I'm glad you decided to come with me. We're almost to Riverwood.
You better get that armor on. Give that sword a few swings, too.
I'm sorry. We'll make sure your remains are returned to Elsweyr.
All right. You infiltrate their camp, we'll cover you. Good luck!
I need to get back to Solitude and let them know what's happened.
I thought you could help us out. Food, supplies, a place to stay.
I'm sorry. We'll make sure your remains are returned to Cyrodiil.
I'm sorry. We'll make sure your remains are returned to Orsinium.
Break our men out and head to the courtyard. We'll meet you there.
Let's take our time. I think we can sneak by her if we're careful.
Don't you even know what's going on? A dragon is attacking Helgen!
Listen, as far as I'm concerned you've already earned your pardon.
Another refugee? Gods really have abandoned your people, dark elf.
I'm sorry. We'll make sure your remains are returned to High Rock.
I'm sorry. We'll make sure your remains are returned to Morrowind.
I'm sorry. We'll make sure your remains are returned to Valenwood.
We'll stand guard out here and rush in as soon as we hear fighting.
So that's what's in there? Coins and weapons? How do you know that?
Good to see you, my friend! I knew you'd make a proper Legionnaire.
We'd better push on. I'm sure the others will find another way out.
I'm sorry. We'll make sure your remains are returned to Hammerfell.
You didn't know? That was Ulfric Stormcloak and his top lieutenants.
I'm sorry. We'll make sure your remains are returned to Black Marsh.
We must be near the entrance for that bear to have made its den here.
You'll want to talk to Legate Rikke. She deals with the new recruits.
Still alive, prisoner? Keep close to me if you want to stay that way.
Be careful on the way to Solitude. Damn Stormcloaks could be anywhere.
You're a long way from the Imperial City. What're you doing in Skyrim?
I'll stay here and guard the shipment. You get back to camp with news.
If those rebels think they can best us, they have another think coming.
I wonder how many of us think we'll be the lucky ones and survive this?
Been so long since I killed a Stormcloak, my sword arm's getting flabby.
We've got them pinned in! It's only a matter of time now. Keep fighting!
Do colors seem brighter to you? Everything seems bigger, too. Strange...
An old storeroom. See if you can find some potions. Might come in handy.
Thief, eh? It's never too late to take charge of your own fate, you know.
Have them send some men - with a new wagon. This one isn't going anywhere.
I don't blame you for being angry about it. I would be, too, in your shoes.
I'll be sure to put in a good word for you if I get back to Solitude first.
This looks like the way out! I was starting to wonder if we'd ever make it.
I'm sorry. We'll make sure your remains are returned to the Summerset Isle.
That was crafty. I'm sure having a steward in the pocket will come in handy.
Really? It just so happens we've been tracking a wagon! For about a day now.
Since nobody's trying to kill me just now, maybe we should rest for a minute.
He surrendered pretty meekly, too. So much for his death-or-glory reputation.
We should keep moving. Come here. Let me see if I can get those bindings off.
See if you can get it open with some picks. We'll need everything we can get.
Or if you're feeling lucky, you can take this bow. Might take her by surprise.
What're you doing here, Redguard? You a sellsword? A sailor from Stros M'kai?
You should wait until night fall before trying to sneak in. Talos preserve you.
The Stormcloaks will be here any minute! Hurry! Get inside and lock your doors!
So, do you get that weird taste in your mouth before battle, too? What is that?
Oh, right, you were caught trying to cross the border into Skyrim, weren't you?
All right. You infiltrate their camp, we'll cover you from the ridge. Good luck!
Citizen. You have been warned. You leave me no choice but to resort to violence.
These old ruins... They don't want us here, the ancient ones. Better left alone.
I don't know where to start. You know I was assigned to General Tullius's guard.
We're going to take out their sentry, then sneak into their camp and ambush them.
Hey, I found a crown over here on this corpse. Is this the one we're looking for?
Great work! Get back and report our success. I'll stay here and clean up the mess.
That wagon recently had a little accident. They're stranded now, just up the road.
I don't think Rikke even knows my name. Probably because I'm not "The Dragonborn."
Gunnar, take care of the boy. I have to find General Tullius and join the defense.
You're not with the Thalmor Embassy, are you high elf? No, that can't be right....
Well? Where the hell are those damned Stormcloaks? Let's get this over with already.
If I make it back to Solitude, I'll put in a good word for you with General Tullius.
You with one of the trade caravans, Khajiit? Your kind always seems to find trouble.
With a bit of luck, we'll catch them completely off guard and even the odds a little.
Shh.. Uncle, please. Keep your voice down. I'm fine. But we should go inside to talk.
He's always used the ban on the worship of Talos to stir people up against the Empire.
Citizen. I'm warning you. Your presence is interfering with Imperial business. Be gone!
I hope so. The Legion is Skyrim's only hope right now. Come on, we'd better get moving.
Maybe that claw has a clue on it? You know, how to set the rings so the door will open.
You're always thirsty. Just like you're always drunk. Now let me get back to my rounds.
Not now. If I don't pull night duty, I'll drink a round with you at the Sleeping Giant.
These are dark days. But at least we know we're doing our part to make things better, eh?
We'd better keep moving. I feel like that dragon might be up there watching us right now.
Warrior, good! I knew you shouldn't have been on that cart the minute I laid eyes on you.
You, too, huh? I'd much rather a straight up fight than creeping around a place like this.
I need this to go well. This is my first real command since they promoted me after Whiterun.
And if the rebels have themselves a dragon, General Tullius is the only one who can stop them.
Listen, I'm going to lay up here for a while. You can make your own way to Solitude from here.
And if the rebels have themselves a dragon, General Tullius is the only one who can stop them.
It's probably best if we split up. Good luck. I wouldn't have made it without your help today.
Not much more to tell. This dragon flew over and just wrecked the whole place. Mass confusion.
First, we're going to take out their sentry, then we'll situate ourselves overlooking the camp.
What the rebels like to forget, is that the Empire's what's keeping the Dominion out of Skyrim.
My father was a Legionnaire, and his before him. I guess I never considered doing anything else.
We'd better get out of here. I don't think this place is going to stand up to much more of this.
But you can bet he'll be trying to figure it out. This could shift the whole balance of the war.
General Tullius has his headquarters in Castle Dour, in the city of Solitude, northwest of here.
But it was all a mistake. You weren't supposed to be on that cart with those Stormcloak traitors.
Closest town from here is Riverwood. My uncle's the blacksmith there. I'm sure he'd help you out.
He's a friend. Saved my life in fact. Come on, I'll explain everything, but we need to go inside.
All right, if you insist. But we'll come running if it sounds like things have gotten out of hand.
I remember you... you were with us at Helgen, and Whiterun. Glad to have you with us. How are you?
She's a friend. Saved my life in fact. Come on, I'll explain everything, but we need to go inside.
Lucky for us, that the wagon recently had a little accident. They're stranded now. Just up the road!
You'd better get out of that Stormcloak gear. Today's not the day to go around dressed like a rebel.
Looks like we're the only ones who made it. Was that really a dragon? The bringers of the End Times?
If you want to help stop that dragon, your best bet is to go to Solitude and join up with the Legion.
I guess... I'll hold down the fort by my lonesome. Hurry back and tell them to send a garrison. Quickly!
Sure, I understand. It's not easy to go from being executed by the Legion one day to joining up the next.
These are the Guardian Stones, three of the thirteen ancient standing stones that dot Skyrim's landscape.
You sure you won't change your mind? Riverwood isn't far. I'm sure my uncle will be glad to help you out.
I don't know if anyone else got out alive. I doubt I'd have made it out myself if not for my friend here.
Hey there! Can you believe this place? So cold my breath turns to icicles! What are we even doing up here?
Next, you'll infiltrate their position and get their attention while we hit them with a barrage of arrows.
Looks like he's gone for good this time. But I don't think we should stick around to see if he comes back.
I'm glad you're with us. I don't like the looks of this place... and I'm not talking about the Stormcloaks.
See that ruin up there? Bleak Falls Barrow. When I was a boy, that place always used to give me nightmares.
Guess Tullius wants to make sure everything's locked down before launching a serious campaign for Eastmarch.
You know, some nights when I close my eyes, I see the battle stretched out before me, like I'm still there...
You know, some nights when I close my eyes, I see the battle stretched out before me, like I'm still there...
Listen, you should go to Solitude and join up with the Imperial Legion. We could really use someone like you.
There's a grate outside the wall. Looks like it used to be buried in snow. I don't think they know it's there.
No. Not yet. After all, a dragon... something out of old tales and legends... no one could have expected that.
Of course! I know, today wasn't the best introduction to the Legion, but I hope you'll give us another chance.
This time, the General turned the tables on him. Ulfric rode right into our ambush with only a few bodyguards.
I thought we were taking Ulfric back to Cyrodiil, but I guess the General changed his mind. You know the rest.
Even I'll admit it hasn't been the Empire's finest hour. But it wasn't like the Emperor had any choice, did he?
You haven't heard of the civil war in Skyrim? I guess down in Cyrodiil people have other things to worry about.
I'd recommend heading to Whiterun, just down the road from here. From there you can take a carriage to Solitude.
It's pretty simple. Ulfric founded the Stormcloaks years ago, as a sort of private army to advance his ambitions.
But never mind all that. We're the Emperor's soldiers. We'll do our duty no matter what stands in our way, right?
So did I! If the damn Stormcloaks somehow found one, or woke it up... the war might be about to take an ugly turn.
But, if you find more trouble than you can handle, run out to the courtyard. We'll come as soon as we hear fighting.
I can't remember the last time I visited home. Think I've forgotten what a proper bed looks like, let alone feels like.
Stick close. Damn rebels could be anywhere. Don't worry, though. General Tullius will have them on the run soon enough.
It's the capital of Whiterun Hold, the biggest and the best of the nine holds of Skyrim. But don't take my word for it.
I'm glad you'll be with me in there. I don't like the looks of this place... and I'm not talking about the Stormcloaks.
I guess I'm doing all right. I need this to go well. This is my first real command since they promoted me after Whiterun.
They claim to be fighting for Skyrim's freedom, but the war is really all about Ulfric wanting to be High King of Skyrim.
If he hadn't signed the peace treaty with the Thalmor, they would have destroyed the Empire - then where would Skyrim be?
I remember you... You were with us at Helgen... I heard you came around and picked the right side. Welcome to the Legion!
He never succeeded in getting much support, so a few months ago he murdered the High King! That got the Empire's attention.
And the Imperial Legion needs your help, especially now, with that dragon out there somewhere, and Ulfric back on the loose.
Changed your mind? Come with me. I'm heading to Riverwood. It's just up the road and my uncle Alvor is the blacksmith there.
I'd rather not tangle with her right now. We might be able to sneak by. Just take it nice and slow, and watch where you step.
There's a sentry patrolling the area. We'll go in quiet and drop him with a combined arrow barrage. Keep low, fire on my word.
There's a sentry patrolling the hill. We'll go in quiet and drop him with a combined arrow barrage. Keep low, fire on my word.
I guess that wasn't such a big deal elsewhere in the Empire, but here it's caused a lot of resentment. Native son and all that.
You're not kidding. Ulfric's the leader of the Stormcloaks - you know, the traitors trying to break Skyrim away from the Empire.
A masterstroke by General Tullius! He's only been in charge here for a few months, but he's turned things around for the Empire.
The Legion's always looking for strong, capable warriors. If you think you've got what it takes, our headquarters is in Solitude.
You go report our success. I'll set up a garrison here. A shame none of the prisoners made it, but their names will be remembered.
But I think you'll see that the Legion is Skyrim's only hope for real peace right now. I know you'll make the right choice in the end.
But until we get that confirmed by General Tullius, just stay clear of other Imperial soldiers and avoid any complications, all right?
We've been trying to catch Ulfric since the war started, but he always seemed to slip through our fingers... like he knew we were coming.
There you go. Take a look around, there should be plenty of gear to choose from. I'm going to see if I can find something for these burns.
Anyway, I'm glad you're here. We're outnumbered again, more so than when we took that wagon. But if we work together, we can pull this off.
Draugr creeping down the mountain to climb through my window at night, that kind of thing. I admit, I still don't much like the look of it.
Hard to believe it was just a coincidence, that the first dragon anyone's seen for centuries attacks just as Ulfric was about to be executed.
I need you to sneak in there, and free our men, killing anyone inside as you go. Once you've rescued the prisoners, head out to the courtyard.
That's the part that Ulfric's supporters always conveniently forget about. Unless the Empire stands together, the Thalmor will destroy us all.
Ever wonder if maybe we should a call a truce with the Stormcloaks, join forces for just a little while, and take care of some of these dragons?
Ever get the impression Rikke's not always sure what side she's on? I'm not doubting her loyalty. Just, she seems rather sympathetic to the rebels.
I know it's what everyone says, "To go to new places and see the world." But it's true in my case... I never bargained on seeing more than I liked.
I signed on 'cause I couldn't watch the Stormcloaks tearing everything apart and not do something. But, most of my family disagrees with my choice.

Galmar.line: Get over to Falkreath.
We should get started.
Now let's keep moving.
Where'd you come from?
Take out their archers!
For the sons of Skyrim!
Go on, get out of here!
Get over to Winterhold.
Are you two quite done?
I... Willl... kill you!
You're as good as dead!
Come on, show yourself!
You can't hide forever!
I'll enjoy killing you!
Must've scared 'em off.
That's the end of that.
They're up on that wall.
Up in that tower! Quick!
Get over to the capital.
Damn Imperial arrogance!
I'm going to find you...
We all have faith in you.
Damn faithless Imperials.
You're going to the fort.
Watch yourself out there.
Don't play dumb, Tullius!
You, go pack your things.
We're ready when you are.
You see a lever anywhere?
You'll... pay for this...
I knew I heard something!
This is hopeless! Retreat!
Good. Don't die out there.
Stormblade. It's an honor.
Damnit woman, stand aside.
The people are behind you.
That's what I like to hear.
You ready to take the Oath?
Got my hands full as usual.
Someone take out that mage!
Get down to the front line.
That's what I like to hear.
I said, on your knees, Elf!
It'll be there. You'll see.
Down with you, damned worm!
You can't get away from me!
Mage up there! In the tower!
We're through! Keep at them!
I knew I could count on you.
Are you looking for a fight?
Go home and lock your doors.
Next Imperial I see is dead.
You're going to Fort Kastav.
The Emperor can kiss my ass!
You're going to Fort Dunstad.
You're going to Fort Neugrad.
You're going to Fort Sungard.
May the gods preserve us all.
Good luck with that Icewraith.
Archers. Behind those windows!
You're going to Fort Snowhawk.
You're going to Fort Greymoor.
Well? What are we waiting for?
Now you're just making me mad.
I must've been hearing things.
That'll teach you to cross me.
We'll see about that, won't we?
We'll see about that, won't we?
All right. Let's take a look...
Look! Up on the wall. A wizard!
You're going to Fort Greenwall.
Turn back! Before he finds you!
What's left of Skyrim to wager?
How long are you going to wait?
Nice touch about the High King.
Are you trying? 'Cause I'm not.
Guess it was nothing after all.
There's plenty that needs doing.
Enough talk. There's much to do.
If it isn't Ice-Veins. Hi there.
Rrrrrraaaaaaaaggggghhhhhhh!!!!!!
The rest of you lot are with me.
But just think of the advantage.
Come on, you're not even trying!
Along the fence, do you see them?
By that rock over there. See 'em?
This doesn't concern you, friend.
What do you say? Can you do this?
The mist is gone! A glorious day!
You have your orders. Get moving.
Good work! Let's see what you got.
Here. Take this for your trouble.
Unlike your Imperial friends here.
Not so sure of yourself after all?
I can always count on you, can't I?
They got archers up along the wall!
That's it! We're pushing them back!
Yeaaaah! Come on, we can take them!
Ralof... get away from there, fool!
The mist is gone, my mind is clear.
Can't wait to kill another Imperial.
I'm not sure you belong here, friend.
They're hiding over by those stables!
Fine. Have it you way. A fight it is.
That Empire is dead. And so are you.
If he's not with us, he's against us.
When have I ever been false with you?
You see what talking to them gets us?
It's a foregone conclusion, you know.
Have you not heard that ancient verse?
If the crown exists, it will be there.
I'll water the ground with your blood.
Excellent. These should come in handy.
Always good to see someone I can trust.
They're over there, next to that house!
There is no escape. Courage is useless.
Alright, get the crown off that draugr!
The kind men use to measure themselves.
Wizard! Hiding over there by that fence!
And sabotage enemy controlled resources.
Do you know the way? I'm weary and lost.
Hrongar won't give us a straight answer.
We are the sons and daughters of Skyrim!
Not while I'm still breathing, it's not.
Time to show what you're really made of.
I'll go gather the men in the courtyard.
Are you ready to for Serpentstone Island?
Someone put an arrow in that damn wizard!
They got archers over there by the fence!
Balgruuf won't give us a straight answer.
Then let them die with their false kings.
Ha! Skyrim will never bow to the Thalmor!
I'll fight and die, if such fate is mine.
Your blood is such a pretty shade of red!
You're a cold hearted bastard, aren't you?
Look sharp. There they are. In the window!
Good job out there. Glad you were with us.
Besides, Jarl Laila will never agree to...
Enough talk. Let's go kill some Imperials.
I eat Legionnaires like you for breakfast!
One way or another, get me those documents.
One way or another, get me those documents.
Dragonborn, even in death you dog my steps?
Shor's bones, where will these demands end?
Have it your way. Let's get that crown men!
Good. I'm spread thin. I could use the help.
Fall back, fall back! Regroup and fall back!
Thanks. Couldn't have done that without you.
I need you to create some chaos in the hold.
They'll have set barricades to slow us down.
Aye! We haven't forgotten Markarth, Tullius!
Don't give them a chance to warn the others!
I've toured our camps. We're ready, Ulfric...
At the end of the day, every warrior is alone.
Keep it together! There's not many of us left!
Think you might be in the wrong place, friend.
Don't make me tell you again. Get out of here.
Ready to spill some Imperial blood for Skyrim?
The World-Eater is dead! The way is now clear!
You saved us all. We will sing of you forever.
Beware! The World-Eater waits within the mist!
Stand aside woman. We've come for the General.
We need to move faster. Keep them off balance.
Give the word, my lord, and Whiterun is yours.
For Skyrim! For Shor! For Sovngarde's freedom!
Aye, I'll hate it, but I'll do it if I have to.
They're in! Don't let them gain further ground!
Someone get that thing out of here and burn it.
What are you people doing outside? Go home. Now!
They'll never suspect an attack from the inside.
Wouldn't want to sully his reputation, would we?
The Imperials are here in force. Stick together.
The Jarls are upset. They don't all support you.
We're not leaving until we get what we came for.
Just kill him and let's be done with it already.
Aye. But in the meantime, we have a war to plan.
It's where men have tested their mettle for ages.
Alright, if it's a fight you want. You've got it.
We're not quite ready to muster the final attack.
Get down there and join Galmar on the front line.
...Jarl of Windhelm and true High King of Skyrim.
This is it men! It's time to make this city ours!
Careful, boys. There's bound to be more up ahead.
How's it going, Unblooded? You find anything yet?
We must move quickly to prevent further violence.
So we're ready to start this war in earnest then?
So long as you can back up those words with steel.
First, we're attacking the fort. I want that fort.
One lives for the things he is willing to die for.
They've got a mage hiding in that window up there!
That's what I wanted to hear. Good luck out there.
I warned you. You leave me no choice but violence.
Battle reveals who a man really is. Remember that.
All hail the Dragonborn! Hail her with great praise!
All hail the Dragonborn! Hail him with great praise!
Hmm, looks like this is as far as the Imperials got.
Pick a man and put him down. We attack on my signal.
Maybe you can find a way through somewhere above us.
Speak with Ulfric, he's expecting word from Whiterun.
Fair enough. But are you willing to die for your home?
Get over to Windhelm. Tell Ulfric of our victory here.
What? Get over with the rest of the men at Korvanjund.
Listen up. Those Imperials aren't here by coincidence.
Good job. I knew you'd come back with something for me.
Here. I want you to have this. Don't ask. Just take it.
By the Nine, I hate the waiting. The goddamned waiting.
When has any good ever come from talking to the Empire?
Come on, boys. Let's spread out and see what we've got.
I misjudged you. You're definitely Stormcloak material.
Our men are getting massacred out there. Damn Imperials.
Ah! The Hall of Stories... we must be getting close now.
But first, tell me. Why's a cat want to fight for Skyrim?
Good. And remember - keep the gods, and they'll keep you.
We'll show those faithless dogs who this land belongs to.
But first, tell me. Why's an Elf want to fight for Skyrim?
But first, tell me. Why's an Orc want to fight for Skyrim?
It's the damn outlanders and Empire that need the reasons.
Gah. Let's do this already. I'm itching to kill something.
We sure showed them, eh? Thanks for your part in all that.
Which means you get to tag along on a little trip with me.
I don't much like this truce. Feels like a betrayal to me.
There is a burning city out there that needs a government.
If by message you mean shoving a sword through his gullet.
I'd follow you into the depths of Oblivion, you know that.
Always was a stubborn woman. She's not going to stand down.
Set an ambush on the road between, or camp one of the inns.
Hero of the Nords I name you, Dragonborn, let none deny it.
But first, tell me. Why's a Breton want to fight for Skyrim?
But first, tell me. Why's a lizard want to fight for Skyrim?
It's time we made this official. You ready to take the Oath?
They want this as much as you do. Perhaps they want it more.
I don't suppose we would ever really trust the thing anyway.
I got something for you. But you'll have to come and get it.
But nothing a band of Stormcloaks can't handle, I assure you.
You slaughtered them - the women, the children! You butchers!
But first, tell me. Why's a Redguard want to fight for Skyrim?
Everyone on me. Let's show these Imperials what we're made of!
If you ladies are finished bickering, there's much to be done.
Keep your wits about you and watch your shield brother's back.
But first, tell me. Why's a foreigner want to fight for Skyrim?
This is it! Hold at all costs! Don't give them any more ground!
Excellent. Put the fear of the Nine into those motherless dogs.
Once you find the courier's route, kill him and take his place.
There may be some way to get through on the level above. Hurry!
All hail the Stormcloaks, the true sons and daughters of Skyrim!
That we have forgotten them, this I fear the gods will remember.
Hmm... They know more of our plans than I thought... Not good...
See if you can't "convince" one of those innkeepers to help you.
See if you can't "convince" one of those innkeepers to help you.
...even to my lord as to my fellow brothers and sisters in arms.
All the more reason then. The crown would legitimize your claim.
And you, go sit on that damn chair and start acting like a Jarl.
Secondly, we plan on liberating a settlement from Imperial hands.
Get the crown. We will keep an eye out for more of those dustmen.
Don't you worry about her. I've sent my best men to round her up.
Don't you worry about him. I've sent my best men to round him up.
My hatred for the Empire is bested only by love for my countrymen.
What will Skyrim have become if her children submitted to tyranny?
Good. You've got real fire in you. I like that... Talos guide you.
Oh, and here. You're a Stormcloak now, you ought to look the part.
That's done it! Alright, boys, let's get moving. We've got more...
Good job! Ulfric was right about you. I'm glad to have you with us!
Ready now! Everyone, with me! For the sons and daughters of Skyrim!
The Jagged Crown! It heralds back to a time before jarls and moots.
Do you think we could persuade one of the dragons to join our side?
Looks like the door will open if we turn these rings the right way.
No. But neither is every man able to give that order when he must.
I do swear my blood and honor to the service of Ulfric Stormcloak...
I do swear my blood and honor to the service of Ulfric Stormcloak...
Not this Empire. The world's better without it. Certainly Skyrim is.
As Talos is my witness, may this oath bind me to death and beyond...
Is there anything on the claw that might show how to open this door?
Skyrim needs that king. You will be that king, Ulfric. You must be.
I'm in the middle of something. But don't go far. I'll need you soon.
The imperial butchers are on their way. Get home and lock your doors!
They'll continue to strike out at us, whenever and wherever they can.
For what it's worth, I'm sorry to see you go. You fought well for us.
I wouldn't do that again unless you know for sure it's going to open.
Found this among the dead. Something tells me I should give it to you.
I would hate to think what would happen if this fell into enemy hands.
I still say you should take them all out like you did Deadking Torygg.
I'll fight the entire Legion myself if they dare show their faces here.
I'll die before elves dictate the fates of men. Are we not one in this?
The faster we take them out, the faster we can claim this city as ours!
Now, if were done being sentimental, it's time for you to take the Oath.
Sometimes I wonder if Ulfric understands what I'm dealing with out here.
I long to be out there, with my brothers, waging war against the Empire.
Need you to deliver some false orders to the Imperial Legate in Morthal.
Why don't you grab yourself a drink at the Windpeak before heading back?
We've got them pinned in! It's only a matter of time now. Keep fighting!
Need you to deliver some false orders to the Imperial Legate in Morthal.
No higher fate than fall in battle at Sovngarde's gates, a glorious end!
You mistake me. I'm not saying no - just wondering about your intentions.
We'll need trained, disciplined and creative warriors ready for all that.
They'll have barricades set, destroy them quickly. Keep pressing forward.
Those milk drinkers? Might as well be elves. Think they're better than us.
Get yourself kitted out with what you need. Welcome to the fight, brother!
That's the way I like it. Short and bloody. They never knew what hit them.
We now fight our way to Castle Dour to cut the head off the legion itself!
You are weak. Sooner you realize it, the sooner we can see the back of you.
Aye, and the sons of Skyrim will greet that dawn teeth and swords flashing.
Alright everyone! Keep your guard up. No telling what we'll find down here.
Destroy them quickly, and take out the archers in the towers along the way.
Keep looking, Unblooded. There's got to be some way to get that thing open.
Let's hope that's the last of them. Come on, let's see what's down this way.
Better to die with blade in hand than feed his hunger, hopeless in the mist.
Before I fall, you'll feel the bite of my sharp blade, as Shor's my witness.
Not at all. That was as much a test of your abilities as it was your resolve.
The crown must be around here somewhere. Spread out, and keep your eyes open.
First of all, there's someone in particular I'd like to win over to our cause.
The inns at Dragonbridge and Solitude are frequent stops for Imperial runners.
You kill an Ice Wraith out there, and I'll have all the proof I need about you.
When you kill that Ice Wraith, then we'll talk about you becoming a Stormcloak.
When you kill that Ice Wraith, then we'll talk about you becoming a Stormcloak.
We're taking Falkreath. Get over there with the others. Fight well or die well.
What we do here today, we do for our country! For all the true Nords of Skyrim!
How many of their sons and daughters follow your banner? We are their families.
The inns at Dragonbridge and Rorikstead are frequent stops for Imperial runners.
What's taking them so long? Did they get lost? Let's get this over with already.
Never said that. Just that we don't get many of you wanting to join, that's all.
Steady now! They may be uglier than Imperials but they'll go down just the same.
We'll stick around here for a while and see if we can find anything else useful.
What? We've got nothing to talk about until you come back from Serpentstone Isle.
Next, I need dirt on the Jarl's Steward. Heard there's blackmail potential there.
It was anger that brought each of us here, but it is love that keeps us fighting.
But first we need to get our hands on some Imperial orders to make the forgeries.
Changed your mind? You ready to pledge your blood and honor for Skyrim's freedom?
The look on Tullius's face while the Greybeard read the terms was worth the trip.
The Empire is weak. That is why we fight them. Skyrim must be ruled by the strong.
The Imperials aren't going to leave us alone. They still have camps in the hills.
Everyone on me. Let's show these Imperial milk drinkers what true Nords look like!
Not so sure? Oh, then perhaps we should take a moment to look around a little, eh?
One thing at a time. We're here for the Crown. Any of these carvings show a crown?
I wasn't sure your heart was in it. But you've proven me wrong. And I respect that.
Get to Windhelm with the crown as quick as you can. Tell Ulfric he owes me a drink.
Unblooded, do what you do best. See if you can find some way to get that gate open.
If you don't mind king, we'll just be taking that crown of yours and be on our way.
Hmm... that looks like some kind of claw from a statue. I wonder what it's used for.
But you are that man, Ulfric.  You've been that man before, and you'll be him again.
It exists. And it'll be the symbol of the righteousness of our cause. Think about it.
This won't be easy. But that's why I'm sending you. You got the grit to get this done?
Beyond that are two exterior gates. Find a way to open them and we can take this city!
Give me a moment while I make a few, um, "corrections" to these reports... There we go.
That crown should be around here somewhere. Just keep an eye out for the bonewalkers...
They're the meanest, toughest sons of bitches Skyrim has to offer. And they want this.
You're always thirsty. Just like you're always drunk. Now let me get back to my rounds.
Not now. If I don't pull night duty, I'll drink a round with you at the Sleeping Giant.
We're liberating Morthal. Join the brothers gathering for the attack. Talos be with you.
And take out resources like sawmills, flour-mills, and smelters, with a little sabotage.
Don't wait around for me. I bet I'll beat you to Korvanjund even though you start first.
If you two are finished with the touching homecoming, there's a city in chaos out there.
By Talos, the stones on this one! You're in no position to dictate terms to us, Tullius!
Not a peep. Those prissy Bretons can't be made to lift a finger to help their neighbors.
Our objective is the drawbridge. If we can find a way to drop it, the city will be ours!
Even the Imperials might eventually decide to patrol their perimeter. Get yourself ready!
Steady. A few dusty bonewalkers aren't going to stop us anymore than the Imperials could.
Your words give voice to what we all feel, Ulfric. And that's why you will be High King.
We had the advantage of surprise this time, but things won't be so easy from here on out.
And these men and women - they call themselves Stormcloaks because they believe in you...
But the day words are enough, will be the day when soldiers like us are no longer needed.
My hatred for the Empire is exceeded only by my hatred for their witch-elf puppet-masters.
They've barricades to block us, but we'll tear through them and the Imperials behind them!
Before you can stand next to your shield brother, you need to be able to stand on your own.
Don't take too long figuring it out. Might mistake your hesitation for Imperial sympathies.
Reasons? Since when does a man need a reason to protect his family, to defend his homeland?
No. I will have no part of any such Empire. And I will gladly gut anyone who says otherwise.
Find enemy controlled resources like sawmills, flour-mills, and smelters, and sabotage them.
I oppose tyranny. I oppose those who tell me how to live, what to think, and what to believe.
If you run across any enemy couriers, take them down, and bring me whatever they're carrying.
You're alive. I owe Ulfric a drink. I have to admit, I didn't think we'd be seeing you again.
Can't wait to see the look on their faces when they find us here waiting to spill their blood.
Search out enemy soldiers in the wilderness, or guarding towns and settlements, and kill them.
But do not make the mistake of underestimating the Legion. Plenty of them are Nords same as us.
You must also pledge unswerving loyalty to your fellow Stormcloaks, to Skyrim and to her people.
They say that our cause is false and that we are nothing more than thieves, thugs and murderers!
First of all, a son of Skyrim rots in an Imperial prison. We'll be getting him out of there soon.
Secondly, we got a couple of Imperial spies roaming through the hold. Need to put an end to that.
Good work. Knowing more about the enemy's position makes it that much easier to take it from him.
Can't fault anyone for that! As long as you hate the Empire as much as I do, that I can work with.
It's not easy running messages, what with Imperials scouts crawling all over the place. Well done.
My scouts tell me the inns at Dragonbridge and Rorikstead are frequent stops for Imperial runners.
Jarl Black-Briar put your government together. Do it now, or there will be rioting in the streets.
I see. Well, make it quick, or do you have better things to do than fight for the people of Skyrim?
And of course, they'll be caught off guard thinking we're off doing something altogether different.
Whiterun's walls are tall, but they are old and crumbling, like the Empire whose Legion lines them.
You can stick a sword through an Imperial any day you want. But that doesn't make you a Stormcloak.
True or not, who would dare deny Ulfric's claim, when the legendary Jagged Crown sits upon his brow?
We grow that much stronger with each son and daughter of Skyrim who joins us. Down with the Emperor!
And now, I present to you, Ulfric Stormcloak, hero of the people, liberator and High King of Skyrim!
All that remains then is the exterior gate. Find a way through to open it and the city will be ours!
Before you're one of us, you must swear fealty to Jarl Ulfric Stormcloak, future High King of Skyrim.
That godless Emperor can shove his damn treaty into the steaming nether-reaches of my you-know-where.
Can't say I'm looking forward to that. But, I'll also be training men and women for their new armies.
I've found the final resting place of the Jagged Crown. Can you believe it? Well, I'm almost certain.
Follow me. Quickly and quietly now. I want their guts on the ground before they even know we're here.
Countless dead haunt countless sleepless nights... when Skyrim becomes whole again, perhaps so will I.
They have some of our boys locked up there. But with a little luck, we can turn that to our advantage.
I don't know how you convinced Ulfric to talk to the Empire. Worse than a waste of time if you ask me.
First off, it seems that we might be able to blackmail the Jarl's Steward. I need to find out for sure.
I need to send someone out to do a little recruiting. There's someone in particular I'd like to join us.
The approach to Riften is guarded by watchtowers. They will be full of archers. Move fast, and stay low.
Here. Take this. You'll need it. Use it on the island. Don't lose it. That's all the help you're getting.
Here. Take this. You'll need it. Use it on the island. Don't lose it. That's all the help you're getting.
Excellent. That's one less settlement in Imperial hands, and one more sign to all that our cause is true.
Good job! With those Imperial scouts out of the way, I can be more aggressive about where I send our men.
Legion soldiers gleam like fresh-fallen snow and clank like a kitchen. They head this way, we'll know it.
Bring me whatever he's carrying, and I'll send you to Morthal with something convincing but full of lies.
Damned Imperial lies! My men would never stoop to such methods, even in retaliation for your butchery at...
The walls of Markarth may be high, but their age will have created weakness or secret entrances. Find them!
Curses notwithstanding, the tombs of kings are always full of traps to ward off grave robbers, are they not?
We found this in the Jarl's treasury after we captured the city. I thought you should be the one to have it.
In life we were enemies, but eternity's too long to hold a grudge - my heart's now free of such old hatreds.
Ulfric Stormcloak is counting on us to bring him back that crown, and that's exactly what we're going to do.
You were there with us. You saw it. The day the Empire signed that damn treaty was the day the Empire died.
Gretta, Engar, stay and guard the entrance. We don't want any Imperial reinforcements taking us by surprise.
There's a strange rock formation, built by the ancients. Something about that place attracts the Ice Wraiths.
We're taking the city. Meet the brothers gathering for the attack. Fight well or die well. Talos be with you.
We're taking Whiterun. Meet the brothers gathering for the attack. Fight well or die well. Talos be with you.
Oh, what's this? Fort Snowhawk is in need of reinforcements... We'll make sure they won't be getting those...
Of course not! What will you offer for Riften, Tullius? Some empty promises? Maybe some more Imperial bluster?
All right. But before I can put you to use, I need to know how much you can take. I have a little test for you.
We found this in the Jarl's treasury after we captured the capital. I thought you should be the one to have it.
We're taking the capital. Meet the brothers gathering for the attack. Fight well or die well. Talos be with you.
Ulfric can tell you more than most anyone. He was studying to be a Greybeard himself, back before the Great War.
But no! We are farmers! We are craftsmen! We are sons and daughters of shopkeepers, maid servants and soldiers!
Aye. The Great Hunt killed old King Borgas. Gods only know with what profane arts those elves cursed his remains.
Imperials? Well, if they're keeping themselves comfortable, let's slip in and send them to their graves that way.
We also have the opportunity to slay an Imperial Legate. That'll put fear and confusion into those faithless dogs.
Some say Ulfric desires to be king and nothing more... have they not seen that his anger floats on a sea of tears?
Ulfric, you can't be taking this demand seriously? We can hold Riften against anything the Empire can throw at it.
You sure about that? Then please, be my guest and go strolling on in there. We'll stay here and watch your back...
Come back when you're committed to the cause, or go join the Legion. They're taking anyone with a pulse these days.
I fought in the Imperial army in the War against the Dominion. I bled and spilt blood for the Empire. And for what?
I need you to create some chaos in the hold so I can maneuver men into position without drawing too much attention.
You there, Unblooded, see if you can find another way through. We'll charge in to help as soon as we hear fighting.
We're not just fighting Imperials. We're fighting to restore Skyrim to her glory and give her the king she deserves.
I don't like the look of this. Perfect spot for an ambush. Ten to one they're just waiting for us on the other side.
I'm a man. Skyrim is man's homeland. That's a fact. A fact I'm proud of. There's no shame in that. Read your history.
Your fellow Stormcloaks are already assembling outside the tomb. I'll meet you there as soon as I'm finished up here.
Hmm. Helgen, eh? Ulfric told us quite the story. If you made it through all that, you're likely worth something to me.
Only the ones I'm not sure about. This will prove your abilities, but more importantly, it will prove your commitment.
If you know any true sons and daughters of Skyrim, tell them to head to Windhelm. Ulfric Stormcloak wants to see them.
And I might be old myself, but I'll kick those damn walls down with my bare feet! - if you would only ask me to do it!
By the gods! If it's a good ending to some damn story you're after - perhaps the Dragonborn should be the one to do it.
But, if confronted with proof of his belief, you might be able to "persuade" him to aid our cause, indirectly of course.
You're alive! It was a gamble - but as Talos keeps us, we keep each other - I wasn't going to just let them rot in there.
We're headed to Korvanjund. But, if old King Borgas is indeed buried there, we're bound to run into trouble of some kind.
Back to the time when a king was a king because his enemies fell before him, and his people rose because they loved him.
Heh. Ulfric's put me in charge of keeping an eye on the new Jarls. You know, make sure they're generally following orders.
You'll need to be careful with this one. The Jarl's men won't look kindly on you rummaging through the steward's quarters.
Their spies must've found out we know about the Crown, and they don't want us to have it. But they won't stand in our way.
First off, there's a band of Imperials hiding in a nearby village. We're going to flush them out and put them to the sword.
The measure of a man is taken at the moment of his death. And know this for truth, the ghosts of many heroes walk among us.
I've come to rely on you. There's something I want you to have. It was my father's. It would be my honor if you carried it.
See what you can figure out, Unblooded. I'm going to check out these carvings over here. Let me know if you find something.
Even if one of these pictures tells us where the Crown is, I'm betting we're going to have to find a way through that door.
And perhaps more importantly, we have a brother rotting in an Imperial prison. He won't languish in there long, will he now?
And we hear a handful of Imperials are realizing they are Stormcloaks at heart. We need to send them a little encouragement.
To begin with, killing any Legionnaire you find out in the wilderness. I've reports they've hidden military camps out there.
Make sure those forged documents get to the Legate in Morthal. They should give them the wrong idea... and us the advantage.
First off, there's a couple of Imperials skulking about the hold. Need to cut their throats so they can't make their reports.
And then there's a tiny problem with a giant attacking our supply caravans. A giant! As if I didn't have enough to deal with.
And we have come this far because our cause is true. Because we fight as one. And because our hearts are bursting with anger!
And then, it seems an Imperial worm wiggles it's way back home, carrying information stolen from us. We need to steal it back.
We're ready to launch our attack on Solitude. But the Emperor's visiting! We can't risk attacking now. So we'll bide our time.
How come you here? The king of this realm will cast you out - cursed be your name by all sons of Skyrim, with scorn unceasing.
Don't be so sure of that. We've intercepted couriers from Solitude. The Empire's putting a great deal of pressure on Whiterun.
It just so happens, I've got some scouts along the road. Meet up with them, and together see if you can't overpower the caravan.
I'm sending you to Serpentstone Island. If you survive, you pass. If you die, well, you weren't going to be much use to me anyway.
I've sent some men to scout out the area. Meet them, see if you can't find a way to slip in, free our men, and take over the fort.
You gave those mongrels a real whipping, eh? Good work. We'll be sure to send a garrison to reinforce the area and keep the peace.
What's the Legion doing here? Damn Imperial spies... Well, should be fun for us, at least. They don't seem to know we're here yet.
I pray Talos overlooks our less than honorable approach. But he knows our purpose is just and true. I'm glad you made it out alive.
If there was someone else Ulfric could trust with all this planning nonsense, I'd be out there breaking heads with the rest of you.
Meet the brothers preparing for the attack. Then join them in wiping out the Imperials. Once you prevail we will garrison the fort.
When we've reclaimed our homeland from the Imperials, we'll take the fight to the Thalmor. They are the true enemy. Make no mistake.
And in that moment, the gods will look down and see Skyrim as she was meant to be. Full of Nords who are mighty, powerful, and free!
We're not looking for sellswords. The Stormcloaks need dedicated men and women who're devoted to the cause and willing to die for it.
First, if we can assassinate their commander, we'll be able to strike them while they're busy tucking their tails between their legs.
The Emperor would turn Skyrim into a puppet of the Dominion. I for one would not care to dance for the amusement of those foul elves.
To begin with, killing any Legionnaire you find out in the wilderness. I've reports they've hidden military camps out there out there.
Maw unleashing razor  snow, Of dragons from the blue brought down, Births the walking winter's woe, The High King in his Jagged Crown.
Through sources that shall remain nameless, I've tracked down what I believe to be the final resting place of King Borgas - Korvanjund.
Well done! I'll send men with a wagon to collect our prize. We'll put the weapons to use here, and I'll send the coin back to Windhelm.
The Empire would have us become slaves to the Thalmor. We are not slaves. We are men. Free men who will slay anyone who begs to differ.
We're mounting our attack on Markarth. Get over there and join the brothers gathering for the attack. This is it now! Talos be with you.
First we'll kick the Thalmor and their bloody Imperial puppets out of the country. Then we'll rebuild Skyrim into the land she once was.
That's the spirit. By swearing this oath you become one of us. A hero of the people. A true son of Skyrim. A Stormcloak. Repeat after me.
Go quiet, break him out, then run. This isn't a suicide mission. Don't risk combat if you can avoid it. If you can't, then give 'em hell.
First, I need to know what the Imperials are planning. Need to slip into their headquarters, grab plans, maps, something useful like that.
First off, there's a damn Orc hanging around getting in the way. He's been yammering on about needing help with some kind of vision quest.
Before we wage war upon our foes, we must wage a war against our lesser selves. Cleave to your higher voice and with it shatter your foes.
The Empire would have us become their slaves as they have become slaves to the Thalmor. I will not live as a slave, nor will I die as one.
The Empire to bend its knee before those evil elf bastards? Signing a treaty meant to kill the heart of the Empire itself. To deny Talos?
Going way back to King Harald's time or even before, the High King always wore the Jagged Crown. It was the symbol of his might and power.
The Empire might send over a few Legionnaires to make sure we're serious, and of course, we'll be taking the fight to the elves soon enough.
Also, I need a peek inside Imperial headquarters, ideally lifting some documents detailing their plans. I need to know what to concentrate on.
We're ready to launch our attack on Solitude. But the Emperor's cousin is getting married! We can't risk attacking now. So we'll bide our time.
Rumor has it the Jarl's Steward, Raerek, is a faithful Talos worshipper, if not a true son of Skyrim - he still supports the Empire, after all.
When we are done with that, we will take our army to the Dominion, and show those pointy-eared bastards not every man is fit to be their slave.
The crown is made from the bones and teeth of ancient dragons, and it is said to contain a portion of the power of  every king who has worn it.
First off, we hear a group of Imperial legionnaires are coming around to our way of thinking. With a little encouragement they'll join our side.
First, we've learnt the whereabouts of an Imperial cur fetching documents for his master in Solitude. We need to put a muzzle on that, don't we?
And as if that wasn't enough. I got this damn Orc hanging around, getting in the way, yapping about needing help with some kind of vision quest.
Next problem - Imperial thieves have, yet again, stolen an important resource.  So we're gonna take it from them permanently - by destroying it.
That's the spirit. By swearing this oath you become one of us. A heroine of the people. A true daughter of Skyrim. A Stormcloak. Repeat after me.
No. I trust you'll do more good out there in the world, wherever you damn well please to go. You're a true son of Skyrim. You'll know what to do.
Ah, good. Looks like the reinforcements for the fort are on the way. And we have some information about enemy troop movements as well. Excellent.
It's true, the location of the crown was lost with King Borgas. After the Great Hunt killed him, while he was off on his damned Alessian campaigns.
Secondly, I want to take the fort here. Having a fortified position will make it easier for us to amass men for our ultimate attack on the capital.
Thanks for dealing with that orc. Something tells me that's not the last time we'll see him. And, something tells me that might not be a bad thing.
We're mounting our final attack on Solitude. Get over there and join the rest of the army. They'll be launching the attack soon. Talos be with you!
First problem here is a pack of Imperial dogs drooling over resources stolen from the people. A little sabotage will sour the taste of that victory.
The last few supply caravans have been attacked... by a giant. As if I didn't have enough to deal with. Need that damn hairy brute straightened out.
The Imperials are here. All lined up pretty-like in their shiny armor. We'll put a few dents in those bastards before we give up the city. Move it!
Look deeply into the faces of the warriors around you. These are the true sons and daughters of Skyrim. See her strength and nobility in their eyes.
We hit 'em where it hurts. As soon as we can, we'll take over - make the needed repairs. In the meantime, the people no longer slave for the Empire.
But legend holds, his body was secretly returned to Skyrim and buried with the crown. Its exact whereabouts lost in the following Wars of Succession.
No. I trust you'll do more good out there in the world, wherever you damn well please to go. You're a true daughter of Skyrim and'll know what to do.
I know some of you are ex-legion and may know men on the other side. But remember this. They are the enemy now and they will not hesitate to kill you.

Boethiah.line: An astute observation.
Prove yourselves to me!
We shall soon find out.
Failure is not an option.
Do this, and I shall favor you.
My previous champion displeases me.
I should slay you for such insolence!
Which brings me to my present concern.
I have little need for mindless thralls.
But which among you truly exceeds the rest?
Go to Solitude. Stalk the Jarl and slay her.
Ahhhhgh. Wearing flesh is so... distasteful.
Remember always this: As you will it, so it shall be.
My good pleasure is not some trophy for mortal sport!
You have earned the right to call yourself my Champion.
It is time he is replaced - in the traditional fashion.
You have caught my attention, mortal. That is most unwise.
I have a special task for whichever of you is left standing.
I shall write your name upon the Tablet of Absolute Darkness.
Tell me, why have you slain those who profess to follow in my ways?
So, perhaps you are not entirely useless... We shall soon find out.
Were you certain in your purpose, you would have no need to apologize.
Tell me, why have you slain this one, who trusted you, here upon my shrine?
I have need of a warrior. Only the most ruthless, cunning, and agile will do.
Well, well. You've slain my followers. I should destroy you for your impudence!
Slay him in the coldest of blood. Do not give him the dignity of defending himself.
A god? Assuredly not! When was the last time you saw evidence of Aedra in the world?
But my purpose was ultimately gained. My previous champion was erased, and a new one born.
On the other hand, you've weeded out the weak from among those who would deign worship me.
I am loath to utter his name... one cannot erase a thing if it has a name to be remembered.
No, we Daedra are far more powerful than any god. And among my brethren, I am the most feared.
You will find him holed up at Knifepoint Ridge, where he plays king to bandits and highwaymen.
No, we Daedra are far more powerful than any god. And among my brethren, I am the most feared.
Skyrim is a beautiful and harsh mistress, but her people cling to such a petty notion of honor.
If you can learn to be subtle in rendering death, perhaps I will share more of my power with you.
You have much to learn... But you have proven your will is strong and your heart is devoid of pity.
I desired stealth and secrecy, but you executed the task with as much subtlety as a enrage mammoth.
You have done well, my Champion. You have earned my respect, a feat few manage and live to tell about.
You have done well, my Champion. You have earned my respect, a feat few manage and live to tell about it.
And now you must perform the task for which I created you. You have already proven to possess the needed skills.
Is that so? You should be. Though a heart without fear is something I can use... Perhaps you are the one I seek.
Now the time has come for a final proving. Are you able to cast aside your honor and strike with the hidden blade?
You have gathered here night after night, sacrificing fools upon my shrine, and sparring aimlessly with yourselves.
Now go. I have strings to pull that require my full attention. You may pursue your own course wherever it leads you.
Remember always this: You need answer to no authority, neither mortal, nor divine... As you will it, so it shall be.
Now go. I have strings to pull that require my full attention. You may pursue your own course wherever it leads you.
I abide only those whose will is aligned to my own. Those who oppose my desires perish in the most dreadful of ways.
You may keep my Ebony Mail, a token of my appreciation to my new champion. Its gifts will resonate with your talents.
Succeed, and you may keep your title and my armor, and I shall write your name upon the Tablet of Absolute Blackness.
I have spread rumors of an assassin. It will make your job harder, but will allow me to place blame where I desire it.
I am called Boethiah. Many names have mortals given me. But I am not interested in names. I am interested only in deeds.
A god? Assuredly not! Gods are weak and ineffectual. When was the last time you heard or saw evidence of the Aedra in the world?
Indeed. But I abide only those whose will is aligned to my own. Those who oppose my desires perish in the most dreadful of ways.
My servants serve their own purposes. But, if their will is insufficient, nor suitably aligned with mine, I simply destroy them.
Well done. You have proven the strength of your will and your tongue's gift for lies. You have shown ferocity and prowess in combat.
I have a purpose for which you might prove useful. But first I must test the deftness of your guile, and the strength of your purpose.
Once his corpse lays cold upon the ground, and all trace of his followers erased, retrieve my Ebony Mail. A gift fit only for my true champion.
For a time he served his purpose, but that time has ended. He uses my gifts for his own amusement, and the only amusement I tolerate is my own.
You will need stealth and lies to get past her guards. You will assassinate her swiftly and quietly. And if combat finds you, survive the escape.
Kill every single member of his band. Do so as silently and invisibly as you can, for this is not about you. You are to be my instrument in this.
You could be the one I seek... if your will is strong, lure a thrall to my shrine. Convince him to embrace the Pillar of Sacrifice. Then slay him.
Do this and I shall remember you and bestow an important task upon you. Do this not, and I shall forget you, and your soul will waste in obscurity.
Make a thrall. Lure him to my shrine. Prove your will is stronger. Convince him to embrace the Pillar of Sacrifice, and then slay him without mercy.

Septimus.line: Elder Scrolls. Indeed.
Into the dark with you!
Extraordinary. I see it now.
Open to the will of Septimus!
Your depths will open to the mind!
But no, it is not in my possession.
I know of one. Forgotten. Sequestered.
Trust Septimus. He knows you can know.
In the depths of Blackreach one yet lies.
In time, he brought me here. To the box.
It lick the panes and smokes the glass...
On the cosmological scale, it's all nearby.
This Dwemer lockbox. Look upon it and wonder.
But he won't reveal how to open it. Maddening.
The box contains the heart. The essence of a god.
Delve to its limits, and Blackreach lies just beyond.
The scroll will give the deep vision needed to open it.
Ooooh, an observant one. How clever to ask of Septimus.
Oh, but the Dwemer had more than even Septimus expected.
The sealing structure interlocks in the tiniest fractals.
Under deep. Below the dark. The hidden keep. Tower Mzark.
Two things I have for you. Two shapes. One edged, one round.
The ice entombs the heart. The bane of Kagrenac and Dagoth Ur.
Alftand. The point of puncture, of first entry, of the tapping.
Inside is the heart. The heart of a god! The heart of you. And me.
Dwemer blood can loose the hooks, but none alive remain to bear it.
Lucky then they left behind their own way of reading the Elder Scrolls.
I've seen enough to know their fabric. The warp of air, the weft of time.
Well, here as in this plane. Mundus. Tamriel. Nearby, relatively speaking.
You see this masterwork of the Dwemer. Deep inside their greatest knowings.
The crucial nature is pure apparent. Mundane sight traded for another sort.
Oh, I am well. I will be well. Well to be within the will inside the walls.
But it was hidden away. Not by the Dwarves, you see. They were already gone.
To harness it is to know. The fundaments. The Dwemer lockbox hides it from me.
Dig, Dwemer, in the beyond. I'll know your lost unknown and rise to your depths.
But I cannot go to it, not poor Septimus, for I... I have arisen beyond its grasp.
I can almost... hear them. I feel their life energy. Come, I will make the mixture.
When the top level was built, no more could be placed. It was and is the maximal apex.
The Elder Scroll gives insight deeper than the deep ones, though. To bring about the opening.
How long will it be sung? My feet were set upon the rock but it turned to mud and drew me down.
The blood of Altmer, Bosmer, Dunmer, Falmer, and Orsimer. The elves still living provide the key.
Septimus is clever among men, but he is but an idiot child compared to the dullest of the Dwemer.
Sometimes they even look up. What do they see then? What if they dive in? Then the madness begins.
The Dwemer found a loophole, as they always do. To focus the knowledge away and inside without harm.
To glimpse the world inside an Elder Scroll can damage the eyes. Or the mind, as it has to Septimus.
The round one, for tuning. Dwemer music is soft and subtle, and needed to open their cleverest gates.
Bear you hence this extractor. It will drink the fresh blood of elves. Come when its set is complete.
This will take time to decryptify. Be on your way, and Septimus will find  you if he has further need.
