Find Mzark and its sky-dome. The machinations there will read the Scroll and lay the lore upon the cube.
One block lifts the other. Septimus will give what you want, but you must bring him something in return.
But the Elder Scrolls... they look left and right in the stream of time. The future and past are as one.
The Empire. They absconded with them. Or so they think. The ones they saw. The ones they thought they saw.
For not even the strongest machinations of the Dwemer can hold off the all-sight given by an Elder Scroll.
What is this... it's... it's just a book?! I can see. The world beyond burns in my mind. It's marvelous....
Have you heard of Blackreach? "Cast upon where Dwemer cities slept, the yearning spire hidden learnings kept."
But not all can enter there. Only Septimus knows the hidden key to loose the lock to jump beneath the deathly rock.
The edged lexicon, for inscribing. To us, a hunk of metal. To the Dwemer, a full library of knowings. But... empty.
Oh, a brutish one. Septimus has no fear of you. But as one block raises another, perhaps ourselves could help us each.
He asks a price -- to work his will. A few murders, some dissent spread, a plague or two. For the secrets I can endure.
Someone else. Unseen. Unknown. Found the heart, and with a flair for the ironical, used Dwarven trickery to lock it away.
A panoply of their brethren could gather to form a facsimile. A trick. Something they didn't anticipate, no, not even them.
The Daedric prince of the unknown. Hermaeus Mora. I thought there were no secrets left to know. Until I first spoke to him.
The Dwemer were the last to touch it. It was thought to have been destroyed by the Nerevarine, but my lord told me otherwise.
You look to your left, you see one way. You look to your right, you see another. But neither is any harder than the opposite.
I have devoted my life to the Elder Scrolls, but their knowledge is a passing awareness when compared to the encompassing mind of divinity.
The deepest doors of Dwemer listen for singing. It plays the attitude of notes proper for opening. Can you not hear it? Too low for hearings?
Place the lexicon into their contraption and focus the knowings into it. When it brims with glow, bring it back and Septimus can read once more.

Mephala.line: Excellent work, child.
Let him guide your path.
First, you must open this door.
I am Mephala, the Lady of Whispers.
I am Mephala, the Lady of Whispers.
And those winds issue from my whispers.
The child is spirited, but lacks... agency.
Ah, the blood of deceit is a nourishing flow.
At last, my blade is returned to its full glory.
Their tears shine the ebony to a sharpest gleam.
I can feel their heartbreak swelling in my blade.
Regrettably, I cannot reach your plane so directly.
You, though, I expect will prove far more malleable.
Eventually your curiosity will drive you back to me.
I've been waiting for someone more fit to carry out my will.
I forgive your not knowing my name. Few can hear my whispers anymore.
To return to its past glory, it must first drink the blood of deceit.
It has languished too long outside the winds of alliance and betrayal.
But I forgive you for not knowing who I am. Few hear my whispers anymore.
Suit yourself. But those who listen at doors always wonder what is beyond them.
I tug at the web of connections between mortals. Love, hatred, loyalty, betrayal.
I tug at the web of connections between mortals. Love, hatred, loyalty, betrayal.
The boy was good at sussing out secrets. You, I expect to take a more active role.
To return to its past glory, it must reacquaint itself with the hatred of this world.
The Jarl trusts few, and they will be his undoing. The dark child knows of what I speak.
I'd much rather it be in the hands of an ambitious and talented person such as yourself.
Now go forth, child. Continue your tiny subversions against the orders of trust and intimacy.
A piece of my power has been locked away behind it, and even my eyes cannot see past the seals.
Now, I trust you're sharp enough to see that the sword doesn't match the description of the Ebony Blade you may know.
The boy is good at sussing out secrets, but the corruptibility of children is ultimately too limited for my purposes.
You may think so. But in the end, you mortals will always flit like fire with the winds of your desires and conveniences.
The whole of Whiterun is ripe with paranoia and tensions. The Jarl's court is right to fear the power I hold behind this door.
Seek out those closest to you. The final pluck of their misguided heartstrings will accompany my blade in the song of your grandeur.
Your world is admirably seeped in lies and inclinations. My blade is a darling leech that feeds on deceptions, and nourishes its master.
The blade will feed on the ire of Tamriel. Bathe in your infamy. Feel the weight of their loathing, and my power will course through it once more.

Hagraven.line: You, meat! Come, come.
We go up, yes? Always up.
You belong to Hircine now!
Such a kind nibble you are.
I spit on you, dead sister.
Do you like my pretty tower?
Petra! Petra! Curse her eyes!
Your blood is ours, Companion!
Don't leave poor Melka to die.
Oh. Yes. You all look the same.
Pretty, pretty flesh. Let me out!
You won't take my eye, man-thing!
Petra, you traitorous grouse! Die!
Because you are such a very nice Orc?
...rise from death, Blood of our Blood.
...rise from death, Blood of our Blood.
Who enters? Will nobody save poor Melka?
Because, because you are such a nice Breton, yes!
Ah, my parlor. No finer place to boil eyes, no...
Reason, reason, reason. Your kind and their reasons.
You can't have the ring back! You made a commitment!
Yes, kind morsel. Let us go up, and mind Melka on the way.
What? That flower-chewer's died? Then let me out. My tower!
Yes, yes. My staff, take it. Kill something pretty with it.
Heart of thorn... bones of the wild... in life, Forsworn...
Darling! I've been waiting for you to return, to consummate our love!
Press only middle button! Clever trick, yes? Nobody ever thinks of the middle.
Live in my tower, roost in my home. Mix pretty poisons and collect shiny eyeballs, yes.
Careful here, there's a trick to this. Trick is to not bleed to death. Ah, and there's a lever, too.
Petra! Evil Petra put me here, stole my tower. Hate her, chew her bones! Let me out, kind, kind meat.
What? You want it for that hussy Esmerelda, with the dark feathers - don't you? I won't let her have you!
We're not done talking. You fell for that hussy Esmerelda, with the dark feathers - didn't you? I won't let her have you!
Ah, I have a pretty staff. Help me find Petra, wring her neck, pluck her eyes. Take my prize staff, I just want my tower back!

Tullius.line: We should get started.
What was that, Legate?
Where'd you come from?
Go on, get out of here.
It's nothing. Carry on.
I hate giving speeches.
I... Willl... kill you!
You're as good as dead!
Come on, show yourself!
You can't hide forever!
I'll enjoy killing you!
Must've scared 'em off.
That's the end of that.
I'm going to find you...
That's putting it mildly.
That's enough out of you.
What in Oblivion is that?
And we gave it to Elisif?
And we gave it to Erikur?
You'll... pay for this...
I knew I heard something!
You heard the man, Ulfric.
I am proud of all of you.
I expected better from you.
You can't get away from me!
I suppose that's true. Fine.
Well, then. Repeat after me.
Well, then. Repeat after me.
I knew I could count on you.
I pray you're right, Legate.
And now, to deliver a speech.
What are you waiting for? Go!
Elisif! I said I'd handle it.
You have your orders, soldier.
I'm promoting you to Quaestor.
I want every arrow in the sky!
Jarl Elisif, I'll handle this.
Rrrrrraaaaaaaaaaaaahhhhhhh!!!!
Rrrrrraaaaaaaaaaaaahhhhhhh!!!!
I must've been hearing things.
That'll teach you to cross me.
Lead with those arrows, dammit!
Good. Let's get this over with.
You people and your damn Jarls.
Fine. Let's get this over with.
Guess it was nothing after all.
Get those documents to Whiterun.
What are you waiting for? Do it!
You heard what she said, Ulfric.
So, Dragonborn. What do you say?
As much as can be expected, yes.
Now, back to the matter at hand.
We aren't the bad guys you know.
Song or not, I just want it done.
Well, I'm not quite done myself.
Do I know you? I didn't think so.
I'm glad we see eye to eye on this.
It's not a cause. It's a rebellion.
Deliver your report to Legate Rikke.
I trust this is something important?
I'm sure you have something in mind.
Keep an eye out for Stormcloak spies.
Thought I told you to run for it! Go!
We'll let it seem like it's his idea.
You... what? How could you possibly...
That's not for you to decide. Soldier.
As I expected, you favor your friends.
My coat? What do you need my coat for?
Guards, get the townspeople to safety!
Legate. We represent the Emperor here.
Welcome to the Imperial Legion, soldier.
Don't just stand there! Kill that thing!
You're losing this war, and you know it.
I hope we haven't just created a martyr.
Are you prepared to make that commitment?
Is everyone here? Let's get this started.
You made your decision. Now live with it.
You Nords and your bloody sense of honor.
Good job out there. Glad you were with us.
Someone get the battlemages out here. Now!
Come, Rikke. There's still much to be done.
Do you have some reason to be here, citizen?
Why don't you have a chat with Legate Rikke?
Thanks. Couldn't have done that without you.
Long live the Emperor! Long live the Empire!
They'll have set barricades to slow us down.
But I at least will negotiate in good faith.
One of the prisoners, if I recall correctly.
For once you'll actually pay for your crimes.
Damn you mages! Aren't you good for anything?
Legate, with me. We have a lot of work to do.
You realize this is exactly what they wanted.
Hadvar! Into the keep, soldier, we're leaving!
There isn't someone less busy who can help you?
Keep an eye on these Thalmor. You never know...
Soldiers, townspeople, and dregs into the keep!
He'd be insane to try. He doesn't have the men.
Get down there and join Rikke on the front line.
All hail the Emperor. All hail his Legionnaires!
Well Ulfric, you can't escape from me this time.
It's not really a fair comparison, if you ask me.
I'm ready. I made my peace with the gods long ago.
No, I suspect Skyrim to be my home for many years.
What? Have you forgotten your oath to the Emperor?
The rebellion is over. Ulfric Stormcloak is dead.
Thank you, Jarl Elisif. I appreciate your loyalty.
I've seen fresh recruits with more skill than you!
I don't remember seeing you at one of these before.
But... we could use the breathing space to regroup.
We want compensation for the massacre at Ivarstead.
We want compensation for the massacre at Riverwood.
Well, I did see something interesting not long ago.
Make it quick. I get enough delays from politicians.
Get over to Riften. We're mounting our final attack!
You've little time to glory in your accomplishments.
I can see now that this is not a negotiation at all.
We want compensation for the massacre at Heljarchen.
We want compensation for the massacre at Rorikstead.
We want compensation for the massacre at Stonehills.
Get over to Morthal. We're mounting our final attack!
The Thalmor? Do you have any idea what you're asking?
The Thalmor? Do you have any idea what you're asking?
We want compensation for the massacre at Anga's Mill.
We want compensation for the massacre at Karthwasten.
We want compensation for the massacre at Old Hroldan.
Well, the men will be expecting some kind of speech.
Get over to Dawnstar. We're mounting our final attack!
Get over to Markarth. We're mounting our final attack!
Get over to Whiterun. We're mounting our final attack!
Get over to Windhelm. We're mounting our final attack!
Don't worry, I'll come to this peace council of yours.
We want compensation for the massacre at Granite Hill.
We want compensation for the massacre at Loreius Farm.
We want compensation for the massacre at Sarethi Farm.
We want compensation for the massacre at Shor's Stone.
I suppose that's the fairest deal we're likely to get.
Get over to Falkreath. We're mounting our final attack!
I'm impressed at your even-handed treatment. Thank you.
The Greybeards? What do those old hermits want with me?
Perhaps you're right. But then what does that make you?
Get over to Winterhold. We're mounting our final attack!
But I can see now that this is not a negotiation at all.
We want compensation for the massacre at Half-moon Mill.
We want compensation for the massacre at Heartwood Mill.
We want compensation for the massacre at Kolskeggr Mine.
We want compensation for the massacre at Whistling Mine.
Get over to the capital! We're mounting our final attack!
But at least we ended up with the damn crown. Now then...
We want compensation for the massacre at Barleydark Farm.
I'll not argue that. Heard any interesting gossip lately?
We sure showed them, eh? Thanks for your part in all that.
Upon my honor I do swear undying loyalty to the Emperor...
That could have gone worse. Now it's up to you to deliver.
I can only imagine the headaches this is going to cause...
We want compensation for the massacre at Frost River Farm.
I have no problem killing you. How do you feel about dying?
Has everyone here forgotten their training? Kill that thing!
All right men. On me! For the Emperor! For his Legionnaires!
Is it good fortune? The blessings of the Divines? Who knows?
You're needed in Winterhold. Report to our hidden camp there.
Ulfric Stromcloak's head rolling away from his lifeless body.
Well said! You may be fighting on the wrong side in this war.
But it seems you have no intention of dealing fairly with me.
Ready now! Everyone, with me! For the Empire! For the Legion!
After that, Ulfric... there will be a reckoning. Count on it.
I know you, Ulfric. You have no intention of coming to terms.
Hadvar's alive? I hope that's true. He's a damn good soldier.
In the meantime, why don't you have a chat with Legate Rikke.
You've been even-handed so far. What do you say to our demand?
Tell me again, why I'm wasting men chasing after a fairy tale.
Stay alert. Never know when those Stormcloak dogs might charge.
You're right. Ulfric isn't in any position to ask for Markarth.
You are a traitor to the Empire, and deserve a traitor's death.
You speak truly. Have you heard any interesting rumors of late?
...and unwavering obedience to the officers of his great Empire.
...and unwavering obedience to the officers of his great Empire.
If you say so. If this were anyone but you I would lock them up.
We want Hjaalmarch back, and Idgrod Ravencrone restored as Jarl.
Once again, the Empire takes the blame for the crimes of others.
And we'll need to hand the city over to that Free-Winter fellow.
These terms blatantly favor the rebels. Everyone here knows that.
Hardly worth giving up Markarth for, but not a bad starting point.
So what - Ulfric gets this crown and then suddenly he's High King?
Having another port would ease our supply situation considerably...
If we're not going to get Riften, we need you to give us something.
Make your way to our camp in Falkreath. We're going to take it back.
I would refuse, but I don't want to jeopardize the peace between us.
Now that I think of it, she would have made a fine Imperial general.
Take it. Take the coat, have your fun. I'll see you at the festival.
I don't blame you, Dragonborn. You made the best of a bad situation.
I'm willing to let the Dragonborn decide the fairness of my request.
You're here to blacken the Empire's name, and I won't be party to it.
Speak with Legate Rikke. She will determine if you are Legion material.
Are my men now giving free reign to anyone who wanders into the castle?
But he hasn't reported in yet, so he can't exactly confirm your story.
What? Do you want to be that thing's next meal? Into the keep, already!
But the Empire will accept them, until the dragon menace is dealt with.
The faster we take them out, the faster we can claim this city as ours!
Changed your mind? Decided you want to soldier for the Empire after all?
Been so long since I killed a Stormcloak, my sword arm's getting flabby.
Here, use my sword to do it. I made sure to sharpen it for the occasion.
Here, take this gift as a sign of the Empire's appreciation, and my own.
May those above judge me, and those below take me, if I fail in my duty.
Now, if I'm not mistaken. Legate Rikke has a special assignment for you.
In exchange for Markarth, the source of most of Skyrim's silver? Hardly.
I should have known better than to expect you to give us a fair hearing.
The harshness of Skyrim has a way of carving a man down to his true self.
I trust you've come to see the error handing the hold over to the rebels?
Upon my honor I do swear undying loyalty to the Emperor, Titus Mede II...
I'm glad you agree. I was starting to wonder whose side you were on here.
We're backing Elisif. When the Moot meets, they'll do the sensible thing.
We're backing Erikur. When the Moot meets, they'll do the sensible thing.
They'll have barricades set, destroy them quickly. Keep pressing forward.
Speak to Beirand, he's normally out by the forge. He'll get you outfitted.
Elenwen is a formidable woman. She lacks for neither cunning nor ambition.
I'm sorry, that's just not possible. It would cause far too many problems.
If you think you can hold Riften, you're as deluded as you've always been.
You'd better be able to deliver, Dragonborn. I sacrificed a lot here today.
I'm sorry to hear that. I thought you'd finally seen through Ulfric's lies.
I think you overestimate Ulfric's chances. He's stretched himself too thin.
We consider even sitting down to talk with these rebels more than generous.
All right. It's time to deliver the final blow to the Stormcloak rebellion.
Destroy them quickly, and take out the archers in the towers along the way.
You have all fought bravely, and sacrificed much to bring us to this point.
We have no need to negotiate when the rebels are so clearly losing the war.
In the meantime, we'll continue to root them out and put them to the sword.
They seem to think it's at least a dozen, but I'm sure they're exaggerating.
So be it. The Empire at least puts the greater good above our own interests.
New orders for the soldiers, Legate. Make sure they're distributed as noted.
What makes you think I'll give that to you, if I wouldn't give you Markarth?
But make no mistake. What we do here today, we do for Skyrim and her people.
There something I can do for you? Perhaps direct you to the nearest prison...
Besides, I'm sure your being imprisoned was all a terrible misunderstanding.
I believe the Legate gave you an order. I expect you to follow it. Dismissed.
Oh, so you're trying to play both sides, is that it? What do you really want?
Fair? This is war, soldier. War's about winning, not being fair to the enemy.
Don't you think I know that? But these commands come straight from Cyrodiil.
We get Riften back. Now we'll see if there's anything behind your fine words.
By defeating Ulfric at Whiterun we have solidified the allegiance of the Jarl.
If Ulfric's making a move for Whiterun, then we need to be there to stop him.
You know as well as I do that that we can't hand over Markarth on these terms.
Yes, I agree with you. Have you heard any news from the other parts of Skyrim?
Now that I think on it, I did hear something rather interesting the other day.
They are getting to be a problem. But I wasn't sent to Skyrim to fight dragons.
I know you, Ulfric. If I hand over Markarth, you'll be ready with a new demand.
No, you're right. After all this time, Ulfric still holds almost half of Skyrim.
Never mind. I can see that these are the best terms we'll get from this council.
It matters little to me. Either way I'll be sending your heads back to Cyrodiil.
Thank you, Legate. In the future, I'll let you know when your opinion is needed.
I couldn't agree more. By the way, have you heard any interesting rumors lately?
Get over to our camp in Hjaalmarch. We need to strengthen our defensive position.
Hmm. I guess I'll defer to your judgment on this one, but I think it's a mistake.
Yes. Yes, fine, I'll come to this Greybeard council. For all the good it will do.
Where do these demands stop, Ulfric? Do you expect me to surrender all of Skyrim?
Any last requests before I send you to... to wherever you people go when you die.
First, let's be clear. This council wasn't my idea. I think it's a waste of time.
I know just what you mean. Heard anything interesting lately? Any news or gossip?
I wasn't expecting to see you here! I assume you have your reasons. Everyone does.
Fralia Gray-Mane is convinced that the Battle-Borns have abducted one of her sons.
This is an important day for the Empire and for the Legion. And for all of Skyrim.
You hope to gain in council what you've been unable to take in battle, is that it?
We're gathering for our final assault on Windhelm. Report to our camp in Eastmarch.
That's what I like to hear. I'll get the full report from Legate Rikke. Now then...
Fair enough. Although I'm beginning to wonder whose side you're really on. Still...
You should be glad enough of a chance to lick your wounds at this point in the war.
Ulfric, you can't seriously expect us to give up Markarth at the negotiating table.
After all, Eorlund works the Skyforge. How is any smith going to compete with that?
Let this day be a final warning to all who would still call themselves Stormcloaks.
By cutting out the disease of this rebellion, we will make this country whole again!
We are turning the city over to Brunwulf Free-Winter, an honorable and faithful man.
You withdraw from Falkreath, and allow Siddgeir to resume his rightful place as Jarl.
She's part of the Imperial delegation. You can't dictate who I bring to this council.
We couldn't have done this without you. The Empire glories in your accomplishments...
On behalf of the Empire, and myself, please accept this gift along with your new rank.
Are you done? Did you just come here to make speeches? Or can we get down to business?
Beyond that are two exterior gates. Find a way to open them and we can take this city!
Riften must be returned to Imperial control. That's our price for agreeing to a truce.
These Stormcloaks are nothing more than an armed mob. This war won't last much longer.
I suspect we might have use for someone resourceful like you. Not many survived Helgen.
The Thalmor invite me here to remind everyone that they can tell the Empire what to do.
Your actions here were a disgrace. You betrayed your oath to the Emperor at every turn.
My job is to quell this rebellion, and I intend to do just that, dragons or no dragons.
And on that day, we will confiscate his city, and restore the rule of law to his lands.
They've barricades to block us, but we'll tear through them and the rebels behind them!
Well that's an excellent question. You haven't overlooked that little detail, have you?
You'd do well to get out of here before I change my mind and have you locked in irons...
Since we're all here at your request, I'd like to hear what you think Markarth is worth.
You are hereby dishonorably dismissed and removed from the rolls of the Imperial Legion.
Winterhold would allow us to directly threaten the rebel supply lines out of Windhelm...
Our objective is the drawbridge. If we can find a way to drop it, the city will be ours!
I look forward to the day when this rebellion is put down, and we once again have peace.
Just remember, we take care of our own. Once you're in the Legion, you're in it for life.
I don't blame you, Dragonborn. You treated us fairly despite your known rebel sympathies.
Don't you Nords put any stock in your own traditions? I thought the Moot chose the king.
I wouldn't have sent you in the first place if it wasn't going to be worth it. Now then...
It's true that Whiterun is dangerously exposed as long as the Stormcloaks hold Markarth...
Here, take this. Assuming they even honor it, you should be able to get your prisoner out.
We want Kraldar removed as Jarl of Winterhold, and an Imperial candidate put in his place.
I don't know about the end of the world, but this dragon situation has gotten out of hand.
Well... you did show remarkable initiative. And you didn't have to bring the crown to me...
Yes. Well, I didn't respond as quickly to the Legate's suggestion as perhaps I should have.
The only reason I'm here is because my refusal to attend would cause a diplomatic incident.
You should think harder on your oath to the Emperor next time you're deciding what's right.
I need someone I can trust to deliver a message of great import to Jarl Hrongar of Whiterun.
Make your way to our camp in the Rift. We have a few surprises for the Stormcloaks lined up.
This is the day we send a message to Ulfric Stormcloak and the rebel Jarls who support him.
Come on, Ulfric. You have to give a little ground here. The dragons are a threat to us both.
I hope they'll serve the roast venison with bittergreen sauce that we had at the last party.
I need someone I can trust to deliver a message of great import to Jarl Balgruuf of Whiterun.
We will oust Ulfric from Windhelm soon enough. And when we do, I'll have his head on a plate.
What do you think? I can't let Ulfric dictate terms to me before the negotiations even start.
Has he? I suppose he doesn't want to miss a chance to bluster about the Empire's many crimes.
Hmm. You may have a point. I'm always surprised by how seriously the Nords take these things.
I expected better of you, Dragonborn. You swore an oath to serve the Emperor. Don't forget it.
You always were a fool, Ulfric. You're no better at diplomacy than you are on the battlefield.
It's been a brutal winter. I can't remember the last time it was this cold, and for this long.
Well, I did hear that the Argonians have been banned from living inside the walls of Windhelm.
I see. Then there's nothing further to discuss. If you change your mind, speak with the Legate.
And don't think I've forgotten that you were the one that gave it to Ulfric in the first place.
Come see me in Solitude when this is all done. The Emperor can always use another good soldier.
Of course, she would never admit such a thing. Captain Safia is far too proud a woman for that.
Any king who denies the Emperor will wake one morning to face the Legion staring down his gates.
We've made you a fair offer. Are you serious about these talks, or are you just here to posture?
You... you disappointed me. You gave too much away. More than was necessary to secure this truce.
His head will be sent to Cyrodiil where it will adorn a spike on the walls of the Imperial City.
Perhaps the Stormcloaks will soon lose heart for this little rebellion of theirs once and for all.
I heard Elenwen say she's going open a bottle of her best Firebrand Wine later. I can hardly wait.
I remember you, you were at Helgen. Speak to Legate Rikke. I suspect we could use someone like you.
Now, while I'm certain this is all terribly interesting to you, I have important matters to attend.
Now that we have Winterhold, I can garrison some troops there until I'm ready to march on Windhelm.
Well... we could use some breathing space to regroup. Things haven't been going well for us lately.
Well, if he wants to stand outside the protection of the Empire, fine. Let Ulfric pillage his city.
This war has taken a toll on the merchants of Skyrim. Let's hope that it ends soon, for their sake.
You can prove your new allegiance to me, by delivering an important message to the Jarl of Whiterun.
Maybe so, but we shouldn't be making concessions before negotiations even start. Makes us look weak.
All that remains then is the exterior gate. Find a way through to open it and the city will be ours!
I believe the Legate gave you an order. Why are you standing around here flapping your jaw? Move out!
Battles are won by trained and disciplined men. Wars are won by talented and exceptional individuals.
What do you think? Are we going to let Ulfric dictate terms to us before the negotiations even start?
The Legion will be staying here for quite some time. I don't expect there to be any further violence.
I'm raising you to Praefect. Take this blade, as a gift and symbol of your new rank. You've earned it.
Once the Emperor is convinced of the need, then you will see the full strength of the Imperial Legion.
Don't hand me a mug of sheep's piss and call it Colovian brandy. These terms are still not acceptable.
Why? There's nothing to discuss as long as that traitor Ulfric is in arms against his rightful Emperor.
I'll wager it was Ulfric himself that started the rumor. Maybe he thought it would bolster recruitment.
You know I can't agree to these terms. The Emperor would repudiate this treaty and I would be recalled.
Take for example, Ulfric Stormcloak and his little "rebellion." But rest assured, his days are numbered.
Excellent work, soldier. I have to admit, I had my doubts it even existed. Did you run into any trouble?
I think you may have joined the wrong side. You showed yourself to be a true friend to the Empire today.
I guess I have no choice but to let the Dragonborn decide. Although I'm starting to doubt your fairness.
The approach to Riften is guarded by watchtowers. They will be full of archers. Move fast, and stay low.
The Empire can live with these terms, yes. For a temporary truce, until the dragon menace is dealt with.
I heard those mages up in Winterhold have been trying to figure out how many dragons there are in Skyrim.
You may think this war is nearly won. You're wrong. I've been fighting you with a mere handful of troops.
Maybe so, but I can't be making concessions before negotiations even start. It makes the Empire look weak.
In exchange for Markarth, the source of most of Skyrim's silver? Hardly. I might rather consider Whiterun.
Can't say I'll ever get used to the damn cold, or understand these Nords... but, I've come to respect them.
Hmm. The Rift would help secure our communications with Cyrodiil... and threaten Ulfric's southern flank...
You may have a point. It's getting difficult to even move troops around without attracting a dragon attack.
Fair enough. We're driving the Stormcloaks back well enough at the moment, but we're already overstretched.
The walls of Markarth may be high, but their age will have created weakness or secret entrances. Find them!
If this truce will help the Dragonborn here put an end to that menace, we both gain. Remember that, Ulfric.
You've got to admire their courage, doing something like that with dragons on the loose and a war going on.
Have you visited Rorikstead lately? No matter how harsh the winter, the crops that grow there never falter.
You don't really expect compensation every time a farm gets burned in a war that you started, do you Ulfric?
We're here to arrange a temporary truce to allow the Dragonborn here to deal with the dragons. Nothing more.
Even when every other farm in the hold is barely scraping by, the harvest at Rorikstead is always bountiful.
Soon enough I'll have you back under the headsman's axe, and this time there won't be any dragon to save you.
Markarth is off the table. Your rabble is on the verge of defeat. We have no need of a truce at such a price.
The fiercest of the remaining rebels will continue to harass us, but by in large, the people here desire peace.
Your concerns are duly noted, Legate, but unfounded. I assure you, this war will be over in another six months.
Taking control of the Rift was a great victory. Ulfric must be rather nervous with us so close to his door step.
Well. You surprise me. I was expecting you to favor Ulfric. Maybe this council will achieve something after all.
Because this is the end for them, the Stormcloaks will fight like cornered rats. They will be fierce and crafty.
But let me warn you - joining the Legion is for life. Any previous loyalties... I trust we understand each other.
True. A pause to consolidate our gains would be useful. And the Empire can afford to look magnanimous in victory.
We must maintain order and put the rebellious Jarls in their place, before we have a full scale war on our hands.
Here's your pay. It's never enough for what we do, but war gives us warriors its own kind of benefits, doesn't it?
You've come a long way. I don't think we could do without you. I'm hereby raising you to Tribune. Congratulations.
Don't listen to him. Ulfric's been telling lies about himself for so long, he's actually starting to believe them.
I just want to make clear that the only reason I agreed to attend this council was to deal with the dragon menace.
By taking back Falkreath, we control access to the Reach, Whiterun, and the Rift. We should never have given it up.
I swear Elenwen holds these parties just to make the Empire look bad. Almost makes me want to join the Stormcloaks.
Ulfric showed his true colors today, eh? Don't worry. You aren't the first to be taken in by his self-serving lies.
If you think that winning a few battles means you're winning the war, you're even worse at strategy than diplomacy.
I'm sure that will be comforting to your comrades that gave their lives for the territory you just handed to Ulfric.
But, as you say, these negotiations may offer a chance for his supporters to gracefully return to the Imperial fold.
You started this war, plunged Skyrim into chaos, and now the Empire is going to put you down, and restore the peace.
We need the Rift. We need to regain control of it before we can march on Windhelm without worry about our rear guard.
Hurry over to our military camp tucked away in the Reach. Ulfric's been enjoying those silver mines for far too long.
Oh, and Legate. It's been an honor to have you at my side. As a token of my appreciation, I want you to keep my sword.
You always were a fool, Ulfric. You'll never get a better chance to salvage something from the ruins of your ambition.
Some say the ship suffered damage to her hull when she struck an iceberg, and Captain Safia can't afford the repairs.
The rebels have agreed to cease hostilities until we hash out this treaty. Suffice it to say I'm not holding my breath.
The Gray Quarter in Windhelm is little better than a slum. It's shameful that the dark elves are made to live that way.
The only way into Windhelm is across this bridge. Keep moving, stay close, and we'll be through their gates in no time.
What I'm not so sure about is the peace we've made with the Thalmor. But we'll keep that between the two of us, alright?
I can't believe we lost Whiterun. The rebels have a great advantage in controlling the center now. We must take it back!
You... you were at Helgen. One of the prisoners. What makes you think I'm interested in someone like you for the Legion?
Excellent! You aren't the first to have a change of heart, after seeing what Ulfric's reckless pride is doing to Skyrim.
From the Imperial City, our war here is just a sideshow. An interlude before the main event against the Thalmor resumes.
We want the Pale returned to Imperial control. The traitor Skald the Elder will be replaced with a more loyal candidate.
With the soldiers all fighting in the war, there's nobody to hunt down the bandits. Travel has become dangerous of late.
They're only allowed on the city docks now. Most of them sleep together in one building, and they're as poor as wretches.
You'll never defeat the Empire, and you know it. But you're willing to sacrifice thousands for your own selfish ambition.
I've never liked having the rebels right on Solitude's doorstep. Retaking Hjaalmarch would help secure our supply lines...
You disappoint me, Dragonborn. I accepted your invitation to this council, despite your known association with the rebels.
In appreciation for your exemplary service, I am doubling your pay and compensation to the widows of your fallen comrades.
I see... Well, the Legion will still be here when you change your mind. Rikke believes in you. And I'm starting to see why.
Make sure you take the Auxiliary here. You can send her back when you get there and find nothing but old bones and cobwebs.
Make sure you take the Auxiliary here. You can send him back when you get there and find nothing but old bones and cobwebs.
They're probably trying to scare us into giving them more money and influence, so they can save us from the dragons threat.
Apparently, the Red Wave has been docked in Solitude for well over a month, and with no sign of shipping out any time soon.
This missive should convince him. Be aware soldier, these documents contain sensitive intelligence for the Jarl's eyes only.
You'd be wasted as a regular soldier. I have special plans for you. You'll be of greater use to me with greater flexibility.
Consider this. What greater glory than to serve the Emperor and his citizens here in Skyrim, in these days of greatest need?
Better than nothing, I suppose. But Ulfric will need to offer a lot more if he wants me to give up Markarth without a fight.
This missive should convince him. Be aware soldier, these documents contain sensitive intelligence for the Jarl's eyes only.
The Emperor will be paying close attention to what happens here today. Men who distinguish themselves will be well rewarded.
Ulfric's insurrection has reached its high tide. Soon enough the rebel Jarls will realize what it means to oppose the Empire.
I couldn't have said it better myself. It's been some time since I've had news from elsewhere in Skyrim. What have you heard?
That is correct. Those orders have been delivered by special courier. I can't trust some of our normal distribution channels.
You disappoint me, Dragonborn. I accepted your invitation on trust in your good name. But it seems you intend to favor Ulfric.
Riften seems like a better choice to me. Well-fortified, easily resupplied across Lake Honrich. Plus all the mead we can drink.
Not enough to outweigh the loss of Markarth. With the Reach in enemy hands, our whole position in Solitude would be threatened.
By all accounts the Stormcloaks are suffering just as badly. Even Ulfric might see the sense of a truce under these conditions.
Well, I did hear that Ulfric Stormcloak was slain while defending Windhelm against a dragon, but it sounds too good to be true.
With luck, they'll soon lose heart and return to their homes and families. If not, we'll be forced to put them all to the sword.
Hjaalmarch is an important buffer zone between the rebel forces and the capital. Ulfric will try to break through at some point.
The Legion's always looking for strong, capable warriors. If you think you've got what it takes, our headquarters is in Solitude.
You'll be doing whatever Rikke tells you to do. And, I expect you to find creative ways to disrupt the Stormcloaks along the way.
In joining the Legion, you'll be taking an oath binding you to the service of the Emperor and thus to every citizen of the Empire.
I'll be the judge of that. Besides, by all reports the Stormcloaks are suffering just as much as we are from these dragon attacks.
He's probably hoping I'll refuse so he can blame the Empire for being unreasonable. I think I'll have to disappoint him yet again.
You are guilty of insurrection, murder of Imperial citizens, the assassination of King Torygg, and high treason against the Empire.
The Legion's always been here. Without us to keep order, the provinces would fall into barbarism and lawlessness. Especially Skyrim.
Fair enough. I was hoping you could put aside your loyalties for the greater good, but I see you're firmly in Ulfric's camp. Still...
The Thalmor. They stirred up trouble here. Forced us to divert needed resources and throw away good soldiers quelling this rebellion.
Ulfric will want to take Whiterun from us. But we will hold fast. We can't give the rebellion free reign through the center of Skyrim.
Regaining Pale Pass would help our supply situation considerably... and Whiterun is dangerously exposed with the enemy in Falkreath...
Perhaps... I'm entrusting you with what resources I can spare. But I'm warning you, if this turns out to be a waste of time and men...
Things are quiet for the moment. That won't last long. As soon as a new campaign starts, I want you there. Stay close. See me tomorrow.
It was important we reclaimed the Reach. Letting Ulfric control the flow of silver was a mistake. One I'm glad we've finally corrected.
Look around the room and you'll see what we're up against. Just between you and me, a lot of what Ulfric says about the Empire is true.
Most of the Legion is tied down on the border with the Aldmeri Dominion. The Emperor can't afford to risk weakening Cyrodiil's defenses.
You are traitors and will die traitors' deaths. Stand down and face public execution, or advance and face summary execution by my hands.
Well, there might be nothing to it, but I've heard that the feud between the Gray-Manes and the Battle-Borns in Whiterun has grown ugly.
I'm originally from Cyrodiil, sent here at the request of the Emperor himself. I'm often ordered to places with problems that need fixing.
I have come to rely heavily upon you. It is only fitting that you join the upper echelon. I am hereby elevating you to the rank of Legate.
I'm not sure that it's news, but lately it seems like every blacksmith in Skyrim is out to prove that he's the equal of Eorlund Gray-Mane.
However, I'm disappointed to hear that you fled from the field of battle. We do not reward cowardice in the Legion. I expect better of you.
By holding onto Falkreath, we control access to the Reach, Whiterun, and the Rift. Ulfric will try for it soon. We'll be there to stop him.
What?! This is exactly why... don't they understand it's hard enough to fight a war without these juvenile attempts to undermine authority?
Ulfric thinks he can hide behind the walls of his castle. But we will fight our way in and drag him out through the rubble to face justice.
I have no authority to negotiate a permanent settlement, unless Ulfric is ready to sue for peace and turn himself over to Imperial justice.
I'm losing my patience with this damn war. We must take Hjaalmarch back! The Stormcloaks will be beating upon the doors of the capital soon.
That's what comes of trying to win a war with a bare handful of legions. If the Emperor would just give me the reinforcements I've requested!
Make your way to our hidden military camp in the Pale. Rikke will have important tasks for you, and will need you when we reclaim the capital.
Ulfric Stormcloak. Some here in Helgen call you a hero. But a hero doesn't use a power like the Voice to murder his king and usurp his throne.
There were several Khajiit camped outside the walls of Whiterun. Seems they've been traveling around the whole of Skyrim peddling their wares.
We have it on good authority that Ulfric has raised enough men to attack the city of Whiterun. The Jarl, however, refuses the Legion's support.
We have it on good authority that Ulfric has raised enough men to attack the city of Whiterun. The Jarl, however, refuses the Legion's support.
Ulfric is dead and his army destroyed. There's not much more I could ask of you. The gods, however, I suspect, may have plans for you elsewhere.
Take this blade, a symbol of your new station and responsibility. I'll keep an eye on your progress. The Empire rewards excellence, and so do I.
But they are no match for Legionnaires. You are the best and the brightest warriors in Tamriel. Professional soldiers, fearless and devastating.
We need to stem the flow of silver to the rebellion. Until we retake the Reach, the Stormcloaks will plunder it's mines to fund further violence.
It's good we hold the Reach. Were the rebels to gain control, the silver mines would give them coin to hire more thugs and expand their violence.
I almost can't believe it's over. The rebellion is squashed, Windhelm is free. I must admit, Ulfric put up a stronger fight than I was expecting.
Draft another letter with the usual platitudes, but this time share some of your intelligence regarding Ulfric's plans. Embellish if you have to.
Many of you will be staying in Windhelm to aid the Jarl in restoring order and stamping out any embers of rebellion that may still smolder here.
I fear losing Falkreath may give too much advantage to Ulfric. The rebels now control access to the Reach, Whiterun, and the Rift. I want it back.
That said, Stormcloak true believers are still operating out of hidden military camps in the hills. If you encounter any on you travels, kill them.
Controlling the Pale gives Ulfric a port town and takes the pressure off of Windhelm. But it's only a matter of time before it's back in our hands.
And, soldier. You better help win me back Markarth, or die trying. It was your brilliant strategy to give it over to the rebels in the first place.
There's not much up there, but if we can regain Winterhold, our proximity to Windhelm should keep Ulfric wary and wasting troops garrisoned at home.
Reclaiming Hjaalmarch lets me worry less about the rear guard, and push more men to the front. It was a mistake to let Ulfric control it for so long.
Taking the Pale gives us another port in Dawnstar, and puts us within striking distance of Windhelm. That should make Ulfric a bit more cautious, eh?

Elenwen.line: We should get started.
Where'd you come from?
You're as good as dead!
Come on, show yourself!
Must've scared 'em off.
That's the end of that.
I'm going to find you...
You'll... pay for this...
I knew I heard something!
Are you enjoying the party?
Razelan. What are you doing?
Razelan. What is it this time?
I must've been hearing things.
That'll teach you to cross me.
Guess it was nothing after all.
I hope you're enjoying yourself.
It pleases me to hear you say so.
Is my staff seeing to your needs?
I'm so glad you could attend today.
Please, help yourself to more wine.
Is the food and drink to your liking?
Splendid. Call if you have need of me.
Excellent. I'll leave you to it, then.
But first, you were introducing yourself.
Very well, Ulfric. Enjoy your petty victory.
Remove him. He's disturbing the other guests.
I could swear I've seen you somewhere before...
Ah yes. I remember your name from the guest list.
Razelan. And you promised to behave yourself this time.
A very pretty speech, but what does it have to do with...
The hospitality of the Thalmor Embassy is at your service.
But I'm sure you didn't come here for a lecture on high politics.
Of course. I've told you before not to bother me with such trifles.
I'm here to ensure that General Tullius does not exceed his authority.
And so we meet again. But this time I know who and what you really are.
Please, tell me more about yourself. What brings you to this... to Skyrim?
You gave Ulfric more than he deserved. This will certainly prolong the war.
But I also enjoy learning the latest gossip and hear the most intriguing rumors.
My apologies. We'll have to get better acquainted later. Please, enjoy yourself.
Welcome. I don't believe we've met. I am Elenwen, the Thalmor Ambassador to Skyrim.
I trust that this is just the begining of a long and mutually beneficial relationship.
These terms are satisfactory to my government. The truce is unlikely to last long in any case.
Is that so, Erikur? And you with such delicate sensibilities. It must have been most upsetting.
We only want what is best for Tamriel. Surely, the rebels will accept peace when they realize this.
The worship of the false god Talos contributed to the unfortunate friction that led to the Great War.
The Empire, in its wisdom, has come to recognize Talos worship as a barbaric remnant of a bygone age.
Have you? All good, I trust. But you have me at a disadvantage. I'm afraid I know nothing about you...
My duty is simple - oversee the Thalmor Justiciars, and make sure they perform their duties admirably.
This is only a taste of the benefits that accrue to those that align themselves with Thalmor interests.
Ulfric, why so hostile? After all, it's not the Thalmor that's burning your farms and killing your sons.
Don't worry, my parties are always well-stocked with the finest food and drink available in this country.
One of my main duties is to foster a better understanding between the Aldmeri Dominion and the people of Skyrim.
I'll give you credit for boldness. But associating yourself with the Blades is a choice you will come to regret.
Quite often, yes. The only thing I enjoy more than luxury is sharing that luxury with those who can appreciate it.
The Thalmor will treat with whatever government rules Skyrim. We would not think of interfering in your civil war.
Following the Great War, the White-Gold Concordat set out the terms for peaceful coexistence between the two powers.
Talos was a heroic man, but not a god. It pains the Altmer that we must remind our younger cousins of the difference.
Well. Whatever the truth of it, I'm sure a few words with Master Rulindil will have a salutary effect. Take her downstairs.
Of course not. One does not gather the most important men and women of Skyrim and then serve them cheap ale and stale bread.
I also oversee the activities of the Thalmor Justiciars throughout Skyrim, although this has been unfortunately disrupted by the war.
The Empire and the Dominion agreed that the worship of Talos was a relic of the past, and must be eliminated in the interests of peace.
The Thalmor Justiciars are so often misunderstood. Our primary goal is to preserve the peace between the Empire and the Aldmeri Dominion.
As long as the Empire continues to uphold its treaty obligations, my government does not concern itself with the internal politics of Skyrim.
I have every right to be at this negotiation. I need to ensure that nothing is agreed to here that violates the terms of the White-Gold Concordat.

Gallus.line: How do you know of me?
Good luck, Nightingale.
Karliah... she's still alive?
Yes, my friend? How may I help?
I owe you a great deal, Nightingale.
I haven't heard that name in a long time.
I wish to speak to Karliah for the moment.
Then... it's over and my death wasn't in vain.
My only regret is that you had to undertake this task alone.
My only regret is that you had to undertake this task alone.
My only regret is that you had to undertake this task alone.
From the moment I arrived here, I've felt myself... well... dying.
I was blinded. Blinded by dark treachery masquerading as friendship.
Nocturnal calls me to the Evergloam. My contract has been fulfilled.
When your debt to Nocturnal has been paid, we'll embrace once again.
The Key! You have the Skeleton Key! I never thought I'd see it again.
As I get closer to the Ebonmere I begin to feel myself slipping away.
I don't recognize you, but I sense that you're one of us. Who are you?
Were I able to provide it, I'd shower you in wealth for what you've done.
Our access to those bits of luck are what separates us from common bandits.
Whatever is affecting the Nightingale Sentinels is starting to affect me too.
I feared she'd befallen the same fate, ending up a victim of Mercer's betrayal.
That's quite a noble sentiment for someone in our... well, in your line of work.
However, ever since that day, I've felt my power waning... slowly draining away.
When Mercer stole the Key, that conduit closed, severely limiting our ties to her.
Yes, absolutely. Your skill is your own... don't let anyone ever tell you otherwise.
Precisely. There are a few who still call Nocturnal "Lady Luck" and for good reason.
We were betrayed by one of our own kind. In fact, I'm to blame for what's happened here.
Nothing would bring me more pride than to return the Key, but I'm afraid it's impossible.
Even right this moment, I feel strange... when I don't think I should be feeling anything at all.
I wish I could help you, but I've been a prisoner in this very chamber for the last quarter century.
But Nocturnal, she influences our luck; nearly imperceptible assistance we get when we ply our trade.
If it were not for the actions of this Nightingale, your fears would have come true. He honors us all.
A pick breaking when it shouldn't have, the clouds in the nighttime sky clearing at the wrong moment...
If it were not for the actions of this Nightingale, your fears would have come true. She honors us all.
They're shadows of their former selves. They no longer remember their true purpose or their original identities.
You've done the Guild a great deed. And although they may not show it, I'm certain they appreciate your sacrifices.
Perhaps if I had been more vigilant, then Mercer Frey wouldn't have lured me to my fate and stolen the Skeleton Key.
The last of the Nightingale Sentinels, I'm afraid. I've defended the Sepulcher alone for what seems like an eternity.
Think about the Guild... about the state it was in when you began. Think about all the little things you might have heard.
Whatever damage has been caused can only be corrected by following the Pilgrim's Path to the Ebonmere and replacing the Key.
With the Ebonmere closed, and their sudden severance from the realm of Evergloam, I fear they've undergone a drastic change.
My spirit didn't manifest itself in the Sepulcher immediately, so fortunately I wasn't present when the Ebonmere was sealed.
The Ebonmere is a conduit through which Nocturnal influences our world. Not through magic or blessings, but purely through luck.
I'm afraid so. I'm weakening, and I can feel myself slipping away. The years without restoration of my power have taken their toll.
The Sepulcher isn't merely a temple or a vault to house the Key. Within these walls is the Ebonmere... a conduit to Nocturnal's realm of Evergloam.
The only possible help I've come across are the remains of some poor fellow who was trying to follow in your footsteps. Perhaps his journal can help?

Astrid.line: A kill you must repay.
Understood... husband?
Where'd you come from?
Soon, my dearest. Soon.
You're as good as dead!
Come on, show yourself!
Must've scared 'em off.
That's the end of that.
And... what did she say?
Until next time, sister.
I'm going to find you...
Have you found something?
Do it, Listener. Kill me.
Watch yourself out there.
Until next time, brother.
You'll... pay for this...
I knew I heard something!
Represent us well, sister.
Represent us well, brother.
By Sithis, you're not joking.
And they say romance is dead.
By Sithis, I'll see you dead!
I heard about what happened...
I must've been hearing things.
That'll teach you to cross me.
Well now. Funny you should ask.
Guess it was nothing after all.
You won't get that lucky again!
The very thought breaks my heart.
By Sithis, you will pay for that!
It's all up to you now... Listener.
Very well. I respect your discretion.
The Dawnstar Sanctuary? Whatever for?
Ah, but there is a slight... problem.
Do I trust him? Not in the slightest.
Was Mallory willing to buy the amulet?
Like inside the Night Mother's coffin.
Like inside the Night Mother's coffin.
Now go. And give my best to the bride.
Tell me, sister. Are the stories true?
But to be outnumbered twenty to one...
Have you... gone... completely... mad?
Tell me, brother. Are the stories true?
So it's done. You've killed the Gourmet.
Am I to take your silence as acceptance?
Word certainly travels fast, doesn't it?
And now Titus Mede II is as good as dead.
Alive.... You're alive... Thank Sithis...
Let's see his eminence try to ignore this.
Here, take this - it's called Jarrin Root.
I don't even know what to think anymore...
No, nothing. At least nothing I'm aware of.
You're back. Good. You'll want to hear this.
Amaund Motierre? I have no idea who that is.
You must be talking about the caravan job...
Tell me, husband. Is it true what I've heard?
You're referring to that blacksmith contract?
Good, good. Does it say where he may be headed?
Remember the passphrase: "Silence, my brother."
Find Arnbjorn. Make sure my husband's all right.
Remember, when you kill - we all wield the blade.
Oh yes. And I've decided you will have the honor.
Ah, at last! I hope you found the place all right.
Now go. And give the bride a special kiss, from me.
Shouldn't you be out ruining someone's special day?
That twisted jester will pay for what's he's done...
Ah, you must mean my little adventure on the ship...
Oh yes. You've got to kill the bride. At her wedding.
Never mind, it doesn't matter. You need to leave. Now.
But first... find my husband. Make sure he's all right.
I'm afraid I don't have a primary contract for you yet.
And the Emperor will be forced to come clean up the mess.
It's time we put a little kink in the Emperor's security.
Volunruud? Well, that is interesting. I know this place...
Your latest exploit certainly has the Sanctuary talking...
Now go, my friend. Go, and fulfill your destiny as Listener.
Maro is dead, I know. But we've got bigger problems right now!
But Volunruud... that I have heard of. And I know where it is.
Excellent. Once again, you've proven yourself a born assassin.
I want you to find that miserable little fool and end his life!
Please, Listener. Release my soul, and let Sithis be the judge.
You think I'd abandon an opportunity to lead my Family to glory?
Hmm... Yes, the silence suits you. Gives you an air of mystery.

Oh. No, no, no. Don't you understand? Guilt, innocence, right, wrong.... Irrelevant. What matters is I ordered you to kill someone, and you obeyed.
You see, there's a contract out on one of them, and that person can't leave this room alive. But... which one? Go on, see if you can figure it out.
Believe it or not, Festus likes you. He senses your power, your potential. Follow his instructions to the letter, and you may even earn his respect.
They'll never think to check it. Who in their right mind would hide inside? Now go. Before they meet. And report back to me with whatever you learn.
I'm thinking we need that amulet appraised. I want to know where it came from, how much it's worth, and if we can actually get away with selling it.
Of course. And you've repaid your debt, in full.  Here's the key to the shack. But why stop here? I say we take our relationship to the next level.
If you turn around, you'll notice my guests. I've "collected" them from... well, that's not really important. The here and now. That's what matters.
I had an uncle, you see. He made certain... unwanted advances. So I killed him. And liked it. Then I killed again. And liked it even more. And so on.
Give me the letter. Bring Mallory the amulet. Find out everything you can, and sell it if he's willing. He'll offer a letter of credit - that's fine.
Seems the Emperor realized his presence would necessitate a more direct role in the ongoing hostilities. A role he was, obviously, unwilling to take.
Every moment counts, so I want you to take my horse. His name is Shadowmere. You'll find him outside, by the pool. Let's just say he's... one of us.
The Gourmet is scheduled to cook for the Emperor at a special dinner. You'll kill him, steal his Writ of Passage, and assume the roll of master chef.
My only worry is her "Keeper." I'm not sure what Cicero expects to gain by bringing the Night Mother here, but he'll soon learn this is my Sanctuary.
Indeed. For you, my friend, seem to understand what's truly important. When I give an order to spill blood, you follow it. No questions. No remorse.
You! You're responsible for the siege of that... city! By Sithis, this undermines all our plans! Fool! Finish that mess, and only then will we speak!
I'd be lying if I said I didn't enjoy myself. But more importantly, I made this Sanctuary some much needed gold. In the end, that's all that matters.

Maven.line: Never. Now move aside.
Where'd you come from?
I... Willl... kill you!
You're as good as dead!
Come on, show yourself!
You can't hide forever!
Must've scared 'em off.
That's the end of that.
Pack your things and go.
I'm going to find you...
About time you showed up.
It's over! Everyone stop!
You'll... pay for this...
I knew I heard something!
I take care of my friends.
Well, is it taken care of?
To maintain relationships.
Cease fighting. It's over.
Nothing Vulwulf.... cheers.
You can't get away from me!
Some layabout named Sabjorn.
It's over. No more violence.
I surrender... I surrender...
I think I'll like living here.
I must've been hearing things.
That'll teach you to cross me.
You're a firebrand, aren't you?
Guess it was nothing after all.
Vulwulf, it's always a pleasure.
Let me give you a word of advice.
Come, Legate. There's much to do.
Really? Now that is interesting...
I trust you have good news for me.
Everything is splendid, thank you.
They will sing stories of this day.
Good. One less worry for my people.
Get away from me, you filthy woman.
Faith? I don't have faith in anyone.
When you're standing downwind of me.
The throne suits me, don't you think?
I hope not. This is an important job.
I'm aware of who stole Frost. And why.
Why aren't you at Honningbrew Meadery?
The execution will proceed as planned.
And you think that I care? Interesting.
This is exactly what I'm talking about.
Very much so, and thank you for asking.
Very much so, and thank you for asking.
A bit melodramatic, even for you Laila.
Somehow I doubt that will persuade them.
What in the name of Oblivion do you want?
Really? I wrote that off a long time ago.
We have a relationship of mutual respect.
Shouldn't you be on your way to Whiterun?
Give him one more week to think about it.
There is no point in further talk. Go now!
Good. Here, take the key to the warehouse.
Vald? Why would you want to help that idiot?
Oh? I don't expect anyone to miss my meaning.
Good. Make sure not one of them escapes alive.
Why are the potions I requested not completed?
I presume you're bothering me for a good reason?
I'm doing as you asked. Not for nothing, I hope?
You help my people, and I'll make you my Thane.
Any word about our little problem in the Ratway?
I don't have time for idle chit-chat. What is it?
It's not really a fair comparison, if you ask me.
Been a thorn in my side for the last few years now.
Well, I did see something interesting not long ago.
I'm amazed you even bothered to show your face here.
Then put the thumbscrews on him and hit him... hard.
So you're the one that burned down Goldenglow Estate.
So, you're the one. Hmm. You don't look so impressive.
Well, I suppose I need to fulfill my end of the bargain.
Grelka, how fast can you get your hands on some longbows?
I'll not argue that. Heard any interesting gossip lately?
Results speak volumes over words and you've just proved it.
Not so fast, there's a matter of residence to be discussed.
Haelga, I like you, so I'm only going to say this one time.
I don't like being made a fool of, Brand-Shei. Not one bit.
So, I've been hearing your name in court quite a bit lately.
Welcome back. I've left a few surprises for you to discover.
Jarl Black-Briar. I must admit, I do like the sound of that.
Is it good fortune? The blessings of the Divines? Who knows?
Ah yes, Sarthis... a thorn in my side for the last few years.
Excellent, I needed some more kindling for my kitchen hearth.
I believe Brynjolf wishes to speak with you. We're done here.
Then I swoop in and take over the place. No more competition.
Ah, so they've finally sent someone to deal with that lunatic.
I suppose congratulations are in order. Now don't screw it up.
Not now, you idiot. We'll talk after this ridiculous ceremony.
Hemming, I want you to personally supervise the next shipment.
Hemming, have you dispatched that letter I gave you yesterday?
You never were able to see the forest for the trees, were you?
You speak truly. Have you heard any interesting rumors of late?
Excellent. You've done us a great service. Here is your reward.
Then you should know that Louis and I have a certain... history.
Have you learned nothing about me? I have dealings with everyone.
I'm certain you're aware of the loss I sustained a fortnight ago.
Don't worry, I'll treat those shipments as if I owned them myself.
You should bring this information to the Thieves Guild immediately.
You should bring this information to the Thieves Guild immediately.
And don't worry about any rioting, Legate. I have it under control.
I'm very disappointed. I'd advise you to rethink that decision soon.
Jarl Maven Black-Briar. Has a pleasant sound to it, wouldn't you say?
My title is just a formality. I've always been in charge around here.
Exactly. With Sabjorn in prison, his meadery will be forced to close.
I can promise you when he's ready, he'll find the cell door unlocked.
Thank you, Laila. I wanted to discuss the protection of my shipments.
I don't even want to know, do I? But for you... all right. I'll do it.
I'm telling you, Vulwulf, it's the investment you've been waiting for.
Is it true that you refused to sell one of your... trinkets to Hemming?
Good. If Riften should fall, it's only right that its leader be spared.
Then I want you to head out there immediately and eliminate all of them.
Do this task for me and you'll be well on your way to a title in Riften.
Once again Brynjolf sends me someone with no backbone, no determination.
I can't imagine where he found the gold to take it to market so quickly.
Did you happen to discover where the fetcher was getting his skooma from?
I hope for all your sakes you find out who's behind this supposed "plot."
Have our scouts come up with any information on these reports of dragons?
If they want my city, they're going to have to take it from me personally.
Do you have any idea what that little stunt you pulled is going to cost me?
Not a day goes by that I don't regret letting Sabjorn get as far as he did.
Relax, Marise... I have no quarrel with you. I just wish to speak a moment.
I guess all that's left is to bestow the title. What were those words again?
Have you lost your mind? I thought it was clear I didn't want anyone killed.
They seem to think it's at least a dozen, but I'm sure they're exaggerating.
Pay dear old Sarthis a visit and tell him Maven sends her regards, would you?
At least you remembered to bring me the information I requested. Give me that.
Yes, I agree with you. Have you heard any news from the other parts of Skyrim?
Now that I think on it, I did hear something rather interesting the other day.
One more time in case I wasn't clear. You butcher this job and you'll be sorry.
You'd do well to remember that the next time you make such a stupid observation.
I also want to know how they managed to get the place up and running so quickly.
Hemming, I need you to bring a note to Sibbi. I need him to do something for me.
I couldn't agree more. By the way, have you heard any interesting rumors lately?
However, I could only grant the title to someone who is known throughout my Hold.
For what it's costing me, I should be handed the merchandise on a silver platter.
I know just what you mean. Heard anything interesting lately? Any news or gossip?
Fortunately, the quill required an ink with a unique formula known only to myself.
I care little about the politics and rivalries between the Thalmor and the Empire.
I have a competitor called Honningbrew Meadery that I want to put out of business.
Fralia Gray-Mane is convinced that the Battle-Borns have abducted one of her sons.
After all, Eorlund works the Skyforge. How is any smith going to compete with that?
Is that confidence I hear... or is it arrogance? Strange how often they're confused.
You help my people and purchase a house from my steward, and I'll make you my Thane.
Perhaps you should start at the docks and work your way out towards Goldenglow Estate.
Yes, we spoke at length while you were in Whiterun dealing with our meadery situation.
I've known Bersi a long time. Even before he opened that rubbish heap he calls a shop.
These Stormcloaks are nothing more than an armed mob. This war won't last much longer.
After the place is yours, come back to me and we'll deal with the rest of the nonsense.
He owes me a steep debt. In fact, he's lucky all I did was force him to work for Mercer.
Well, whoever this mysterious marking represents, they'll regret starting a war with me.
Well, whoever this mysterious marking represents, they'll regret starting a war with me.
I look forward to the day when this rebellion is put down, and we once again have peace.
I dare say the Guild owes its survival as much to my family as it does to its own people.
And by the Eight, aren't you fortunate that my steward has just the place for you to buy.
In order to have the title bestowed upon you, you need to purchase a house within Riften.
A few years back, I commissioned a unique quill from a good friend of mine in Winterhold.
I have payments to make you realize. If I can't make them, I can't make Black-Briar Mead.
Vald was tasked with getting it here safely and it ended up at the bottom of Lake Honrich.
I surrender! Give me a few hours to gather my household, and I'll depart. You have my word.
Just remember that you're only a part of my court, this doesn't make you one of the family.
The quill was supposed to allow me to duplicate any writing I'm viewing in exacting detail.
You have to understand, it's been a long time since Brynjolf's sent me anyone I can rely on.
Next time a Black-Briar asks for something, I suggest you respond quickly and affirmatively.
Laila, Riften is my home, but if my meadery can't be safe here, I'll just move it elsewhere.
I hope they'll serve the roast venison with bittergreen sauce that we had at the last party.
I don't recall seeing you at one of these before. And I know everyone who's anyone in Skyrim.
Are you going soft on me, Hemming? Perhaps I made the wrong choice installing you as steward.
The only thing keeping me from having you killed is Brynjolf. He's stepped in and made amends.
When people talk about the decadent, tottering Empire, it's people like you they have in mind!
I don't trust them. Come to think of it, I don't trust you either, but at least you're family.
It's been a brutal winter. I can't remember the last time it was this cold, and for this long.
Well, I did hear that the Argonians have been banned from living inside the walls of Windhelm.
But I do respect power, and the Thalmor have that. They leave me alone, and I leave them alone.
Now I have to spend a fortune to keep the whole thing quiet. Guess where that money comes from.
I don't care what it costs. I need four longbows and four score of arrows. Just make it happen.
Ingun, how much longer do you intend to waste your life concocting your foolish little potions?
Of course, she would never admit such a thing. Captain Safia is far too proud a woman for that.
Go. Tell your commanders you have won. I, my family, and my men will be gone before they arrive.
Doesn't matter... the title is yours. I henceforth name you Thane of Riften. Don't disappoint me.
Having Goldenglow kept in line by the Thieves Guild has kept the honey flowing with minimal cost.
The Goldenglow Estate job has undoubtedly interrupted the supply of honey I need to make my mead.
You watch the market, you hear the rumors around Riften. What have you heard about these dragons?
Now, mysteriously, some sort of anonymous adversary shows up and takes the Estate out of my hands.
I heard Elenwen say she's going open a bottle of her best Firebrand Wine later. I can hardly wait.
We will soon begin publicly executing captured men. That should send a clear message to the people.
This war has taken a toll on the merchants of Skyrim. Let's hope that it ends soon, for their sake.
Give this document to Vald. It frees him from the debt, but I never want to see him in Riften again.
Don't mention the Guild. In fact, it's better if you don't speak to me at all. This is not the place.
Of course he wasn't you stupid whore; he's a man. Now I'm not going to warn you again. Is that clear?
Think about your choices carefully from now on. You'll go much farther in Riften with me on your side.
Well, now. There's no need to ruin the party on my account. I don't think he'll be bothering me again.
The wizards called it the Quill of Gemination and demanded a tremendous amount of gold to have it made.
I had Vald meet the caravan from Winterhold on the western end of the lake so he could avoid the roads.
Now, leave me. I don't want to arouse suspicion by talking to someone that clearly doesn't belong here.
I'll wager it was Ulfric himself that started the rumor. Maybe he thought it would bolster recruitment.
I don't believe in getting my hands dirty. You have people that handle those sort of things... use them.
Head to the Bannered Mare in Whiterun and look for Mallus Maccius. He'll fill you in on all the details.
The idiot got lost in the mist and ran his boat right over some rocks, sending it straight to the bottom.
There's also the matter of your payment. I believe you'll find this more than adequate for your services.
I heard those mages up in Winterhold have been trying to figure out how many dragons there are in Skyrim.
This doesn't tell me much. The only thing that could identify Sabjorn's partner is this odd little symbol.
This doesn't tell me much. The only thing that could identify Sabjorn's partner is this odd little symbol.
In only a few short years, he's taken that bile he calls mead to market and a chunk of my profits with it!
All I care about is cause and effect. Did the job get done and was it done correctly. There's no gray area.
I tolerate your son because he'll do anything I ask, without question. I'd expect the same from my own son.
You've got to admire their courage, doing something like that with dragons on the loose and a war going on.
Have you visited Rorikstead lately? No matter how harsh the winter, the crops that grow there never falter.
See that disgusting drunk over there? A high-ranking official in the East Empire Company, believe it or not.
Even when every other farm in the hold is barely scraping by, the harvest at Rorikstead is always bountiful.
You're a disgrace to the Empire. To think that you're actually the representative of the East Empire Company.
Interesting. Keep your ears open for me and tell me if you hear anything more. I'll make it worth your while.
However, since it wouldn't behoove me to make you disappear, I'd rather have you on my side as Thane of Riften.
Vald said his rowboat struck some rocks... I'd check under the water near one of the small islands in the lake.
Well, that's good to know. I suggest you tell your people at the College to keep a tighter leash on their mages.
Here, this should suffice as your cut of the... or rather, a reward for your actions. Now, if you'll excuse me...
He's too stupid to remember where he was, and any attempt I've made to find it in the past has been unsuccessful.
The only reason we're having this conversation is due to Brynjolf's assurance you won't botch another assignment.
Sabjorn could use this interruption to his advantage and collect a larger share of the market. I can't have that.
Excuses are not acceptable in my court. When I ask for something I expect results, and I expect them immediately.
Of course. Nothing gets done without my approval in this city. I have the Jarl's ear, and the guards in my pocket.
Turning him in to the guards? Have you joined Mjoll as official peacekeepers of Riften? You cost me a lot of coin.
You may have everyone else impressed around here, but you don't impress me. You couldn't touch me if you wanted to.
Anyone makes trouble for me and I pay a visit to the Thieves Guild. Make me angry and I contact the Dark Brotherhood.
He claims you possess some sort of uncanny aptitude for your line of work. Quite frankly I find that hard to believe.
So if you're getting any ideas about selling it to someone else or keeping it, you'll find it would hold little value.
Good. If I need to light a few fires in order to get what we need to defend this place from the Stormcloaks, so be it.
Some say the ship suffered damage to her hull when she struck an iceberg, and Captain Safia can't afford the repairs.
If you think you can steal my throne by winning the hearts of the people, that's where you're wrong. Keep that in mind.
We surrender. The capital is yours. If you pledge no harm will come of my people, I turn over the seat of power to you.
The Gray Quarter in Windhelm is little better than a slum. It's shameful that the dark elves are made to live that way.
With the soldiers all fighting in the war, there's nobody to hunt down the bandits. Travel has become dangerous of late.
I want you to speak to the city guard. Make sure there's a contingency plan in effect in case Riften falls under attack.
They're only allowed on the city docks now. Most of them sleep together in one building, and they're as poor as wretches.
Since I know losing the farm's payments will harm the Guild, I intend to make a substantial contribution for compensation.
I'm sure you can understand how useful that would prove in a business situation, especially where signatures were involved.
They're probably trying to scare us into giving them more money and influence, so they can save us from the dragons threat.
Apparently, the Red Wave has been docked in Solitude for well over a month, and with no sign of shipping out any time soon.
However, if you'd wish to eliminate that burden for me... well, let's just say it would go a long way with earning my favor.
If you were to bring me the deed to Goldenglow Estate, so I was the full owner, that would be a step in the right direction.
And a hefty cut for yourself too I'm sure. Just see to it the items are secured and the rest disposed of properly this time.
When you've been doing business like I have for decades, you quickly discover that the only person you can trust is yourself.
If you ever try and "practice the Dibellan arts" with Hemming again, I'll make sure that pretty little face loses its beauty.
I couldn't have said it better myself. It's been some time since I've had news from elsewhere in Skyrim. What have you heard?
Quite. To be brief, I need compensation for the lost shipment. Since Riften was responsible for it, Riften should pay for it.
I've been informed by my steward that you've made quite an impression in the Rift; the people won't stop blathering about you.
Exactly. What better way to help the cause than to put weapons into the hands of young Nord warriors dying for their homeland?
Oh don't pretend to be modest. I'm talking about your new role as champion. Apparently, you've taken my people under your wing.
If you can locate the quill and return it to me, something Vald was too stupid to do himself, I'll consider the debt satisfied.
Well, I did hear that Ulfric Stormcloak was slain while defending Windhelm against a dragon, but it sounds too good to be true.
I can't afford to have another Sarthis blunder into Riften because they think we're too lazy to disrupt their pitiful operation.
I've checked with my court and apparently you're fully eligible... there's nothing standing in the way of presenting you the title.
That's General Tullius himself. He can't stand Elenwen, but she insists that he attend these parties. Just to piss him off, no doubt.
My dear, you have no idea who you're trifling with. Now I suggest you turn around and rattle someone else's cage before you get hurt.
The Black-Briar family has always been allied with the Guild. Our connections with the Empire and within Skyrim make for a perfect fit.
You've got a lot of nerve, suggesting something like that, to me of all people! If this was Riften, I'd have you thrown in the dungeon!
By the way, I don't know who you are, but unless you want me to blow your cover, I'd advise that you avoid me for the rest of the party.
There is room in my court for a new Thane. It's an honorary title, mainly, but there are a few perks someone like you could make use of.
There is room in my court for a new Thane. It's an honorary title, mainly, but there are a few perks someone like you could make use of.
