Well, there might be nothing to it, but I've heard that the feud between the Gray-Manes and the Battle-Borns in Whiterun has grown ugly.
However, I could only grant the title to someone who is known throughout my Hold, and who owns at least one piece of property in my city.
Next time you speak to him, tell him to clean up his own messes. I'm not about to jeopardize this position for a bunch of low-life thugs.
Tell you what. Go along with his plans for now. I'll keep an eye on you both and, when the time is right, I'll settle accounts with Louis.
I'm not sure that it's news, but lately it seems like every blacksmith in Skyrim is out to prove that he's the equal of Eorlund Gray-Mane.
I've tried repeatedly to eradicate that meddler, but he has ears within the city guard. Gives us the slip every time we raid the warehouse.
I expect that you'll distance yourself from the affairs of state. In other words, keep your nose out of my business and we'll do just fine.
I'll send my men to secure any of the remaining goods you left behind. We can't have that substance falling into the wrong hands now, can we?
It's about time Brynjolf sent me someone with business sense. I was beginning to think he was running some sort of beggar's guild over there.
Well, I'm also certain you're aware that our own city guard failed to provide the protection required to ensure safe passage of the shipment.
There were several Khajiit camped outside the walls of Whiterun. Seems they've been traveling around the whole of Skyrim peddling their wares.
In honor of your service in battle, I am hereby granting you permission to purchase property in Riften. Talk to my steward if you're interested.
Your interests lay where I wish them to lay otherwise my favorite daughter may end up homeless and without a septim to her name. Is that clear?
Don't tell me - Sibbi and Louis Letrush? Well, Sibbi is in jail and Letrush is certainly welcome to try. My hired help is more than a match for him.

Spectral Lachance.line: The Dread Lord awaits!
I weep for you, mortal.
I return to you, Father.
I... am Lucien Lachance.
Back... to... the Void...
Ha ha! I've suffered worse!
Another thrall for the Void!
Another soul for you, Father!
There is no pain in the Void!
You would shut out the darkness?
I grow restless. As does my blade.
Shadowmere, my old and dear friend...
Lead, and I will follow, child of darkness.
There is murder in the air. I can taste it.
Windhelm. White as bone, and cold as the Void.
Your enemies will soon know the wrath of Sithis.
The Sanctuary must be purged of its invading filth!
One day, you will serve our Dread Father as I do now.
You wish to kill me? Someone has already had that honor.
A poison stew, eh? I was always partial to apples, myself.
Dreary, dank, and dimly lit. Truly a Sanctuary to call home.
We must return to the Sanctuary! Your Family is in dire peril!
The Dread Father works through me. And his work has just begun.
All hail the savior of the Dark Brotherhood! All Hail the Listener!
We are bonded now, you and I. Joined, through the powers of the Void.
You know, a good Purification might be just what this Sanctuary needs...
I remember Skyrim from my youth, and the glistening crimson on fields of white.
Perhaps we should find a random stranger to murder. Practice does make perfect.
Yes. Kill the chef, and then steal his very identity. For that is the true death.
Killing the Gourmet will leave an emptiness in the collective soul of Skyrim. This pleases me.
Do you feel it? Magic. Deep and ancient. It resonates from the College, like the beating of a heart.
In life, I was but a Speaker for the Black Hand. But you have been named Listener. There is no higher honor.
Ah, yes. Solitude. Seat of the High King of Skyrim. Messy business, kingslaying. Ah, but so very satisfying...
The Keeper is a sacred position within the Dark Brotherhood. Ask yourself - do you trust the wisdom of our Lady?
In my time, the Blades protected the Emperor. It would seem these Penitus Oculatus will prove equally incompetent.
We are the messengers of death, you and I. Together, we will unleash the unholy wrath of our Dread Father, Sithis.
My time saw the assassination of an Emperor as well. Alas, the Dark Brotherhood did not have the honor of that kill.
The time has come to fulfill your destiny, Listener. Kill the Emperor, and restore the Dark Brotherhood to greatness.
Whiterun, home of the Skyforge. It is said a blade forged in its fires can cut through sinew as if it were parchment.
I will kill this jester if you so desire, but there is a disturbance in the Void. Our Dread Father does not wish this.
I do so love Riften. I journeyed here in my youth. A thief took my purse... so I took his eyes. It was a fair exchange.
You stand now at the precipice of the Void. I am reminded of another Listener, a protege I knew long ago. So long ago...
Stalking your prey, planting false evidence, destroying an innocent man's reputation. Truly a contract worthy of the Listener.
Have you heard the tale of Mathieu Bellamont, and the great treachery of Cheydinhal? Kill a boy's mother, and vengeance festers in the son...
An ancient city, Markarth. Build by the Dwemer, the dwarves of old. To think, an entire race... eradicated. Not even I can take credit for that feat.

Ancano.line: You dare challenge me?
Where'd you come from?
I... Willl... kill you!
You're as good as dead!
Come on, show yourself!
You can't hide forever!
Must've scared 'em off.
That's the end of that.
I'm going to find you...
We will see about this...
You'll... pay for this...
I knew I heard something!
You can't get away from me!
You've come for me, have you?
Still you persist? Very well.
Another new Apprentice, I see.
No! You will not deny me this!
I must've been hearing things.
That'll teach you to cross me.
Guess it was nothing after all.
Come then. See what I can do now.
It is my job to know these things.
You cannot take this away from me!
Well? What is the meaning of this?
You there. I have questions for you.
I'll be the one asking the questions.
Thank you for your help. You may go now.
Find out why he's here, and what he wants.
Are you quite sure we need to be speaking?
Yes, of course. The Arch-Mage has my thanks.
My dear, I am merely observing the proceedings.
Really? Well, allow me to clarify the situation.
I need you to come with me immediately. Let's go.
I am at the Arch-Mage's disposal if he requires advice.
I would suggest that you not further spread this rumor.
Well, perhaps that is why you're merely an Apprentice here.
Whatever it is those Psijics are up to. They cannot be trusted.
No. No, I'm quite sure I have no idea what you're talking about.
I'm disappointed to say I've learned little during my time here.
I am beyond your pathetic attempts at magic. You cannot touch me.
I know full well that you have. Please do not insult my intelligence.
You dare approach me here? Are you mad? You cannot hope to defeat me!
I assure you that I shall be watching you -- all of you -- very closely.
More importantly, I'd like to know why he's asking for you specifically.
I expect to be informed if they make any future attempts to contact you.
You think I don't know what you're up to? You think I can't destroy you?
I have many important things on my mind. Your concerns are not among them.
I suggest you mind your own business and return to whatever it is you do here.
Perhaps not as often as he should. In time, I believe he will be more trusting.
Yes, I've no doubt of its gravity. This, however, is a matter that cannot wait.
Tolfdir is still there now, is he? I shall expect a full report when he returns.
I'm not sure what just happened but I assure you I will get to the bottom of it.
Yes of course. I'm simply trying to understand the reasoning behind the decision.
I have made it quite clear that my only role here is as an advisor to the Arch-Mage.
I'm afraid I must intrude. It is urgent that I speak with your associate immediately.
You were in Saarthal, yes? It has come to my attention that something was found there.
My role as advisor to the Arch-Mage is aided by knowing everything that transpires here.
I am an advisor to the Arch-Mage. The Thalmor wish to promote relations with your College.
To guide its people through this time of transition, to help lead them to a better future.
What? What trickery is this? You're not going anywhere until I find out what you're up to!
That sounds precisely like the sort of thing that should matter to everyone. Especially me.
So we're going to go have a little chat with him, and find out exactly what it is he wants.
The power to unmake the world at my fingertips, and you think you can do anything about it?
I'd like to know why there's someone claiming to be from the Psijic Order here in the College.
I had hoped your scholars would be on a level comparable with my own colleagues. They are not.
Your superior and I were simply having a... discussion about my level of access to the College.
For all we know, he's here to scout out the College. I'm sure they want to undermine our efforts.
Are you of the sort that believe you're here to change the world? Or are you only in it for yourself?
Now that stability has been established between the Dominion and the Empire, we seek only to help Skyrim.
Technically, that is true. But I still report to the Aldmeri Dominion, and I cannot ignore this situation.
They have clashed with the Aldmeri Dominion before, and I have no intention of allowing that to happen here.
Don't play coy. You asked to see a specific member of the College. Here he is. Now what is it that you want?
Don't play coy. You asked to see a specific member of the College. Here she is. Now what is it that you want?
Nothing you need to concern yourself with. I shall be quite capable of observing everything that goes on here.
All you need to know is that the Psijic Order is a rogue organization, believing themselves to be above the law.
Preposterous, and just the sort of thing I would expect from mages who have nothing better to do with their time.
I am well aware. Tolfdir is still in Saarthal, is he? I shall be expecting a full report from him when he returns.
Don't worry, you can return to your petty squabbles and meaningless 'research' as soon as this matter is resolved.
Now, you are going to speak to this... Monk... and find out why he is here, and then he will be removed from College grounds.
This! This... Orb. The Eye of Magnus, whatever you want to call it. It's clearly powerful, and no doubt they want it for themselves!
Something was discovered in Saarthal that was significant enough that Tolfdir sent a new member of the College, alone, to deliver word.

Vex.line: Come on, I won't bite.
Where'd you come from?
Not like that it isn't.
Well? You done or what?
I... Willl... kill you!
You're as good as dead!
Come on, show yourself!
You can't hide forever!
Must've scared 'em off.
That's the end of that.
I'm going to find you...
Been itching for a fight.
You'll... pay for this...
I knew I heard something!
You're nothing but a joke!
Out with it. Time is money.
Got plenty, take your pick.
You can't get away from me!
Back with good news I trust.
I have no business with you.
I have no business with you.
Good, we could use the coin.
Didn't even work up a sweat.
Sound like you can handle it?
Got more than you can handle.
All right, whatever you want.
Let's get this party started.
You have the gold yet or what?
I must've been hearing things.
That'll teach you to cross me.
Suit yourself. You're the boss.
Guess it was nothing after all.
You already know all that I know.
Good. Here's what you need to do.
Fine. We do it your way. For now.
Steal anything worth talking about?
That son-of-a-bitch! I'll kill him!
There's nothing more I can teach you.
I bet you are. Which one do you want?
Then we understand each other... good.
I believe you owe the Guild something.
Fine. Just keep it clean. Here you go.
I hope you come up with that gold soon.
If you get arrested, it's on your head.
Keep to the shadows and you'll do fine.
I have work if you've got the backbone.
Here you go. Hope you don't muck it up.
Whatever you're selling, I'm not buying.
I'm busy. Don't you have something to do?
All right. Take this and get out of here.
Fine. Here's everything you need to know.
Fine. I guess you'll need your coin then.
There's no need to hide. I won't hurt you.
You've already learned all I can teach you.
Whatever, just get it done. Here's the job.
Hey, I still have plenty of work to do here.
We can talk after I find out what's going on.
I hope you brought a little something for me.
No problem, I'll get someone else to take it.
You ready for some good old-fashioned burglary?
I suppose you want your share now? Here you go.
Anything you steal, you bring it straight to me.
Without it, we're just a bunch of petty thieves.
No one dies. Remember that. We're not assassins.
It was like he was daring us to come and get him.
Well, well. If it isn't Brynjolf's little protege.
So, feeling loose? How about you run a job for me.
Didn't muck it up I hear. Good. Here's your share.
I think you and I are going to get along just fine.
Vald? Good side? I think you have the wrong person.
If you want to get paid, keep your weapon sheathed.
Feel like doing a bit of shopping... the Guild way?
Fine. I'll just keep all this coin for myself then.
Need a few spare septims? I got the cure right here.
These jobs aren't going to finish themselves you know.
Here's your gold. Try not to spend it all in one place.
All right, but if you get arrested don't blame it on me.
I suppose it's tough for an old dog to learn new tricks.
That's how I slipped in there. Should still be unguarded.
Come on, take another job so we can both make some money.
Hey, where do you think you're going? We have work to do.
You're not getting anything until you pay the Guild back.
No problem, I'll find someone else. Don't worry about it.
If you want my opinion I say it's just plain old bad luck.
Before we begin, I want to make two things perfectly clear.
Need to stay focused in case Frey comes back, make it fast.
Oh, you're back? I suppose you'll be wanting your pay then.
After you talk to Brynjolf and Mercer, come back and see me.
Funny, I'd have thought she would've gone back to him by now.
Well, ain't gonna find her here. Haven't seen her in a while.
That pig? Oh, I have info on him. More than you care to know.
It's all here. Well, congratulations. You're one of us again.
Again? Didn't we teach you enough of a lesson the first time?
First, let's count the coin, then we can get back to business.
Get the job done and get back alive. That's all there is to it.
Ready for some work or you just going to wander around all day?
You need advice? Talk to Brynjolf... I got better things to do.
Our Guild Master graces me with a visit. What can I do for you?
I'm not getting any younger. Let's get this ceremony done with.
And when the coin dried up, that's when people started to leave.
I don't want a trail of dead bodies leading here. Keep it clean.
I'm not here to hold your hand. Figure this one out for yourself.
Make up your mind, will you? You're starting to make us look bad.
Amazing what people leave laying around for us to take, isn't it?
The only thing Vald understands is gold. A man after my own heart.
And with no strings attached. Not bad. Here, got some coin for you.
Now get out of here and don't come back until your pockets are full.
I suppose you deserve it. Couldn't do any worse than Mercer, anyway.
You idiot. He said "firs" not "furs." It was a damn logging caravan!
I'm not going to sugar-coat it for you. We're in a bad way down here.
If I see Frey, I'll pluck his eyes from his skull with my bare hands!
You think you're finished? I've got plenty more where that came from.
Hey, you approached me. You want to play coward, that's your problem.
If we want to stay on top in Skyrim, the Guild needs that paragon back.
Fine. Next time, save me the trouble and pass on it from the beginning.
Anyway, you know the drill. You want to buy back in, and it'll cost you.
We had the best of everything down here... the Ratway was a damn palace.
Did you think we'd accept a heartfelt apology and let bygones be bygones?
I knew you had it in you. After all, finding the Stones was no easy feat.
While you're in there, help yourself to anything in Frey's manor. I would.
When you're in your grave and I'm standing over it. Now get out of my way!
Who knows. Old Delvin thinks it's some kind of a curse. I think he's crazy.
Hmph, yeah I did. That wood elf s'wit... he's a lot smarter than I expected.
I think there were twenty-four in all. Most people keep them as a curiosity.
Every now and again, we like to remind the shopkeepers that we're in charge.
Look, I don't like wasting my time. Come back when you decide to get serious.
You did the job and you did it clean; that's all we ever ask. Enjoy the gold.
Delvin, that last shipment we heisted on the Cyrodiil frontier was worthless!
This is the Thieves Guild, genius. How do you think? With gold. A lot of gold.
The catch is you can't kill anyone who lives there or you forfeit the payment.
Don't give up now... there's plenty of loot out there just ripe for the taking.
Here. Just don't make me look like an ass in front of the Guild and mess it up.
So I gave her some advice, pointed her towards Hobs Fall Cave and that was that.
And two, you follow my lead and do exactly as I say... no questions, no excuses.
If you can bring it back, we could restore the paragon... and reap its benefits.
The owner of this place just inherited an heirloom from a relative. You want in?
Walking away without taking a job is like passing a bloated coinpurse on a table.
Now you I like. I've been getting sick of the pushovers that usually walk in here.
Keep turning down jobs like this and the Guild will begin to lose respect for you.
Not interested? Fine. Then let me offer it to someone with a little more backbone.
And, while you're at it, make a little bit of coin on the side. Not a bad deal, eh?
I think you should finish that special assignment Delvin gave you, then we'll talk.
First you take the job, now you don't even want it. I wish you'd make up your mind.
Good, just remember the order: do job first, get drunk second. Now take this and go.
Sure, but he'll ask for a whole lot. I mean, you are asking him to betray Mercer Frey.
And here I expected you to hop the next caravan to Morrowind. What have you got for me?
Yeah. Someone ticked off Maven and she wants to teach them a lesson... our way. You in?
Well, what the hell is Mercer going to do about this mess? Have you even talked to him?
That door has the best puzzle locks money can buy. There's no way it can be picked open.
Been a while since I've seen one of those. What you've got there is a Stone of Barenziah.
You've broken the rules and now you need to pay... and it's going to cost you quite a bit.
I heard Brynjolf is looking for you. I think it's time for the Guild to have a new leader.
Got a good one here. Personal score to settle between myself and this mark. You up for it?
If you're interested, I could point out a good place that could use a little housecleaning.
If the stones were remounted on the crown, it would be of great benefit to the Thieves Guild.
You an assassin or a thief? Next time, keep your blade sheathed if you want to make any gold.
Well, there's an old sewer tunnel that dumps into the lake on the northwest side of the island.
I'm going to make this short and sweet, because frankly I don't think you deserve another shot.
Are you kidding me? Ever since the Guild's luck turned sour, we haven't had a coin to our name.
I handle the burglary, shill, sweep and heist jobs. Most of them involve breaking and entering.
Easy, huh? So, should I just hand you purses of coin or are you going to work to earn your keep?
The stone was pried off of Barenziah's ceremonial crown by a thief in order to cover his tracks.
So, you're the new recruit everyone's been talking about, eh? I guess it's about time we talked.
This one angered someone pretty high up in the keep. Can I count on you to get this done for me?
I want to pay you, but I had to cover up the murder. Didn't expect you to go on a killing spree.
If you don't like that kind of work, talk to Delvin. He runs the more up-close and personal jobs.
You idiot! We have to cover your mess up and that costs us gold... which means you don't get any.
The Flagon was once a city beneath a city. We had our own smith, our own alchemist... you name it.
Just got a tip on this house... should be stuffed with loot. Care to head over there and find out?
Can you believe that fetcher had more than tripled the guard? There must be eight of them in there.
Sure, sure. We're always looking to get our hands on items from all over Skyrim. How about this one?
Like I told you, last time I saw her she was headed for Hobs Fall Cave. No idea what happened to her.
Best of all, we'd have enough gold to throw around so we can start living in the lap of luxury again.
Got a shop owner with a very choice item a client wants to get his hands on if you do the dirty work.
Good going... you got arrested. Now how do you think that looks for the Guild? Better luck next time.
Are you out of your mind? We said no bloodshed! Well, guess what. You can just forget about your gold.
Next time you decide to pull a stunt like that again, just remember how much it hurt to give this away.
If that means breaking into their strongboxes and stealing something valuable from them, then so be it.
I told her it was a bad idea, that she wasn't cut out for what'd be necessary, but she wouldn't hear it.
Of course, you could just run him through and take what you need off of his corpse... I could care less.
Pretty simple. Break into a house, grab a specific item and get out before they realize what's going on.
These people just inherited some heirlooms from a relative. Good timing for us, bad timing for them, eh?
Not exactly. The crown is what the Guild calls a paragon; an object that enhances our thieving abilities.
Have a house with a good bit of valuables inside, but one particular item's caught my eye. You up for it?
Brynjolf finally got some dirt on the mark and the evidence to back it up. Think you can give him a hand?
Well, I'm not one to welch on a deal, so here's your reward. I hope you put it to good use. I know I have.
At least, that's what I'm told it does... there hasn't been a paragon in this Guild for hundreds of years.
If you're ever in need of some quick coin for items you find on the job, Tonilia is the best fence around.
This one should be easy. Get in, plant the goods and get out. Quiet and neat. Sound like your kind of job?
I thought it was crystal clear... no one dies on these jobs. Remember that next time you want to get paid.
You drew blood, so you aren't getting paid. That's the rules. You don't like it, take it up with Brynjolf.
Sure, sure. Got one that should be pretty easy, as long as you keep your weapon sheathed. Can you handle it?
Look, I told the client you probably had a good reason to kill the mark, but well, they demanded reparations.
Sorry, I don't have a single septim left after paying the town guard to keep quiet about the mark you killed.
You need to start pulling your own weight around here, otherwise I might "forget" to offer you any more jobs.
I've picked up some new clients and did a few jobs on my own, but I'm not sure if that will cover our expenses.
Now, it's time to get your feet wet and I don't want to waste a lot of time talking about anything but business.
Now, it's time to get your feet wet and I don't want to waste a lot of time talking about anything but business.
You can get out there and start making a name for us again... make them start fearing us like they did long ago.
Unbelievable! You must be the first person to set eyes on all twenty-four of them in... well, the last two eras.
If we can make a name for ourselves in Skyrim once again, I can almost promise you those merchants would return.
Tell you what, if you find the rest of them, talk to me again. Otherwise, keep it and maybe it'll bring you luck.
Got another personal score to settle and I'd consider it a favor if you'd take the job. Think you can help me out?
She came through a while ago, wanted to know where she could get her hands on something valuable. Anything, really.
You'd better. Because until you do, you don't get a single coin from us... and you won't be able to use our fences.
Got a client in desperate need for this item. He won't say what it's for, and we don't care. Can you take this one?
Got a tip on a desirable item at this location. We want you to break in there and bring it to us. What do you think?
One of the quickest ways to make some coin in Skyrim is to clear out some of the wealthier homes of their valuables.
Being connected to a criminal didn't make the client happy... next time, act like a real thief and don't get caught.
If something's being handed to you on a silver platter, then there's a catch... steal the platter instead. You get it?
Good, got a buyer in a rush for this item. If you hurry we can turn it around and make a quick profit. Can you get it?
'Jiit caravan brought this shop a nice piece from Elsweyr. Think you can break into their strongbox and get it for us?
Got a message from one of my people that this place just came into some coin. Should be a good target. Want to take it?
Look, if you just want to sit around the Flagon all day getting drunk, that's your business. Just quit wasting my time.
Some of the Guild members have tried to locate them over the years, but they haven't been successful... well, until now.
I think we have a nice wealthy home for you to break into. Think you can clear the place of valuables and keep it quiet?
When I told you the set had to be complete, I left out a small detail... I honestly didn't think you'd ever get this far.
One, I'm the best infiltrator this rathole of a Guild's got, so if you think you're here to replace me, you're dead wrong.
And besides myself, Delvin, Niruin and Vipir can help you with any sort of training you might need to sharpen your skills.
He came nosing around Riften a while back, making a lot of noise about me. He was... persuaded to leave before he found me.
Got a good one here involving a shop, a waylaid shipment of goods and an angry client. You fill in the blanks. You want in?
I have it on good authority that the crown is within Tolvald's Cave; dropped by a Dunmer caravan leaving Morrowind long ago.
Look, I only buy things I can turn around quickly for a profit. And no one wants those stones unless they have the whole set.
I'd say it's time you put your nose to the grindstone and figure it out for yourself. You claim to be a thief... act like one.
Someone wants to see this mark suffer. They're paying a good bit of coin to have us plant the evidence in their house. You in?
Sure. A client's relative lost this item in a gambling match and they're paying us to get it back. So much for fair play, huh?
I have just the place. The owner likes to stash some lovely trinkets in his strongbox like this beauty. Can you get it for us?
The last time I checked, gold coins aren't falling from the heavens. You better take some jobs if you want to earn your share.
Your best bet would be to erase his debt with Maven Black-Briar. If you talk to her, she might be able to give you the details.
We send you to plant some phony evidence in the mark's house, and then tip off the town guard... the rest takes care of itself.
The mark must have ticked-off our client badly. He's impatient and keeps asking if the job's done. Can you get him off my back?
The owner of this shop stiffed us on their last protection payment, but we're taking it anyway. You want to help us on this one?
The only way this place will ever return to its glory days is if we can finish these extra jobs and start the gold flowing again.
Pretty angry actually. I can't believe I botched the Goldenglow job. That should have been like stealing a sweetroll from a baby.
We've actually stolen this item before and somehow it ended up back in some other mark's house. Guess it's time to reclaim it, eh?
Good timing. The people in this home recently got one of your fellow Guild mates sent to jail. How about you make them pay for it.
The owner of this place just got an inheritance from a relative lost in the war. Sound like something you can sink your teeth into?
We've been trying to get this item for a while, but no one with the skills has come along to claim it. Can I count on you to get it?
Someone with connections to our Guild wants us to hit this place and send a message by clearing it of valuables. You want to handle it?
As long as you can keep things quiet and don't get yourself killed in the process, this place has exactly what we're looking for. You in?
We're looking to recover an heirloom for a client. They claim ownership of it, and as long as they're paying we don't really care. You in?
You can give this house a once over. I hear they're fairly well-off and probably have lots of good stuff ripe for the picking. Sound good?
When a client needs us to eliminate another, we don't operate like the Dark Brotherhood. We find alternate means to get them out of the way.
Pick pockets, knock over houses... heck, I don't care if you have to bust into a palace. You do what you have to do to come up with the coin.
Let me give you a small bit of advice. Nothing in this line of work is easy. If it was, every dreg in the Ratway would be robbing Riften blind.
Brynjolf, something stinks around here and it's more than just the Ratway. We all know it. The question is, what's Mercer going to do about it?

Mercer Frey.line: Where'd you come from?
Do I make myself clear?
Must you keep stopping?
I... Willl... kill you!
You're as good as dead!
Come on, show yourself!
You can't hide forever!
You can't hide from me!
Hm? Something's near...
You can't hide from me!
Must've scared 'em off.
That's the end of that.
I'm going to find you...
This better be important.
Yes, yes, what is it now?
I'll spit on your corpse!
Hmph. Hardly a challenge.
This shouldn't take long.
You'll... pay for this...
I knew I heard something!
Good, you're finally here.
You've breathed your last!
Quickly, what did you want?
You can't get away from me!
Is that the best you can do?
You're making this too easy!
That should do it. After you.
I've killed legions like you!
You always were a quick study.
Coward. Come out and fight me!
I can hear your heart beating.
That was hardly worth my time.
Your death will be my triumph!
I must've been hearing things.
That'll teach you to cross me.
Enough of this mindless banter!
There's two more of them ahead.
Your blood will adorn my blade!
Guess it was nothing after all.
If only we knew where she was...
You can't kill what you can't see!
Hiding? The shadows are my domain!
This one doesn't look too difficult.
You've just written your own epitaph.
A word of advice. Don't disappoint me.
Keep your eyes open and your mouth shut.
Come, "Nightingale!" Find me if you can!
His real name is Gulum-Ei. Slimy bastard.
This better be important, I'm quite busy.
Your feeble attempt to hide is insulting!
That wasn't a fight, it was an execution.
Brynjolf will provide you with the details.
You're not a threat... merely an annoyance.
I'll find you... it's only a matter of time.
If you see Karliah, don't hesitate. Kill her.
Yes, I'm afraid you're right. Such a shame...
Let's get going. I want her head on my mantle.
There's little time for talk, what do you want?
I should've finished you when I had the chance!
When you're ready, meet me at Snow Veil Sanctum.
Let's wait until Brynjolf's here before we begin.
Speak to Vex. We have nothing else to talk about.
Did Gulum-Ei give up any information on our buyer?
Don't you have better things to do than disturb me?
We have to go out there before she disappears again.
I'm sorry, I was under the impression I was in charge.
Gallus trusted her too much and let her get too close.
Karliah's taught you nothing! Your lessons begin anew!
Now, did Gulum-Ei give up any information on our buyer?
We both agree it's best to keep these matters in house.
I've mastered the shadows... and now your life is mine!
You're quite noisy for someone who claims to be a thief.
Farewell. I'll be certain to give Brynjolf your regards.
Aringoth was a fool to think he could get away with this.
Instead you've chosen to fall over your own foolish code.
Karliah's close, I'm certain of it. Now let's get moving.
Karliah, when will you learn you can't get the drop on me?
Karliah's been through here. What does she expect to find?
We're on the right track. She's been through here as well.
No, it.. it can't be. I haven't heard that name in decades.
Yes, I'm going with you and together we're going to kill her.
Yes, I'm going with you and together we're going to kill her.
Ah, it's one of the infamous Nordic puzzle doors. How quaint.
You're leading and I'm following. Does that seem clear to you?
Good. Then I think it's time we put your expertise to the test.
I've scouted the ruins and I'm certain Karliah is still inside.
Revenge is it? Have you learned nothing from your time with us?
Come, Karliah. It's time for you and Gallus to become reunited!
She was a stubborn Dunmer... always had to do everything her way.
How interesting. It appears Gallus's history has repeated itself.
Gulum-Ei is our inside man at the East Empire Company in Solitude.
That's enough of this unnecessary chatting, we need to keep going.
That door up ahead... looks perfect for hiding an ambush. Be ready.
The stench in here... this place smells of death. Be on your guard.
Have your weapon at the ready. I'm certain Karliah knows we're here.
Brynjolf, have you had a chance to speak to our contact in Whiterun?
Remind me why we even bother to allow him to be a part of the Guild.
This better not be another waste of the Guild's resources, Brynjolf.
Sides quickly formed behind these men and the Ratway became a bloodbath.
Goldenglow Estate is critically important to one of our largest clients.
I'll be damned if I'm going to lose her to... well, to whatever they are.
Just make certain you keep your eyes open. Karliah is as sharp as a blade.
Look, in case you hadn't noticed, these aren't the glory days of the Guild.
One thing's certain. I intend to find out before she draws her last breath.
Then the die is cast, and once again my blade will taste Nightingale blood!
I thought it was painfully obvious that Gulum-Ei was important to the Guild.
When Brynjolf brought you before me I could feel a sudden shift in the wind.
They say that these ancient Nordic burial mounds are sometimes impenetrable.
Because, even after all their posturing and planning, they've made a mistake.
Greed? Jealousy? Spite? Who can say what drove her to such an iniquitous act.
Wait a moment... do I detect a hint of genuine avarice from this noble thief?
And at that moment, I knew it would end with one of us at the end of a blade.
Goldenglow Estate provided a huge payout for us before Aringoth locked us out.
Gallus had his wealth and he had you. All he had to do was look the other way.
I trust you've applied the appropriate pressure in order to change their mind?
If Sabjorn ever managed to push Maven off her pedestal, we'd all be in trouble.
I need you to put all your efforts into this job. We can't afford any mistakes.
Before I could even draw my blade, her second arrow found its mark in my chest.
Do you honestly think your arrow will reach me before my blade finds your heart?
This is grave news indeed, she's someone I hoped to never cross paths with again.
If you want to call it that, yes. Me? I think she was softening him for the kill.
Gallus would call her his "little nightingale." He was absolutely smitten by her.
Nocturnal doesn't care about you, the Key or anything having to do with the Guild.
Let's get moving, I want to catch her inside while she's distracted. Take the lead.
There's no need to be concerned. We've been through more difficult times than this.
It was so lucrative, I was considering moving the Guild to Solitude to save us time.
The last thing I need is you blundering into a trap and warning her that we're here.
I'll speak to the Jarl and see what I can do. Just keep them at bay a little longer.
Maven looked the other way as long as the honey kept flowing and we kept him in line.
Just don't mistake my admiration for complacency; our nemesis is going to pay dearly.
Just don't mistake my admiration for complacency; our nemesis is going to pay dearly.
Fortunately, I persevered and the other groups were either killed or they left Skyrim.
Then, maybe a few years ago, it all stopped. He didn't even bother to contact us anymore.
However, don't mistake my admiration for complacency; our nemesis is going to pay dearly.
Speak to Vex to make the proper reparations if you want back in. Now get out of my sight.
Bone chimes... clever. Rigged to wake the draugr I'd bet. Don't blunder into any of them.
You claim this recruit possesses an aptitude for our line of work. If so, let him prove it.
You claim this recruit possesses an aptitude for our line of work. If so, let her prove it.
If you're not sure maybe you don't belong here. I'll ask again, are we clear on all of this?
Perhaps Karliah and Brynjolf misjudged you and your true nature is no different than my own.
No, I found her horse. Don't worry, I've taken care of it... she won't be using it to escape.
But she was also the best... bringing in more coin a month than some thieves heist in a year.
Then it appears the shadows shroud more than your presence... they blind your wisdom as well.
It's clear you'll never see the Skeleton Key as I do... as an instrument of limitless wealth.
Fortunately, these doors have a weakness if you know how to exploit it. Quite simple, really.
The Guild will not tolerate the kind of behavior you've been displaying. Too much is at stake.
Karliah always was a nimble minx... slipping passed these draugr must be child's play for her.
But do you know what intrigues me the most? The fact that this was all possible because of you.
I'd like to know why you're wasting time asking about it when you should already be on your way.
Karliah and I were like partners. I went with her on every heist. We watched each other's backs.
Let's not kid ourselves. Black-Briar Mead is swill. The only thing keeping it successful is Maven.
Pull the chain over there, and watch out for the spikes. Looks like Karliah reset all of the traps.
When Gulum-Ei was getting us merchandise from the East Empire Company, we were making a hefty profit.
After we discovered what she'd done, we spent months trying to track her down, but she just vanished.
Our actions have always been one in the same; both of us lie, cheat and steal to further our own end.
You're a clever girl, Karliah. Buying Goldenglow Estate and funding Honningbrew Meadery was inspired.
Gulum-Ei started to get greedy and cut us out of the deal. Almost can't say I blame the scaly bastard.
When I arrived, Gallus stepped from the shadows. Before he uttered a sound an arrow pierced his throat.
I'm betting he acted as a go-between for the sale of Goldenglow Estate and that he can finger our buyer.
Prepare yourself and meet me at the ruins as soon as you can. We can't let her slip through our fingers.
I know her techniques, her skills. If she kills me, there'll be no one left that could possibly catch her.
I'm taking you out of the game for now. No jobs will be offered and you won't be able to fence your goods.
It started out innocent enough; he kept claiming the shipments were light because of Imperial interference.
Before we continue, I want to make one thing perfectly clear. If you play by the rules, you walk away rich.
You break the rules and you lose your share. No debates, no discussions... you do what we say, when we say.
Do we have anyone reliable to send out there? I don't want to look the fool again in front of her, Brynjolf.
There's only one place that could be. The place where she murdered Gallus... a ruin called Snow Veil Sanctum.
Since Brynjolf assures me you'll be nothing but a benefit to us, then you're in. Welcome to the Thieves Guild.
To this day I've regretted allowing her to escape, even if it meant I had died trying. I owed Gallus that much.
The infighting had taken months to subside which gave her time to go into hiding and carefully cover her tracks.
Karliah has provided me with the means to be rid of you, and this ancient tomb becomes your final resting place.
It would seem our adversary is attempting to take us apart indirectly by angering Maven Black-Briar. Very clever.
They're well-funded and they've been able to avoid identification for years. I'm impressed it reached this point.
The Guild was thrown into disarray. Several stepped up and tried to claim Gallus's former position as Guild Master.
Word on the street was that Honningbrew tasted better, cost less and Sabjorn didn't shove it down everyone's throat.
Karliah destroyed everything this Guild stood for. She murdered my predecessor in cold blood and betrayed the Guild.
You jest, but they've been able to avoid identification for years. They're obviously well-funded, driven and patient.
Twenty-five years ago I was standing outside these very same ruins. Gallus told me to meet here but he wouldn't say why.
What's Karliah been filling your head with? Tales of thieves with honor? Oaths rife with falsehoods and broken promises?
Don't dismiss our adversary so easily. They're well-funded, patient and have been able to avoid identification for years.
Get out there, shake him down and see what you come up with. Talk to Brynjolf before you leave if you have any questions.
I staggered away from the ruins and my vision began to blur. It's then that I realized the bitch had poisoned her arrows.
The last thing I saw was Karliah dumping his body into a opening atop the ruins; an unceremonious end for a remarkable man.
However, the owner has suddenly decided to take matters into his own hands and shut us out. He needs to be taught a lesson.
Quite simple really, I don't know what the fuss is about these locks. All it takes is a bit of know-how and a lot of skill.
The parchment you recovered mentions a "Gajul-Lei." According to my sources, that's an old alias used by one of our contacts.
I've consulted my contacts regarding the information you recovered from Goldenglow Estate, but no one can identify that symbol.
I'm sorry to say your payment for the job will have to go to elsewhere since you feel like taking things in your own direction.
I have a long-standing arrangement with the Dark Brotherhood. If I need someone in the Guild taken care of, we do it ourselves.
We've lost more clients in the last decade than I'd care to count, but Maven's stuck with us through throughout the entire ordeal.
I saw what they did to Gallus. I wanted to use the Guild's resources to hunt down Karliah. The others didn't even care he was gone.
Karliah was a master marksman and her greatest weapon was the element of surprise. I was lucky... she missed my heart by mere inches.
I'll let that comment go because you're new here. Ask things out of turn again, and we have a problem. Now, are we clear on all of this?
Without the matching claw, they're normally impossible to open. And since I'm certain Karliah already did away with it, we're on our own.
Here's your payment for Solitude. Prepare yourself and meet me at the ruins as soon as you can. We can't let her slip through our fingers.
When will you open your eyes and realize how little my actions differ from yours?  Both of us lie, cheat and steal to further our own end.
Karliah, I'll deal with you after I rid myself of your irksome companions. In the meantime, perhaps you and Brynjolf should get better acquainted.
I spent thousands of septims and used every contact at my disposal, but it was as if she had simply vanished... like I said before, she was the best.

Delvin Mallory.line: Where'd you come from?
Wait just one moment...
You're as good as dead!
Come on, show yourself!
Must've scared 'em off.
That's the end of that.
Oh yes. Oh yes, indeed.
Gettin' to old for this.
I'm going to find you...
I'm endin' you right now!
You'll... pay for this...
I knew I heard something!
Sure, pick on the old guy!
I don't have time for this.
You've got moments to live.
Consider it forgotten, boss.
All right. What's your fancy?
Here's your share. Well done.
Great. What do you want to do?
I must've been hearing things.
That'll teach you to cross me.
So, what can I do for ya today?
Good... here's your assignment.
Nicely done. Here's your share.
Guess it was nothing after all.
Pull up a seat and have a drink.
So, you in for a little fishin'?
You already know all that I know.
Feel like doing a bit of writin'?
Wait just a blessed moment, Bryn.
They picked on the wrong old man.
Here you go. This should cover it.
Haven't fought like that in years.
Here you go, boss. All the details.
No killin' or no pay. Remember that.
Something troublin' ya? I'll listen.
Not now, it's time for the ceremony.
There's nothing more I can teach you.
What's in that book? What did it say?
Buy it? This? An Elder Council amulet?
All-righty. Good luck out there, boss.
Great. Watch yourself out there, boss.
We could use someone with your skills.
The gold, the jewels... it's all gone.
Come on, show an old man what you got!
What exactly are we talking about here?
Got lots of jobs available. Interested?
Get over there and see what you can do.
Good, my clients are gettin' impatient.
This should compensate you for your find.
Somethin' ya need? Anything for the guild.
Where you off to? There's work to be done.
Tonilia, can I offer some friendly advice?
You've already learned all I can teach you.
When you find your nerves, then we'll talk.
Good job. Now here's your part of the deal.
Now that's what I like to hear. Off you go.
Finally, someone with backbone. Here you go.
Need some gold? I may have some work for you.
What a delightful find! This should cover it.
Nice. Here, this is what you'll need to know.
I'll cut out your eyes and sell 'em for coin!
There's plenty more work for the likes of you.
So now the pupil is the master, eh? Good show!
You're a natural. Never seen anythin' like it.
Well, you're makin' friends all over, ain't ya?
Always a pleasure, boss. What can I do for you?
Bloody good timin'. Just got some more clients.
A pleasure as always, boss. Here's your payment.
Come back later and I may have some work for you.
Well, well. I was looking for this little beauty.
If break-ins are more your thing, go talk to Vex.
Don't make me the fool and let me down, all right?
You're not going to get richer with that attitude.
Ah, but business! Of course. What kind of business?
Use your skills, not your blade and you'll do fine.
Find me if you're ever lookin' for some extra coin.
Talk to me again if you prove yourself to Brynjolf.
Your not winnin' points with me with that attitude.
Sure, no problem. We'll find someone else to do it.
Do what he says, Vex. This isn't helpin' right now.
Hold your thoughts 'til after we untangle this mess.
All right, anythin' in particular strike your fancy?
Oh, really? Well then... how can I make that happen?
Can't talk long, got to keep my eyes open for Mercer.
Don't even give it a second thought. Maybe next time.
They aren't my boys. Do I look like the leadin' type?
What's the matter, afraid of getting your hands dirty?
I think you'll find this payment to be more than fair.
'Bout time. Brynjolf's been gettin' on me about money.
Couldn't have done it better myself. Here's your coin.
Yeah, your right. Probably a waste of your time anyway.
Tell me what you need, and I'll get my people get on it.
Stick with me, and they'll never even know you're there.
Now, if you're ready, I got another special job for you.
And here's the spoils. Plenty more where this came from!
Remember, keep it clean. We don't want blood on our hands.
I hear Endon's quite pleased to have his merchandise back.
I can pat you on the back all day, but coin's coin, right?
I think Brynjolf can give you all the information you need.
Good news. I found that dwarven hammer you were looking for.
You bring that back to your lovely mistress. With my regards.
Keep droppin' these jobs and I might quit assignin' them to you.
Look around you. The Flagon, the Guild... it's all fallin' apart.
Sorry. We got nothin' to talk about. Vex will straighten you out.
And here I thought you were a master criminal. Guess I was wrong.
This is gettin' dangerous. Don't let anythin' get the jump on you.
Don't forget your represntin' the Guild. Don't do anythin' stupid.
You're a little light on coin there, my friend. No gold, no refit.
Oh my... umm, you won't let Mercer hear about this will you? Ahem.
Banners, huh? Trying to pretty up the place? All right, you got it.
Stabbed in the back. It's like the Dark Brotherhood all over again.
Where oh where did you get this? Don't answer - I don't want to know.
I've got another special request and they specifically asked for you.
It wasn't long before we lost what we depend on to survive... respect.
This kind of work suits you... and is going to end up makin' you rich.
Only way this guilds going to continue to grow is by takin' extra work.
Got a mark taxed with carryin' a heavy item. Care to lighten' the load?
Ah, but we can discuss that later, yeah? What does the Brotherhood need?
Got all your gold ready, boss. You can count every coin, it's all there.
Too bad, looks like I need to find someone else to take care of business.
Here's everythin' you need. You get caught, and we've never heard of you.
I think you better listen to Mercer and Brynjolf first. We can talk later.
If you don't watch it, I may actually start to respect you. Job well done.
Keep this up and you'll be rich enough retire before you know it. Good luck.
We've got half of Skyrim back under our wing and the coin's startin' to flow.
Yeah, funny thing about that. The price was a hundred higher than I expected.
Who in their right mind would be stupid enough to pay you to steal from Maven?
I hardly noticed you were gone. Got your share of the spoils right here, boss.
Go talk to Vex. She'll tell you the why's and how's if you want to get back in.
Just remember the rules... keep it clean and keep it quiet. Here's the details.
Lyin' to my face isn't gonna win you any favors with me, I can promise you that.
All you need to do is ask and we can both come out of this smellin' like a rose.
Not only that but they're givin' us thieves a bad name by murderin' their marks.
Let me make this plain and simple. No more jobs until you pay Vex. No get to it.
Aren't you supposed to be in Markarth? Finish that job and we'll talk about more.
I want you to finish that job in Whiterun first. Then we'll talk about more work.
Sorry, boss. Had to use your share to cover expenses after you done and got messy.
What do you mean? That wine tastes finer than anythin' Surilie Brothers ever made.
Bein' a thief is like a second skin for you, ain't it? Never seen anythin' like it.
Still have quite a good bit of jobs available if you're looking for some extra coin.
Before I'm goin' to give you any more work, you need to finish that job in Solitude.
Fine, but you're not gettin' any wealthier by weaslin' out of everythin' I offer you.
I've used my key, but the vault's still locked up tighter than a drum. Now use yours.
Be careful out there... wouldn't want to lose such a promisin' cutpurse now, would we.
He's a powerful ally in Markarth. Should help the Guild regain a foothold in the west.
Puttin' together another shipment from Morrowind, Vekel. Lookin' for anythin' special?
Can I give you a little tip? Take every job you can get or else you'll end up a beggar.
If you don't watch out, you might even earn Vex's respect. Now that'd be something, eh?
I handle the fishin', numbers and bedlam jobs... the ones with the more personal touch.
It's worth quite a bit actually. Quite a catch! Here you go, this should make you smile.
You need to bloody well get your priorities straight if you want to make it around here.
So, you're the one Brynjolf's been jabberin' about. Been waitin' to have a word with you.
In fact, I just got a special job request in. I haven't gotten anythin' like it in years.
Torsten was gratified the death of his daughter was avenged and the heirloom was returned.
Torsten was gratified the death of his daughter was avenged and the heirloom was returned.
Steal from anywhere in the hold, and you even get to keep everythin' you stole. So, you in?
Maybe. That stuff's gettin' tough to bring across the border with all the Argonian patrols.
More importanly, he's pledged the full support of the Battle-Born clan to the Thieves Guild.
This ain't the Dark Brotherhood. Next time, you keep the job clean and you'll get your coin.
Just keep your eyes open and your mouth shut and you'll do fine. Let me give you the details.
Most of the establishments in Skyrim keep their transactions recorded within business ledgers.
Yeah, sure. Got a business on tap that could use a little number rearrangin'. What do you say?
How can Mercer open up a vault that needs two keys? It's impossible. Could he pick his way in?
I got one last special request of you. Finish this one and all of Skyrim's ours for the takin'.
Got word from Torsten Cruel-Sea in Windhelm that a rival Guild is tryin' to get itself started.
If you like I'd be happy to take it over the operation for you. Take the source off your hands.
Are you crazy? What were you thinkin'? You can't just go around doin' things the way you please!
Contacts are springin' up all over and I've got young footpads beatin' down the door to join up.
We actually got a request from one of the most powerful families in Whiterun, the Battle-Born's.
Best of all, he's also pledged to open doors for us in Solitude and to get things rollin' again.
At this very moment, he's layin' the groundwork for the Guild to reestablish itself in Windhelm.
At this very moment, he's layin' the groundwork for the Guild to reestablish itself in Windhelm.
What's the matter with you? We said keep it clean! Well, you can just forget about your payment.
Now we have to kick you out until things cool down. Can't give you any jobs or payouts for goods.
Erikur assures me that Captain Volf will spend pretty much the rest of his life clapped in irons.
If you think you'll make your fortune kickin' around the Flagon all day, that's where your wrong.
So, if you've got the nerve, I've got plenty of extra jobs to help get the Guild back on its feet.
Am I responsible if the vintage was off on that case? Sorry, you know the rules Vekel. No refunds.
I also suggest you spend some time in the trainin' room. Just talk to Vipir. He'll show you around.
Guess I should have known. Maven and Sibbi have been at odds ever since she had him tossed into jail.
Success means that the Guild is gettin' stronger. We've picked up another merchant and a new recruit.
Are you bloody serious? You're not going to get ahead by quitting around here, that I can promise you.
Of course. Don't forget to keep out of sight, it doesn't count if they catch you red-handed. You ready?
So, uh... how is Astrid doing' these days? Tell her to stop by some time. We can have a drink. Catch up.
Your job would be to change the numbers in those books so the shortfalls from our other jobs look legit.
If I told you that, I'd be lying. We haven't had a bite in weeks. Our clients are starting to get angry.
I need you to make your way to Solitude and talk to Erikur. He's lookin' to run some kind of a shill job.
We've got a long way to go, but don't let that bother you... it's a lot farther than we've been in years.
That's where you come in. I've got plenty of work available that could guide us down the road to recovery.
Sure you finished, but you killed someone to do it. Next time listen to the rules if you want to get paid.
Well now, how is Astrid doin' these days, uh? Tell her to stop by some time. We can have a drink. Catch up.
On the other hand, takin' some of these extra jobs off my hands, now that'll get you to the top of my list.
Watch yourself out there. The mercenaries Aringoth hired are trained killers. Just ask our poor little Vex.
I want you to head out to Markarth and speak to Endon. He's a silversmith... has some kind of a shop there.
As far as the Guild's concerned this is between Sibbi and Maven, so we're going to officially stay out of it.
Well, it appears your actions are startin' to make waves out there. People are talkin' about the Guild again.
I always have bedlam jobs. Just remember to hit them fast and don't let them see you. Feel like going for it?
You needed to do this clean and quiet, and you failed. Now we need to use your payment to cover your mistake.
I think it's time we make this Guild Master thing official. Go and talk to Brynjolf, he's makin' preparations.
Got ourselves a freelancer thinks he can muscle in on our territory. How about you teach him some manners, eh?
So, what do you think, my dear? Could a beautiful woman like you ever see yourself with an old codger like me?
Look, I know the others think I'm a bit daft for sayin' stuff like this, but I'm gonna give it to you straight.
Hey, this guild needs the coin... walkin' away without takin' work is not going to make you popular around here.
All right. Got a simple one. Just get in, change the books and be back in time for last call. So how's about it?
I hope you weren't expectin' to get paid for this blundered job. Next time, the mark stays alive... you hear me?
By the Eight... you actually got your hands on it! This alone is worth more than some thieves earn in a lifetime.
I don't know what your made of, but I've never seen anyone tackle this many jobs without endin' up dead or in jail.
I'm goin' to tell you'll somethin' you'll never hear me say again, and if you tell anyone I told you, I'll deny it.
Okay, got just the one for you. Ledger's just beggin' to be changed and the Guild could use the coin. How about it?
These marks keep their ledger all neat and tidy, but they're ripe for some change if you know what I mean. Take it?
It's nice to shake the cobwebs off and get things rollin' again. Keep this up and I can see a bounty headed our way.
Your makin' a name for yourself, that's for sure. But more important, you're makin' a name for the Guild. Keep it up.
Olfrid sent ahead his compliments. Looks like his friend Arn is soon to be released thanks to his brand new identity.
Gettin' pinched and endin' up in jail doesn't cut it with our clients. If you want to get paid next time, stay clean.
Slow down, you have that special request in Windhelm to finish first. After that's done, I'll send you some more jobs.
We're still growin' by leaps and bounds thanks to you. Another merchant's just moved into the space outside the Flagon.
This business is a regular target for the Guild. They keep pretty shoddy records, so the job should be a cinch. You in?
Must have come from Mercer's place. He'd admired the Gray Fox for some time. Sure, I'll buy it from you... here you are.
When you walk through the Guild, don't be ashamed to hold your head high 'cause you're the best damn thief in the place.
This brilliant soul decided to pick a fight with a fellow Guild mate. Time for them to pay the piper. Will you help out?
Taking an easy job, eh? Well, go and do some damage. Just stick to the shadows and keep out of sight. Want to go for it?
We want to waylay a shipment on the way from this business without them even noticin' anything went missin'. Want the job?
Brynjolf want's this mark's head on a pike, but we'll settle for what the bastard's carryin' instead. Can I count on you?
A few decades ago, this place was as busy as the Imperial City. Now, you're lucky if you don't trip over a skeever instead.
I'll tell you what we do. We spit in that curse's face and turn things around down here. Put things back the way they were.
His father was a good friend of the Guild back in the day, and we could really use his family's influence back on our side.
I think you'll find that the Guild is beginnin' to grow. We've had a merchant move into the space right outside the Flagon.
I don't know where you find your sources, but that last shipment of skooma was some of the best quality I've seen in years.
That'll come in handy, yeah? An alchemy station, maybe a little garden with some choice plants. All right, consider it done.
If you happen across any other unusual trinkets like this, be sure and bring them to me. I promise it'll be worth the effort.
Have a client lookin' to have a rival's business ledger changed. Don't know the reason, and don't want to know. Count you in?
Here. It's a letter of credit. Usable, by Astrid only, for any service or item I can provide. As per our standard arrangement.
Sure, I've got a mark for you. Stole something quite important from a friend of the Guild and we take care of our own. You in?
Ah, now you must be lost. Best ya scurry off while you're able. The Ratway, well, it has a habit of swallowin' up the uninvited.
Look, if I were you, I'd proceed carefully. Crossin' Maven is one of the first things you learn not to do when you're down here.
Somethin' out there is piss-drunk mad at us. I don't know who or what it is, but it's beyond just you and me. We've been cursed.
I've got a mark who just got an item from a relative outside of Skyrim. They probably should have been quieter about it. You in?
That's impossible. I distinctly heard that lout in the tavern say it was a full shipment of furs. They should be worth a fortune.
I wished you could have sent more of a message to those Summerset Shadows upstarts, but hopefully they'll stay out of our business.
Maven wants this mark's most prized possession. The bloody fool carries it on his person all the time. So, think you can handle it?
I'm afraid I had to pay off a few officials to keep you out of jail, boss. Expensive to get murder off the books, know what I mean?
I'm afraid that's it, my friend. I've done all I can. I hope the place is at least livable now. Good luck with, your, um... murders.
You can't keep quittin' jobs when it strikes your fancy. You're going to make us all look like a bunch of amateurs. Think next time.
Make your way up there and look for Olfrid. He says he's got a matter that requires a delicate touch, and I suggested you for the job.
Simple right? Almost. The catch is it doesn't count if anyone sees you take it. The message needs to be clear; we work in the shadows.
The Dawnstar Sanctuary? That where you lot are holing up in now? Hmm... Tell you what. It'll cost you - a lot - but I can help you out.
Burnin' that banner was a nice touch, too. Should send a clear message to the Summerset Shadows that we're a force to be reckoned with.
Sometimes we like to remind a city that we mean business, so we hit it hard. Just steal whatever we can until we've made an impression.
Now don't get cross with me, but the officials are demanding compensation for the mark you killed. Had to do it... had no choice, boss.
By doin' these extra jobs and puttin' some fear into the people, we can take back the cities and start bein' taken seriously once again.
Got a personal request from Brynjolf on this numbers job. He says we may hit 'em hard later, and wants to prep their books. Help us out?
One of our marks seems to have an item of value we want to add to our vault. Get it for us and you'll receive the usual pay. Sound good?
Thanks to Maven Black-Briar, we still have some pull in Riften. But get arrested in Whiterun and you'll be tossed right into the prisons.
The Guild's at full strength and we have you to thank for it. We've had our share of dark times, and now they seem like a distant memory.
Why don't you just formally join up? Quit sittin' here wheelin' and dealin' when you could be out there makin' a heck of a lot more coin.
Look, it ain't my business ta tell the Dark Brotherhood its business, but if you killed a member of the Elder Council, you'd better belie-
Years ago, the Guild used to have a foothold in every major city in Skyrim. You wouldn't dare even lift an apple without checkin' with us.
When things started goin' downhill around here, it became difficult to keep it all together. We lost fences, influential contacts and coin.
Well, I never thought I'd see the Guild this way ever again. The place is growin' into what it used to be when Gallus was runnin' the show.
See, that kind of attitude comes from someone who wants to get rich and stay alive long enough to enjoy it. We're goin' to get along nicely.
If you're lookin' to get some coin for the fruits of your labor, you should talk to Tonilia. She sounds tough, but she'll cut you a fair deal.
I had to use your pay to smooth things over with the client. They didn't want to be associated with a criminal. Next time, don't get arrested.
Oh ho. Now that's the Dark Brotherhood I know and love. This for business or pleasure? Ah, what's it matter, right? I'll see that it gets done.
The only way to get that special job is by doin' smaller ones in those cities until we catch their ear... then they'll contact me and we're off.
This is an amulet of the Emperor's Elder Council. Specially crafted for each member. Worth a small fortune. Ain't somethin' you'd give up lightly.
There are all sorts of people wandering around carryin' their valuables on their person... just waitin' for someone like you to pick their pocket.
Brynjolf, huh? Lemme guess. He plucked you off the street and dropped you into the thick of things without tellin' you which way is up. Am I right?
Now, he's not exactly goin' to welcome you with open arms, but keep your cool. Do this job right and we've just made an important friend out there.
Yeah, we can do that. I'm thinking a sewer grate on the outskirts of Dawnstar. Leads to a tunnel, then inside. You'll be in or out, quick as a coney.
Heh. Chamber fit for a king and all that, right? No problem. Give ya a big bed, decorate the room real nice, maybe even throw in a few extra baubles.
If we can gain the confidence of someone very influential who lives there by doing a unique job for them, we'd be able to have some leverage there too.

Mirabelle Ervine.line: Where'd you come from?
Welcome to the College.
You're as good as dead!
Come on, show yourself!
Must've scared 'em off.
That's the end of that.
I'm going to find you...
Yes, thank you Arch-Mage.
You'll... pay for this...
I knew I heard something!
May I help you, Arch-Mage?
I don't see the relevance.
Yes, Arch-Mage. Thank you.
That can't be a coincidence.
By the gods... what was that?
Get moving, now. I'll be fine.
I must've been hearing things.
That'll teach you to cross me.
Guess it was nothing after all.
Savos. Before... before he died.
Were you able to learn anything?
Are you all right? Can you walk?
Very good. Then we're done here.
Wish I could say the same for us.
I'm not certain what use it would be.
Well, please let me know when you are.
You were told to find it, weren't you?
No more than anyone else, I don't think.
We need to find out what Ancano's up to.
Well? Is everything out there all right?
I believe I've made myself rather clear.
Come on, get up... I need you on your feet.
I'd like to know as much as we can about it.
Are you... Are you sure? The staff is there?
Should we consider investigating the matter?
Find Savos, and get him back here if you can!
Well, that depends on what you're looking for.
All right, that's enough. Back to bed, please.
Just what have you gotten yourself involved in?
I need you on your feet. We're in trouble here.
That's nothing you need to concern yourself with.
Wonderful. Please follow me, and don't wander off.
Well, you're certainly making a name for yourself.
Well, perhaps it's time for some independent study.
He... He gave me something just a little while ago.
Urag can be very helpful. Just don't antagonize him.
I have no idea what happened. Ancano did it, I think.
Your schedule is always clear, Arch-Mage. I see to that.
An elf that studies stars? Where did you hear about that?
After I've finished showing you the grounds, we can talk.
Forgive me, but at what point does it become our concern?
If we can't stop this, what happens to the rest of Skyrim?
I continue to hear things about dragon sightings in Skyrim.
Yes, just a brief overview of our facilities. Are you ready?
Our newest members are housed here in the Hall of Attainment.
All right, that's enough. Let's return to our business please.
Would you like me to send someone? Faralda or Phinis, perhaps?
I certainly hope so. I don't know how long we can keep this up.
Whatever is going on, whatever you're up to... Be very careful.
I understand you're the one that found this... Orb in Saarthal.
Besides, you're the one having all of these visions, aren't you?
The sort of embellishment wizards of ancient times loved to make.
Have you managed to track down the Synod, or the Staff of Magnus?
Have you managed to track down the Synod, or the Staff of Magnus?
There were also two items requiring disciplinary action recently.
I don't know. It's like a ward, but who's casting it? Ancano? How?
Please don't bother the Arch-Mage unless it's absolutely necessary.
Now, if you'll please follow me, I'll show you the living quarters.
I'll be fine. I just need a minute to catch my breath. Find Savos...
Well now that's an odd question. Why in the world would you be asking?
No one has seen or heard from them in over a hundred years now, though.
I suppose someone else will need to look after the Apprentices for now.
Ancano is doing something with that thing... the Eye. We can't stop him!
Faralda can teach Destruction spells, and offers training in that school.
Another new student... I'm surprised at how many of you there are lately.
We don't anticipate any real violence, but it never hurts to be prepared.
I warned them it might be dangerous. It's not my fault they didn't listen.
You might be able to learn something from it. Keep looking, but be careful.
They're a very old order. Pre-dated the Imperial Mages Guild by quite a bit.
Initially you'll be learning from Tolfdir, one of our most esteemed Wizards.
I need you to find the Arch-Mage, and I need you to do it quickly. Get moving!
The College of Winterhold has been a fixture in Skyrim for thousands of years.
Well then "observe" from a greater distance. You're making people uncomfortable.
Sir, the Imperial Legion has again requested assistance. What shall I tell them?
The Arcanaeum is located above the hall, and the Arch-Mage's quarters above that.
I've had yet another complaint. Please stop barging in on experiments in progress.
They were apparently under the impression we were keeping it in a closet somewhere.
If there are any problems, let me know. It's my job to keep things running smoothly.
It sounded like they were heading there, though they were rather secretive about why.
Hmm. I can't say I've any idea what they were up to. Have you found this observatory?
I have responsibilities to attend to. We'll continue this discussion some other time.
There are no expectations. This College is a place to study and practice magic freely.
It became clear they're trying to hoard powerful artifacts, looking to consolidate power.
I'm not really sure. Made and used by Magnus himself, if you believe those sorts of things.
But let's not jump to any conclusions, or assign it importance beyond what we're certain of.
I can't imagine what that might be, but if it's safe, you might see if they were successful.
Very well. It's not something often discussed, as it might be... misunderstood by the locals.
At least tell me where you're going, so we can send someone after you if you don't come back.
As long as the Synod don't blame us for this mess, I have more pressing matters to deal with.
I haven't seen Savos since the explosion. He must've been blown clear, and he may be injured.
I believe I've heard it said that it's the only thing that could adequately contain his power.
Mine is not a popular position, but someone needs to keep things running smoothly around here.
I'll give you a brief tour, and then we'll get you to your first class. Are you ready to begin?
Phinis is one of the best Conjurers in all of Skyrim, and can help with spells from that school.
I see. Well yes, I suppose I did mention it, though I'm not sure what he expects me to tell you.
I'm sorry, but you've been suspended from the College. I can't be seen talking to you right now.
He told me it was from Labyrinthian, and that I would know what to do with it when the time came.
I only brought it to his attention a few months back when the Synod showed up here looking for it.
Day-to-day operations are my responsibility, which means if you become a problem, you get sent to me.
Tolfdir and I can try and keep this contained. You need to get your hands on the Staff of Magnus. Now.
This is where you'll be staying. This bed and desk are yours. Please try and be considerate of others.
Let's not jump to conclusions. We'll do all the research we need to figure out what we're dealing with.
I've got to stay here, to see if we can get to Ancano and stop this. I need someone I can trust to do it.
Take it, and get out of here. Bring back that staff before Ancano brings the whole College down around us.
If you can find him and focus his attention, there's a great deal Drevis can teach you about Illusion magic.
Yes, from what I've heard I expect Tolfdir will be occupied for some time. Hmm, that is a problem, isn't it?
Also, I think you should take this amulet. It belonged to Savos, but I think would do you the most good now.
You are a guest of the College, here at the pleasure of the Arch-Mage. I hope you appreciate the opportunity.
Like it or not, this College is a direct influence on the public's impressions of magic and those that use it.
The only time I've heard it mentioned was when those Synod characters showed up some months ago looking for it.
Mages based out of Cyrodiil. They fancy themselves the Imperial Authority on magic these last few hundred years.
Now, let's go back to the Hall of the Elements, where most of the members gather for lectures and study sessions.
Unfortunately, we've had to implement more stringent entry procedures, due to some problems with the local Nords.
I can appreciate that this... thing, this Orb... It's very impressive. Very unique, and definitely worth studying.
Well, I do recall warning them it might not be the safest of locations. I hope this doesn't become a problem for us.
Arch-Mage, the Synod have contacted us again, asking if we've any word on their researchers. What shall I tell them?
You've been suspended from the College for what you did. You'd better talk to Tolfdir if you want to get it sorted out.
The Augur is down in the Midden, below the College. I don't recommend going down there unless it's absolutely necessary.
Well, it's said to be very powerful. Has the capacity to store an incredible amount of magical power, as the story goes.
It is up to each of us to present ourselves publicly as maturely as possible, and to remain above petty local squabbles.
I think... I think he meant this for you, then. I'm not sure why, but there was something very personal about it for him.
You could get some useful information about whatever they were up to. Sounds like it won't be of use to them now, anyway.
The Synod seemed convinced it was somewhere in Skyrim. They inquired about the ruins of Mzulft, but that's all I remember.
Check with Urag gro-Shub in the Arcanaeum. See if he's aware of any references to this... thing that you and Tolfdir found.
I don't see the Arch-Mage. He must've been blown clear. I need you to find him immediately, all right? Make sure he's safe.
Now I'll show you to your quarters. You're going to be sharing space with your fellow Apprentices, who you'll meet shortly.
I'll ask that you please keep your voice down while inside, as others may be working on research or... delicate experiments.
Hopefully any discoveries made in your pursuits will be shared with the members of the College first. That way we all benefit.
You may be used to the Empire bowing to your every whim, but I'm afraid you'll find the Thalmor receive no such treatment here.
I was quite surprised to find them on our doorstep. They seemed amiable enough, but their line of questioning made me... uneasy.
The Staff is said to be able to absorb great amounts of energy. Perhaps it can interact with the Eye in a way that nothing else can.
And finally, there's Colette. She may be... difficult to get along with, she's very knowledgeable when it comes to Restoration magic.
Tolfdir is likely already addressing the new Apprentices. Go on in, and if you have any problems, let one of our senior members know.
Well, first you'll need these. While you're not required to wear them, you may find them more to your liking than your current clothes.
My understanding is that all they really do is make noise in an attempt to curry favor from the Emperor. Lots of politics, little magic.
The prominent feature here is the Hall of the Elements. It's our primary location for lectures, practice sessions, and general meetings.
If the stories about the staff are true, if it really can absorb a tremendous amount of power, maybe we can break through Ancano's magic.
That they had arrived, asked for information which was provided to them, and went on their way with no suggestion of further interaction.
In fact, the Mages Guild was founded in opposition to the idea of the Psijic Order, that only an elite few should have control over magic.
I suppose if you're intent on looking for the staff, there's a chance they might be in Mzulft yet. Just don't expect them to be cooperative.
While technically in charge of the College, the Arch-Mage's responsibilities often keep him occupied. Thus, I run the day-to-day operations.
I'm dealing with a few new research proposals, evaluating the results of three long-term projects, and keeping an eye on our new Apprentices.
Don't let Tolfdir fool you; he's the pre-eminent scholar on Alteration. One of the best in Tamriel, and always willing to pass on his knowledge.
Why don't you head into town and get a drink at the Frozen Hearth? I hear old Nelacar still has a room there. He knows a lot of stories, that one.
Why don't you head into town and get a drink at the Frozen Hearth? I hear old Nelacar still has a room there. He knows a lot of stories, that one.
But it's more myth than anything at this point. I've no doubt that it actually exists, but no one has seen it in what, decades? Longer? I'm not sure.

Hircine.line: Well met again, Hunter.
You've done well, hunter.
Not a failure, my servant.
And they were no base prey.
You may even be my champion. Perhaps.
They will hunt him while you delay. Your choice.
There are others who would gladly accept my favor.
Your fealty is precious to me. I will make good use of it.
And found my favor. That skin will serve you well, child.
You continue to amuse and impress. Go forth, with my blessing.
I may consider it. But you must first do a service for my glory.
The one who stole it has fled to what he thinks is his sanctuary.
By bringing down my other Hunters, you turned the chase inside out.
Just as a bear climbs a tree to escape the hunt, but only ends up trapping himself.
Seek out this rogue shifter. Tear the skin from his body, and make it an offering to me.
Look more closely at it. My glories shall protect you from all this world's grievances.
I am the spirit of the hunt, just one glimpse of the glorious stalker that your kind calls Hircine.
And skillfully, too. I've been watching you for ages, it seems. You have the makings of a fine hunter.
