There is no retribution in the hunt. It is not vengeance I seek, but the blood course of a living hunt.
Fly, my hunter. There are others who vie for my favor. A bit of competition. Don't dally while the prey flees.

Amaund Motierre.line: And what might that be?
But... we had a deal...
So I'll cut right to the chase.
Now, if, um... you'll get going?
This... this is... an outrage...
Is there... something else you need?
Is there... something else you need?
What is this madness? Rexus, help me!
Ha! Well, I certainly do feel that way.
Why, this is astounding news! Wonderful news!
I suppose you should go to the docks and... swim?
Oh, for the love of Mara, would you please just go?
Very well, then. If you'll just... get going now...
What is it? I said I didn't wish to... be disturbed.
But I digress. Here, take these to your, um... superior.
Oh, wonderful. You don't know how happy I am to hear you say that.
But what I ask of you... it must be done. There is too much at stake.
By the gods. You... you're alive! But I had heard... your Sanctuary...
It's in the Solitude Inlet - that's the channel just north of the docks.
Your payment awaits. And don't worry, I'm not stupid enough to betray you.
I'm sure someone of your disposition will probably even find it enjoyable.
His killing ushered in, shall we say, a necessary change in Imperial policy.
I know! I know! I received the news not moments ago! Ha ha! This is glorious!
Rexus will now give you two items which must be passed along to your superior.
But I digress. Here, take these. They need to be delivered to your, um... superior.
If you do this, if you kill the Emperor... Oh, how the masses will fear and respect you.
You must deliver those items to your superior. And I... I must get out of this foul place.
Ah, but you care little for politics, am I right? You want money! And money you shall have!
You mean, after all that's transpired, the Dark Brotherhood will still... honor the contract?
But I digress. Here, let me give you these. They are to be delivered to your, um... superior.
I performed the Black Sacrament, contacted you people, because I thought you guaranteed discretion.
There are those now who wish for a similar change. I am sorry, but that's all I'm at liberty to say.
You assassins and your riddles. It really does get very annoying, you know. Now if you'll excuse me...
Please, make haste. The Emperor's ship won't stayed moored forever. We won't get a chance like this again.
By the almighty Divines. You've come. You've actually come. This dreadful Black Sacrament thing... it worked.
So much has led to this day. So much planning, and maneuvering. It's as if the very stars have finally aligned.
You think I don't know that? If anyone learned of this meeting, my corpse would swing over Solitude for a decade!
Yes, um.... So it would seem. Well, I won't waste your time. I would like to arrange a contract. Several, actually.
Still... everyone assumes the Dark Brotherhood has been completely destroyed. They may be a bit more lax than usual.
The sealed letter explains everything else, in detail. It should be delivered to your superior. Whomever that may be.
Your payment waits for you at a dead drop. It is inside an urn, in the very chamber where we first met, in Volunruud.
Oh, my furtive friend. When Emperor Titus Mede II lies dead, there will be gold... a fortune in gold. But so much more!
Oh, Rexus is no mere servant. He has been with the Motierre family since I was a child. I trust the man with my life.
The real reason I'm speaking with a cutthroat in the bowels of this detestable crypt. For I seek the assassination of...
But you should know that these killings are but a means to an end. For they pave the way to the most important target.
I daresay, the work I'm offering has more significance than anything your organization has experienced in, well, centuries.
Now please, go. Collect your money, and let us never look upon one another again. Our business, thank the gods, is concluded.
That is correct. What I ask is no small thing, of course. But you represent the Dark Brotherhood. This is... what you do? No?
As I said, I want you to kill several people. You'll find the targets, as well as their manners of elimination, quite varied.
My friend, you may not realize it, but you have served the Empire, indeed all of Tamriel, in ways you cannot possibly imagine.
Oh, I know. I know. But I'm so glad you're here. Please, allow me to state my business. Surely your time is as valuable as my own.
Please! You mustn't think I had anything to do with that! I wanted the Emperor dead! The true Emperor! I still do! It was Maro! He...
As I mentioned, the amulet I gave you is exceptionally valuable, and will certainly cover the costs of all these other unpleasantries.
Ah, yes, I can imagine you'd want to settle that score. Last I heard he was at the Solitude Docks, conducting the Emperor's departure.
Don't you have all the information you need? Please, the Emperor isn't going to kill himself. Hmph. Now wouldn't that be a blessing...
Right, then. You prefer to listen, is that it? Well, you must represent the Dark Brotherhood. I certainly wasn't expecting anyone else.
You must understand. So much has led to this day. So much planning, and maneuvering. Now, it's as if the very stars have finally aligned.
I would like to arrange a contract. Several, actually. I daresay, the most important work your organization has had in, well... centuries.
I would like to arrange a contract. Several, actually. I daresay, the most important work your organization has had in, well... centuries.
If you can get onboard that ship. Kill Titus Mede II, as contracted... I will reveal the location of the dead drop that holds your payment.
It's a shocking request, I know. But it is inside the purview of what you Dark Brotherhood types do. Isn't it? If history is to be believed?
In the year 3E 41, Emperor Pelagius Septim was murdered in the Temple of the One in the Imperial City. Cut down by a Dark Brotherhood assassin.
The sealed letter will explain everything that needs to be done. The amulet is quite valuable - you can use it to pay for any and all expenses.
You must understand. So much has led to this day. So much planning, and maneuvering. Now you're here, as if the very stars have finally aligned.
The Emperor is still in Skyrim, but not for long. He's onboard his ship, the Katariah, moored offshore in the Solitude Inlet. But you must hurry!
This is the true Emperor, not some half-Septim lookalike. He'll be surrounded by elite bodyguards, I'm sure. You'll have your work cut out for you.
It is said that the Dark Brotherhood, in recent years, has been in decline. That you lack the power, wealth, and respect of days past. Is it not so?
The logistics of such an endeavor will be staggering, I know. There will be several minor assassinations necessary. Leading up to the... main target.

Alduin.line: My time has come again.
Ruth wah nivahriin joor!
Maar saraan ko Sovngarde.
Zu'u ni faas gaaffesejul.
But first... dir ko maar.
Geh, Sahloknir, kaali mir.
Faal Kel...?! Nikriinne...
Bahloki nahkip sillesejoor.
Face me, then! Zu'u Alduin!
Hin sil fen nahkip bahloki.
Sahloknir, krii daar joorre.
Alok, Nahagliiv. Tiid boaan.
Alok, Viinturuth. Tiid boaan.
Alok, Vuljotnaak. Tiid boaan.
You are no match for me, joor!
Fen du hin sille ko Sovngarde!
You will pay for your defiance!
Nahagliiv! Ziil gro dovah ulse!
Sahloknir! Ziil gro dovah ulse!
Sahloknir! Ziil gro dovah ulse!
Sahloknir! Ziil gro dovah ulse!
Viinturuth! Ziil gro dovah ulse!
Vuljotnaak! Ziil gro dovah ulse!
Paarthurnax is weak. I am strong.
My teeth to your neck, Dovahkiin!
My teeth to your neck, Dovahkiin!
Nust wo ni qiilaan fen kos duaan.
Dovahkiin, hin kah fen kos bonaar.
You are no match for me, Dovahkiin.
Pahlok joorre! Hin kah fen kos bonaar.
I will feast on your soul in Sovngarde!
I have feasted on many greater than you.
Zu'u Alduin, zok sahrot do naan ko Lein.
Die now and await your fate in Sovngarde!
Meyz mul, Dovahkiin. You have become strong.
Dovahkiin, you call yourself? Arrogant mortal.
To feed my power when I come for you in Sovngarde!
You will die in terror, knowing your final fate...
Ful, losei Dovahkiin? Zu'u koraav nid nol dov do hi.
Aav uv dir. Join me or perish with your mortal friends.
Zu'u lost kriaan hi ont, nu hin sille fen nahkip suleyki.
Dovahkiin, you should not have come here. Nu hin sil dii.
Meyye! Tahrodiis aanne! Him hinde pah liiv! Zu'u hin daan!
You cannot prevail against me. I will outlast you... mortal!
I have already defeated your friends once. Beyn. I do not fear them.
Suleyki mulaag, Paarthurnax. My power has waxed, while yours has waned.
You are persistent, Dovahkiin. Pruzah ol aar. A fine slave you would have made.
You may have picked up the weapons of my ancient foes, but you are not their equal!
Bahloki nahkip sillesejoor. My belly is full of the souls of your fellow mortals, Dovahkiin.
You do not even know our tongue, do you? Such arrogance, to dare take for yourself the name of Dovah.
But I am Al-du-in, Firstborn of Akatosh! Mulaagi zok lot! I cannot be slain here, by you or anyone else!
Nivahriin joorre! What have you done? What twisted Words have you created?! Tahrodiis Paarthurnax! My teeth to his neck!

Arngeir.line: Speak, and then begone.
You both agree to this?
Sky above, Voice within.
Master Borri is waiting.
Are we ready to proceed?
That's it. One more time.
Very well. You may enter.
Master Einarth is waiting.
Use both words, if you can.
You learn quickly. Once more.
Try it again. Breath and focus.
Take your seat and we can begin.
Is this your best effort? Again.
We all wait upon you, Dragonborn.
It seems we may have an agreement.
We are all in your debt, Dragonborn.
But they don't say how he does this.
Before the Blades wiped them all out.
What would the Empire want in return?
We will see if they still remember us.
You are now ready for your last trial.
We will see if you truly have the gift.
But regrets are pointless. Here we are.
You must feel the Word within yourself.
Strike us with the power of your Voice.
Now that that's settled, may we proceed?
Your training proceeds well, Dragonborn.
Who would like to open the negotiations?
Come, Dragonborn. Attend to Master Borri.
You weren't ready. You still aren't ready.
What further business do you have with us?
Jarl Ulfric, what would you want in return?
These are the terms currently on the table.
You learn quickly, as only a Dragonborn can.
Shout at us, and let us taste of your Voice.
You might be able to trap him there, exactly.
Meyz nu Ysmir, Dovahsebrom. Dahmaan daar rok.
Meyz nu Ysmir, Dovahsebrom. Dahmaan daar rok.
Meyz nu Ysmir, Dovahsebrom. Dahmaan daar rok.
Meyz nu Ysmir, Dovahsebrom. Dahmaan daar rok.
Well done. You have now passed all the trials.
That may be what you are here for, Dragonborn.
Do not be afraid. Your Shout will not harm us.
Use Clear Skies to open the way to Paarthurnax.
Show us, Dragonborn. Let us taste of your Voice.
I am Master Arngeir. I speak for the Greybeards.
Now tell me, Dragonborn, why have you come here?
Come, we will do your next test inside the hall.
Dragonborn. It is you. Welcome to High Hrothgar.
Come inside. The other masters will meet us there.
Why delay? Please, take your seat so we can begin.
Ah! You've retrieved the Horn of Jurgen Windcaller.
Or will your name be a curse to future generations?
Or will you merely fade from history, unremembered?
The Blades have never followed that path, of course.
Strike the targets as they appear. Breath and focus.
You were not invited here. You are not welcome here.
Now let us see if you are willing and able to learn.
Where did you learn of a Shout used to defeat Alduin?
Although I fear the Jarl may not be easy to persuade.
Good. Then you will be ready for whatever lies ahead.
This is how you repay our trust? Paarthurnax's trust?
I hope that we have all come here in the spirit of...
We will now see how you learn a completely new Shout.
No. I cannot teach it to you because I do not know it.
I have given my answer. Go and reconsider your course.
Now we will see how quickly you can master a new Word.
I cannot teach you the Shout, because I do not know it.
He lives in seclusion on the very peak of the mountain.
Does this mean... is it done? Is Alduin truly defeated?
Now we will see how quickly you can master a new Shout.
Follow me. Master Borri will test your Whirlwind Sprint.
We are honored to welcome a Dragonborn to High Hrothgar.
No. Not now. Not until you return to the path of wisdom.
Do you know Dragonsreach? The Jarl of Whiterun's palace?
We are the Greybeards, followers of the Way of the Voice.
Remain true to the Way of the Voice, and you will return.
You had best take advantage of this truce while it lasts.
Were you? The hubris of the Blades truly knows no bounds.
Clear Skies will blow away the mist, but only for a time.
You are Ysmir now, the Dragon of the North, hearken to it.
Where did you learn of that? Who have you been talking to?
Now let us see how quickly you can master your new Thu'um.
Yes, of course! But how to force a dragon to talk to you...
I'm sure Jarl Ulfric does not expect something for nothing.
The decision whether or not to help you is not mine to make.
This is why so few follow it, and why the world is as it is.
But that is for the gods to decide. You have done your part.
Master Borri will teach you "Wuld," which means "Whirlwind."
Have you considered that Alduin was not meant to be defeated?
Have you considered that Alduin was not meant to be defeated?
At last. It is over. Perhaps it was all worth it, in the end.
Master Wulfgar will now gift you with his knowledge of "Dah."
Very few are permitted to study with us here at High Hrothgar.
Shout for us, Dragonborn. Then we may have time for questions.
Even the Greybeards must bend to the winds of change, it seems.
Follow me. I will show you how to open the path to Paarthurnax.
I'm sure General Tullius does not expect something for nothing.
We will do your next test in the courtyard. Follow Master Borri.
There is one possibility. But... no, the Jarl would never agree.
You have made your choice, Dragonborn. You are not welcome here.
Don't worry, though. You will find all three Words in Ustengrav.
You have shown that you are Dragonborn. You have the inborn gift.
If the world is meant to end, so be it. Let it end and be reborn.
If the world is meant to end, so be it. Let it end and be reborn.
Then it is done at last. Perhaps it was all worth it, in the end.
Lingrah krosis saraan Strundu'ul, voth nid balaan klov praan nau.
Lingrah krosis saraan Strundu'ul, voth nid balaan klov praan nau.
Lingrah krosis saraan Strundu'ul, voth nid balaan klov praan nau.
Lingrah krosis saraan Strundu'ul, voth nid balaan klov praan nau.
You learn a new word like a master... you truly do have the gift.
Those who are ignorant find the Way of the Voice easy to ridicule.
A mindless vendetta urged on you by a cabal of Akaviri barbarians!
Now that everyone is here, please take your seats so we can begin.
Approach Master Borri and he will gift you his knowledge of "Nah."
Jarl Vignar, I assume you are familiar with the Dragonborn's plan?
The Dragonrend Shout is evil. Would you cure one evil with another?
Approach Master Borri and he will gift you his knowledge of "Kest."
Approach Master Borri and he will gift you his knowledge of "Wuld."
Now it is up to you to decide what to do with your power and skill.
Long has the Stormcrown languished, with no worthy brow to sit upon.
It is called "Dragonrend", but its Words of Power are unknown to us.
Such blasphemies are the calling of mages, not followers of the Way.
Arngeir. Rek los Dovahkiin, Strundu'ul. Rek fen tinvaak Paarthurnax.
Arngeir. Rok los Dovahkiin, Strundu'ul. Rok fen tinvaak Paarthurnax.
You have completed your training, Dragonborn. We would Speak to you.
Jarl Baalgruf, I assume you are familiar with the Dragonborn's plan?
So... a Dragonborn appears, at this moment in the turning of the age.
The path to Paarthurnax is perilous, not to be embarked upon lightly.
Oh, this was back in the days of Olaf One-Eye, thousands of years ago.
Alduin... we heard the Dragonrend Shout from here... you defeated him?
Now you see why I've warned you against them! Bloodthirsty barbarians!
Use both Fus and Ro. Together, they will make the Shout more powerful.
Keep moving, stay focused on your goal, and you will reach the summit.
Use your Unrelenting Force shout to strike the targets as they appear.
We will perform your next trial in the courtyard. Follow Master Borri.
I see that you have already begun to learn the Whirlwind Sprint Shout.
You show promise. Remain focused, and you will fulfill your potential.
They have always sought to turn the Dragonborn from the path of wisdom.
They may be able to tell you something about the Elder Scroll you seek.
Impressive. Your Thu'um is precise. You show great promise, Dragonborn.
You stand in High Hrothgar, on the slopes of Kynareth's sacred mountain.
Come with me. It is time for us to recognize you formally as Dragonborn.
So... you spoke to Paarthurnax. The dragonblood burns bright within you.
Will you be a hero whose name is remembered in song throughout the ages?
Master Borri will teach you the first Word of a new Shout. Attend to him.
No. Indeed not. But beware that your skill does not outstrip your wisdom.
I see. The dragon will lead you to Alduin, but without the Jarl's help...
Yes, yes. Which is why I allowed this... violation of all our traditions.
But the difficulty remains - how to lure a dragon to Dragonsreach at all?
First, let us see if you truly are Dragonborn. Let us taste of your Voice.
May the strength and truth of your Voice serve you well. Breath and focus.
I can see it in your eyes - you've seen the land of the gods and returned.
Markarth remains allied to the Empire, under the authority of Jarl Igmund.
Paarthurnax is the only one who can answer your questions about Dragonrend.
The Empire will pay appropriate compensation for the massacre at Ivarstead.
The Empire will pay appropriate compensation for the massacre at Riverwood.
You are the only one that has been revealed thus far. That is all I can say.
Whether you are the only Dragonborn of this age... that is not ours to know.
In those days, dragons could still be found among the remote mountain peaks.
I feared as much. I thought it was him we saw flying east after your battle.
The others do not speak. Their Voices are too powerful for you to withstand.
The Empire will pay appropriate compensation for the massacre at Heljarchen.
The Empire will pay appropriate compensation for the massacre at Rorikstead.
The Empire will pay appropriate compensation for the massacre at Stonehills.
Master Wulfgar will demonstrate Whirlwind Sprint. Then it will be your turn.
You should focus on honing your Voice, and soon your path will be made clear.
He is our leader. He surpasses us all in his mastery of the Way of the Voice.
The Empire will pay appropriate compensation for the massacre at Anga's Mill.
The Empire will pay appropriate compensation for the massacre at Karthwasten.
The Empire will pay appropriate compensation for the massacre at Old Hroldan.
Master Einarth will now teach you "Ro," the second Word in Unrelenting Force.
But again, we see that the natural order will be restored, one way or another.
Take your question to the College of Winterhold. They may be able to help you.
You must trust in the rightness of your cause, and the strength of your Voice.
The Empire will pay appropriate compensation for the massacre at Granite Hill.
The Empire will pay appropriate compensation for the massacre at Loreius Farm.
The Empire will pay appropriate compensation for the massacre at Sarethi Farm.
The Empire will pay appropriate compensation for the massacre at Shor's Stone.
When your Voice can open the path, you will know you are ready to speak to him.
That is for you to discover. We can show you the Way, but not your destination.
Did he tell you what you wanted to know? Did he teach you the Dragonrend Shout?
You must find out how he does this, before he regains his strength and returns.
Perhaps this would be a good time to get the Dragonborn's input on this matter.
Imperial troops will withdraw from Winterhold, and Kraldar will return as Jarl.
With all three words together, this Shout is much more powerful. Use it wisely.
You must hear the Word within yourself before you can project it into a Thu'um.
No, no, of course not. Forgive me, Dragonborn. I have been intemperate with you.
Paarthurnax has made the decision to help you. This is the road we have to walk.
As I said, you will know you are ready when your Voice can open the path to him.
This council is unprecedented. We are gathered here at the Dragonborn's request.
The Empire will pay appropriate compensation for the massacre at Half-moon Mill.
The Empire will pay appropriate compensation for the massacre at Heartwood Mill.
The Empire will pay appropriate compensation for the massacre at Kolskeggr Mine.
The Empire will pay appropriate compensation for the massacre at Whistling Mine.
The Stormcloaks will pay appropriate compensation for the massacre at Ivarstead.
The Stormcloaks will pay appropriate compensation for the massacre at Riverwood.
He was a great war leader of the ancient Nords, a master of the Voice, or Tongue.
In order to learn and use this Shout, you will be taking this evil into yourself.
The Stormcloaks will pay appropriate compensation for the massacre at Heljarchen.
The Stormcloaks will pay appropriate compensation for the massacre at Rorikstead.
The Stormcloaks will pay appropriate compensation for the massacre at Stonehills.
The Empire will pay appropriate compensation for the massacre at Barleydark Farm.
Master Borri will teach you "Nah" - "Fury" - the second word of Whirlwind Sprint.
Ulfric will withdraw his forces from Falkreath, allowing Jarl Siddgeir to return.
There is only silence right now. Perhaps later we will hear one of the lost Words.
Five. Our leader, Paarthurnax, lives alone on the peak of the Throat of the World.
We do not regret this loss. Dragonrend holds no place within the Way of the Voice.
But thanks to the Blades, you now have questions that only Paarthurnax can answer.
Its name is no accident. The palace was originally built to hold a captive dragon.
Dragons have always been able to Shout. Language is intrinsic to their very being.
Master Borri has agreed to teach you the first Word of a new Shout. Attend to him.
The Empire will pay appropriate compensation for the massacre at Frost River Farm.
The Stormcloaks will pay appropriate compensation for the massacre at Anga's Mill.
The Stormcloaks will pay appropriate compensation for the massacre at Karthwasten.
The Stormcloaks will pay appropriate compensation for the massacre at Old Hroldan.
Falkreath will be turned over to Ulfric, and Dengeir of Stuhn will return as Jarl.
Perhaps only its creators ever knew it. But I am not the one to speak of it to you.
You'd require the Jarl's cooperation, of course. That might not be easy to come by.
Ah! Dragonsreach! There are many difficulties, but I cannot think of a better plan.
The Stormcloaks will pay appropriate compensation for the massacre at Granite Hill.
The Stormcloaks will pay appropriate compensation for the massacre at Loreius Farm.
The Stormcloaks will pay appropriate compensation for the massacre at Sarethi Farm.
The Stormcloaks will pay appropriate compensation for the massacre at Shor's Stone.
Master Borri will teach you "Kest" - "Tempest"- the third word of Whirlwind Sprint.
Perhaps not. But this Shout was used once before, was it not? And here we are again.
So be it. Tell Ulfric and General Tullius that the Greybeards wish to speak to them.
The old tales say that he can travel into Sovngarde to devour the souls of the dead.
Masters Einarth, Borri, and Wulfgar will each demonstrate a Shout. Make your choice.
The path to Paarthurnax lies through this gate. I will show you how to open the way.
We will do our best to teach you how to use your gift in fulfillment of your destiny.
The Stormcloaks will pay appropriate compensation for the massacre at Half-moon Mill.
The Stormcloaks will pay appropriate compensation for the massacre at Heartwood Mill.
The Stormcloaks will pay appropriate compensation for the massacre at Kolskeggr Mine.
The Stormcloaks will pay appropriate compensation for the massacre at Whistling Mine.
Falkreath will be returned to Imperial control, under the authority of Jarl Siddgeir.
Only Paarthurnax, the master of our order, can answer that question, if he so chooses.
But stopping Alduin is one thing. Helping you wipe out dragons again is quite another.
For most people, long years of training are required to learn even the simplest Shout.
The Stormcloaks will pay appropriate compensation for the massacre at Barleydark Farm.
You are ready to learn the final word of Unrelenting Force, "Dah," which means "Push."
Stand next to me, and prepare your Whirlwind Sprint. Master Borri will give the signal.
The Stormcloaks will pay appropriate compensation for the massacre at Frost River Farm.
Hjaalmarch will be turned over to Ulfric, with Sorli the Builder assuming the Jarlship.
First, Master Wulfgar will teach you the final word of Unrelenting Force. You are ready.
But for you, the dragon speech is in your blood, and you learn it almost without effort.
The Stormcloaks will withdraw from the Rift, allowing Imperial troops unhindered access.
We spoke the traditional words of greeting to a Dragonborn who has accepted our guidance.
Yes. For matters of such gravity, we need to know where you stand. Or who you stand with.
The Stormcloaks will withdraw from the Reach, allowing Jarl Igmund to return to Markarth.
Please. If we have to negotiate the terms of the negotiation, we will never get anywhere.
Stand in the center of the room and prepare yourself to taste the Voice of the Greybeards.
But not Dragonrend. The knowledge of that Shout was lost in the time before history began.
If you wish to stand alone, so be it. If you wish for our help, you'll answer my question.
Jarl Laila Law-Giver will step down, and Maven Black-Briar will become the Jarl of Riften.
Ulfric will allow Imperial troops to enter Winterhold. Korir will replace Kraldar as Jarl.
But in your case, Dragonborn, it is a privilege to guide you towards mastery of your Voice.
The old tales say that he is able to travel into Sovngarde to devour the souls of the dead.
He may be permitted to return at the end of time to fulfill his destiny as the World-Eater.
When you learn a Shout, you take it into your very being. In a sense, you become the Shout.
Riften remains allied to the Stormcloak cause, under the authority of Jarl Laila Law-Giver.
We study the Way of the Voice, according to the teachings of our founder, Jurgen Windcaller.
So be it. If he believes it is necessary for you to learn this... we will bow to his wisdom.
You have the ability, but you need to learn discpline if you wish to fulfill your potential.
What you have already learned in a few days took even the most gifted of us years to achieve.
It was created by those who had lived under the unimaginable cruelty of Alduin's Dragon Cult.
What I want is irrelevant. This Shout was used once before, was it not? And here we are again.
We have nothing to say to each other. Paarthurnax's murder is beyond my powers of forgiveness.
The Empire will withdraw its troops from Winterhold, allowing Jarl Korir to return from exile.
The Stormcloaks will withdraw from Hjaalmarch, and Idgrod Ravencrone will resume the Jarlship.
You have learned so much already, Dragonborn. Growing your gift too quickly would be dangerous.
The Empire will withdraw its troops from the Rift, allowing the return of Jarl Laila Law-Giver.
The Pale will be turned over to the Empire. Brina Merilis will assume the Jarlship in Dawnstar.
The Dragonborn is an exception to all the rules - the Dragon Blood itself is a gift of the gods.
After all, repentance is something you deny to Paarthurnax by meting out your so-called justice.
As part of your initiation, Master Einarth will allow you to tap into his understanding of "Ro."
Jarl Igmund will go into exile, with Thongvor Silver-Blood taking his place as Jarl of Markarth.
Those who overthrew him in ancient times only postponed the day of reckoning, they did not stop it.
Those who overthrew him in ancient times only postponed the day of reckoning, they did not stop it.
He speaks to us only rarely, and never to outsiders. Being allowed to see him is a great privilege.
You were given this gift by the gods for a reason. It is up to you to figure out how best to use it.
You misunderstand our authority. The Greybeards have never involved themselves in political affairs.
Retrieve the Horn of Jurgen Windcaller, our founder, from his tomb in the ancient fane of Ustengrav.
But heed my warning - the Blades may say they serve the Dragonborn, but they do not. They never have.
Perhaps we would have dragon allies against Alduin if the ancient Blades had not acted so arrogantly.
We have felt the whisper of a Word. Give me your map, and I will show you where its echo can be found.
Ah. I sometimes forget you are not versed in the dragon tongue as we are. This is a rough translation:
You did well here today. I don't think the truce will last long, but that will not be on your account.
The Empire will withdraw its troops from the Pale, allowing Jarl Skald the Elder to return from exile.
Ulfric will withdraw his forces from Hjaalmarch, allowing Jarl Idgrod Ravencrone to return to Morthal.
Your destiny requires you to use your Voice - why else would Akatosh have bestowed this power upon you?
I fear this truce will not last. You gave the Empire too much. Ulfric will not let that stand for long.
I know what path you set her on. And now Paarthurnax's wisdom is lost to us, just when we need it most.
I know what path you set him on. And now Paarthurnax's wisdom is lost to us, just when we need it most.
If you remember to use your Voice in service to the purpose of Akatosh, you will remain true to the Way.
Take your seat at the council table and let us see what wisdom we can find among these warriors of Skyrim.
There is indeed much that we know that you do not. That does not mean that you are ready to understand it.
As for where to find it... such blasphemies have always been the stock in trade of the mages of Winterhold.
I will grant you my understanding of Clear Skies. This is your final gift from us, Dragonborn. Use it well.
Ro means "Balance" in the dragon tongue. Combine it with Fus - "Force" - to focus your Thu'um more sharply.
The Empire will withdraw its troops from the Reach, allowing Ulfric's forces unhindered access to Markarth.
Their Voices are too powerful for anyone not trained in the Way to withstand. Even a whisper could kill you.
Here we commune with the voice of the sky, and strive to achieve balance between our inner and outer selves.
But do you have the discipline and temperament to follow the path laid out for you? That remains to be seen.
Begone, before even my philosophy is tested beyond the breaking point. We are men of peace, and you are not.
His noble spirit... the wisdom of ages... his deep understanding of our existence... all gone, and for what?
Imperial forces will withdraw from The Pale, and Jarl Skald the Elder will be allowed to return to Dawnstar.
To get the answers you seek, you must travel to the top of the mountain and speak to our master, Paarthurnax.
Deliver the message to the warring parties. If they will listen, I will do what I can to bring them to terms.
I fear this truce will not last. General Tullius will be looking for any opportunity to regain the advantage.
True mastery of the Voice can only be achieved when your inner spirit is in harmony with your outward actions.
That is commendable. But remember, the Dragon Blood is itself a gift of Akatosh. Do not try to deny that gift.
Stand next to me. Master Borri will open the gate. Use your Whirlwind Sprint to pass through before it closes.
Without training, you have already taken the first steps towards projecting your Voice into a Thu'um, a Shout.
Not hate. Distrust and fear. The Blades have always sought to turn the Dragonborn away from the path of wisdom.
Jurgen Windcaller's mastery of the Voice eventually overcame all opposition, and the Way of the Voice was born.
Perhaps, perhaps not. Dragons are not like normal mortal creatures, and Alduin is unique even among dragonkind.
This is High Hrothgar, our temple, sanctuary, and home. We ask that you respect our solitude while you are here.
By our breath we bestow it now to you in the name of Kyne, in the name of Shor, and in the name of Atmora of Old.
You're learning, Dragonborn. Doing nothing can be the wisest choice, although strangely often the most difficult.
If not for Paarthurnax, Alduin could not have been overthrown. It was he that first taught men to use the Thu'um.
Winterhold will be turned over to the Empire, although the College itself will retain its traditional neutrality.
The same could be said for any of us, could it not? Are you so guiltless as to stand in judgment upon Paarthurnax?
Although this gift has often been misused, the only true use of the Voice is for the worship and glory of the gods.
Only those whose Voice is strong can find the path. Come. We will teach you a Shout to open the way to Paarthurnax.
I should not have agreed to host this council. The Greybeards have no business involving ourselves in such matters.
So, you've done it. The men of violence are gathered here, in these halls whose very stones are dedicated to peace.
Dovahkiin. You have tasted the Voice of the Greybeards, and passed through unscathed. High Hrothgar is open to you.
Stand between us, and prepare yourself. Few can withstand the unbridled Voice of the Greybeards. But you are ready.
Forgive me. I was... intemperate. I allowed my emotions to cloud my judgment. Master Einarth reminded me of my duty.
Their whole lives were consumed with hatred for dragons, and they poured all their anger and hatred into this Shout.
We know what path you've set him on. But he has made a different choice. Paarthurnax is still safe from your malice.
Naal Thu'umu, mu ofan nii nu, Dovahkiin, naal suleyk do Kaan, naal suleyk do Shor, ahrk naal suleyk do Atmorasewuth.
Naal Thu'umu, mu ofan nii nu, Dovahkiin, naal suleyk do Kaan, naal suleyk do Shor, ahrk naal suleyk do Atmorasewuth.
Naal Thu'umu, mu ofan nii nu, Dovahkiin, naal suleyk do Kaan, naal suleyk do Shor, ahrk naal suleyk do Atmorasewuth.
Naal Thu'umu, mu ofan nii nu, Dovahkiin, naal suleyk do Kaan, naal suleyk do Shor, ahrk naal suleyk do Atmorasewuth.
In the contemplation of the sky, Kynareth's domain, and the practice of the Voice, we strive to achieve this balance.
We know what path you've set her on. But she has made a different choice. Paarthurnax is still safe from your malice.
Do not be so sure about that. Beware - the Blades may claim to serve the Dragonborn, but they do not. They never have.
We have never concerned ourselves with the Scrolls. The gods themselves would rightly fear to tamper with such things.
They carried out a genocide of dragons when they first came to Tamriel, and now they will do it again, with your help.
The Voice was a gift of the goddess Kynareth, at the dawn of time. She gave mortals the ability to speak as dragons do.
You are not the first. There have been many of the Dragon Blood since Akatosh first bestowed that gift upon mortalkind.
Peace? I doubt it. They may put their weapons down for a moment, but only to gather strength for the next bloodletting.
He finally came to realize that the gods had punished the Nords for their arrogant and blasphemous misuse of the Voice.
In mythic times, when mortalkind was in great need, the goddess Kynareth granted us the ability to speak as dragons do.
All Shouts are made up of three Words of Power. As you master each Word, your Shout will become progressively stronger.
Remember, the Way of the Voice trains you for more than battle. Indeed, we believe that battle is the least of its uses.
We therefore seek to guide you on the proper use of your gift, which transcends the restrictions which bind other mortals.
We are not warriors. What is overlooked in the Dragonborn is not permitted to any other followers of the Way of the Voice.
Listen to your inner Voice before acting. Do not allow the Blades to bully you into doing something you will later regret.
Yes. But understand - during the days of Alduin's rule, all dragons were his allies. There was nothing else they could be.
Have you learned nothing from us? Would you simply be a tool in the hands of the Blades, to be used for their own purposes?
As for me, I kept from you only what you were not yet ready to know. Are still not ready to know, as your question reveals.
You've shown yourself mighty, both in Voice and deed. In order to defeat Alduin, you've gained mastery of dreadful weapons.
The same words were used to greet the young Talos, when he came to High Hrothgar, before he became the Emperor Tiber Septim.
They are not yet tired of war. Far from it. Do you know the ancient Nord word for war? "Season unending"... so it has proved.
If we accept one gift, how can we deny the other? As Dragonborn, you have received the ability to Shout directly from Akatosh.
So the Blades have survived yet another disaster of their own making, and continue to meddle in things they do not understand.
The Blades! Of course. They specialize in meddling in matters they barely understand. Their reckless arrogance knows no bounds.
Finding these lost Words would be a sufficient test, to temper your abilities with experience. Ask when you are ready to search.
He was the first to understand that the Voice should be used solely for the glory and worship of the gods, not the glory of men.
Ulfric will allow Imperial forces into the Pale. Skald the Elder will go into exile, and Brina Merilis will assume the Jarlship.
Well, that is how the rest of us learn Shouts. As Dragonborn, you can absorb a slain dragon's life force and knowledge directly.
The Blades were born of dragon-hating crusaders. They still carry that legacy although they may not remember from whence it came.
Ah, the prophecy. Be wary of prophecy. Some are meant as a guide. Some as a warning. Are you sure you can tell one from the other?
A few mortals are born with similar abilities -- whether a gift or a curse has been a matter of debate down through the centuries.
When you Shout, you speak in the language of dragons. Thus, your Dragon Blood gives you an inborn ability to learn Words of Power.
But there are many Words of Power in Skyrim, carved in the Dragon tongue. Even from here, we can feel the Thu'um resonate from them.
Dragons have the inborn ability to learn and project their Voice. Dragons also are able to absorb the power of their slain brethren.
We are here to guide you in that pursuit, just as the Greybeards have sought to guide those of the Dragon Blood that came before you.
I ask that you all respect the spirit of High Hrothgar, and do your best to begin the process of achieving a lasting peace in Skyrim.
Do not confuse inaction with indifference. Often the wisest course is to wait and watch events unfold, until the time to act is clear.
Kynareth has placed the Voice of wisdom within you, Dragonborn. All you need do is learn to listen to it, and your path will be clear.
Your quick mastery of a new Thu'um is... astonishing. I'd heard the stories of the abilities of Dragonborn, but to see it for myself...
Markarth will be handed over to Ulfric's forces. Jarl Igmund will step down, and Thongvor Silver-Blood will become the Jarl of Markarth.
No doubt. The appearance of a Dragonborn at this time is not an accident. Your destiny is surely bound up with the return of the dragons.
Dragons are part of the natural order of the world. The Blades thought to change the order to suit themselves, by wiping out the dragons.
Do not let your easy mastery of the Voice tempt you into the arrogance of power that has been the downfall of many Dragonborn before you.
The Empire will allow Ulfric's forces to enter Falkreath. Jarl Siddgeir will step down, and Dengeir of Stuhn will take his place as Jarl.
Riften will be handed over to Imperial control. Jarl Laila Law-Giver will step down, and Maven Black-Briar will become the Jarl of Riften.
After the disaster at Red Mountain, where the Nord army was annihilated, he spent many years pondering the meaning of that terrible defeat.
But learning a Word of Power is only the first step... you must unlock its meaning through constant practice in order to use it in a Shout.
There is no difference in the dragon tongue between debating and fighting. Shouting comes as naturally to a dragon as breathing, or speaking.
I trust that you will make the right choice, in the end. Listen to the Voice that Kynareth has placed within you, and your path will be clear.
The Empire will allow Ulfric's forces to enter Hjaalmarch. Idgrod Ravencrone will go into exile, with Sorli the Builder replacing her as Jarl.
Some believe that Dragonborn are sent into the world by the gods, at times of great need. We will speak more of that later, when you are ready.
Let the Way of the Voice be your guide, and the path of wisdom will be clear to you. Breath and focus, Dragonborn. Your future lies before you.

Gormlaith.line: I serve the Dragonborn.
I vowed to have your head.
Down with you, damned worm!
No escape this time, foul worm!
Clear Skies - combine our Shouts!
Hakon! A glorious day, is it not!
My sword will taste of your blood!
If I die today, it will not be in terror.
Know that Gormlaith sent you down to death!
We shall see soon enough. Alduin approaches!
For Skyrim! For Shor! For Sovngarde's freedom!
You worry too much, brother. Victory will be ours.
You cannot defeat us! The Dragonborn still stands!
All hail the Dragonborn! Hail her with great praise!
All hail the Dragonborn! Hail him with great praise!
Use Dragonrend, brothers! Bring him to swords-reach!
Let those that watch from Sovngarde envy us this day!
If it is our lot to end, we'll live in song if not in soul.
You feel fear for the first time, worm. I see it in your eyes.
The endless wait gives way to battle! Alduin's doom, his death or ours!
Stand fast! His strength is failing! Once more, and his might will be broken!
We've bloodied him well. Four of his kin have fallen to my blade alone this day.
They did not have Dragonrend. Once we bring him down, I promise I will have his head.
To battle, my friends! The fields will echo with the clamor of war, our wills undaunted.
At Sovngarde's gates I'll meet my fate with mirthful heart, and know I fell in noblest cause.
Even here, where heroes throng, few can match this mighty deed. What glory! The gods themselves must envy us this well-earned honor!
At long last! Alduin's doom is now ours to seal - just speak the word and with high hearts we'll hasten forth to smite the worm wherever he lurks.

Dremora.line: A challenger is near...
No... not by a mortal...
Another who seeks death.
There you are, weakling!
You meet your end, mortal.
No, I... I submit. No more.
I am not yours to toy with!
Have it your own way, meat.
I will feast on your heart.
You return, foolish mortal?
You control nothing, mortal!
There could be no other end.
It cannot... end like this...
Hiding will not save you now.
I am not your plaything, worm!
I'll tear out your heart first!
Are you prepared for your death?
See you on the other side, mortal.
I honor my lord by destroying you!
Where am I? You! Out with it, then.
I... I submit. What is your bidding, master.
Clever try, scab, but it won't be that easy!
You dare bring me here? You must be punished.
Where I belong is back aboard my ship, on the Abecean.
Release me, and I'll share a bit of my wealth with you.
Yes, my lord. Summon me again, and I shall have your stone.
Yes, my mistress. Summon me again, and I shall have your stone.
Your sigil stone. Lord Dagon is... less than pleased at its loss.
Their souls are trapped in Oblivion all the same, and here I stay.
You've no hope of defeating me, pup. Don't bother. Why not broker a bargain, instead?
There's a smart lad. Take the map. The loot's hidden there. It'll show itself when you get near.
There's a smart lass. Check the map. The loot's hidden there. It'll show itself when you get near.
Ah, I was beginning to think the day would never come. So you broke the binding spell, did you? Matters little.
Names have power, lad. You should know by now. Just say "Velehk Sain, I release you.", and "Poof!", I'm on my way.
Names have power, lass. You should know by now. Just say "Velehk Sain, I release you.", and "Poof!", I'm on my way.
Those kids are dead anyway. Killing me won't bring them back. I, on the other hand, have done quite a bit with my time on Mundus.
Years back, I had to ferret away some booty nearby. We'd sunk an Imperial Galleon off the coast. Good plunder, but we needed to travel light.
We stashed what we couldn't carry, and I hid it with a trick I know. I can point you in the right direction, and we'll each go our separate ways.

Dragon Priest.line: Zu'u uth nall thurri dein daar miiraak.
Fools! You will not hold me here any longer, Valdar!

Paarthurnax.line: Drem Yol Lok. Greetings.
What would you ask of me?
Use Dragonrend, Dovahkiin!
Use Dragonrend, Dovahkiin!
Unslaad hokoron! Never again!
The Word calls you. Go to it.
Hurt him while he is grounded!
Indeed.  Alduin wahlaan daanii.
Ah. I have expected you. Prodah.
But why? Why must you stop Alduin?
I have spoken. The Rotmulaag waits.
Lost funt. You are too late, Alduin!
Now, Dovahkiin! Now he is vulnerable!
You are likely better informed than I.
Drem. Patience. I am answering, in my way.
Dovahkiin! Use Dragonrend, if you know it!
By long tradition, the elder speaks first.
Why do you delay? Are you Dovahkiin or not?
Why do you intrude here, if not for tinvaak?
Perhaps you begin to see the world as a dovah.
Hahaha! You have much to learn of the dov, then.
You once told me you did not believe in destiny.
Which calls to you, Dovahkiin? Fus, Feim, or Yol?
A fine place to trap one of Alduin's allies, hmm?
Krosis. Sorrowfully, no. It cannot be known to me.
You see - Paar-thur-nax - a Thu'um - a Shout, yes?
Goraan! I feel younger than I have in many an age.
Yes. Vahzah. You speak true, Dovahkiin. Forgive me.
Use Dragonrend! It is the only chance to defeat him!
Use Dragonrend! It is the only chance to defeat him!
You could learn Dragonrend from those who created it.
Drem Yol Lok. Greetings, wunduniik. I am Paarthurnax.
A gift, Dovahkiin. Shul. Understand Sun as the dov do.
A gift, Dovahkiin. Yol. Understand Fire as the dov do.
This should shake the loyalty of the dov who serve him.
The hofkahsejun has been known as Dragonsreach ever since.
A gift, Dovahkiin. Toor. Understand Inferno as the dov do.
Hmm. Yes. They are very protective of me. Bahlaan fahdonne.
I am as my father Akatosh made me. As are you... Dovahkiin.
Who are you? What brings you to my strunmah... my mountain?
Perhaps. At least it will continue to exist. Grik los lein.
This is your chance, Dovahkiin! Strike with all your force!
But this is only a small part of their power. Zofaas suleyk.
And so, perhaps, your destiny will be fulfilled. Who can say?
Knowing a Word of Power is to take its meaning into yourself.
Do not delay. Alduin will be coming. He cannot miss the signs.
Our hadrimme, our minds cannot even... comprehend its concepts.
Do not be afraid. Faasnu. Let me feel the power of your Thu'um.
Dovahkiin, vosaraan! Use Dragonrend before Alduin consumes you!
Dovahkiin, vosaraan! Use Dragonrend before Alduin consumes you!
He is too strong on the wing! Bring him to gol with Dragonrend!
He is too strong on the wing! Bring him to gol with Dragonrend!
Alduin believes that he will prevail, with good reason. Rok mul.
Now, show me what you can do. Greet me not as elf, but as dovah!
Now, show me what you can do. Greet me not as orc, but as dovah!
But willing or no, they will hear it! Fare thee well, Dovahkiin!
Go then. Fulfill your destiny. Take the Scroll to the Time-Wound.
Come, Dovahkiin. Nin Yol. Strike me with the fire of your Thu'um.
Nid. If you wish me to answer your question, you must answer mine.
Trust your instincts, Dovahkiin. Your blood will show you the way.
Dahmaan - remember, Alduin also follows his destiny, as he sees it.
Now, show me what you can do. Greet me not as mortal, but as dovah!
Now, show me what you can do. Greet me not as khajiit, but as dovah!
If you can see your destiny clearly, your sight is clearer than mine.
Now, show me what you can do. Greet me not as Argonian, but as dovah!
Hear my Thu'um! Feel it in your bones. Match it, if you are Dovahkiin!
True... But qostiid - prophecy - tells what may be, not what should be.
They see me as master. Wuth. Onik. Old and wise. It is true I am old...
Vahrukt unslaad... perhaps none but me now remember how he was defeated.
But I do not know the Thu'um you seek. Krosis. It cannot be known to me.
You have not answered my question. Why do you need to learn this Thu'um?
He will not resist the challenge of your Voice, Dovahkiin. He will come.
Hmm, yes. I have been pondering on exactly that question. Lingrah morah.
But, I do not know the Thu'um you seek. Krosis. It cannot be known to me.
Evenaar bahlok. There are many hungers it is better to deny than to feed.
You are bold, wunduniik. You dare enter a Dovah's home without permission.
Some would say that all things must end, so that the next can come to pass.
Tell me. Why do you come here, volaan? Why do you intrude on my meditation?
Not intentionally. Some hoped he would be gone forever, forever lost. Meyye.
You will see them... wuth fadonne... my friends - Hakon, Gormlaith, Felldir.
Then you will feel - know - Dragonrend, in the power of its first expression.
And so you fulfilled your destiny, which you once said you did not believe in.
I am glad you believe that. At least it will continue to exist. Grik los lein.
I knew better. Tiid bo amativ. Time flows ever onward. One day he would surface.
But they did not trust us. Ni ov. Their inner councils were kept hidden from us.
Dez motmahus. Even to the dov, who ride the currents of Time, destiny is elusive.
Alduin komeyt tiid. What else would you seek? Alduin and Dovahkiin return together.
The Blades are wise not to trust me. Onikaan ni ov. I would not trust another dovah.
Qostiid sahlo aak. Just because you can do a thing, does not always mean you should.
Pruzah. As good a reason as any. There are many who feel as you do, although not all.
Of course. Alduin wahlaan daanii. I would not have helped you if I thought otherwise.
Your kind - joorre - mortals - created it as a weapon against the dov... the dragons.
I have taught the Way of the Voice for centuries and the Thu'um since long before that.
What is better - to be born good, or to overcome your evil nature through great effort?
Tiid krent. Time was... shattered here because of what the ancient Nords did to Alduin.
Ro fus... maybe you only balance the forces that work to quicken the end of this world.
Krosis. No. I know little of what has passed below in the long years I have lived here.
Dreh ni nahkip. Discipline against the lesser aids in qahnaar... denial of the greater.
Drem. All in good time. First, a question for you. Why do you want to learn this Thu'um?
You have it. The Kel - the Elder Scroll. Tiid kreh... qalos. Time shudders at its touch.
I have tasted the voices of Alduin's allies on the wind. Pogaan nahlaas, vokrii wah jun.
I have overcome my nature only through meditation and long study of the Way of the Voice.
Hmm, yes. But your su'um is strong. I do not doubt that you can convince him of the need.
Hmm. How to explain in your tongue? The dov have words for such things that joorre do not.
Not until introductions are complete. Zin. Heyv. Test your Thu'um against mine, Dovahkiin!
Even we who ride the currents of Time cannot see past Time's end. Wuldsetiid los tahrodiis.
Indeed, you saw more clearly than I - certainly more clearly than Alduin. Rok funta koraav.
It is called "Force" in your tongue. But as you push the world, so does the world push back.
But Odahviing, he is... headstrong? Boziik. Rash. Even among the dov, he was known for this.
There is one who I remember well. Odahviing. He is the one to tell us where Alduin has gone.
If you brought that Kel, that Elder Scroll back here... to the Tiid-Ahraan, the Time-Wound...
Do you know why I live here, at the peak of the Monahven - what you name Throat of the World?
I meditate on the Rotmulaag - the Words of Power. I counsel in their use. It is enough for me.
It was the Kel - the Elder Scroll. They used it to... cast him adrift on the currents of Time.
No day goes by where I am not tempted to return to my inborn nature. Zin krif horvut se suleyk.
In your tongue, the Word simply means "Fire." It is change given form. Power at its most primal.
This is the most sacred mountain in Skyrim. Zok revak strunmah. The great mountain of the world.
Return it here, to the Tiid-Ahraan. Then... Kelle vomindok. Nothing is certain with such things...
But you have indulged my weakness for speech long enough. Krosis. Now I will answer your question.
Vomindok. I do not know. Perhaps in the very doing they erased the knowing of it from Time itself.
Those who try to hasten the end, may delay it. Those who work to delay the end, may bring it closer.
But I forget myself. Krosis. So los mid fahdon. Melancholy is an easy trap for a dovah to fall into.
Ponder the meaning of spirit. Unslaad zii. Where mortal flesh may wither and die, the spirit endures.
Drem. Patience. There are formalities which must be observed, at the first meeting of two of the dov.
Savor your triumph, Dovahkiin. This is not the last of what you will write upon the currents of Time.
Perhaps now you have some insight into the forces that shape the vennesetiid... the currents of Time.
I was far from here on the day of Alduin's downfall. But all dov felt the... sundering of Time itself.
Now - hear his name. Odahviing. Taste it on the wind. Od-ah-viing. Know it in your su'um. Od-ah-viing!
Alduin always was pahlok - arrogant in his power. Uznahgar paar. He took domination as his birthright.
But, I bow before your certainty. In a way I envy you. The curse of much knowledge is often indecision.
You would not come all this way for tinvaak with an old dovah. No. You seek your weapon against Alduin.
I do not know how he came to be caught. But the bronjun... the Jarl... was very proud of his pet. Paak!
Lot krongrah. You truly have the Voice of a dovah. Alduin's allies will think twice after this victory.
Think of the way force may be applied effortlessly. Imagine but a whisper pushing aside all in its path.
True. But few now remember that this was the very spot where Alduin was defeated by the ancient Tongues.
Do you have no better reason for acting than destiny? Are you nothing but a plaything of dez... of fate?
So, it is done. Alduin dilon. The Eldest is no more, he who came before all others, and has always been.
Contemplate the meaning of a Rotmulaag. You will become closer to that Word, as it fills your inner self.
It has been long since I held tinvaak with a stranger. I gave in to the temptation to prolong our speech.
Here the ancient Tongues, the first mortal masters of the Voice, brought Alduin to battle and defeated him.
I can be trusted. I know this. But they do not. Onikaan ni ov dovah. It is always wise to mistrust a dovah.
The first mortals that I taught the Thu'um - the first Tongues. The leaders of the rebellion against Alduin.
And he is no fool. Ni mey, rinik gut nol. Far from it. He began as the wisest and most far-seeing of us all.
There is no question. You are doom-driven. Kogaan Akatosh. The very bones of the earth are at your disposal.
Do not delay. Alduin must feel the tremors in Time by the Scroll's presence here. He will know what it means.
That is "Fus." Let its meaning fill you. Su'um ahrk morah. You will push the world harder than it pushes back.
I cannot tell you in detail. I never heard it used. Kogaan. It was the first Thu'um created solely by mortals.
Ah, I forget how little you know of the dov. Our names are always made up of three Rotmulaag - Words of Power.
But no, Dovahkiin. Others do not come here to train anymore. Saraan. You are the first in over a hundred years.
There is no distinction between debate and combat to a dragon. Tinvaak los grah. For us it is one and the same.
The Nords of those days used the Dragonrend Shout to cripple Alduin. But this was not enough. Ok mulaag unslaad.
There is nothing else but philosophy to a dovah. It is no accident that we do battle with our Thu'um, our Voices.
Yes. Alduin... zeymah. The elder brother. Gifted, grasping and troublesome as is so often the case with firstborn.
So. You have made your way here, to me. No easy task for a joor... mortal. Even for one of Dovah Sos. Dragonblood.
Perhaps this world is simply the Egg of the next kalpa? Lein vokiin? Would you stop the next world from being born?
Perhaps the hofkahsejun - the palace in Whiterun... Dragonsreach. It was originally built to house a captive dovah.
His doom was written when he claimed for himself the lordship that properly belongs to Bormahu - our father Akatosh.
Yes. There were a few of us that rebelled against Alduin's thur... his tyranny. We aided the humans in his overthrow.
But I cannot celebrate his fall. Zu'u tiiraaz ahst ok mah. He was my brother once. This world will never be the same.
Dov wahlaan fah rel. We were made to dominate. The will to power is in our blood. You feel it in yourself, do you not?
Happy? No, I am not happy. Zeymahi lost ont du'ul Bormahu. Alduin was once the crown of our father Akatosh's creation.
Which is why I have lived here. For thousands of mortal years I have waited. I knew where he would emerge but not when.
That is "Feim." Let that meaning fill you. Su'um ahrk morah. You will find that your spirit will give you more strength.
Will I teach you, Dovahkiin? What Word calls you to deeper understanding? There are three to master: Fus, Feim, and Yol.
The dov are children of Akatosh. Thus we are specially... attuned to the flow of Time. Perhaps also uniquely vulnerable.
I warned them against such a rash action. Even I could not foresee its consequences. Nust ni hon. They would not listen.
Yes... one of his allies could tell us. Motmahus... But it will not be so easy to... convince one of them to betray him.
Think of this as the fire builds in your su'um, in your breath. Su'um ahrk morah. What will you burn? What will you spare?
But I believe the Scroll's bond with the Tiid-Ahraan will allow you a... a seeing, a vision of the moment of its creation.
You did what was necessary. Alduin had flown far from the path of right action in his pahlok - the arrogance of his power.
That is the true meaning of "Yol." Suleyk. Power. You have it, as do all dov. But power is inert without action and choice.
With the Elder Scroll that was used to break Time, you may be able to... cast yourself back. To the other end of the break.
And, as you told me once, the next world will have to take care of itself. Ful nii los. Even I cannot see past Time's ending.
Even I cannot see past Time's ending to what comes next. Niid koraav zeim dinoksetiid. We must do the best we can with this world.
He is not compelled to come, but the dov are prideful by nature. Few could resist such a challenge. Especially from you, Dovahkiin.
The Kelle... Elder Scrolls, as you name them, they have often been used for prophecy. Yes, your prophecy comes from an Elder Scroll.
Yes and no. Viik nuz ni kron. Alduin was not truly defeated, either. If he was, you would not be here today, seeking to... defeat him.
You have won a mighty victory. Sahrot krongrah - one that will echo through all the ages of this world for those who have eyes to see.
Yes. This was ages ago, you understand. There were more of us then. Before the bruniikke - the Akaviri - came and killed all my zeymah.
I used to visit him from time to time. Nearly crazed by loneliness and captivity. Tiiraz sivaas. He did not even remember his own name.
It was said to force a dragon to experience the concept of Mortality. A truly vonmindoraan... incomprehensible idea to the immortal dov.
Aaah... yes! Sossedov los mul. The dragonblood runs strong in you. It is long since I had the pleasure of speech with one of my own kind.
They were mighty, in their day. Even to attempt to defeat Alduin...  sahrot hunne. The Nords have had many heroes since, but none greater.
Many of the dovahhe are now scattered across Keizaal. Without Alduin's lordship, they may yet bow to the vahzen... rightness of my Thu'um.
Fade in your tongue. Mortals have greater affinity for this Word than the dov. Everything mortal fades away in time, but the spirit remains.
It is an... artifact from outside time. It does not exist, but it has always existed. Rah wahlaan. They are...hmm... fragments of creation.
Ni liivrah hin moro. True, this is not the final krongrah - victory. But not even the heroes of old were able to defeat Alduin in open battle.
Odahviing will come to your challenge. Boziik. Krilot. He will not be able to resist your call, from the Hill of the Dovah itself... he will come.

Sheogorath.line: More tea, Pelly my dear?
Actually, you do. Sort of.
Honestly. Have you any idea?
Oh. Is it your... first time?
...but only during... the day?
Actually... I do. Little joke.
Wrong on all accounts, aren't I?
But seriously. What's the message?
Now you. You can call me Ann Marie.
Inside the mind of Pelagius, silly.
Wait! Don't tell me! I want to guess!
So congratulations! You're free to go!
Oho! I thought you'd never figure it out.
Luggage! Now where did I leave my luggage?
But someone still has quite a bit to do. Hmmm?
But the deepest, keenest hatred was for himself.
But you're not done yet. And you could still die...
Well? Spit it out, mortal. I haven't got an eternity!
Sheogorath, Daedric Prince of Madness. At your service.
You have to find the way out first. Good luck with that.
You... didn't mean... yourself. You meant... Pelagius...
The Wabbajack! Huh? Huh? Didn't see that coming, did you?
I... have been known to change my mind. So... go. Really.
Is it just me or is it getting saner and saner these days?
Word of advice if you ride a Narwhale. Mind the pointy end.
Honestly, I can't keep up. But by all means, don't stop now.
Conquering paranoia should be a snap after that ordeal, hmm?
But you're not out of the woods yet! Get it? Out of the woods?
Still can't wake him up? Perhaps you're barking up the wrong tree?
Because that's... crazy. You do realize who you're dealing with here?
Ha! No matter! Honestly, I don't want to know. Why ruin the surprise?
If not... Then call me Sheogorath, Daedric Prince of Madness. Charmed.
How rude! Can't be bothered to host an old friend for a decade or two.
Oh! That's right! Pelagius! Yes, yes, now I recall. Well done, well done.
I see you've been minding your own business. So how is the mind business?
Yes, yes, that's quite enough celebration. Let's send you ahead, shall we?
Don't get too comfortable here, mortal. You may actually earn your way out.
Do you mind? I'm busy doing the fishstick. It's a very delicate state of mind!
The butler did it! Or is it the advisor? Whoever that man behind the throne was.
Let's make sure I'm not forgetting anything. Clothes? Check. Beard? Check! Luggage?
Yes, yes, you're entirely brilliant. Conquering madness and all that. Blah blah blah.
Um... We're not talking about Barbas, are we? Clavicus Vile's... dog? Oohh... awkward.
I have a saying - I'm not going to feel what you have to like. Or something like that.
Pelagius stopped by and mentioned he hated you. Or I mentioned he hated you and he agreed.
Well! Same congratulations apply! Just, you know, for what you did for the insane emperor.
Wonderfully done. Pelagius is finally ready to love himself... and continue hating everyone else.
Hmm... Your creature doesn't appear to be fairing any better than before. Methinks your aim is off.
Oh... lovely. Now all my dear Pelagius has to worry about are the several hundred legitimate threats...
I hope you're having a good time. Actually, I hope I'm having a good time. I could care less about you.
He would lie in bed, paralyzed with fear, imagining his own nightmares coming to life. Ooohhh... Scary.
Ha! I do love it when the mortals know they're being manipulated. Makes things infinitely more interesting.
But only if you're partial to being flayed alive and having an angry immortal skip rope with your entrails.
Ah, wonderful, wonderful! Why waste all that hatred on yourself when it can so easily be directed at others!
The objective here is simple, you simpleton!  Use your Wabbajack to defeat the enemy, while they do the same!
You've headed down the path of dreams. Unfortunately for you, Pelagius suffered night terrors from a young age.
Now that's the real question, isn't it? Because honestly, how much time off could a demented Daedra really need?
Pelagius Septim the Third, once the Mad Emperor of Tamriel, now so boringly sane. I always knew he had it in him!
Oh, pardon me. Were you saying something? I do apologize, it's just that I find myself suddenly and irrevocably...
Ah, now this is a sad path. Pelagius hated and feared many things. Assassins, wild dogs, the undead, pumpernickel...
But more to the point. Do you - tiny, puny, expendable little mortal - actually think you can convince me to leave?
That woman wielded fear like a cleaver. Or did she wield a cleaver and make people afraid? I never get that part right...
Ah! Good, good! No more barking at all hours, and chewing up my slippers. You used the hemlock, then? Damned good idea! I...
Ah, so now my dear Pelagius can hate himself for being legitimately afraid of things that actually threaten his existence...
All you need to do is find something to wake our poor Pelagius up. You'll find his terrors easy to repel... but persistent.
The attacks he makes on himself can be seen here fully. They are always carried out on the weakest part of his fragile self.
Well, I suppose it's back to the Shivering Isles. The trouble Haskill can get into while I'm gone simply boggles the mind...
And as for you, my little mortal minion... Feel free to keep the Wabbajack. As a symbol of my... Oh, just take the damn thing.
Well now, that's something to crow about. With Pelagius up and about you're moving right along. We'll both be home in no time.
Ah, but there I go, waxing poetic about my misspent youth. Now where were we? Ah yes. You're the mortal messenger. An I am...?
Oh, good choice. Well, good for me. I find everyone being out to get you so terribly entertaining. You might find it... less so.
You seem to be having a small problem... or perhaps it's a big problem? Maybe if you shrunk the whole thing down a little first?
Oh, I am so happy for you! My, what a burden to have carried. But you've done it! You've conquered your own inner demons! Bravo!
Hafrumph! Well then, if you're going to be like that... Perhaps it's best I take my leave. A good day to you sir. I said good day!
You take care of yourself, now. And if you ever find yourself in New Sheoth, do look me up. We can share a strawberry torte. Ta ta!
The self-loathing enhances Pelagius' anger! Ah, but his confidence will shrink with every hit. You must bring the two into balance.
Ah, but no matter. Heartless mortal that you are, you've actually succeeded and survived. I am forced to honor my end of the bargain.
Care to take a look around? This is not, I dare say, the Solitude botanical gardens. Have you any idea where you are? Where you truly are?
So here's what I'm going to do. I'm going to leave. That's right. I'm done. Holiday... complete. Time to return to the hum drum day-to-day.
But only half right. I'm a mad god. The Mad God, actually. It's a family title. Gets passed down from me to myself every few thousand years.
Oh, this is going to be fun. You've got to use your Wabbajack to defeat the enemy while - get this - they use their Wabbajack to defeat you!
Welcome to the deceptively verdant mind of the Emperor Pelagius III. That's right! You're in the head of a dead, homicidally insane monarch.
You are the best Septim that's ever ruled. Well, except for that Martin fellow, but he turned into a dragon god, and that's hardly sporting...
Oh, no no no! No mistake at all. What you made was a choice. Granted, not a very wise choice, but these things happen. Ah, the folly of youth...
Oh, but she taught her son well. Pelagius learned at a very early age that danger could come from anywhere. At any time. Delivered... by anyone.
You know, I was there for that whole sordid affair. Marvelous time! Butterflies, blood, a Fox, a severed head... Oh, and the cheese! To die for.
Now, I know what you're thinking. Can I still rely on my swords and spells and sneaking and all that nonsense? Sure, sure. Or... you could use...
You know, you remind me of myself at a young age. All I cared about was riding narwhales and sleeping in honeycombs and drinking babies' tears...
Now surely even you know about Pelagius' decree? On his deathbed - oh, and this was inspired - he forbade... death! That's right! Death! Outlawed!
I mean, really. Here you stand, before Sheogorath himself, Daedric Prince of Madness, and all you deem fit to do is... deliver a message? How sad.
With the threat gone Pelagius is under the delusion that he is safe, which means you've helped him... sort of. And we're that much closer to home.
Hmmmm... "Fixed" is such a subjective term. I think "treated" is far more appropriate, don't you? Like one does to a rash, or an arrow in the face.
You are far too hard on yourself, my dear, sweet, homicidally insane Pelagius. What would the people do without you? Dance? Sing? Smile? Grow old?
Ooh, ooh, what kind of message? A song? A summons? Wait, I know! A death threat written on the back of an Argonian concubine! Those are my favorite.
Was it Molag? No, no... Little Tim, the toymaker's son? The ghost of King Lysandus? Or was it... Yes! Stanley, that talking grapefruit from Passwall.
I am a part of you, little mortal. I am a shadow in your subconscious, a blemish on your fragile little psyche. You know me. You just don't know it.
You see, Pelagius' mother was... well... let us say "unique." Although, I suppose in the grand scheme of things, she was fairly average for a Septim.

Hakon.line: Feel the bite of my axe!
It worked... you did it...
My debt is repaid at last.
What is your need, Dragonborn?
Your doom has come, damned worm!
Felldir! We agreed not to use it!
I shall sing of thee in Sovngarde!
We must clear this confounding mist.
Nowhere to hide! Hunter becomes our prey!
It's no use! Use the Scroll, Felldir! Now!
We're ready, Dragonborn, our wrath to unleash.
We must Shout together these shadows to dispel.
It is glorious to battle once again in Tamriel!
Gormlaith! We're running out of time! The battle...
All hail the Dragonborn! Hail her with great praise!
All hail the Dragonborn! Hail him with great praise!
No. We will deal with Alduin ourselves, here and now.
Have you no thought beyond the blooding of your blade?
Does his strength have no end? Is our struggle in vain?
I may fall to your malice, but your fate is already written.
Stand fast! The fell worm's death is ours at last, the light returns!
Felldir speaks wisdom - the World-Eater, coward, fears you, Dragonborn.
Why does Alduin hang back? We've staked everything on this plan of yours, old man.
For a hundred lifetimes my heart has burned for revenge on Alduin too long delayed.
Alduin will hide in this lightless mist until we Shout together, and shatter his power.
The battle below goes ill. If Alduin does not rise to our challenge, I fear all may be lost.
Through fire and time, the terror has lived. No more, I say! I've set my heart on terrible deeds, death's grim art.
We must drive away his mist, Shouting together, and then unsheathe our blades in desperate battle with our black-winged foe.
I'll look for you, friend, when at last you return to Shor's hall. The honor will be ours when you rejoin the ranks of Sovngarde.

Draugr.line: Ha ha ha ha ha ha ha ha!

Meridia.line: You have found my beacon.
A new supplicant approaches.
A new hand touches the beacon.
Fool! He is perverting my Light.
Return my beacon to Mount Kilkreath.
Of course you will. I have commanded it!
My beacon is sure to attract a worthy soul.
Take my beacon to the mount above Kilkreath.
But first, you must restore to me my beacon.
May the light of certitude guide your efforts.
But if you are wise, you will heed my bidding.
It is time for my splendor to return to Skyrim.
If they love me not, how can my love reach them?
Can you not hear me? Are your ears full of hate?
A new day is dawning. And you shall be its herald.
A Light that could illuminate Skyrim and her people.
Do you prefer heedlessness to my luminous glory? Go!
Go now, with haste. Take my beacon to Mount Kilkreath.
And I will make you the instrument of my cleansing light.
Go now, the artifact must be reclaimed and Malkoran destroyed.
Restore to me my beacon, that I might guide you toward your destiny.
It is done. The defiler is defeated. Take Dawnbreaker from its pedestal.
You will enter my shrine, destroy Malkoran, and retrieve this mighty blade.
But it is not time for rejoicing. The defiler continues to profane my temple.
Worse still, he uses the power stored within my own token to fuel his foul deeds.
I shall guide you unto it. Find it and return here. And great shall be your reward.
Guide my light through the temple to open the inner sanctum and destroy the defiler.
Malkoran has forced the doors shut. But this is my temple, and it responds to my decree.
I will send down a ray of light. Guide this light through my temple and its doors will open.
But a single candle can banish the darkness of the entire Void. If not you, then someone else.
Return to my figure on Mount Kilkreath, and I shall make you the instrument of my cleansing light.
Listen. Hear me and obey. A foul darkness has seeped into my temple. A darkness that you will destroy.
Look at my temple, lying in ruins. So much for the constancy of mortals, their crafts and their hearts.
Malkoran is vanquished. Skyrim's dead shall remain at rest. This is as it should be. This is because of you.
I have brought you here, mortal, to be my champion. You will enter my temple, retrieve my artifact, and destroy the defiler.
Mortals call it Dawnbreaker, for it was forged in a holy light that breaks upon my foes, burning away corruption and false life.
But the token of my truth lies buried in the ruins of my once great temple, now tainted by a profane darkness skittering within.
The Necromancer Malkoran defiles my shrine with vile corruptions, trapping lost souls left in the wake of this war to do his bidding.
Take the mighty Dawnbreaker and with it purge corruption from the dark corners of the world. Wield it in my name, that my influence may grow.
It matters not. The plant cares nothing for the rays that bring it the warmth of the sun. As you carry Dawnbreaker, so will my light touch the world.

Molag Bal.line: You're mine now, Logrolf.
You pledge your soul to me?
Yes. Further. Into the bowels.
So close. Your prize is waiting.
Weak. He's weak. You're strong. Crush him.
You forsake the weak and pitiful Boethiah?
What do you see from that little cage? Speak.
You will kill. You will kill, or you will die!
No. Kill him. Crush his bones. Tear at his flesh.
Ah. But I have my own champion this time, Logrolf.
The Mace of Molag Bal! I give you its true power, mortal.
Yes. Your reward is waiting for you, mortal. Further down.
Crush the spirit from Logrolf's bones. Make him bend to me.
Mortal. I give you my mace, in all its rusted spitefulness.
Ah ha ha! You mortals and your frail, limp, pathetic bodies. Try it again.
Fine. I offered you a reward. You'll get the freedom your kind enjoys so much.
When your enemies lie broken and bloody before you, know that I will be watching.
But Boethiah's priest is imprisoned as well. But not by me. He is hurt. Suffering.
Fool! Did you think Molag Bal, the Lord of Domination, would so easily reward you?
Now, I have a soul in Oblivion to claim. Take care of the house while I'm gone. Ha ha ha!
Sharp, aren't they? This was the last thing many saw before they were sacrificed in my name.
Save him. Let him perform his rites one more time. And when he does, I will be waiting for him.
Save him. Let him perform his rite one more time. And when he does, we will be waiting for him.
Now, I have a soul in Oblivion that needs claiming. Take care of the house while I'm gone. Ha ha ha!
Rusted. Dry. There was a time when this mace dripped with the blood of the feeble and the worthless.
Revenge? No. I want submission. I want the priest who did this to bend his knee and give me his soul.
But a Daedric Lord has his enemies, and my rival Boethiah had her priest desecrate the altar. Until you came.
Know your place, mortal. Or Molag Bal may just send another champion to claim his mace from your broken corpse.
Yes. It's an altar. Men would come and sacrifice the wretched in my name. The weak would be punished by the strong.
He comes by to perform Boethiah's insulting rites at my altar, but he's been missing. Captured and bound. Left to rot.
But a Daedric Lord has his enemies, and my rival Boethiah had her priest desecrate it. Left it here to decay. Until you came.
But a Daedric Lord has his enemies, and my rival Boethiah had her priest desecrate it. So long since it's tasted blood. Until you came.

Tsun.line: Well met, mage of Skyrim.
I am Tsun, shield-thane to Shor.
By what right do you request entry?
You fought well. I find you worthy.
The joy of battle burns in your heart!
Here in Shor's house we honor it still.
Shor's Hall awaits, if worthy you prove.
I've waited long for such a worthy opponent.
The path is closed, till your courage is tested.
You trespass here, shadow-walker. Shor does not know you.
Perhaps before the end you will earn the right to pass this way.
Prove worthy by warrior's test, and cross the bridge to the blessed hall.
Tarry not too long - the land of the dead is not meant for mortals to linger.
Shor's favor has found you, Dragonborn. The Hall of Valor, heart's-ease, awaits.
Ah! It's been too long since last I faced a doom-driven hero of the dragon blood.
When you are ready to rejoin the living, just bid me so, and I will send you back.
The eyes of Shor are upon you this day. Defeat Alduin, and destroy his soul-snare.
None may pass this perilous bridge 'til I judge them worthy by the warrior's test.
They will sing of this battle in Shor's hall forever. But your fate lies elsewhere.
No shade are you, as usually here passes, but living, you dare the land of the dead.
where welcome, well-earned, awaits those I judge fit to join that fellowship of honor.
Welcome I do not offer, but your errand I will not hinder, if my wrath you can withstand.
But Shor restrained our wrathful onslaught - perhaps, deep-counselled, your doom he foresaw.
It is long since one of the living has entered here. May Shor's favor follow you and your errand.
What brings you, wayfarer grim, to wander here, in Sovngarde, souls-end, Shor's gift to honored dead?
This was a mighty deed! The doom of Alduin encompassed at last, and cleansed is Sovngarde of his evil snare.
The Nords may have forgotten their forefathers' respect for the Clever Craft, but your comrades throng this hall.
Your doom already binds you to your dark mistress, but your errand I will not hinder, if my wrath you can withstand.
