Living or dead, by decree of Shor, none may pass this perilous bridge 'til I judge them worthy by the warrior's test.
The Whalebone Bridge he bade me guard and winnow all those souls whose heroic end sent them here, to Shor's lofty hall
A fateful errand.  No few have chafed to face the Worm since first he set his soul-snare here at Sovngarde's threshold.
Return now to Nirn, with this rich boon from Shor, my lord: a Shout to bring a hero from Sovngarde in your hour of need.
When you have completed your count of days, I may welcome you again, with glad friendship, and bid you join the blessed feasting.
I welcome the chance to challenge the blade of Ysgramor's heir, honored shield-sister to Kodlak Whitemane, whom I've watched for in vain.
Do not mistake the night-shrouded thief's stealthily-taken spoils, stolen and unearned, for a warrior's plunder, won in honorable battle.
I welcome the chance to challenge the blade of Ysgramor's heir, honored shield-brother to Kodlak Whitemane, whom I've watched for in vain.

Black Door.line: What is the music of life?

Nocturnal.line: My, my. What do we have here?
Very well, the conditions are acceptable. You may proceed.
Farewell, Nightingale. See to it the Key stays this time, won't you?
And in the future, I'd suggest you refrain from disappointing me again.
You surprise me, Karliah. This offer is definitely weighted in my favor.
Ah, Karliah. I was wondering when I'd hear from you again. Lose something did we?
Fear not. You'll have your trinkets, your desire for power, your hunger for wealth.
So... once again the Key has been stolen and a "champion" returns it to the Sepulcher.
The Oath has been struck, the die has been cast and your fate awaits you in the Evergloam.
Very well. I name your initiates Nightingale and I restore your status to the same, Karliah.
You're already mine, Karliah. Your terms were struck long ago. What could you possibly offer me now?
I bid you to drink deeply from the Ebonmere, mortal. For this is where the Agent of Nocturnal is born.
Don't mistake my tone for displeasure, after all, you've obediently performed your duties to the letter.
But we both know this has little to do with honor and oaths and loyalty. It's about the reward; the prize.
It's been a number of years since I've set foot on your world. Or perhaps it's been moments. One tends to lose track.
What you fail to realize is your actions were expected and represent nothing more than the fulfillment of your agreement.
Now that Ebonmere has been restored, you stand before me awaiting your accolades; a pat on your head... a kiss on your cheek.

Odahviing.line: Do not try to hide from me.
Amativ! Mu bo kotin stinselok.
Your Thu'um is no match for mine.
Faas nu, zini dein ruthi ahst vaal.
I will conquer where Alduin failed.
Joor mey! What are you doing back there?
Free me, and I will carry you to Skuldafn.
I claim the glory of your defeat, Dovahkiin!
Zok frini grind ko grah drun viiki, Dovahkiin.
Unslaad krosis. Innumerable pardons. I digress.
Ah. I forget. You do not have the dovah speech.
You have reconsidered my offer, hmm? Onikaan kron?
Are you ready to see the world as only a dovah can?
Horvutah med kodaav. Caught like a bear in a trap...
Begone, mage. Do not test my promise to the Dovahkiin.
Your Thu'um is strong, Dovahkiin. But not strong enough!
Hin aar, orin nu. And yet here I am, still your prisoner.
So this is how you defeated Alduin. Ruth... zu'u ni Alduin.
Zu'u Odahviing. Call me when you have need, and I will come if I can.
Hind siiv Alduin, hmm? No doubt you want to know where to find Alduin?
Of course... I could fly you there. But not while imprisoned like this.
Pruzah wundunne wah Wuth Gein. I wish the old one luck in his... quest.
Hmm... krosis. There is one... detail about Skuldafn I neglected to mention.
Among ourselves, of course. Mu ni meyye. None were yet ready to openly defy him.
Aam? Serve you? ...no. Ni tiid. If and when you defeat Alduin, I will reconsider.
Kreh zini! The freedom of the sky beckons! Yet I stay here, nau gol, as promised.
Ahraan. You wound me, Dovahkiin. I may not tell the whole truth, but I am no liar.
Ah. Well. Hmm... krosis. There is one... detail about Skuldafn I neglected to mention.
If Alduin himself could not stand against your Thu'um, I feel no shame in my own defeat.
This is far as I can take you. Krif voth ahkrin. I will look for your return, or Alduin's.
Zu'u bonaar. You went to a great deal of trouble to put me in this... humiliating position.
His door to Sovngarde is at Skuldafn, one of his ancient fanes high in the eastern mountains.
Many of us have begun to question Alduin's lordship, whether his Thu'um was truly the strongest.
Zu'u lost ofan hin laan... now that I have answered your question, you will allow me to go free?
Go and see for yourself. Zu'u ni bo nol het. I will be here... unless Alduin returns before you do.
Saraan uth - I await your command, as promised. Are you ready to see the world as only a dovah can?
Indeed. Orin brit ro. I cannot leave here until you defeat Alduin, which you cannot do without my help.
You will release me - ro laan - if in return I promise to take you to Skuldafn and stop helping Alduin?
And so Alduin's lordship passes to you. Thuri, Dovahkiin. I gladly submit to your mastery of the Thu'um.
And you can trust me. Zu'u ni tahrodiis. Alduin has proven himself unworthy to rule. I go my own way now.
Zok brit uth! I warn you, once you've flown the skies of Keizaal, your envy of the dov will only increase.
Rinik vazah. An apt phrase. Alduin bovul. One reason I came to your call was to test your Thu'um for myself.
Only this. You have the Thu'um of a dovah, but without the wings of one, you will never set foot in Skuldafn.
But I doubt many will wish to exchange Alduin's lordship for the tyranny of Paarthurnax's "Way of the Voice".
As for myself, you've proven your mastery twice over. Thuri, Dovahkiin. I gladly acknowledge the power of your Thu'um.
Mindoraan, pah ok middovahhe lahvraan til. I surely do not need to warn you that all his remaining strength is marshalled there.
Zu'u ni tahrodiis. It was you that lured me here and took me prisoner... vobalaan grahmindol. I have done nothing to earn your distrust.
My... eagerness to meet you in battle was my... undoing, Dovahkiin. I salute your, hmm, low cunning in devising such a grahmindol - strategem.
He has travelled to Sovngarde to regain his strength, devouring the sillesejoor... the souls of the mortal dead. A privilege he jealously guards...

Felldir.line: Feel the thunder of my voice!
You are not worthy of Sovngarde!
Use Dragonrend, to drag him down!
Again, together! Alduin is strong!
Today you die, your doom has come.
I answer the call of the Dragonborn.
We cannot fight the foe in this mist!
I've waited an eternity for this day.
Die, World-Eater, in despair and fear!
Stand together and we shall defeat him!
Again, as one! The World-Eater fears us!
Which is why I brought the Elder Scroll.
Now ends this tale, my eternity be damned!
His power crumbles - do not pause for breath!
You will die for the glory of the Dragonborn!
We can shatter his power if we Shout together!
You may strike me down, but your doom is near.
One more time, and the World-Eater must face us!
All hail the Dragonborn! Hail her with great praise!
All hail the Dragonborn! Hail him with great praise!
I never agreed. And if you are right, I will not need it.
Alduin escaped us long ago. Today we take our well-tempered revenge.
Hold, comrades - let us counsel take before battle is blindly joined.
Yes, the World-Eater is gone... may the spirits have mercy on our souls.
But none have yet stood against Alduin himself. Galthor, Sorri, Birkir...
You are banished! Alduin, we shout you out from all our endings unto the last!
Our ancient debt for Alduin's reprieve is now repaid - the long night is ended!
Alduin's mist is more than a snare - its shadowy gloom is his shield and cloak.
Again, and we break his grim hold over Shor's realm, and redeem it for eternity!
He will come. He cannot ignore our defiance. And why should he fear us, even now?
You do not understand. Alduin cannot be slain like a lesser dragon. He is beyond our strength.
But with four Voices joined, our valor combined, we can blast the mist and bring him to battle.
Hold, Alduin on the Wing! Sister Hawk, grant us your sacred breath to make this contract heard!
Begone, World-Eater! By words with older bones than your own we break your perch on this age and send you out!

Emperor.line: I thank you for your courtesy.
You will hear me out, then? Good.
Now, on to the business at hand...
Well, on to the business at hand then, hmm?
Now, on to the business at hand I suppose, hmm?
Well. Perhaps you'll at least consider it, hmm?
Hmph. Pity. I had hoped to obtain even a modicum of justice.
Well? I won't fight you, so we may as well get this over with.
Yes, I must die. And you must deliver the blow. It is simply the way it is.
Yes, I must die. And you must deliver the blow. It is simply the way it is.
This person, whomever he or she may be, must be punished for their treachery.
This person, whomever he or she may be, must be punished for their treachery.
Come now, don't be shy. You haven't come this far just to stand there gawking.
But I wonder... would you suffer an old man a few more words before the deed is done?
But I wonder... would you suffer an old man a few more words before the deed is done?
But I wonder... would you suffer an old man a few more words before the deed is done?
Oh, you can save your sinister bravado. I'll not go to my grave whimpering like an infant.
I know why you're here. I must die. And you must deliver the blow. It is simply the way it is.
But of course. You and I have a date with destiny. But so it is with assassins and emperors, hmm?
You and I have a date with destiny, it would seem. But so it is with assassins and emperors, hmm?
Tsk. How unfortunate. I had hoped you would honor an old man's dying wish. Well, let's be done with it then.
And, once more, I prove Commander Maro the fool. I told him you can't stop the Dark Brotherhood. Never could.
You will kill me, and I have accepted that fate. But regardless of your path through life, I sense in you a certain... ambition.
You will kill me, and I have accepted that fate. But regardless of your path through life, I sense in you a certain... ambition.
Once you have been rewarded for my assassination, I want you to kill the very person who ordered it. Would you do me this kindness?
Once you have been rewarded for my assassination, I want you to kill the very person who ordered it. Would you do me this kindness?
So I ask of you a favor. An old man's dying wish. While there are many who would see me dead, there is one who set the machine in motion.
So I ask of you a favor. An old man's dying wish. While there are many who would see me dead, there is one who set the machine in motion.

Babette.line: I heard about what happened...
Tell me, sister. Are the stories true?
But to be outnumbered twenty to one...
Tell me, brother. Are the stories true?
Word certainly travels fast, doesn't it?
You must be talking about the caravan job...
You're referring to that blacksmith contract?
Ah, you must mean my little adventure on the ship...
Your latest exploit certainly has the Sanctuary talking...
Come now. What everyone else is saying. It can't possibly be true...
It's the part with the severed head I find most impressive. My compliments.
The temple contract? Well, I certainly won't deny my involvement. He he he he he.
It was a suicide mission! Astrid said as much. Yet here you stand, hale and hearty.
I'm just trying to imagine you hiding inside the body of a mammoth. Bloody brilliant idea.
To kill triplets, secretly, and then have the whole thing blamed on the mother. Remarkable.
Ah, contracts like that are few and far between, it seems. Makes me long for the old days...
The Redoran contract? Yes, you heard correctly. In and out of Vvardenfell in less than forty eight hours.
Well, I just did what had to be done. Nothing more. Nothing less. I'm just glad I lived to tell the tale.
But really. A horker, some twine, three wood elves, and a hatchet? Points for creativity, if nothing else.
Oh, you mean in that manor house? During the thunderstorm? Heh. What can I say? It happened, just like you heard.
In truth? It was so easy, I feel a bit guilty. Once again, the sweet, innocent child earned more trust than was wise.
You give me too much credit. For how could I fail? I am, after all, just a little girl. What threat could I possibly pose? He he he he he.
You infiltrated the position, eliminated the target, and made it look like an accident. Impressive. I'd say you earned your bonus on that one!
I am the perfect weapon. My innocent, childish form grants me nearly unrestricted access. Poison or a good bite usually takes care of the rest.
By Sithis, you slew everyone! Well, except for that one you kept alive, to serve as witness. Does your wickedness, or your skill, know no bounds?

Hermaeus Mora.line: Come closer. Bask in my presence.
Now it is in your hands. Let us work wonders together...
When that time comes, you shall take his place as my emissary. What say you?
Indeed. Speak with me when the box has been opened, and all shall be revealed.
Go forth. Do the biddings of Septimus, for he is working my will in your plane.
Once that infernal lockbox is opened, he will have exhausted his usefulness to me.
Be warned. Many have thought as you do. I have broken them all. You shall not evade me forever.
For hundreds of years it's been shut away from the world. Septimus was a useful tool for unleashing it.
Now you have my Oghma Infinium. It contains the knowledge of the ages as revealed to Xarses, my loyal servant.
I am Hermaeus Mora. I am the guardian of the unseen, and knower of the unknown. I have been watching you, mortal.
Your free will is an illusion. Whether you acknowledge me or not is your own business. But I will be in your mind.
Your continuing aid to Septimus renders him increasingly obsolete. He has served me well, but his time is nearing its end.
Who do you think brought Septimus here? Who do you think protected you on your journey to open the box and loose my knowledge on this world?

Potema.line: Yes! Yes! Return me to this realm!
You ants don't have the power to bind me!
What! What are you doing?! You fools! You cannot bind me to your wills!

Azura.line: Tell me when you are ready, mortal.
Have faith, mortal. I will be watching over you.
As was destined, you are free to use my Star as you see fit.
Are you ready to enter the Star and purge the soul of Malyn Varen?
No, only one option remains. I will send you inside the Star. You will banish Malyn's soul there.
Farewell, mortal. Know that Azura will be guarding over the threads of your fate in the Twilight.
The Star is free to purify itself. Don't worry, mortal. I will return you before you are cleansed.
Go now. I have seen the threads of your fate in the Twilight, and you still have much to accomplish.
My Star has been restored and Malyn's soul has been consigned to Oblivion. You have done well, mortal.
But his soul still resides within, protected by his enchantments. Until he is purged, my artifact is useless to you.
Greetings, mortal. You have followed my guidance through the veils of Twilight and rescued my Star from Malyn Varen.
Eventually, the Star will fade back into my realm in Oblivion, but I doubt you have the hundred or so years it would take to wait.
Oblivion has been watching you since the day you were conceived, mortal. Do not think that your life has been served by your will alone.

Aventus Aretino.line: Grelod the Kind. Is she... you know?
It's Honorhall Orphanage, in Riften.
You've come at last! I knew you would!
This is the best news I've ever gotten!
Finally! My prayers have been answered!
Well? Grelod the Kind. Is she... you know?
It worked! I knew you'd come, I just knew it!
Wait! That's not how this is supposed to work!
What are you doing? I thought we were friends!
Please don't kill Constance Michel. She really is kind.
Grelod is the headmistress of Honorhall Orphanage. But not for long.
You came to visit! It's so much better here with Grelod gone. Thanks to you.
Of course you are! I prayed, and you came, and now you'll accept my contract!
It took so long. So very long. But now that you're here, you can accept my contract.
Aha! I knew you could do it! I just knew it! I knew the Dark Brotherhood would save me!
Please... How long must I do this? I keep praying, Night Mother. Why won't you answer me?
So now I'm all alone. And the Jarl said I had to go to Honorhall Orphanage. It's not fair!
Grelod, you old crone. You'll get what you deserve. The Dark Brotherhood will see to that...
Here, just like I promised. This should fetch you a nice price. And thank you. Thank you again.
I'll go back to the Orphanage in a while. I'll give them time to, you know... clean up the mess.
I have a family heirloom you can have. Supposed to be sort of valuable. I hope that's all right.
When I grow up, I'm going to be an assassin. That way I can help lots of children, just like you.
You don't have to say anything. There's no need. You're here, so I know you'll accept my contract.
I mean, I knew the Dark Brotherhood was good... just not that good! You killed the old hag before I even asked!
So I ran away, and came home. And performed the Black Sacrament. Now you're here! And you can kill Grelod the Kind!
My mother, she... she died. I... I'm all alone now. So they sent me to that terrible orphanage in Riften. Honorhall.
Sweet Mother, sweet Mother, send your child unto me, for the sins of the unworthy must be baptized in blood and fear.
Sweet Mother, sweet Mother, send your child unto me, for the sins of the unworthy must be baptized in blood and fear.
The headmistress is an evil, cruel woman. They call her Grelod the Kind. But she's not kind. She's terrible. To all of us.
Please hurry. To be honest, I'm kind of lonely here. As much as I hated getting sent to Honorhall, I really miss my friends there...
I've never been more sure about anything in my entire life. Someone like Grelod doesn't deserve to live one more day. She's a monster.
I did the Black Sacrament, over and over. With the body, and the... the things. And then you came! An assassin from the Dark Brotherhood!
She got sick, last winter, when the snows came. And she just... she never got better. Not all year. One night she fell asleep and...  never woke up.
Oh, and please, take this as payment. It's an old family heirloom, supposed to be pretty valuable. It should fetch you a nice price. Thank you again!

Clavicus Vile.line: You have business with Clavicus Vile?
And I was so close to being rid of you.
Now, as for your wish... Power, was it?
Ah, you've got the axe! And my dog. Splendid.
Now, as for your wish... An end to the war, was it?
You're worth putting in the extra effort for, pooch.
Ah, there you are. I've been waiting for you to arrive.
Oh, if I had my full power, granting that would be trivial.
But only if you use the axe to kill Barbas. Simple as that.
Ugh. That insufferable pup? Forget it. Request denied. No deal.
So, what's your heart's desire? What kind of deal can we strike?
Come to think of it, I know of a win-win situation for both of us.
Hrmph. You're no fun at all. Guess I'll have to make my own fun elsewhere.
I'd simply snap my fingers, and everyone in Skyrim would die. War resolved.
And I'm sure Barbas doesn't want me to have that axe. He'd want it this way.
Collect the Rueful Axe from Rimerock Burrow, and I believe we'll have a deal.
And I could absorb the spirit of poor, dead Barbas. He'd still be reunited with me.
Oh, sure, he'll be back in a century or two, but think of the fun I can have 'til then.
Oh, fine. Have my boon and be done with it. I've got more interesting deals to make, anyway.
Well... perhaps there is a way he could earn his place back at my side. Maybe. But no promises.
Yeah, yeah, dog gets master, master gets cosmic axe, everyone's happy. Just get over here, mutt.
And with the pup back, I'll be restored to my full power. There's a whole world just waiting for me!
It's nice to be rid of that detestable dog. Things are so much more fun without his incessant preaching.
Yeah, rub it in. Just imagine what I could get done with my full power and none of these pesky "morals."
There's an axe. An incredibly powerful axe. An axe powerful enough for me to have quite a bit of fun, indeed.
The choice is yours, friend. We're all counting on you to make the right decision. Put him out of our misery!
If you bring it to me, I'll grant you my boon. No strings attached. No messy surprises. At least, not for you.
Absolutely! Now I can reabsorb his body, and return to full power. All without having to listen to his whining.
I'm glad to be rid of him. Even if it does mean I'm stuck in this pitiful shrine, in the back end of... nowhere.
Enjoy that axe! And don't worry about poor old Barbas. After all, he really should have picked a better friend...
It's a shame you wished for something so dull as me taking back the mutt. Quite the lack of imagination on your part.
I'll make sure this war ends, all right. Just as soon as the dragons swarm across Skyrim and wipe out all of you puny...
In Rimerock Burrow, there's an artifact called the Rueful Axe. Bring it to me, and I'll take Barbas back. Simple as that.
Ah, that feels so much better! You forget how nice supreme power feels until you've been stuck in a cave for a few years.
A lack of ambition like that really ought to be punished. Perhaps by turning you into a worm, or maybe a few decades of...
Excellent work. A hero and his faithful companion, retrieving the ancient artifact for the prince. It's almost... storybook.
Really? Power? You're a dragonborn; you already have more power than most people who aren't immense, fire-breathing monsters.
Ah, but it almost seems a shame to give a weapon like that away, doesn't it? I suppose I could be persuaded to let you keep it...
By all means, let's hear it. It's the least I could do, since you already helped me grant one final wish for my last worshippers...
Still, I've got this axe, and even if I can't use it, I'm sure someone would make a deal for it. Maybe I'll have a word with Molag Bal...
As I recall, it's resting in Rimerock Burrow. Barbas can lead you right to it. The little mutt might even earn his place back at my side.
You can be a part of a tremendous power - all you have to do is let your soul be absorbed by this axe. I never said it would be your power...
They were suffering so from vampirism, and begged me for a cure. Then you came and ended their misery! I couldn't have planned it better myself.
As much as I hate to say it, you're almost as powerful as I am right now. But that's just because half of my power resides in that mutt, Barbas.
Really? Power? Haven't you already killed enough of your foes using the more mundane means at your disposal? Swords, magic, etc.? How... uninspired.

Mehrunes Dagon.line: You think to betray Mehrunes Dagon? Suffer!
Before you go, mortal. One final challenge. Ha ha ha.
Only Dagon can declare if a pawn is worth keeping. I. Have. Spoken.
Kill him. Take your rightful place as my champion, or I will crush you.
You have been a worthy tool. Fill the world with destruction in my name.
I am pleased, mortal. I will give you my Razor. Use it to wreak havoc on Tamriel.
Spare me your pitiful pride. You are but a tool of my ambitions, mortal. Never forget that.
Place your hands on my altar one last time, and you will witness the power of Mehrunes Dagon for yourself.
Place your hands on my altar one last time, and you will witness the power of Mehrunes Dagon for yourself.
You. Mortal. You are worthy of speaking to. You have claimed the pieces of my Razor. It has been an amusing game to witness.
But Dagon does not declare a winner while there is a pawn on the board. Kill Silus. He and his family have served their purpose.

Peryite.line: So you have, mortal. Go now. Kill Orchendor.
Embrace order and hard truth, mortal. Good bye.
And yet you have done my bidding. Why, I wonder?
The pettiness of mortals. I had almost forgotten.
Return when the elf lies dead and it will be yours.
In a sense, but no more poisoned than a fool after too much wine.
Go, seek your fate. I will be watching, and perhaps we will meet again - afterwards.
Breathe deep, mortal. I would have you hear me well, so let these vapors fill your lungs.
I will not stand for betrayal. I want you to go to Bthardamz and kill Orchendor, in my name.
I sent a blessing to Mundus, a wasting plague that infected a scattering of Breton villages.
Another Overseer will replace Orchendor, when the time comes. For now all is cleansed and ordered.
An impertinent question. The elf must die. Either carry out my will or do not. You are not the only mortal agent I could choose.
Did you leave any alive? The Afflicted are mere vessels for my Blessing. It will spread to others through their touch and my own.
I can grant you a powerful token for this task. An escutcheon of Dwemer make, capable of shielding you from both the spell and sword.
One of my monks, the elf Orchendor, was sent to gather these Afflicted. He shepherded them into Bthardamz for me, but has since lost his way.
Well done, mortal. All things are in their order, and Orchendor roams the Pits. His betrayal will be punished, and your obedience is rewarded.
I have watched you for some time, you know. The decisions you've made intrigue me, and I wonder if you are the proper agent for a task of mine.

Night Mother.line: Yet another child has prayed to their mother.
Listener. I am your only salvation. Come. Embrace me.
You must speak with the Farmhand at Chillfurrow farm.
Oh, but I will speak. I will speak to you. For you are the one.
You must speak with Astrid. Here, in the Dark Brotherhood Sanctuary.
But our work is just beginning. Approach me, Listener, and hear my words.
Astrid is dead. It is as it should be. May she find redemption in the Void.
It is done. Emperor Titus Mede II lies dead, and the Dark Brotherhood yet lives.
But first, inform Nazir of your plans. For you are the Listener, and must bind this Family together.
Speak with Amaund Motierre at the Bannered Mare in Whiterun. He will know the true Emperor's location.
Poor Cicero. Dear Cicero. Such a humble servant. But he will never hear my voice. For he is not the Listener.
But while you live, the Dark Brotherhood lives. We must fulfill our contract. Emperor Titus Mede II must be eliminated.
Speak with the Blasphemous Priest at the Temple of Kynareth, in Whiterun. Accept his gold, then kill the target. Hail Sithis!
Tell Cicero the time has come. Tell him the words he has been waiting for, all these years: "Darkness rises when silence dies."
Speak with the Corrupt Agent at the Penitus Oculatus Outpost, in Dragon Bridge. Accept his gold, then kill the target. Hail Sithis!
Speak with the Scheming Servant at the Keep in Markarth. Accept his gold, then eliminate the target. So begins a contract, bound in blood.
Yes, you. You, who shares my iron tomb, who warms my ancient bones. I give you this task - journey to Volunruud. Speak with Amaund Motierre.
Speak with the Indolent Farmer at the Vilemyr Inn, in Ivarstead. Accept his gold, then kill the target. So begins a contract, bound in blood.
Speak with the Nervous Patron at Candlehearth Hall, in Windhelm. Accept his gold, then kill the target. So begins a contract, bound in blood.
Speak with the Desperate Gambler at the Barracks, in Windhelm. Accept his gold, then eliminate the target. So begins a contract, bound in blood.
Speak with the Wary Outlaw at the Bee and Barb, in Riften. Accept his gold, and then eliminate the target. So begins a contract, bound in blood.
Speak with the Unemployed Laborer at the Frostfruit Inn, in Rorikstead. Accept his gold, then kill the target. So begins a contract, bound in blood.
Speak with the Dishonored Skald at the Bards College, in Solitude. Accept his gold, then eliminate the target. So begins a contract, bound in blood.
Speak with the Griefstricken Chef, at the Windpeak Inn, here in Dawnstar. Accept his gold, then eliminate the target. Hail the Listener! Hail Sithis!

Namira.line: Wallow in your wretchedness, my newest champion.
Be grateful for what time I give you until then.
I give you my ring. Wear it, and when you feast on the flesh of the dead, I will grant you my power.
Mortal. I am Namira, the Lady of Decay. Your consumption of the blood and bile of Arkay's own is pleasing to me.
Your mockery will be the end of you. And on that day, another servant will feast on your entrails and discover a ring clenched in her teeth.
